title: Dark Feline
location_name: Abberton (Essex) - Abberton reservoir, south of Colchester
notes: A large black cat was seen at the reservoir.

title: Beast of Bont
notes: Something here killed over fifty sheep in the space of a year; police marksmen were called in to comb the area.

title: Large Prints
notes: The family at the farm reported finding two cat-like paw prints in fresh mud, fifteen centimetres in length.

title: Cat Attack
notes: Police reported that a man had called them saying that a large cat had attacked a deer. The deer had been found with marks around the throat area.

title: Large Black Cat
location_name: Allithwaite (Cumbria) - Outskirts of village, off the main road
notes: Walking along the side of the main road out of village, this witness watched a large black panther-like cat in one of the fields, heading towards a nearby patch of woodland. The witness could compare the height of the creatures to nearby cows and estimated the cat to be three foot (ninety centimetres) high at the shoulder. After a minute, the creature walked into the trees.

title: Black Cat
notes: While driving to the power station, this witness spotted a black cat larger than an Alsatian. The creature had a long bushy tail and sat in a crouched position. The witness watched the creature for three or four seconds and was slightly unnerved by it.

title: Panther-Like Creature
location_name: Anglesey (Gwynedd) - Several areas over the island, including Llandona Beach
notes: A large black cat, thought to be either a puma or panther, has been blamed for attacking horses and stealing foals.
address: Llanddona Beach, Unnamed Road, 8UN, Beaumaris LL58 8UW, UK

title: Jet Black Cat
location_name: Ashford (Kent) - Close to railway line, Ham Street
notes: A man spotted an Alsatian sized cat near his home. The creature was jet black and had a long tail. The witness had recently lost his own pet cat and speculated that it had been eaten by the panther.

title: Trentham Drive
notes: A puma-sized cat, tabbyish in colour, was seen emerging from the garden of a bungalow before crossing the road. The creature made a loud noise before disappearing into another garden.

title: ABC
notes: One of many big cat sightings reported in Suffolk during 1996.

title: Large Feline
notes: A large unknown cat was seen by both motorists and a policeman.

title: Dead Farm Animals
notes: Attacks on animals, including a dead pregnant sheep which had been stripped to the bone, convinced many farmers in the area that a large cat prowled the area.

title: Beast of Baglan
location_name: Baglan (West Glamorgan) - General area and surrounding countryside
notes: Known about by locals for many years, stories of the big cat were not taken seriously until 2005 when an off-duty police officer spotted the creature along Ladies Walk. Hair and print analysis showed the cat to be a puma.

title: Operation Big Cat
location_name: Ballybogy (aka Ballybogey) (County Antrim) - General area and surrounding countryside
notes: A joint army and police team created 'Operation Big Cat' after two dead calves were found and a local man admitted releasing a puma and a panther from his private collection. Although there were dozens of sightings and livestock attacks, the cats were never caught.

title: Lynx
notes: A large cat seen as it stepped in front of a car was later identified as a lynx.

title: Beast of Banburyshire
notes: Named as a big cat hotspot, this area of Oxfordshire has had dozens of reports of large felines over the first five years of the twenty-first century.

title: Beast of Banwell
notes: While walking her dog, Helen Stokes encountered a large black creature that she described as 'very fast'. Both owner and pet ran away from the creature.

title: Big Cat
notes: An unidentified large cat was seen in the woods in the area.
address: Barnhamcross Common, Bury Rd, Thetford IP24 3EP, UK

title: Puma
notes: This large black cat was seen loitering close to the town centre.
address: Civic Centre, N Walk, Barnstaple EX32 2SB, UK

title: Lynx
notes: This cat was shot and killed by a local farmer, who claimed both police and the Government asked him to destroy the body and tell no one about the incident.

title: Darting Large Cat
datetime: March 2016 (cat), phantom horseman on first full moon in March
notes: A cat, reportedly the size of a small horse, darted out in front of a car. The previous year, a panther was observed on the moor, from which the witnesses fled. The moor is also said to be haunted by a phantom horseman who appears once a year.

title: Lion
location_name: Belfast (County Antrim) - Cavehill Park, Upper Hightown end
notes: Several independent witnesses called the police to report a sandy coloured cat in this park. The nearby Belfast Zoo reported no animals missing. A search of the area by police failed to find the feline.

title: Beast of Bennachie
notes: This big black cat was blamed for dead sheep and causing disruption in the neighbourhood.

title: Owd Scriven o' Brompton
location_name: Berrington (Shropshire) - Area once known as Banky Piece (no longer exists) and church
notes: The wooden effigy on display in the church is known as 'Owd Scriven o' Brompton', and is said to have been a knight who encountered a lion in the area. The knight managed to slay the big cat, although was left with a large scar on his face, which is visible in the effigy.

title: Beast of Biddlestone
notes: Another ABC hotspot, dozens of witnesses have encountered a large black cat in the region over recent years. In 2007, a group of off-duty police officers and their families spotted the creature; they described at the cat as being the size of large dog but with a lower profile.

title: Lioness
notes: Seen by the same woman twice in one day, the lion-like cat was described as 'champagne coloured'. A photograph taken in 2008 shows what appears to be a large black cat, though without a scale reference, it may just be a small cat.

title: Panther
notes: A father and son walking through Bleasby spotted a large black panther-like cat, the size of a German Shepherd, across the road and through the hedgerows into a field.

title: Beast of Bodmin
notes: Another famous cryptid, the Beast of Bodmin is thought by some to be a large black cat, while others believe the creature is more paranormal in nature. Regardless, dozens of sightings span years.

title: Black Cat
location_name: Bolton (Greater Manchester) - Farnworth cemetery, Darcy Lever
notes: Alanah and Sherri-Lee Belk spotted a large black cat from around fifteen metres away in a field. The creature ran off in the opposite direction.
address: Cemetery Rd, Farnworth, Bolton BL4 7SF, UK

title: Puma
notes: In 2005, salesman Kevin Hamersley from Wiltshire said he spotted saw a puma along this road, just one of many sightings in the region. Thirteen years later, a panther was spotted on the patio of another West Cliff resident. It is speculated (and highly unlikely) that the creature lives in the nearby Alum Chime.

title: Black Leopard
location_name: Boxted (Suffolk) - Stream near woodland behind houses, and B1066
notes: The Big Cats of Suffolk website reports that a motorcyclist and passenger chased a large black cat with a long tail seen near a stream. The creature disappeared into a hedge. Two other large black cat sightings were also reported along the nearby Boxted to Hartest Road mid 2010s.
address: Boxted, Bury Saint Edmunds IP29 4JR, UK

title: Large Cat
location_name: Braintree (Essex) - Near Capel Hill Golf Course
notes: Walkers reported seeing a large cat in the area.

title: Cheetah
location_name: Brighton (Sussex) - Junction of Mill Road and Dyke Road
notes: Driving home, Douglas Owen encountered a large cat resembling a cheetah lying on a grassy bank. After watching it for a short while, the cat ran off towards nearby houses.
address: Dyke Rd, Brighton and Hove, UK

title: Big Cat Attack
notes: At least five witnesses watched a large black cat attack a dog before running off to chase nearby ponies. The incident was reported to the police, and although the cat remained undiscovered, New Forest keepers found a collie dog with a broken leg.

title: Large Cat
notes: Sighted in fields around this area, this cat appeared to be too large to be a normal feline, and too small to be anything else.

title: Hunting Cat
notes: Two witnesses watched a large cat as it chased two deer across a field. They could see the mud flicking up from the feline's feet as it ran. The witnesses searched the area and found a large paw print, twice the size of their retriever.

title: College Cat
notes: Both a caretaker and the groundsman saw a massive black cat on the grounds. It has been speculated the creature was the so called 'Fen Tiger', a large cat-like cryptid for which sightings around Cambridgeshire date back at least 30 years.
address: St John's College, St Johns St, Cambridge CB2 1TP, UK

title: Clouded Leopard
notes: An escapee from Howletts Park, this leopard lived for eight months in the Kent countryside, before being shot by a farmer.

title: Labrador-Sized Cat
location_name: Carlisle (Cumbria) - Bypass close to wooded area near Asda
notes: Raymond Sant reported seeing a large black cat with a long tail, the size of a Labrador, as he and his wife drove to Asda.

title: Lioness
notes: A reported lioness was spotted roaming the area, though a police search could find nothing.

title: Brown Feline
location_name: Chippenham (Wiltshire) - Railway line near Cocklebury Lane Bridge
notes: A large brown cat could be seen from Cocklebury Lane Bridge as it crossed the railway line.

title: Huge Cat
notes: Driving along the M25, Karen Thomas reported seeing a large black cat on the edge of a field. The creature was described as ten times larger than a dog.

title: Tiger
location_name: Church Fenton (North Yorkshire) - Fields in the area
notes: Police were dispatched to this area after they received two independent reports of a tiger in the district. An RAF aircraft in the area joined the search, which ultimately found no evidence of the cat.

title: Brown Cat
notes: A large brown alien cat was reported in this area in 1996.

title: Killer Cat
notes: This mysterious creature killed dozens of sheep over the summer months, tearing out their throats. Two people who saw the beast said it was dark in colour and larger than a dog. Not all agreed with the belief that the creature was a cat, some stating that the entity was a gwyllgi, a large black dog.

title: Black ABC
notes: A large black cat was spotted along this Colchester road at different times by multiple witnesses.

title: Dark Brown Cat
location_name: Colonsay (Argyll and Bute) - Close to a stone dyke
notes: In 1999, a couple visiting the island observed a dark brown large cat, much bigger than a wild cat, roughly the size of a collie. Thirteen years later, the body of a fox was discovered on the island, worrying the locals as no foxes have ever been found here.

title: Eaten Calves
notes: After two calves found half eaten, fears were expressed that a large cat was on the loose in the area. A local RSPCA inspector said it was unlikely that a dog would be able to cause so much damage to the dead animals.
address: Convoy Townparks, Convoy, Co. Donegal, Ireland

title: Large Black Cat
notes: One witness reported seeing a large black cat, like a puma, in this area.

title: Puma
notes: A sleek black cat was seen hiding in the forest.

title: Fast Feline
location_name: Crostwick (Norfolk) - North Walsham road, Crostwick near Norwich
notes: A large cat quickly ran off after being seen standing around seven metres from a witness.

title: Cat or Seal?
notes: When the decaying remains of a creature the size of a calf with large canine teeth washed up on the beach, many jumped to the conclusion it was the body of the Beast of Exmoor. Later analysis of the carcass suggested it was a seal.
address: Downend Point Camping, Croyde Rd, Downend, Croyde, Braunton EX33 1QE, UK

title: Lion
notes: Several people, including a policeman, reported seeing a cat resembling a lion or lioness near woodland in the area. Public warnings were issued, and a helicopter dispatched, but no big cat was found.
address: Sutherland Way, Cuffley, Potters Bar EN6, UK

title: Lion?
location_name: Darsham (Suffolk) - Near Little Chef restaurant (currently Two Magpies Bakery)
notes: Reported by Big Cats of Suffolk, two witnesses spotted a sandy coloured cat, similar to a lion or Puma, which could be seen sunning itself in the early morning light.

title: Savaged Lambs
location_name: Denby Dale (West Yorkshire) - Farms in the area
notes: A large cat was blamed for several sheep killings in the area after one farmer watched a big black feline skulking around on his land.

title: Lion?
location_name: Denshaw (Greater Manchester) - Between the M62 and Denshaw, along the Saddleworth moor
notes: Driver Helen Morris reported to a local newspaper seeing a large cat in the middle of the road. The creature was described as having a tawny coat and long tail, resembling a lioness.

title: Panther
location_name: Dorchester (Dorset) - Field near Dorchester Football Stadium, and Talbothays Road
notes: This large black cat, said by the witness to be the size of an Alsatian, was observed for several seconds in 2007. The year previous, another (or the same) black cat was spotted along Talbothays Road.

title: Adult Puma
notes: Out walking her dog, a woman spotted what she thought was an adult puma standing on the roadside. It ran off through a hedgerow after a few seconds. Another person also spotted the creature in the same area.
address: Shore Rd, Drigg, Holmrook CA19, UK

title: Missing Sheep
location_name: Dromara (County Down) - Farms around Dromara and Hillsborough; Hill Road, Ballynahinch Road and Mullaghdrin Road areas
notes: A large cat was held accountable for mauling and killing sheep in this area. Police issued a warning for anyone who spotted a large cat to contact them.
address: Ballynahinch Rd & Mullaghdrin Rd, Dromore BT25 1EX, UK

title: Panther?
notes: A husband and his daughter spotted a panther-like cat while out walking.

title: Puma
notes: Several witnesses reported seeing a large golden cat in the area, though it soon vanished without trace.

title: ABC Footprints
location_name: East Mersea (Essex) - Sea wall near Cooper's beach holiday park
notes: More than seventeen ABC footprints were found here, each 6 inches across with extended claws. Bearing in mind that cats do not walk with their claws extended, this could well be either a mistake or a fake.
address: Church Ln, Mersea Island, Colchester CO5 8TN, UK

title: Big Cat
notes: Following up reports of a large cat, the area was combed by police with dogs; nothing was ever found.

title: Labrador-Sized Cat
notes: Whilst walking his two Yorkshire terriers, John Hescott encountered a large black cat. The feline walked away from a children's play area towards a service road. The sighting was reported to the police.

title: Black Cheetah
notes: A taxi driver spotted a black cat, shaped like a cheetah and larger than a big dog, running across the road.

title: Large Black Cat
notes: The witness who saw this cat said it was the size of a small pony - he watched it as it passed within three meters of him.

title: Cat on the Wall
location_name: Eggington (Bedfordshire) - White House, an old people's home
notes: Seen sitting on the wall several times during October, residents called the police when the large cat looked like it was going to have a faceoff with their pet cat.
address: High St, Eggington, Leighton Buzzard LU7 9PQ, UK

title: White Lion
notes: This large albino cat was seen close to the cottages and was reported to resemble a lion. The same area is reportedly haunted by a headless white woman.

title: Big Cats
notes: Elveden residents reported several sightings of a large cat or cats during this month.

title: Lynx
location_name: Evesham (Hereford & Worcester) - Area around Unipart site, Shinehill Lane
notes: A brown, pointed eared cat was spotted on this site by a security guard, the third such sighting at this location.

title: Beast of Exmoor
notes: Police, soldiers, big game hunters... all have staked out the moor at various times over the past thirty years in the hope of catching the big cat that has killed hundreds of sheep. None have succeeded.

title: Large Black Cat
notes: Two sisters spotted what they described as a black panther cross the top of the field by the hedge line. The creature then graciously jumped through the hedge and disappeared.

title: Lynx
location_name: Exmouth (Devon) - Hulham Road, woods (no longer there?)
notes: Two women walking close to woodland spotted a large sandy coloured Lynx in a field.

title: Large Cats
notes: The Big Cats of Suffolk website reported two large cats were reported in the village in 2003, one a large sandy coloured cat, the other black in colour.

title: Lynx
notes: The sightings of a large cat in the neighbourhood are said to date back at least two hundred years.

title: First ABC?
notes: One of the first modern ABC sightings, Water Board worker Ernest Jellett staff spotted a large black cat. Over the following months, others witnessed a cheetah stalking the area - it was never caught.

title: Brown ABC
notes: Part of a rash of sightings of ABCs in and around the Ipswich area during 1996. The creature was nicknamed 'Paws' by local media.

title: Cat the Size of an Alsatian
notes: Seen twice by different people in the same area, this cat possessed a thick coat and left a strong smell of urine from one place where it had lain.

title: Slinky Cat
notes: While feeding their horses, two women spotted a long tailed black cat emerge out of nearby woodland. Several of the horses fled across the field, and when the women approached the black feline, it ran off.

title: Tailless Tiger
notes: Two witnesses running in the area observed a three metre long cat drinking from the river. One of the witnesses said the creature looked like a tailless tiger with white eyes but no pupils. The creature ran off into a nearby field when it spotted the runners. A few days later local papers reported the deaths of several local sheep in the same field where the creature had run.
address: Glen Nevis, Fort William PH33, UK

title: Brownish ABC
notes: A couple of reports claim ABC sightings in this region, both of brownish cats.

title: Cow Sized Cat
location_name: Gatwick (Essex) - Fields parallel to railway track near the airport
notes: A woman travelling on the train spotted a black cat stalking a herd of cows in a field. She reported that the creature was about half the size of one of the nearby cows.
address: London Gatwick Airport (LGW), Horley, Gatwick RH6 0NP, UK

title: Leaping Cat
notes: A witness watched a large black cat as it leapt around in a cornfield, as if hunting. A week later the witness and a friend came across footprints almost the size of dinner plates in the same field.

title: Black Cat
location_name: Glen Prosen (Angus) - Wooded hill in the area
notes: Disturbed by a loud scream, two walkers looked around and spotted a black cat, around four foot long with a long thick tail and shoulders that were higher than its body. The cat looked at the walkers then disappeared into the bushes. Dogs could then be heard barking in the distance from several directions.

title: Black Cat
notes: Policemen, farmers and walkers have all spotted an unidentified large black cat in the area.

title: Yellow Eyed Creature
notes: One witness reported throwing a stone at a large black shape, not realising it was a large cat until it opened its yellow eyes.
address: Bonny Clough Wood, Huddersfield HD7, UK

title: Road Crosser
notes: A woman who stopped her car to answer a mobile phone spotted a large black cat cross the road in front. The cat stood three foot high and possessed a long black tail that drooped downwards. The sighting lasted for around five seconds.

title: Dark Fawn ABC
location_name: Great Wigborough (Essex) - Exact location not known
notes: The dark fawn ABC here was believed to be a mountain lion.

title: Black Leopard
notes: A Greenock couple were surprised to find a large black cat in their front garden which quickly ran away. It is believed that there is at least one adult ABC and two cubs living in the area.

title: White Cat
location_name: Gunthorpe (Nottinghamshire) - River Trent, riverside road leading to West Stockwith at the end of Commonpiece Lane
notes: This light coloured feline was the size of a pig and was said to have vanished when a local man tried to kick the creature.

title: The Catbeast
notes: Dubbed 'catbeast' by local press, this alien big cat would appear to be more than one entity, as descriptions range from 'black panther' to 'tiger'.

title: Grey ABC
notes: Seen at a reasonably close distance, this cat was charcoal grey in colour.

title: Panther
notes: A woman walking her dog watched as a large black cat crossed the road in front of her before vanishing into bushes.

title: Big Black Cat
notes: A large black cat was seen by a witness, around fifteen metres separating them.
address: Hartest, Bury Saint Edmunds IP29 4DH, UK

title: Large Black Cat
location_name: Harton (North Yorkshire) - A64 opposite Harton turning, between York and Malton
notes: Observed twice by a worker in a petrol station over a four week period, this large black cat moved across a field illuminated by the garage lights.

title: Black Panther
location_name: Haverfordwest (Dyfed) - Road just outside the town
notes: Witness Dixie Tilley was cycling near the town when he saw what looked like a black panther. Dixie said the creature disappeared under a fence when he cycled within thirty metres of its location.

title: Puma
location_name: Haverhill (Suffolk) - A1071 Sturnmer Roundabout, and general area
notes: The Big Cats of Suffolk website reports that large felines have been seen in and around Haverhill for over twenty years, ranging from black leopards to creatures resembling puma and lynx.

title: Puma
location_name: Hayle (Cornwall) - St Ives Bay Holiday Park
notes: This cat was videoed by one witness, while other people saw the creature with a rabbit in its mouth. A zookeeper on holiday spotted the creature and identified it as a large cat.
address: St Ives bay holiday park, 71 Loggans Rd, Loggans, Hayle TR27 5BH, UK

title: Roadkill
location_name: Hayling Island (Hampshire) - Exact location not known
notes: A jungle cat was knocked over and killed along a road on the island, though sightings of other large cats continued for a while afterwards.

title: Cream Coloured Wolf
location_name: Henham (Hertfordshire) - Back roads towards Thaxted from Stortford
notes: A cream coloured wolf was spotted by occupants of a car while passing through this area. They stopped close to the creature which did not run off, apparently unfazed by the human presence. A few days later they observed the creature again. Some believe the creature to be more mundane and is a large German Shepherd.

title: Sleek Black Cat
notes: A large cat, described as sleek and black, was spotted by a witness stalking along a hedgerow. The creature was said to be around six foot in length, and between 300 and 500 yards away.
address: Fields, Wheatfield Way, North Perimeter Road, Hinckley LE10 1YG, UK

title: Cats
location_name: Hollesley (Suffolk) - Shingle Street and general area
notes: In 2004 a prison guard spotted a large cat along a road, and four years later another witness encountered a large brown cat. Big Cats of Suffolk website reported that a forester had been warned by colleagues about large cats in the area during the 1970s, and in 2013 stool, hair and field signs were found.

title: Black Panther
location_name: Holton St Mary (Suffolk) - Exact location not known
notes: A 'stooped' large feline was reported in the area.
address: Holton St Mary, Colchester CO7 6NW, UK

title: Big Cats
notes: Several sightings of ABCs occurred in 1985, though it was unclear whether they were of a single creature or a possible family.
address: Honington, Bury Saint Edmunds IP31 1RB, UK

title: Panther
notes: A woman who spotted a large, slinky cat with green / blue eyes while driving along a lane reported the encounter to the local press.
address: Silver St, Hordle, Lymington SO41, UK

title: Church Cat
notes: A large cat was observed by the church, standing around 25 meters from the witness.

title: Cats' Eyes
location_name: Houghton-Le-Spring (Tyne and Wear) - Seven Sisters Round Barrow
notes: While walking along the track passing close to Seven Sisters, a witness spotted cat's eyes reflecting in their torch beam. The witness turned off the light, and when they turned it back on about a minute later, the torchlight reflected multiple pairs of eyes. The witness said the eyes were between knee and waist height and moved very fast.
address: 57 Seaham Rd, Houghton le Spring DH5 8ED, UK

title: Black and Stocky Cat
notes: The local newspaper offered five hundred pounds to the first person to photograph the large black cat which has been spotted locally dozens of times over the past ten years.

title: Waist-High Cat
notes: A man walking his dog encountered a large black cat that stood around waist-height. He reported the encounter to a police officer who he spotted on the way home, although the officer could not locate the cat.

title: Cat Attack
notes: While hunting for the locally named 'Beast of Inkberrow', Nick and Sally Dyke discovered the cat in the churchyard - it knocked Nick over and took a swipe at Sally before running off. She was left with three ten centimetre long claw marks down one side.

title: Deer Hunter
notes: A local hill walker discovered a baby deer which had been torn apart. The man speculated it was killed by a large cat, as the damage done to the dead creature far surpassed anything he had encountered before.

title: Large Cat
notes: Part of a rash of sightings of ABCs in and around the Ipswich area during 1996, and that have continued well into the 2000s.

title: Large Cat
notes: A man walking home late had encountered a handful of police searching the edge of a small, wooded area. He asked the closest police officer if everything was okay, only to be told that they had had a report of a large black cat in this location and were searching for it.

title: A Very Black Cat
location_name: Ipswich (Suffolk) - East Ipswich (Felix Road, Foxhall Road areas)
notes: The local paper reported that two people had seen a large cat, 'very black' in colour, in separate incidents. The first was along Felix Road at around 03:30h, followed by the Foxhall Road sighting two hours later. One witness said that the cat was not as big as a panther but was much larger than a normal cat.

title: Paws
notes: Part of a rash of sightings of ABCs in and around the Ipswich area during 1996. The cat was nicknamed 'Paws' by the local press and a reward was posted for evidence. In the 2000s a witness came forwards to say that the 'cat' was a black Labrador which would run freely around the area, and most locals were aware of this at the time.

title: Big Black Cat
location_name: Ipswich (Suffolk) - Tuddenham Road (near pet cemetery (no longer present))
notes: Seen by a car full of people as it crossed the road, the cat was as large as a tiger with a jet black coat. Locals were unconvinced and named a local dog as the likely explanation.

title: Black Cat
location_name: Isle of Mull (Argyll and Bute) - Area around Loch na Keal
datetime: 1978 and continuing for rest of twentieth century
notes: The first alien big cat was reported close to Loch na Keal, where it would remain (fleetingly spotted) over the next twenty years.

title: Fen Tiger
notes: The police were notified after a large, black cat with a long tail was spotted in the area.

title: ABC
notes: Part of a rash of sightings of ABCs in and around the Ipswich area during 1996.

title: Leopard Cat
notes: A farmer claimed he shot at a large golden cat that was on his land.

title: Beast of Carsington
notes: Seen dozens of times over the nineties, this ABC matched the description common over the country - large and black.

title: Black Cat with Cubs
location_name: Leeds (West Yorkshire) - Between Headingley train station and Burley Park station
notes: Workers on the track have seen a large black cat, around two metres in length, twice. On the first occasion the feline appeared to have two cubs, one light brown and the other black.
address: Burley Park, Burley, Leeds LS6 1LU, UK

title: Long Black Cat
notes: One man watched a long black cat walk from the basketball court, some one hundred metres away, towards him. The creature stopped its approach around fifteen metres distant and watched the witness for a few minutes before walking off towards the lower village of Lighthorne across National Trust land.
address: Leam Rd, Lighthorne Heath, Warwick CV33, UK

title: Large Cat
notes: A driver and passenger spotted a puma cross the road and jump into hedgerow as they travelled south.

title: Attacked Collie
notes: A collie named Basil was attacked by a large creature in the wood; the vet who stitched up the ten centimetre tear on the neck said it looked like it was caused by a cat.

title: ABC
notes: One of many ABC sightings in East Anglia during 1996.

title: Beast of Lytham
location_name: Lytham St Anne (Lancashire) - Green Drive area
notes: Described as being the size of a collie dog, light in colour with large ears and a huge mouth, this large cat was spotted by more than twenty people over the period of a few weeks.
address: Lytham St Annes, Lytham Saint Annes FY8, UK

title: Bob Tailed Cat
location_name: Manchester (Greater Manchester) - Area around the canal near the Pomona Public House, Old Trafford
notes: Anglers fishing in this area reported a cat the size of a cocker spaniel which often appeared at day and night.

title: 'White Hyena'
notes: After receiving reports of a large cat, police officers called to the golf range observed a strange creature, a third bigger than an Alsatian and white in colour. One witness said the front legs were bigger than the hind legs and the creature had a long tail which reached the ground. However, an animal warden would soon go on to recover the animal, which proved to be a German Shepherd/Husky cross, slightly different to the given description.

title: Beast of Balbirnie
notes: Over thirty sightings of a large cat were made in Fife over a twelve month period. Several animal carcasses were found torn to pieces and a ten centimetre long cat footprint was found in soft earth.

title: Black Cat
location_name: Marlborough (Wiltshire) - Marlborough section of the Wansdyke, near to Wernham Farm
notes: While out walking her dog, this witness came across a large black cat close by; the feline looked at her before slowly walking off.

title: ABC
location_name: Martlesham (Suffolk) - Between Foxhall & Seven Hills Roundabouts
notes: Part of a rash of sightings of ABCs in and around the Ipswich area during 1996.

title: Busy Cats
notes: The start of the twenty first century saw a record number of puma sightings in Leicestershire, exceeding 150 reports of the creature(s).

title: Fen Tiger Returns
notes: Cyclist Gavin Elliott-Turner encountered two large black cats in the snow while travelling to work. The creatures ran off across fields, leaving clearly visible footprints.
address: Fowlmere Rd, Melbourn, Royston SG8, UK

title: Large Black Cat
notes: A milkman on his early morning round reported seeing a large black cat in the village - the sighting unnerved the man to such a degree he quickly left the scene and refused to return to complete his work.

title: Red Cat
notes: This ABC, possessing a vivid red coat, was blamed for the carcasses of several deer that appeared to have been eaten.

title: Leaping Cat
location_name: Milton Keynes (Buckinghamshire) - Bleak Hall area, close to railway bridge
notes: Two teenage friends heard something behind them and watched as a large black cat jumped out into the road. It made eye contact with the witnesses before jumping off again into woodland.
address: Bleak Hall, Milton Keynes MK6, UK

title: Beast of Buchan
notes: Local man James Maskame watched a large black cat-like creature leave the woods before running across nearby farmland. This is just one of several in the area, where local media have dubbed the cat 'The Beast of Buchan'.

title: Emerald Eyed Cat
location_name: Mylor Bridge (Cornwall) - Between Mylor Bridge and Carclew
notes: A large cat walked over to a stationary car and stared into the driver's eyes, face to face, through the side window. The creature then took off into the night.

title: Two Kittens
location_name: Nether Stowey (Somerset) - Field off South Lane
notes: Builders on a construction site watched two large black cats on a nearby field. One of the men went to have a closer look and additionally spotted a pair of younger cats; kittens, but much larger than domestic animals.
address: South Ln, Nether Stowey, Bridgwater TA5, UK

title: Cat Attack
notes: A man working in the undergrowth was attacked by a lynx-like creature that tried to bite his hands. Luckily, he was wearing thick gardening gloves that protected him.

title: Road Crossing Cat
location_name: North Walsham (Norfolk) - Northeast of village, close to Bacton Woods / Verona woods
notes: Four witnesses slowly driving in this area spotted a large cat walking from a wheat field, down a bank and across the road. When the witnesses stopped their vehicle, the cat ran up the opposite bank and into Verona woods.

title: Large Cat
location_name: Northallerton (North Yorkshire) - Hailstone Moor, near Scholla Lane
notes: One man reported two sightings of a big cat on consecutive days in December 2011. The creature was bigger than a domestic cat and stood against a fence which was later used as a reference point to measure it; the creature was estimated to be around five foot (150 centimetres) long.

title: Stafford Beast
notes: There were several reports of a large cat in this region over the 2008 summer, including one report of a pet sheep found dead (a local 'expert' saying that a big cat had attacked it).

title: Lion
notes: Two milkmen in the area reported seeing a large cat with a long tail which had a bushy end. Locals claimed that their pets had been quite restless during the night, though a large scale search by police using tracker dogs and helicopters found nothing.
address: Nottingham AIRPORT, Tollerton Ln, Gamston, Nottingham NG2 6NJ, UK

title: Dog sized Cat
location_name: NW3 (Greater London) - West End Lane, area near the church
notes: Deep within London, the report of this large golden cat surprised many locals.
address: W End Ln, London NW3, UK

title: Black Cat
location_name: Okehampton (Devon) - Wooded open ground, end of Wonnacotts Road
notes: Two children reported seeing a black, puma-like cat while playing in this wooded area.

title: Beast of Bexley
location_name: Outer London: Bexley (Greater London) - Bexleyheath Golf Club
notes: A teenager reported seeing a giant black cat trotting across the grass here - police were called but could find nothing. The creature was photographed by a woman from the bottom of her garden in Feb 2006.

title: Large Black Cat
location_name: Outer London: Northolt (Greater London) - Central Line Railway between Northolt & Greenford
notes: It was claimed that this ABC used the local rail tracks to move around without attracting too much attention.

title: Dog-like Cat
notes: A cat the size of a retriever crossed the road in front of a VW van driver.
address: Pakenham, Bury Saint Edmunds IP31 2LN, UK

title: Large ABC Footprints in Snow
notes: While no animal was seen, a large cat left footprints in the snow here in early 1996.

title: Black Panther
location_name: Pershore (Hereford & Worcester) - Outside of Croome Estate
notes: A witness reported seeing a black panther moving through a rapeseed field in 2012, while in 2016, another couple reported seeing another black cat in the same area.
address: National Trust - Croome, near, High Green, Worcester WR8 9DW, UK

title: Black Cat
notes: A typical large cat was seen in the area, black in colour.

title: Fawn Coloured Cat
notes: A witness spoke to the local newspaper about observing a fast moving, Alsatian-sized creature which was fawn in colour in a field close to her home. The witness said friends had seen a similar creature in the area.

title: Cat Calls
notes: A woman with her elderly mother crossing the heath heard a growling noise followed by a loud roar from behind bushes. As they ran off, the roar was heard again. A cyclist and her son have also reported seeing a ghostly woman wearing a grey Victorian dress on the heath, close to the bomb crater used by mountain bikers.

title: Chimera
notes: Three men walking along the beach encountered a wailing animal about the size of a large cat. The creature's hind parts were like those of a rabbit and the front resembled a cat. The head was 'short and thick' and looked like that of a tiger, while the tail was short. The men caught the creature, although its ultimate fate is unclear.

title: Young Puma
notes: The press reported that people should not approach a large cat if seen in this area, following several sightings in quick succession.

title: Rusty Cat
notes: Rusty brown in colour, this cat made a fleeting visit to Rackheath.

title: Large Black Cat
location_name: Randaltown (County Antrim) - Exact location not known
notes: A local man and his friend witnessed a huge, black creature run past their car while heading towards a local chip shop.

title: Spotty Cat
location_name: Red Lodge (Suffolk) - Russet Drive and woodland
notes: In 2008, a local resident reported seeing a cat twice the size of a normal domestic feline early one morning along Russet Drive. The creature was described as 'spotty'. Six years later, two sandy-coloured large cats were sighted in woodland and reported to police, who found nothing when they investigated the area.
address: Russet Dr, Red Lodge, Bury Saint Edmunds IP28, UK

title: Lion
notes: A bus driver contacted police after witnessing a lion cross the road ahead of his vehicle.

title: Cats
notes: A woman driving her car at night spotted a large black cat the size of a Labrador as it ran out in front of her car. The creature disappeared into nearby fields. Later another woman came forward saying she had seen the exact same thing in the same area in the late 1990s.

title: Green Eyed Cat
location_name: Rochdale (Greater Manchester) - Healey Dell Nature Reserve
notes: In 2011, one witness spotted what they thought was an Alsatian-sized dog, but it ran off and jumped over a wall like a cat. Seven years later, the witness's neighbour had a similar sighting and reportedly observed a black panther with big green eyes.
address: Healey Dell Nature Reserve, 121 Dell Rd, Rochdale OL12 6LW, UK

title: Big Cat
location_name: Rochford (Essex) - General area of Rochford and Hawkwell
notes: A large out of place cat was observed several times in this area.

title: Black Puma
location_name: Rougham (Suffolk) - General area, and on the A14 turning
notes: Big Cats of Suffolk website reported three sightings of black cats in this area, two spotted by motorcyclists. The most recent sighting in 2020 described the creature as muscular and square jawed.

title: Big Cats
location_name: Roughton (Norfolk) - General area, and Roughton Road
notes: Roughton was subject to visitation from at least one alien big cat during the summer of 1996. A few years later, one witness observed a big black cat walking through a field of crops before disappearing into the treeline. The witness described the creature as much larger than a dog, but smaller than a horse.

title: Large Black Cat
location_name: Saltford (Somerset) - Near Bristol and Bath Railway Path
notes: A family walking along the path encountered a large black cat poking around a hedge. The creature looked up at the family before walking into a field and vanishing from view.
address: Bristol And Bath Railway Path, Saltford, UK

title: Snared Cat
notes: A medium sized wild cat was caught and killed near the manor house, and later identified as a leopard cat.
address: Yaverland Rd, Yaverland, Sandown PO36 8QP, UK

title: Black Cat
location_name: Sandy (Bedfordshire) - Close to the site of Morgan Matroc
notes: Three witnesses spotted a large black cat, described as five feet (150 centimetres) long with a two foot (sixty-one centimetre) long tail.

title: Black 'Panther' Attack
notes: Local resident Anthony Holder reported being attacked by a 1.8 metre long black cat in his back garden. The creature bit his fingers and left a large gash on his cheek before escaping. Police marksmen called to the scene found nothing.

title: Beast of Sydenham
notes: The press claimed a jogger had reported being chased by a large black cat through this wooded area.

title: Cat with Long Tail
location_name: Shap (Cumbria) - M6 motorway near to junction 39
notes: Several Alien Big Cat sightings have been reported along this stretch of motorway. The ABC reported in this instance was described as possessing a big head and a very long tail.

title: Large Cat
location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - Field near Wales Bar Petrol Station, now a golf course
notes: While crossing a field of sheep, a group of friends watched a large, cat-shaped creature amongst the sheep. The creature spotted the group and crouched, resulting in the friends fleeing the area.
address: Wales Bar Service Station, 16 Mansfield Rd, Wales, Sheffield S26 5PE, UK

title: Big Cat
notes: This out of place feline stood around 350 metres from observers.

title: Line Lion
notes: Trains were delayed for around two hours after police received a report of a lion (and possibly a cub) on the rail tracks at Shepley. After searching the area with twelve officers and a helicopter, nothing was found, and rail travel resumed.

title: Fawn Coloured Cat
notes: Prison Officer Heidi Hawley was driving to work when what she mistook for an Alsatian dog ran out in front of her. Believing it to have been an escaped pet, Heidi stopped her car to investigate further; she climbed up an embankment to follow the 'dog', only to realise it was a large fawn coloured cat with green eyes. The cat paused to look at her before running off.

title: Black Panther
location_name: Shrewsbury (Shropshire) - Field behind Travelodge at Bayston Hill
notes: Police land and air units were called out to investigate the reports of a long black cat seen sitting in the field, though a search failed to find the creature.

title: Hound-Sized Cat
location_name: Skelwith Bridge (Cumbria) - Road between Skelwith Bridge and Ambleside
notes: A witness spotted a grey-coloured creature walking up a small hill with trees on the top. The animal looked about the size of a large dog; it had a greyhound-like head with a long muzzle, but the body was much more like that of a big cat, complete with a long, thick tail.

title: Cat Kill
notes: Reported to The Big Cats of Suffolk website, a muntjac carcass found here featured a bite to the neck, with other cat spoor discovered nearby.

title: Large Cat
location_name: Spixworth (Norfolk) - Field, exact location not known
notes: A witness who spotted a large black cat on the edge of a field estimated the sitting creature to be around one metre in height.

title: Leaping Cat
notes: Witness Martin Heath reported seeing a large cat, dark brindle in colour, and around the size of an Alsatian with a thirty centimetre long bushy tail. The cat ran across the garden and disappeared by leaping into a tree.

title: Black panther
location_name: Sproughton (Suffolk) - Road between village and Chantry Park
notes: The Big Cats of Suffolk website reported that one witness watched a large black cat cross the road.
address: Chantry Park, Hadleigh Rd, Ipswich IP2 0BS, UK

title: Large Black Puma
notes: Military surveillance equipment was set up around the airbase following several reports of a large black cat seen on the site. A spokesperson said that the RAF would make 'every possible attempt to ensure it is captured humanely'. Even though a professional tracker was called in, the creature was never caught.
address: RAF St Mawgan, R A F St Mawgan, Newquay TR8 4HP, UK

title: Lion or Teddy Bear?
location_name: St Osyth (Essex) - Field off Earls Hall Drive
notes: Residents of this village were asked to stay indoors while people at a nearby caravan park were evacuated after the police warned the public that a lion may be on the loose, having been presented with a grainy image of a cat in a field. After police helicopters and armed officers found nothing, the photo was released in the press. The owner of a large domestic cat named Teddy Bear said the 'lion' looked like her pet, which is known to bask in the sun in the same field.
address: Earls Hall Dr, St. Osyth, Clacton-on-Sea CO16, UK

title: Large Cat
notes: This feline stood about 25 metres from the witness.

title: Large Black Creature
notes: A large black cat was spotted by two witnesses who were left unnerved as it moved around a mound and disappeared over the top.

title: Black Cat
notes: One witness observed a large black cat in an alleyway eating something, while a teenage girl camping in a garden peered out after hearing a noise and spotted green eyes looking at her. One theory is that the creature lived in woodland near Higham.

title: Roe Deer Kill
notes: The corpse of a roe deer discovered by a member of the public had wounds similar to animals killed by big cats. The National Trust, who own the land, said they were carrying out tests on the body - these later came back with only deer and fox DNA detected. Video footage was also captured of the 'Stroud Valleys Wildcat' a couple of years prior to this attack, although was only analysed in 2012, with opinions differing to whether the creature was an ABC or just a large domestic cat.
address: Woodchester Park House, Nympsfield, Stonehouse GL10 3UN, UK

title: Large Black Cat
notes: While driving home this witness watched a large black cat, 'as large as a Boxer dog', run from out of a hedge into the car headlights. The creature continued to run along the road for a short distance before vanishing into another hedge.

title: Black Cat
location_name: Taynuilt (Argyll and Bute) - Field in the area
notes: Seen sitting in a field, this large black cat with a very long tail sat in profile to the witness. The creature slunk into the bushes and, despite the witness waiting a while, never reemerged.

title: Black Puma
location_name: Telford (Shropshire) - Areas including Blockleys Brickmaker's and Donnington Wood
notes: Clear CCTV footage taken at Blockleys showed a large (around two meters in length) black cat stalking the neighbourhood. The RSPCA stated the creature was indeed a puma. Another witness spotted the creature in woodland at the bottom of their garden.
address: Trench Lck, Hadley, Telford TF1 5RY, UK

title: Yellow Eyed Cat
notes: After hearing rustling in nearby bushes, a walker approached closer only to hear loud cat-like screeching. As they backed away, a large black cat with yellow eyes was spotted.

title: Fox Savior
notes: A woman walking her dogs encountered a fox being stalked by a large cat. The dogs and owner disturbed the cat which eventually disappeared off, leaving the (no doubt very relieved) fox to walk alongside the woman for safety for a few minutes after the encounter.

title: Black Cat with Glowing Eyes
notes: A large cat with eyes that glowed red was reported in the forest in 1985. More recently, Big Cats of Suffolk website reports there have been sightings of large cats in the area for at least thirty years, most recently a large black cat, built like a lynx, in 2021.

title: Mountain Lion
notes: A Mountain Leader, supervising a group of teenagers on a DofE Gold expedition, cut through the forest to meet up with them. The leader reached a fifty foot (fifteen metre) clearing and on the other side stood a tan coloured Mountain Lion. Man and cat stood and stared at each other for around a minute before the lion turned and slowly walked into the trees. The lion was headed in the direction the leader wanted to go in, so he retraced his steps and walked around the forest instead.

title: Green Eyes
location_name: Thompson (Norfolk) - Unstated road in the area
notes: A witness returning home by car spotted a large cat just a few metres away from a deer. He stated that he saw the perfect side profile of the creature just before it disappeared into the hedge: a long sleek body with a long tail drooping to the ground and then hooking upwards.

title: Light Brown Cat
notes: Licensee of the Masons Arms Lynne Goldfield reported observing a brown cat with high hind legs as she walked her dog along this road.

title: Lion
notes: Dozens of witnesses reported seeing a large cat that resembled a lioness, though no hard evidence was ever produced.

title: Dark Fawn ABC
location_name: Tolleshunt Knights (Essex) - Exact location not known
notes: Possibly the same creature seen in Great Wigborough, the alien big cat observed here was thought to be a lion.

title: Black Cat
location_name: Totnes (Devon) - Area around Dartington Hall Estate
notes: A farm worker watched a large black cat, the size of a dog, leap over a high boundary fence in a single bound.

title: Cat Attack
notes: Joshua Hopkins, aged 11, was attacked by a strange cat 150 centimetres in length. The claw marks that were left on his cheek had to be treated in hospital.

title: Cat Crossing Road
location_name: Tunstall Forest (Suffolk) - Road cutting through the area
notes: While driving through the forest, this woman saw a large unidentified cat cross the road ahead of her. She reported it was between 4.5 feet - 5.5 feet in length, including the tail. When it was mentioned to a forest warden later in the day, he said it was not an uncommon report in that area.

title: White Cat
notes: A mother and daughter watched a large white cat close to woodland over a period of fifteen to twenty minutes. They compared the size of the cat to that of a deer.
address: Turin, Metropolitan City of Turin, Italy

title: Vectis Monster
notes: An early ABC report from the UK, this creature was said to have a head of a lion, though the body resembled a dog. Deaths were reported in the local livestock population.

title: Scratching Post
location_name: Wallis Wood (Surrey) - Footpath at edge of wood near Oakfields estate
notes: A mother and her two children returning from a visit to the mobile library spotted a 1.5 metre long black cat sitting on the path ahead of them. After watching the creature for a minute or two and realising that the cat was not going to move, the family went back the way they came. A year later when in the area, the family found the base of a tree was covered in scratches, as if the large cat had used it as a scratching post.

title: Cheetah
location_name: Walton-on-the-Hill (Surrey) - Road running by Walton Heath
notes: A motorist watched a cheetah run in front of his car as he drove through the area.
address: Walton on the Hill, Tadworth KT20, UK

title: Panther Size Cat
location_name: Warter (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Woods near Warter Priory
notes: While walking their dog, this witness watched a large black cat leap across the path twenty yards (18 metres) ahead of them. The dog, which was running around freely, immediately ran back to the owner, and both walked back the way they had come.
address: The Estate Office, York YO42 1XA, UK

title: Alsatian-Sized Cat
location_name: Welham Green (Hertfordshire) - Station Road, woodland near
notes: Andrew Hollings, out walking his dog in the evening, encountered a cat 'the size of a Labrador or Alsatian with a long tail'. The creature slowly emerged form woodland.
address: Station Rd, Welham Green, Hatfield AL9, UK

title: Beast of Brookmans Park
notes: Named the 'Beast of Brookmans Park' by the local press, this panther was spotted by several people during 2005.

title: Large Black Cat
notes: A driver reported passing along this road and slowing down for what they assumed was a large black dog. As they approached, they realised the creature was in fact a very large black cat.

title: Chiltern Puma
notes: In May 1983, four women encountered the 'Chiltern Puma' while walking through the estate - the creature past within three metres of the group. The cat was seen a half dozen times during that year, in over a three week period, all within a thirteen mile radius. In 2005 another witness reported seeing a large black creature close to the old RAF hospital site.
address: Royal Air Force, Halton Camp, Aylesbury HP22 5PG, UK

title: Beast of the Bay
location_name: Whitby (North Yorkshire) - Several areas, including abbey, St Peter's Court woods, and surrounding villages
notes: A black panther-like cat has been observed a dozen times over a seven year period, on one occasion the cat was accompanied by a cub.
address: St Peter's Ct, Whitby YO22, UK

title: Big and Black
notes: This typical large black cat seen at some distance.

title: Flecked cat
location_name: Winchcombe (Gloucestershire) - Area around Mount View Drive
notes: Several witnesses reported seeing a large cat around this new housing estate. One woman said it was the size of a Labrador with a flecked brown coloured coat. Fur from the creature had been recovered, but at the time of writing, DNA analysis has not been carried out on the evidence.
address: Mountview Dr, Winchcombe, Cheltenham GL54 5EH, UK

title: Sooty Cat
location_name: Winestead (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Area around the former mental hospital
notes: The police were called after three people watched a large black cat padding around a field around fifteen metres from their location.

title: Large Black Cat
location_name: Wivenhoe (Essex) - Outside police station, High Street
datetime: 27 January 2009, early hours of the morning
notes: The so-called Beast of Essex was spotted by a police officer along the High Street - the large black cat fled down the side of the police station. A search for the creature was launched, but nothing could be found.
address: High St, Wivenhoe, Colchester CO7, UK

title: Light Brown Cat
location_name: Wokingham (Berkshire) - Forest Road and surrounding area
notes: At least eight people in the space of a year have reported a large cat in the area. It was said by one person to have ears like a fox, though light brown in colour with a long straight tail. Another man near Woodcray Manor Golf Club stated that he watched a cat with a pair of large green eyes, fifteen centimetres apart, staring at him.

title: Puma
notes: This large brown cat was blamed for attacking a dog being walked by a child. It was seen a few times walking along disused rail tracks in and around the Wolverhampton area.

title: Cat Larger than an Alsatian
location_name: Wombwell (South Yorkshire) - Pontefract Road / Everill Gate Lane, Broomhill
notes: A large black cat was spotted as it darted across the road in front of a car.
address: Everill Gate Ln & Pontefract Rd, Wombwell, Barnsley S73 0YQ, UK

title: Lion
notes: Police spent eight hours searching for a lion after a local man reported seeing the blood-stained creature enter a field along a lane. Police expressed concern that local hunters armed with shotguns had joined the search. Analysis of a paw print discovered in the area revealed it could belong to a big cat.

title: Fawn Coloured Cat
notes: The Big Cats of Suffolk website reported multiple sightings of large cats in this area. In 1997, a wave of sightings were reported of a lynx or cougar, and patrols were established to try to capture the creature. Sightings have sporadically continued, and in 2015 police reported a panther on the loose.

title: Panther
location_name: Wychbold (Hereford & Worcester) - Railway line between M5 and Bromsgrove
notes: A train driver spotted a large black cat with a long tail cross the rail tracks ahead of his engine.

title: Misty Woman
notes: This figure was once seen behind the bar and is blamed for slamming doors when no other cause is apparent. Another entity takes the form of a black dog which patrols both inside and outside the pub. The dog is thought to be protecting a secret passageway.
address: Poplar Farm Inn, Old Salisbury Rd, Andover SP11 7NJ, UK

title: White Hare
location_name: Airdrie (Lanarkshire) - Area which was once known as Lover's Leap, on the road towards Faskine
notes: The manifestation of a white hare would warn the Kerr family of impending doom. The site known as Lover's Leap marked the spot where a Kerr woman was found dead (along with her lover) shortly after this magic white hare was spotted.

title: Pony
location_name: Aldbourne (Wiltshire) - Road leading to the village from Braydon
notes: A white pony silently galloped past two men as they returned to the village. It has been speculated that the pony was King Arthur's mount Sigral (ridden at the Battle of Baydon) or belonging to a local man who committed suicide.

title: Coach
notes: Possibly the same entity that haunts the park, a coach and team of horses are said to erratically travel along the road which skirts the woodland.

title: Crump
notes: Sir John Dutton, nicknamed Crump because of his hunched back, is said to drive a phantom coach and horses under the gates to the park. A white stag may also haunt the parkland.
address: Lodge Park, Aldsworth, Cheltenham GL54 3PP, UK

title: Blue Lady
notes: Believed to have been murdered on the site, a woman dressed in blue has been observed on the landing. A dog is also said to haunt the car park outside the building.
address: Deans Place Hotel, Alfriston, Polegate BN26 5TW, UK

title: Pig
notes: The priest here was hit by lightning - the congregation watched as a small pig appeared and ran about his legs and feet.

title: Thirty-Something Woman
notes: A black animal (either a dog or a large cat) was seen running across this road followed by a woman in her thirties. The animal disappeared into a wall, and the woman vanished as a car braked hard to avoid hitting her.

title: Headless Highwayman
location_name: Atwick (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Roads in the area
notes: This decapitated spectral bandit remains lurking in the shadows of his old ambush area. One version of the story says he remains on his horse.

title: Tabby Cat
notes: This feline apparition has been observed padding around on the first floor, vanishing when it sits down. A white Elizabethan lady is also reported to haunt the same floor, seen sitting in a chair.

title: Green Cat
location_name: Balbriggan (County Dublin) - Exact location not known
notes: A phantom cat, green in colour, was reported to haunt this town.

title: Farmer
notes: A phantom farmer holding a lantern is said to haunt this bridge, though he has not been seen for around one hundred years. Another witness encountered a ghostly animal, 'neither dog or ram', which blocked his path.

title: Strange Bird
notes: This ghostly bird contains the spirit of man executed after stealing wood from the land around the estate. The bird's song is heard by people who are considering stealing something as a warning against their actions.
address: Vaynol Estate/Park Y Faenol, Bangor LL57 4BP, UK

title: Hymns
notes: This theatre has been home to ghost hunts in the past and is reputed to be home to various entities which include a young girl singing hymns, a ghostly cat, and a figure who closes the stage curtains.

title: Large Cat
notes: A large white or ginger cat ran out in front of a car before the driver had a chance to react and disappeared under the vehicle. Both driver and passenger felt a bump and, stopping the car, leap out to check on the animal. However, they could find no blood, fur, or any other evidence they hit something either on, under or near the car. Reflecting on the incident, one of the witnesses thought the creature was very large, seemed too visible under the lighting conditions, and seemed to suddenly appear.
address: High St, Barrow upon Soar, Loughborough LE12, UK

title: Baboon
location_name: Basingstoke (Hampshire) - The Swallows private house, on the outskirts of the town (no longer standing)
notes: Haunted by a large baboon and a huge dark cat, the owners finally left when screaming started coming from the attic. The building was pulled down when no one could be found to move in.

title: Tiny the Dog
notes: William Beckford's pet dog, Tiny, was once buried by the tower, though the creature's ghost returned after the remains were moved. It is also said that William's ghost haunts the graveyard surrounding the tower.

title: Floating Cat
notes: This small ghostly house cat floats along a corridor in the restaurant before vanishing through a wall.

title: Rider
notes: A ghostly figure riding a white horse was spotted galloping across this field.

title: Cat
location_name: Bedford (Bedfordshire) - The Square public house (was the Bullnosed Bat)
notes: A member of staff once reported seeing a ghostly cat that appeared in his bedroom.

title: Persian Cat
notes: While making coffee the occupant watched a grey Persian cat walk across the floor and disappear into the living room. No doors or windows were open, and the cat could not be found. The witness later found out that a cat matching the description had once lived there but had died a few years previous.

title: Stone Dogs
notes: The stone hounds sitting upon the gatepost are said to occasionally animate at night, going to the nearby river to drink. A floating white figure was reputedly seen along the road near Bryanston woods by a dog walker.

title: Sheep
location_name: Blandford Forum (Dorset) - Sheep Market Hill and/or Damory Street
notes: A herd of phantom, ruminant animals are reputed to walk along this street. Some say the sheep are to be headless, while another version says there is in fact only one sheep (complete with head).
address: Sheep Market Hill, Blandford Forum DT11 7DP, UK

title: Hunt
location_name: Blandford Forum (Dorset) - Woods in the area
notes: Thought to be part of a phantom hunt, these ghostly dogs have been heard running and yelping through woodland. It is also thought that a headless shuck haunts the area, though it is less sure whether the entity is connected to the hunt pack.

title: Mary Dore
notes: A famous local witch, the ghost of Mary Dore was said to linger in the woods long after her death. On occasion it would take the form of a hare-like animal, and on others the form of a beautiful woman which flirted with male witnesses before vanishing. Another phantom, this time with a skull as a face, was also reported in the woodland.

title: Hare
notes: The castle was haunted by a phantom hare. Locals believed the creature to be the ghost of a witch once held prisoner in the castle. The entity was said to leap over people or run between their legs, and any dog sent after it would return crying out.
address: Moat Ln, Old Bolingbroke, Spilsby PE23 4HH, UK

title: George Marsh
location_name: Bolton (Greater Manchester) - Smithills Hall (aka Smithells Hall)
notes: Tried and executed for heresy, Marsh is remembered as his footprint (created when he stamped his foot) in the stone floor turns bloody each April 24. His shade is also reported to haunt the Green Room, where he was interrogated, though that is not date dependent. A priest is also reported to haunt the vicinity, as is a photogenic grey lady and a cat.
address: Smithills Dean Rd, Bolton BL1 7NP, UK

title: First World War Soldier
notes: This incorporeal soldier of Indian heritage appears here once a year to help himself to a drink of water. There are also a few reports of phantom horses and carriages being seen outside the building, while a ghostly cat is also said to haunt several of the town hall rooms.
address: Town Hall, Bourne Ave, Bournemouth BH2 6DY, UK

title: Black Monk
notes: This phantom black monk is accompanied by a dog of similar colour.

title: Man in Top Hat
notes: A local reported that an alleyway along the court is haunted by a man wearing a top hat, who slowly fades from sight. The same witness has also heard pigs in the area - before the land was built on, it was a pig farm.

title: Austrian Sheepdog
notes: A favoured pet, this ghost started to appear several weeks after its death.
address: Kelvedon Hall, Kelvedon Hatch, Brentwood CM14 5TN, UK

title: Trotting Dog
location_name: Bridgend (Mid Glamorgan) - Crossroads between Bridgend and Laleston
datetime: Said to manifest every night at midnight (reoccurring)
notes: The phantom hound haunting this crossroads would appear nightly and trot off along a road before vanishing. It was said several people had tried to follow the creature but would always lose sight of it.

title: White Calf
location_name: Brigg (Lincolnshire) - Between Brigg and Wrawby, near a stream
notes: The phantom white calf (also known as the Lackey Causey calf), sometimes headless, was said to emerge from a tunnel and attempt to lure people into the water, though the creature's entrance may no longer exist.
address: Brigg Rd & Wrawby Rd, Wrawby, Brigg DN20 8SH, UK

title: Flying Hare
notes: It is now thought that the phantom flying hare which reputedly haunted this lane was the creation of a local smuggler determined to keep meddling locals out of the way.
address: Moortown Ln, Brighstone, Newport PO30, UK

title: Criminal
location_name: Brighstone (Isle of Wight) - Waytes Court Manor
notes: Criminals were said to have been hanged from an oak tree on the site, and now one of the men haunts the site.

title: Coach
location_name: Brimpton (Berkshire) - Lane running through village, leading to Able Bridge
notes: Carrying its occupants to a ball, the coach was caught up in a storm that washed away Able Bridge - the coach plunged into the water, killing all the passengers. The event is heard once a year after sunset, marking the anniversary of the tragedy.

title: Decapitated Horseman
notes: Ashton Court is best known for its ghostly headless horseman, though there are also reports of grey ladies and a phantom hound.
address: Ashton Court Estate, Long Ashton, Bristol BS41 9JN, UK

title: Ape
notes: After moving into the house, the owner found it impossible to keep his servants - they left after claiming to have encountered a ghostly ape or, at other times, a large black hound. The owner himself heard loud bangs and crashes but was unable to find a source. The site would later be converted into three cottages, but the haunting continued in the area and the new buildings left to go to ruin. Reports of the ghostly dog would continue in fields in the area for many years.

title: Ginger Cat
notes: The hall is reputed to be haunted by a ginger cat and the ghosts of former owners.

title: Cow
location_name: Buckholt (Clwyd) - Farm off the old Abergavenny road
notes: One witness claimed to have tried to slap a cow to waken it - her hand passed straight through the creature before it vanished. The farmhouse itself was said to be the home of a poltergeist which would open and close doors and flick light switches.

title: Cat
location_name: Burton upon Trent (Staffordshire) - Bass museum (no longer open)
notes: Seen by members of staff and visitors, a ghostly cat lurked around the ground floor and the recreation of an Edwardian bar.

title: Red Eared Pigs
notes: Several small white swine with red ears cross the road once a year on Christmas Eve.

title: Monkey
notes: A phantom monkey which haunted this region was reported in the dim and distant past.
address: Gog Magog Hills, Stapleford, Cambridge CB22 5FX, UK

title: A Nun and Brown Dog-Thing
notes: It is claimed that Abbey House is, or at least was, the most haunted house in Cambridge. While I'm not a fan of 'most haunted' titles, when you look at the number and type of the ghosts supposed to haunt the site (a poltergeist, ghostly echoes of chains, a butler, a woman in white, a grey lady, a squirrel, a dog, and a hare all amongst the entities which have popped up), one can understand why the title has been applied here. An exorcism was performed in the 1980s, which may have laid the entities.
address: Abbey House, 42 Abbey Rd, Cambridge CB5 8HQ, UK

title: Flying Bird
notes: A black bird would be seen flying through glass without smashing it. The site was also haunted by a man wearing a white coat.

title: Horses
location_name: Challacombe (Devon) - Broken Barrow (exact location not known)
notes: A man who tried to break into this barrow was driven mad by the sound of horses - each time the robber reached into the hole he had made, the sound of horses could be heard, though the creatures could not be seen. The man eventually lost his sight and hearing, dying three months later.

title: Sitting Hound
notes: A phantom dog was once a common sight here, as it sat on the corner of the road near a railway bridge waiting for an owner which never came.
address: Horderns Rd, Chapel-en-le-Frith, High Peak SK23, UK

title: Mrs Hutchinson
location_name: Checkley (Staffordshire) - Checkley Rectory (New building), and the Hutchinson school
notes: Mrs Hutchinson, former wife of a Vicar who she outlived by twenty years, has been seen walking the building where she once lived accompanied by her dog. Dressed in an old black dress, the ghost has also been heard to call people's names and knock on the front door. The school that was named after her husband also reportedly harbours the entity.
address: Uttoxeter Rd, Checkley, Stoke-on-Trent ST10 4NB, UK

title: Galloping Horses
notes: A pair of galloping horses are said to charge down the hill, vanishing as they approach the bottom.

title: Madam Pigott
location_name: Chetwynd Aston (Shropshire) - A41, and Chetwynd Hall & surrounding area
notes: Having died in childbirth, Madam Pigott was seen dozens of times carrying her baby and combing its hair. Some also reported the lady also having a cat with her. She was eventually exorcised by twelve parsons, though in 1969 two motorists separately reported a white figure on the road near the church.

title: Grey Lady
notes: It has been speculated that this phantom woman drowned in the lake and now walks around seeking rest. A ghostly white deer is also said to haunt the same area.

title: Retriever
notes: This friendly dog is said to escort visitors up the driveway before vanishing. A phantom clergyman also haunts the vicinity, moving between the house and gates.
address: Wilbury House, Newton Tony, Salisbury SP4 0HS, UK

title: Pigs
notes: These phantom animals, said to be small in stature, have been heard fighting and snorting on this ancient site.

title: Trotting
notes: The clip-clop of phantom hooves has been reported in this area, normally accompanied by a strange pale mist which (it is said) sometimes transforms into a horse with a headless rider.

title: Horsemen
notes: While on his way home with his dog, Mr Sampson watched two men on horseback riding around the fort. His dog immediately ran home, leaving Sampson to watch the figures as they dismounted and vanish (special thanks to the Churchtown Village Renewal Trust, from their 'The Annals of Churchtown').

title: Horse and Cart
notes: Walking his dog one early morning, a man watched a horse and cart appear from nowhere. The man was frozen to the spot. The cart was driven by a weathered man, and appeared quite solid, although old fashioned. After the cart had passed the witness, he composed himself and turned to follow the vehicle, but it had vanished.
address: Aynsley Ave, Newcastle-under-Lyme, Newcastle ST5 4ET, UK

title: Sound of a Horse
notes: The driver of a horse and cart and a passenger were left shaken after encountering the sounds of a horse which passed them, and although nothing could be seen, the sounds were accompanied by a cold gust of wind. A murder had occurred along the road a few years previous, where a woman on horseback was decapitated by a former lover.

title: Wide-Eyed Dog
notes: A large dog with saucer sized eyes would sit on the roadside. No one would see it move (it would just vanish from view), although if stones were thrown at the creature, the eyes would grow even larger. It once appeared to one man who discovered the time of the sighting matched the time that his father died. The same area is also haunted by a phantom white donkey.

title: Blue Donkey
notes: This strangely discoloured creature is reputed to haunt the churchyard.

title: Young Lady
location_name: Coity (Mid Glamorgan) - Former lane, may no longer exist
notes: An entity here would initially take the form of a lady so pretty that any man spotting her would fall in love. The woman would lead the man away from the road, taking them to a cottage filled with demons. The entity would also take the form of a hare, changing into a snake when people came too close.

title: Charlotte in White Robe
notes: Located in a hidden part of the grounds, Charlotte's Well was built for a woman after which it took its name. Charlotte is said to have loved the well so much that she could never leave and is said to have been seen since her death around the bathing pool. She may have last been seen walking around a wooded area in a white dress during the winter of 1977, although there are stories that a phantom woman still appears in the area. A phantom coach is also said to ride around the area.
address: Berechurch Hall, 115 Berechurch Hall Rd, Colchester CO2 9NW, UK

title: Chambermaid
notes: A few days from death, a chambermaid from the inn requested that her body be sent back to Anglesey. The landlord at first agreed, though failed to carry out her last wish, and soon after the building was besieged by otherworldly activity. The owner finally had the body returned to her home, but the ghost has still been seen in the hotel. A phantom cat has also been felt climbing over beds at night.

title: White Stag
location_name: Cookham (Berkshire) - Maidenhead Rd, off Whyteladyes Lane
notes: While driving under the railway bridge, a couple braked hard as a white stag ran in front of their car. Believing they had hit the animal, the two people jumped out of the car but saw the stag running away along the road. A woman, a short distance away past whom the stag would have run, did not see anything.
address: Whyteladyes Ln, Cookham, Maidenhead SL6, UK

title: Raven
location_name: Coppenhall (Cheshire) - Area around Don Kinson's Oak
notes: Thought by locals to contain the spirit of a woman who drowned herself in a nearby pool of water, this black bird had to be exorcised by the area's Bishop.

title: Bird Fight
location_name: Cork (County Cork) - Skies over the city
notes: Over a period of four or five days, two massive flocks of starlings gathered over the city, one in the east and one in the west. On the 12 October, the two flocks took to the air, engaging in combat - covering the city ground with dead and wounded birds. Just before nightfall, both bird armies left the area, but returned for another major engagement and final battle on 14 October.

title: Dog Walker
notes: Four friends camping on the beach decided to walk towards Corton in the early hours of the morning. Sitting down for a rest, two of the group spotted a man with a dog had suddenly appeared a short distance away. Man and dog stood completely motionless, looking out to sea. The two witnesses turned to their friends and tried to point out the man and dog, but they had vanished.

title: Galloping Horses
location_name: Coventry (West Midlands) - Moat House Lane, Canley
datetime: Unknown - late twentieth or early twenty-first century?
notes: Walking along the lane, one man heard wheels moving along the road followed by the sound of galloping horses - the witness had to jump out of the way. Other people have reportedly heard farm animals in the area, although none are around.
address: Moat House Ln, Coventry CV4, UK

title: Unlucky Rabbit
notes: This white bunny is only seen just before a personal disaster strikes - the creature was the pet of a young girl killed by a couple of local thugs.

title: Smoky Rabbit
location_name: Cromarty (Highland) - Old castle (no longer standing, replaced by Cromarty House)
notes: Several strange shades frequented the castle, including a rabbit that would turn into smoke, a small pixie-like bearded man, and heavy pacing.

title: Stealth Sheep
notes: Never appearing, this phantom flock can be heard moving along the roads, the occasional 'baaaa' betraying their position.

title: WW2 Soldiers
notes: Soldiers dressed in Second World War uniform have been seen around the castle area, and the site is also said to be haunted by a horse and carriage. As a side note, Bram Stoker is said to have been partly inspired to write Dracula after seeing the castle while staying at Cruden Bay.
address: A975, Cruden Bay, Peterhead AB42 0NE, UK

title: White Owl
notes: An unnaturally large white owl took to hunting over an ancient battlefield, leading locals to believe the creature was magical and should not be interfered with. A visitor to Raglan castle who laughed at the story and killed the owl, himself died a few hours later, choking to death at a dinner table.

title: Donkey
notes: This phantom white donkey appears at night.

title: Hound Attack
location_name: Darlington (Durham) - Old Darlington and Stockton rail station
notes: A night watchman reported being attacked by a man and his dog as they came out of the coal house - the watchman defended himself and tried to punch his attacker, only for his fist to pass through the figure and hit the wall behind. The pair of entities then went back into the coal house and vanished.

title: Sow
notes: This phantom sow and her litter walk the area looking for food.

title: David Davies
notes: Davies tended to the prison sheep between 1869 and 1929. He died soon after being released, but his ghost returned, walking the prison grounds on misty nights. The prison is also haunted by French PoWs from the Napoleonic wars, while some inmates believe jackdaws which fly around the area contain the souls of dead staff.

title: Kitchen Horse
notes: The kitchen was built over the old stables, and the sound of a horse kicking a stable door is said to be occasionally heard. Horseshoes belonging to Shetland ponies have been found in the garden.

title: Monk with White Face
notes: Seen by several people, this phantom monk was almost hit by a car before it vanished into the grounds. A light has also been reported floating around the courtyard, containing a strange face, while inside the hall itself a phantom cat and a woman in blue have been reported.

title: Hound
notes: A black dog haunted this stretch of road, although there have been no sightings for at least one hundred years.

title: Coach & Four
notes: A phantom coach drawn by a team of four horses travel around this area. They are said to look quite solid, though is illuminated by an unknown source.

title: Man with Dog
location_name: Drumcliff (County Clare) - Around the ruins of the round tower
notes: It was said that this phantom man with his ghostly hound would walk quickly around the area.

title: Monkey
notes: This yellow monkey has been seen several times, though only recognised as being a monkey recently. Two other ghosts have been seen here; Lady Anne Douglas walks carrying her severed head, while a little girl has been seen in a bedroom, floating a few feet off the floor.
address: Drumlanrig Castle & Gardens, Thornhill DG3 4AQ, UK

title: White Figure
location_name: Dunwich (Suffolk) - Area around the 'red stile', between Dunwich and Heveningham, aka Devil's Field or Lane.
