notes: This pale phantom was described as wearing a white veil or having black patches around the head. One brave man touched the entity and went numb down his right side. A large black hound-like creature was also reported in the area, and a clanking chain heard.

title: Herds of Cows
notes: Herds of invisible grazing animals were once heard by locals moving along the roads in this area. The creatures are also said to be heard on the beach, where green pastures once lay.

title: Unlucky Cat
notes: The family who lived here for six years reported a suppressive atmosphere and terrible luck. Their children reported a phantom cat, while the mother was too scared to enter certain rooms.

title: Boy or Cat
notes: Local folklore said that the strange sounds reported in one room were created by the phantom of a boy flogged to death nearby. However, years later, the remains of a cat were discovered within the walls, and the sounds attributed to the frantic scrabbling of the creature before it died. Some will say that the poor cat was an apotropaic object; we'll never know for sure.
address: Manor House, Manor Rd, Easingwold, York YO61 3AU, UK

title: Persian Cat
location_name: EC3 (Greater London) - All Hallows by the Tower, Great Tower Street
notes: This phantom feline haunted the original church prior to its destruction by German bombers during the Second World War. In one story, a small group rehearsing Christmas carols claimed to have seen a phantom woman sitting on a chair. The figure disappeared and was replaced by a black cat which also vanished soon after.

title: Bear
location_name: EC3 (Greater London) - Tower of London, Jewel House (now known as Martin Tower)
notes: After seeing a large bear approaching him, a guard tried to bayonet the creature; the weapon passed straight through the ghost, and it continued to move towards the soldier. The soldier collapsed and died a few days later.

title: Cat
location_name: EC4 (Greater London) - House of Mr Edward Pitts, Puddle Dock
notes: A phantom cat seen in this house was said to be the size of a mastiff hound, but it had no legs. The family were also subjected to a poltergeist outbreak, which opened doors, moved items around and lit candles.

title: Plague Victims
location_name: Edinburgh (Lothian) - Mary King's Close, currently buried under the City Chambers
notes: A whole host of spectres haunt this area; women, children, old men and even animals have been reported haunting the site over the past 350 years. More recently, there have been reports of a ghostly little girl and an 'impish' young boy. The last occupant of the close was forced to move out in 1898 to make way for the City Chambers extension, which now stands over the area.

title: Shadowy Figures
location_name: Edinburgh (Lothian) - Private flat in Bruntsfield Place
notes: Several entities reportedly haunt this building, including cats, a dog, and possibly several people who were mistaken as 'real' by one witness.

title: Thomas Holloway
notes: Founded by Thomas Holloway, the man himself is said to haunt the Founder's Building, though he manifests in the form of a black cat. Jane Holloway, Thomas's wife, is said to haunt the fourth floor of the same building.

title: Jeffrey
notes: This building experienced phantom groaning, knocking, and invisible hands that pushed people around. Unlike most poltergeist cases, the entity also manifested in the form of a white figure and a headless badger. The presence left without warning.
address: 1 Rectory St, Epworth, Doncaster DN9 1HX, UK

title: Plague Victims
notes: Several graves belonging to plague victims are thought to be haunted by their occupiers.
address: London Rd, Ewell, Epsom KT17 2AY, UK

title: Horse and Coach
notes: The sounds of a horse pulling a coach have been heard outside this inn, though never seen.

title: Black Pig
notes: This porker is reputed to be the ghost of Judge Jeffreys, cursed to return in animal form after he died.

title: Fast Moving Old Lady
notes: Limping, this OAP runs by witnesses at a rate of knots and disappears into the doorway of a house along the street. The same street is also haunted by a black dog which performs a similar action to that of the old woman.

title: Final Bear
notes: This is said to be the location where the last wild bear in England was pointlessly killed; the creature's ghost now returns in protest.
address: Verdley Pl, Fernhurst, Haslemere GU27 3ER, UK

title: Headless Woman and Friends
location_name: Fimber (North Yorkshire) - Site once known as Fimber Cross Road, where railway station once stood
datetime: Likely to be pre twentieth century
notes: Locals once believed this area to be haunted by a headless woman, a woman on horseback and a couple of cats (one black, the other white).

title: Distressed Horse
notes: Said to have been slaughtered in the abattoir, this horse is said to be heard every six months. There are also reports of the creature being heard galloping away from the area.

title: Charlie Miles
location_name: Frimley Green (Surrey) - Old Guildford Road, and surrounding area
notes: This old man tragically lost his son, and now walks the road with his dog, looking for new friends.
address: Old Guildford Rd, Frimley Green, UK

title: Snuff Box
location_name: Garnstone (Hereford & Worcester) - Unknown pond (may no longer exist) and Field's End
notes: The ghost of a farmer who committed suicide would manifest in the form of a calf. During an exorcism, the entity was trapped within a snuff box - the container was thrown into a pond, where ghost and box remain.

title: Running Dog
location_name: Geldeston (Norfolk) - Lover's Lane, leading to the churchyard
notes: The small lane leading to the local churchyard is reputedly home to a phantom hound - the creature has been spotted running along the lane. It is thought that the location that the hound appears will soon be the site of a crime. A ghostly donkey seen here may be the same entity. The same area is also haunted by a haunted coach as it travels towards Bungay Castle.

title: Bull
notes: This lane is said to be haunted by a bull, containing the soul of the Jew who hanged himself on a tree along the road.

title: Rose
location_name: Godshill (Isle of Wight) - The Bat's Wing Tea Shop
notes: This shop is said to be haunted by a woman in black, named as Rose, who sits in a rocking chair with a ginger cat in her lap.

title: Pistol Shots
notes: This was (briefly) the childhood home to E D Walker, a former mayor of Darlington. The mayor reported several ghostly encounters by his family, including the sound of pistol shots, a bed lifting and being dropped three times in quick succession, and invisible dogs fighting just outside the building. Walker's brother and a visiting joiner fled the site after watching a white man appear in a room and vanish a few moments later. A few years after the family moved out, a skeleton was discovered under the kitchen.

title: A Grey Cat and Old Ruffy
notes: A grey cat has been seen in the building on several occasions, running down a corridor, only to disappear into a wall in the bathroom. Old Ruffy is the affectionate name given to the pub poltergeist, which once had a habit of breaking glasses.

title: Labrador
notes: The history of this doggy spirit is unknown.
address: Warley Rd, Great Warley, Brentwood CM13 3HU, UK

title: Black Monk
datetime: January - first new moon of the year (reoccurring)
notes: The monk that is said to appear at these ruins once a year has been rarely seen since the 1960s. A large chest of gold is also said to be buried in the area, watched over by a raven (or the monk, depending on the story source).
address: Priory Grounds, Church St, Guisborough TS14 6HG, UK

title: Cloaked Man
notes: A couple of witnesses watched a man dressed in a cloak float into the room, being chased by a woman dressed in Tutor clothing. It is unclear whether the female spirit is the same as the woman in grey who has been seen in other areas of the estate, often with a small brown dog.
address: Michelham Priory, Upper Dicker, Hailsham BN27 3QS, UK

title: Big Cat
notes: Invisible, large and friendly, this ghost cat would freak people out as it rubbed itself on their legs, trying to show affection.
address: Sower Carr Ln, Hambleton, Poulton-le-Fylde FY6, UK

title: Cat
notes: A former landlord's cat which had died several years previous could be heard walking up and down on the keys of a piano.
address: Derby St, Hanley, Stoke-on-Trent ST1, UK

title: Soldiers with Mules
location_name: Harlech (Gwynedd) - Roman Steps leading up the Rhinog Fawr mountains
notes: A group of Roman soldiers with pack animals slowly make their way up these steps. Some believe that the ghostly posse will lead anyone who tries to follow them to gold.

title: Cat
notes: Northbrooke House is reportedly haunted by a cat, the reincarnation of a woman murdered by her lover.

title: Woman with Horse
notes: While driving by the entrance of the hall, a police inspector spotted a horse being led by a lady with a bonnet and old fashioned clothing. He turned his car around for a second look, but both the animal and woman had vanished.

title: Man in Grey Suit
location_name: Hastings (Sussex) - Royal Victoria Hotel, St Leonards-on-Sea
notes: Not seen for many years now, the man in grey is thought to have been a previous manger that died in a fire on site. His dog also died in the blaze and has been seen running about in the corridors.
address: Marina, St Leonards, Saint Leonards-on-sea TN38 0BD, UK

title: Rider
notes: A phantom man upon a shaggy horse was once said to haunt this hill, manifesting to other riders and causing their mounts to bolt.

title: Dennis
location_name: Hay-on-Wye (Powys) - Baskerville Hall Hotel (aka Clyro Court)
notes: Amanda Hyde, writing for the Times Online, documented her less than convincing experiences during a ghost hunt on the site, during which she encountered Dennis (via a medium) and communicated with Edward using a glass. More convincing is the encounter a chef reported in 1998. He reported seeing a shadow the size and shape of a large dog in the kitchen a few minutes before feeling a nip on his right thigh. He cried out, three nearby staff asking what was wrong, although they were disbelieving when he told them what he had seen and felt. The following day a heavily bruised bite mark appeared on his leg.
address: Clyro Court, Clyro, Hay-on-Wye, Hereford HR3 5LE, UK

title: St Bernard Dogs
notes: A pair of St Bernard dogs were spotted by a man walking his own dogs. Concerned that their owner was nowhere in sight, the witness closely followed them around a corner, where they vanished. The man later found out that disappeared at the rear of the house where they once lived, even though the alleyway leading to the house had been closed off.

title: White Pig
location_name: Hoe Benham (Berkshire) - General area of village
notes: This village was the haunt for several years of a large phantom pig, said to contain the spirit of a local farmer who took his own life. A handful of people have also reported other ghostly creatures, including a sheep and a flying 'white thing'.

title: Horses' Hooves
notes: The hill is apparently home to the sound of horses moving around, the noise providing an uneasy feeling to any witness.
address: Stutton Rd, Brantham, Manningtree CO11, UK

title: Black Lady
notes: Workers in the castle were too scared to visit the attic, where it is said the ghost haunts. The basement in turn is haunted by a raven that attacks any source of light taken down there.

title: Large Pig
notes: A large phantom hog is said to haunt this country lane, sometimes accompanied by a rolling barrel.
address: Pig Ln, Horton, Devizes SN10, UK

title: Lady in Brown Dress
datetime: Mostly unknown, tap felt in October 1995
notes: Local legend says this ghost is a serving girl whose child was killed when the lord of the manor threw it from an attic window. A pair of disembodied hands have been reported floating around the building, also said to belong to the former servant, while on the odd occasion, a male figure in spurs has been observed. A visitor to the manor in 1995 felt a strong tap on her shoulder. Expecting it to be her boyfriend, she turned, but was alone in the room. There are several other local legends about the manor, including hauntings by a horse and carriage, a phantom woman, and ghostly birds.
address: Hangleton Valley Dr, Brighton and Hove, Hove BN3 8AN, UK

title: Hare
notes: A phantom hare which ran around the area would once be hunted by locals, but it could never be killed.

title: Feline
notes: This spectral cat has been seen running around the property, a shadow of its former self.

title: Godstone
location_name: Inishkea Islands (County Mayo) - Exact areas unknown
datetime: Godstone pre twentieth century, Crane still present?
notes: The Godstone was said to have granted islanders with control over the weather, credited with increasing the speed of potato growth, and could also put out fires. The stone was cast into the sea at some point in the nineteenth century after a religious man took exception to it. The islands were (or are) also home to an immortal crane - the bird is said to have sat alone from the dawn of time, waiting until the end of the world.

title: Gentleman in Top Hat
location_name: Isle of Yell (Shetland) - Windhouse, near the Lumbister RSPB Reserve
notes: Merely one of the spooks found here, the man in the top hat not putting on the Ritz shares the house with a woman dressed in silken clothes, a former pet dog, and a servant who is doomed to spend the rest of time ascending the staircase.

title: White Horse
location_name: Ivinghoe (Buckinghamshire) - Ivinghoe Aston to Slapton Road
notes: While travelling along this road during heavy rain at night, a taxi driver encountered someone riding a white horse. The driver had to stop abruptly to avoid the rider, and when the driver looked up, man and horse had vanished. When the witness later mentioned the encounter to other people, they said it was not the first time the entity had been seen.
address: Slapton Rd, Leighton Buzzard LU7, UK

title: House Cat
notes: A phantom cat is reputed to haunt this building, though tends to be felt rather than seen.
address: 42 High St, Kegworth, Derby DE74 2DA, UK

title: Rusty
location_name: Keighley (West Yorkshire) - Bracken Arms public house
notes: Paranormal investigation groups reported recording a laugh and a phantom dog barking after the landlady called them in. Locals named the dog as 'Rusty', a Jack Russell who died seventeen years previous.
address: 52 Bracken Bank Grove, Keighley BD22 7BE, UK

title: Big Collie
location_name: Keighley (West Yorkshire) - Woods just outside village
notes: This spectral dog appeared briefly to prevent a robbery on the roadside, vanishing once the threat had passed.

title: Lost Girl
notes: The gatehouse is haunted by a little girl who asks for her daddy, a man dressed in black killed in a sword fight, and an old lady who breaks the same candle, time after time. A phantom little boy is reported in the stables, as are ghostly chickens and horses.
address: Kenilworth Castle and Elizabethan Garden, Castle Rd, Kenilworth CV8 1NG, UK

title: Victorian Lady
notes: The ghostly Victorian was just one of several spirits thought to haunt this building; the sound of a small dog was often heard as it ran across the floor, and a man with his head dressed in blood soaked bandages (later named as John Leith, shot by an associate during a duel) was seen by one owner.

title: Headless Horseman
location_name: Kilcummer (County Cork) - Hill in the area
notes: This area was the haunt of a headless horseman who died while racing the country roads. His ghost was put to rest after a local man was brave enough to let the decapitated figure ride by his side.
address: Kilcummer Upper, Kilcummer, Co. Cork, Ireland

title: Black Cat of Killakee
location_name: Killakee (County Dublin) - General area, including the Arts Centre
notes: The Arts Centre found itself haunted by a violent manifestation during the 1970s, which destroyed rooms which the owners securely padlocked up. A short spectral man was seen to transform into a black cat before vanishing, leading to speculation that the building was haunted by a demon once summoned by the nearby Hell Fire Club.

title: Black Pig
location_name: Kiltrustan (County Roscommon) - Road close to old plantation
notes: A phantom black pig made itself known to children. Adults standing in the same area could not perceive it, even when the children shouted that the entity was running around their feet.

title: Horses
notes: Tourists staying in this cottage were said to have been awaken as the building shook due to a stampede of phantom horses. Footsteps have also been heard outside, and the face of a quickly vanishing sailor observed looking in through windows.

title: White Lady
notes: This woman leapt to her death from the battlements after discovering that her father had mistakenly shot dead her newlywed husband. Since her death, she has been seen walking through locked doors and was blamed for knocking unconscious at least two witnesses. The site was also haunted by a rabbit which attacked guards asleep on duty.
address: OPW, Summercove, Kinsale, Co. Cork, Ireland

title: Sheepdog
location_name: Kirkby Overblow (North Yorkshire) - Area surrounding village
notes: A forgetful farmer left his dog out in the sheep's field, where it finally starved to death. The dog's shade now runs around the village, to warn all farmers never to forget their pets.

title: Shaggy Foal
notes: Someone (apparently) encountered a large, shaggy, donkey-like entity in this area.

title: Bell Ringer
datetime: Twentieth century (bell ringer), bird not unknown
notes: This ghostly figure is often spotted out of the corner of one eye, before disappearing. A local legend says that a large, evil bird set up home in the bell tower, interrupting services with its noise - the creature became displaced after the vicar cast a spell with the help of the local community.

title: Black Cockerel
notes: This creature is said to still be imprisoned within the walls of this public house - it contained the spirit of an angry local rector who died after falling (or being pushed) down a flight of stairs. In his cockerel form, the rector attacked everyone he met; a maid at the inn finally managed to trap the creature in an oven, and a builder bricked it up.
address: Punch Bowl Inn, Lanreath, Looe PL13 2NX, UK

title: Headless Chicken Runs Blindly
notes: With an unknown origin, a headless chicken appears and runs blindly around the gravestones.

title: Headless Coachman
notes: A groom and his assistant heard galloping approaching Leam Hall. Expecting the lord and lady of the hall, the pair stood outside awaiting the coach. However, instead of the master's coach, the approaching vehicle was of a 'strange design' and was driven by a headless man. The phantom coach was driven straight past the witnesses and vanished into a closed gate.

title: George
location_name: Leamington (Warwickshire) - Brookhurst (demolished 1960), on site of Leamington Golf Club
notes: Workmen demolishing this old house reported that 'something' was trying to stop them. Brookhurst had a history of hauntings, from the sounds of a large bird-like creature flying around to phantom footsteps and doors which unlocked themselves.

title: Disconcerting Monk
notes: There are mixed opinions to whether the library here is haunted by a grey monk or a white lady. A tormented Cavalier has been seen in the Great Hall, a phantom policeman haunts other parts of the building, a ghostly dog has been reported in the courtyard, a black cat in the Great Hall, and at least one witness claims to have been shocked to see a pair of legs manifest from the portrait of Henry Earl of Huntington hanging in the Major's Parlour. Ghost Scene Investigations and Leicester City Museums released video footage in 2006 of what appears to be a ghostly figure crawling across the floor in one room.

title: Clark's Dog
notes: This malnourished hound, once owned by a criminal hanged nearby, spent the rest of its life as a minor celebrity and now is occasionally spotted lurking around the castle walls.

title: Clark's Dog Too
notes: The hungry phantom hound reported to haunt the castle walls would sometimes try to enter this inn, scratching and barking at the door.
address: The Strugglers inn, 83 Westgate, Lincoln LN1 3BG, UK

title: Boy playing Flute
notes: This figure was seen and heard on the lanes, in trees and behind bushes in this village - the music he produced described as enchanting, but with an underlying danger. The same area is haunted by a pale white calf.

title: Red Eyes
location_name: Liskeard (Cornwall) - Park Fenton, Liskerrett Road and surroundings
notes: Staying on what was a traveller's site, this witness felt a strange 'force' swarm over their van. The benevolent energy remained for around a minute before leaving. The witness ran out of the van and into their friend's coach to share the story, only to find out it had happened to other people who had parked in the same spot. Other travellers had reported red animal eyes looking through their windows, and one other person had seen a procession of ghostly figures down nearby Lake Lane.

title: White Lady
notes: This spook is reportedly a woman whose body was found on the nearby beach. A long dead cat is also reported to play on the beds at night, while a man in 'modern' dress is said to slowly materialise from the feet up.

title: Stabbed Bulldog
location_name: Littleport (Cambridgeshire) - Bulldog Bridge, which connects Littleport & Shippea Hill
notes: After saving a local girl from a friar who attempted to rape her, the dog was killed by her attacker. It can now be seen crossing the bridge.
address: Bulldog Bridge, Littleport, ELY CB7 4SY, UK

title: White Horse
location_name: Llangynwyd (Mid Glamorgan) - Roads in the area
notes: Even the most fearless residents on the village felt terror when they encountered this phantom, snow-white mare along the roads at night.

title: White Horse
location_name: Loch Dornoch (Highland) - Straight piece of road lined with trees on both sides
notes: Witness Mr Morrison stopped his car suddenly when he encountered a white horse ridden by a Victorian man in the middle of the road. Both entities vanished quickly into the distance.

title: White Horse
location_name: Lochgelly (Fife) - Old house (may no longer stand) on the banks of Loch Gelly, Powguild
notes: A white horse once haunted the area around the house. Another animal ghost was reputedly a mole - a former resident declared that she would haunt the garden she loved in the form of the furry mammal, and so she did.

title: Sir John Plumley
location_name: Locking (Somerset) - Manor House, and Coach House public house (was once the manor house's stables)
notes: Hanged for his part in the Monmouth revolt, Sir John and his dog are now seen on his former property. His wife also puts in the occasional appearance - she killed herself after her husband's execution.

title: Hare
notes: This ghost is said to be a witch, who evaded capture by transforming herself into a hare.

title: Dogs
notes: The house was haunted by the sounds of arguing, rapping, and an invisible force that felt like a dog pushing against people's legs. This 'dog' was quite common, many people felt it, and other pet dogs brought into the house appeared happy to play with an invisible friend. A phantom nun was also said to walk the garden.
address: Mid Atholl,Strathtay and Grandtully, Pitlochry PH9 0LW, UK

title: Hare
location_name: Looe (Cornwall) - Hill between Talland and the Jolly Sailor Inn (A387 road)
notes: This white creature has been observed running down the hill, disappearing as it reaches the public house. It is considered an ill omen if seen, and thought to be the spirit of a young woman who took her own life.
address: Princes Square, East Looe, Looe PL13 2EP, UK

title: Cat
notes: People have reported being woken during the night when a small cat jumps on their bed, though they can no trace of it when they turn on the light to investigate.

title: Black Pig
notes: This creature is said to be the ghost of Judge Jeffereys. Another ghost seen in the vicinity is that of Lady Howard.
address: Silver St, Lydford, Okehampton EX20 4BH, UK

title: White Cow
notes: During times of drought or hardship, a white cow would appear in the middle of the circle and allow everyone in the neighbourhood to fill one bucket with milk. Michell, a local witch after whom the circle was later named, milked the cow into a sieve until the creature vanished.
address: Stapeley Hill, White Grit SY15 6DE, UK

title: Winner
notes: A group of miners playing cards invited a passing stranger to join their game. He sat with them, quickly winning all hands, although the game quickly came to an end when the miners fled, having realised their guest had cloven feet.
address: Mid Glamorgan, 107 Llwydarth Rd, Maesteg CF34 9EU, UK

title: Horses
notes: A legend once said that horses were driven around the area of the church by an invisible entity. Fire emerging from the horses' nostrils would be blamed for darkened grass and the flaking paint on fencing. In the same area, a phantom donkey was said to have been banished after a man kicked the creature.

title: invisible Bird
notes: Two occupants of a house on this street felt the sensation of an invisible bird passing through their heads, and out a closed window.

title: Horse
notes: Walking her dogs, this witness heard and felt a large horse trotting towards her. She called the dogs to one side, to let the rider pass, but upon turning around, she could she nothing there.

title: Paddy
location_name: Manchester (Greater Manchester) - House at corner of Spath Road and Holme Road
notes: Seen in the garden of this house, this small black dog's grave is only a few metres away from the spot it haunts.
address: Spath Rd & Holme Rd, Manchester M20 2TX, UK

title: Headless Cat
location_name: Manchester (Greater Manchester) - Private house along Lower Seedley Road, Seedley
notes: For a two month period, until they finally moved out, a family endured the sounds of a cat in terrible pain. During the haunting, the lady of the house was found unconscious - she said she had encountered a headless cat which pawed at her legs.

title: Woman with Pig
notes: A former occupier of the rectory reported seeing a woman driving a pig over the lawn. She turned briefly to call for her husband, and when she looked again the woman and hog had vanished.

title: Golden Coach
notes: Pulled by a team of four headless horses, this golden coach appears at The Hatches.

title: Kitchen Worker
notes: It has been reported that this pub has three phantoms. A female form is said to work in the kitchen, a man has been observed in the garden, and a dog.

title: Roman on Horseback
notes: This Roman centurion passes through the area on horseback.
address: Ridgeway Cl, Market Lavington, Devizes SN10, UK

title: Mr Baker
location_name: Melbury Bubb (Dorset) - Dirt track along Bubdown Hill, known as Murderers Lane?
notes: Mr Baker was a farmer murdered on the hill in 1694. His ghost returns on the anniversary of the crime, possibly driving a horse and carriage.

title: Arthur Bryant Haig
notes: Former owner of the house, Arthur Bryant Haig, is still said to mount his (now phantom) chestnut horse and ride through his lands towards a nearby river.

title: Goose
location_name: Melsonby (North Yorkshire) - Berry Well (may no longer exist)
notes: This well was haunted by a ghostly entity which had taken the form of a white goose.

title: Large Black Tomcat
location_name: Melton (Suffolk) - The Horse and Groom Public House
notes: The pub (particularly room number 3) has been home to a ghostly cat. The phantom feline has a habit of laying across the throat of people sleeping, only to jump off and vanish once they awake.

title: Greyhound
notes: This phantom greyhound began to haunt the area after a local lady drowned herself nearby.

title: Echoing Footfalls
location_name: Monkton Farleigh (Wiltshire) - King's Arms public house
notes: These footsteps, associated with long dead feet, have been reported moving around the building. One group of people in the bar heard the swooping of wings close above them, though no birds could be seen. The pub is also haunted by the sounds of a wailing woman, said to cry on the anniversary of her death, caused when her runaway coach smashed into a wall of the building.
address: Kings Arms, Monkton Farleigh, Bradford-on-Avon BA15 2QH, UK

title: Lucky Venture?
notes: The stadium was briefly haunted by a horse named Lucky Venture which died on the track. The animal's shade was exorcised by a couple of local university students.

title: Heavy Dog
notes: Three members of the family which lived here experienced the sensation of a heavy dog laying on them in bed. The property was also home to a lady in an old fashioned floor length dress, and a man in a suit and waistcoat who told a builder to 'get on with it' - the builder refused to re-enter the house and quit.

title: Bacon's Chicken
notes: The site where Francis Bacon put theory into practice with refrigeration in 1626; the featherless, panicking chicken that has been seen running around the area on cold winter nights is all that remains of that early experiment.

title: Mother Marnes
location_name: N6 (Greater London) - Ye Olde Gatehouse public house, Highgate
notes: Murdered many moons ago for her money, Mother Marnes now meanders around the building with her pet cat. A ghost hunting team claimed to have contacted Francis Bacon (philosopher, not painter) in the basement during an investigation.
address: Public House, Greater N Rd, London N6 4BE, UK

title: The Blue Lady
location_name: Nettlestone (Isle of Wight) - The Priory hotel
notes: This young teenage girl once haunted the former priory along with her little dog, though sightings have been rare as of late. The dog outlived the girl by several years, was stuffed after its death, and is now kept in a glass case within the building. The haunting is said to grow quite intense if the hound is ever removed from the hotel.

title: Heavy Footsteps
location_name: Newcastle upon Tyne (Tyne and Wear) - Willington Mill
notes: Just a small part of the phenomena, the footsteps were heard first. Over a period of weeks, these developed into bangs, whistling, the sounds of machinery, and finally a voice that spoke. Phantom animals were seen around the building. The family finally moved out, though the spirit stayed a little longer (but was much quieter).

title: Sounds of a Horse
notes: While looking for two pet cats in a barn, this witness heard a horse wearing metal shoes walking directly outside. The witness thought the horses had come back from racing so left the barn to help, but nothing could be seen. Two different members of staff later told the witness that they had also heard horses moving around but could never find the creature responsible, and one said that they had been told by the former owner of the yard that the site was haunted by a racehorse.

title: Fox
notes: A creature so cunning that it was never caught by the local fox hunting group, it now gloats by sitting on the golf club walls.

title: Lion
notes: A zookeeper and his granddaughter came across the glowing apparition of a lion while out walking in the park at evening. The creature faded away as they approached it. The keeper knew that one of the lions at his zoo was ill, and when he went into work the following day, was told the sick lion had died the previous evening - at the time of the sighting. More recently, a glowing green figure was observed by a witness in the park after midnight and had actually caused the witness to flee; another person with the witness was unable to see anything. The witness returned to the scene the following day to try to understand what they had seen and realised the figure first manifested in a bush and had moved at a very fast pace.

title: Dazzling Little Donkey
location_name: NW9 (Greater London) - The Hyde, between Hendon and Kingsbury
notes: This ghostly donkey reportedly radiates colour and light as it passes.

title: Horse
location_name: Odstock (Wiltshire) - Lane adjoining St Marys Church
notes: A woman in the sitting in the passenger side of a car opened her window as the vehicle left the church. Suddenly both the woman and driver heard a horse trotting alongside the vehicle, even though no animal could be seen. As the vehicle reached the main road, the trotting ceased.

title: Robert Leigh
notes: One of many ghosts belonging to this building, Leigh was reported to walk around the grounds carrying an unknown person's head under one arm. A woman in white rustling silk glides along the corridors of the building, the sad sounds of a harpsichord can be heard, and on occasion a black dog lurks outside.

title: Knight
location_name: Outer London: Richmond (Greater London) - Kingston Hill area
notes: A phantom knight upon a white horse was once thought to haunt this area.

title: Raven
location_name: Outer London: West Drayton (Greater London) - West Drayton church
notes: For many years, this large black bird haunted the church and its grounds - it would tap and peck at coffins, sit on the pews, and generally create a nuisance.

title: Woman in Crinoline
location_name: Pembroke (Dyfed) - Bush House (currently a boarding house?)
notes: A night watchman spotted a man carrying a gun and accompanied with three dogs walk across the grounds and vanish into a pond. A year later, three guests at the house were awoken by persistent hammering on the door and walls of their room. The following night they spotted a woman wearing a long dress in the garden bathed in a strange light - she disappeared through a wall.

title: White Hare
location_name: Pengersick (Cornwall) - Former castle (on site of current castle)
notes: A former lord of the castle had an affair with a woman in a faraway land, who gave birth to a son. When the lord returned home (and back to his wife) the woman and child followed him. The lord threw his former lover and the child into the sea. The child survived and was picked up by a passing ship, but the woman drowned, returning as a white hare which haunted the lord for the rest of his life.

title: Horse & Dog
location_name: Penselwood (Wiltshire) - Sloane Track - between Penselwood and Stourton
notes: A man who wagered that he could cross the track in record time fell from his horse and snapped his neck. The man on horseback, now minus a head, with a hound in tow, tries again and again to make the journey once a year.

title: Woman with Dog
notes: A farmer and an aid chasing down stray cattle encountered a phantom woman dressed in black crossing the bridge. She was accompanied by a large dog, being led by a diamond chain.

title: Monk
location_name: Pewsey (Wiltshire) - Fyfield Manor, the Monk's Tower
datetime: Monk in mid twentieth century, coach unknown
notes: One man was told by a gardener that a phantom monk could be heard ascending the staircase in the tower. The man went on to spend three years living in the tower and never heard a thing. Another local story states that the driveway of the building is haunted by a phantom coach and four.

title: Dog that is not There
location_name: Pimperne (Dorset) - Road between village and Letton Hill
notes: Heard dragging chains as it ran, this phantom hound is said to be invisible, though it has been touched by witnesses as it passes them. A similar story is told in Stourpaine, Dorset.

title: Alarm
notes: Two employees would be woken at 02:15h by their alarms over a two week period, although neither had set their devices. The figure of a young woman quickly vanished after being spotted by one employee, and a cat would appear in the building before disappearing in areas with no possible exist. Finally, men (but never women) would find one room particularly disturbing, with builders refusing to work within and guests leaving during the night.

title: A Man and his Dog
location_name: Plumtree (Nottinghamshire) - Outside Perkins' Bar & Bistro (formally Plumtree railway station)
notes: The ghosts of a man and his dog haunt the site of former railway tracks just outside this bistro, where it is believed they were killed by a steam engine.
address: Station Rd, Plumtree, Nottingham NG12 5NA, UK

title: Monk
notes: This park is said to be haunted by a figure wearing a habit near the gates of the lodge, a white lady who drowned in a lake while eloping, and a strange animal with very large eyes.
address: Radford Park, Plymstock, Plymouth PL9 7NY, UK

title: Bloody Horse
location_name: Pool (Cornwall) - Road and tunnel close to South Crofty mine
notes: Driving past the mine in bad weather, a driver and his passenger felt a strange negative sensation wash over them. The driver slammed on the brakes and as the car stopped, they spotted a bloody horse standing in the lights. The driver started to climb out of his car to move the horse out of the road, but the creature had gone - there was a steep drop to one side of the road and a wire fence to the other side.
address: South Crofty Mine, Dudnance Ln, Pool, Redruth TR15 3QT, UK

title: Fox Terrier
notes: Elliott O'Donnell wrote that a medium who receded here believed her home to be haunted by several cats, which were mistreated by a former owner of the property. The medium also detected the presence of a fox terrier.
address: 44 Laburnum Grove, North End, Portsmouth PO2 0EP, UK

title: Scratching Monkey
notes: Home to several ghosts, the monkey was accidentally bricked up behind a wall and now tries to escape. Headless figures allegedly sit around the dinner table, duellists re-enact their last fights outside, and a grey lady walks through walls in the east wing.
address: Athelhampton Rd, Puddletown, Dorchester DT2 7LG, UK

title: Miss Effie
notes: The (former) owner of Miss Ellie, a dead hamster which was buried on the goal line of this football ground, claimed that the animal's spirit had prevented Reading FC being relegated.

title: Woman in White
location_name: Reagill (Cumbria) - Country lanes in the area
notes: It is not clear if the woman in white, a large black hound and a black hog which once haunted this area were all the same entity. The phantom (or phantoms) would all suddenly grow once seen, before pursuing any witness.

title: Farm Animals
notes: Built on a farm, one house here was reportedly haunted by pigs in the garden. Poltergeist like activity was reported inside the building itself, including exploding cups and electrical equipment powering itself up and off.

title: Horse
notes: A farmer heard a galloping horse rapidly approaching him, even though he could not see the creature. Moments later, there was a crashing sound, and a large gust of wind passed the man, even though the creature remained invisible.

title: Horses
notes: This lane is reputed to be haunted by the phantom sounds of horses.

title: Coach and Horses
location_name: Rockfield (Gwent) - Road between Rockfield and Monmouth (Ancre Lane?)
notes: A coach pulled by a team of frantic horses reputedly vanishes into a field after crashing through a wall on the roadside.

title: Cat
location_name: Ropley (Hampshire) - Cottage along Old Chapel Lane
notes: An old woman who lived in this cottage was convinced it was haunted by a black cat, which she believed was her mother, due to the way it walked.

title: Shag Foal
location_name: Roxby (Lincolnshire) - Road formally known as Goosey Lane (and/or Boggart Lane)
notes: A ghostly donkey covered in long shaggy hair was said to haunt this lane.
address: Goosey Lane, Spring House, Water Ln, Winterton, Scunthorpe DN15 9RR, UK

title: Hound
notes: A phantom black dog, unlikely to be a shuck, has been reported to walk around the corridors of the inn.

title: Sister Lucy
notes: Sister Lucy ran away from her chapel, but realising she had made a mistake, tried to return. She slipped as she passed the well and drowned in its waters. Her ghost is now said to haunt the area. The same location is also said to be home to a phantom horse.

title: Hare
location_name: Saint Martin (Guernsey) - Area around Profond Camp
notes: A phantom white hare was said to manifest when storms rolled through the area.
address: Rue de Profond Camp, Guernsey GY4, Guernsey

title: Ewe
location_name: Sandwick (Orkney) - Nether Benzieclett farm (no longer standing)
notes: The phantom of a murdered man returned in the form of an ewe, appearing at the scene of the crime every morning at 1am.

title: Tailless Dog
location_name: SE1 (Greater London) - Anchor Inn, Clink Street
notes: This inn is haunted by a dog which lost its tail while trying to protect its master from press gangers.

title: Boy with Cat
location_name: SE13 (Greater London) - Private property along Roxley Road, Lewisham
notes: The London Ghost Hunters group investigated this property after the owners reported strange knocking and phantom footsteps. A little boy with a cat was also spotted by one witness.

title: Dead Dog
location_name: SE3 (Greater London) - Blackheath, exact location unknown
notes: A woman from Bath saw a vision of her dog here, at the exact moment her pet died at home.

title: Fox Hunt
notes: A local legend says that the five trees are where an old witch coven would meet. One of the witches took the form of a fox and lead the local hunt over the edge of a cliff - the scene is repeated if the weather conditions are right.

title: Irish Wolfhound
notes: A large phantom dog that has been reported running along the road is thought to be an Irish Wolfhound looking for his master.
address: Blandford Rd, Shapwick, Blandford Forum DT11 9JL, UK

title: Heron
notes: Two animal ghosts were once said to roam here; a phantom heron would fly down into the lake and a spectral horse roamed the surrounding area.
address: University of Reading, Earley Gate, Whiteknights Rd, Reading RG6 6BZ, UK

title: One Man and his Dog
notes: Two men witnessed a man with a hound, pursuing several horses along a steep rocky ridge. Man and beasts suddenly vanished - the witnesses were at first concerned that they had all fallen over the edge of a cliff, though were unable to find any bodies.

title: Cow
location_name: South Tawton (Devon) - Hamlet just outside the village, exact location not known
datetime: Circa late nineteenth century, said to appear at midnight
notes: A Guernsey Cow said to haunt this location was once a woman who committed a 'terrible deed' and was transformed into this animal for 4,900 years as punishment.

title: Lady with Dog
notes: Late at night, a phantom woman is alleged to appear walking about the hill with her dog.

title: Black Dog
location_name: St Giles In the Wood (Devon) - Lanes in the area
notes: A large black dog was said to haunt the lanes and the farms in this area, as far down as Copplestone.
address: St Giles in the Wood, Torrington EX38 7JH, UK

title: Snorting Boars
location_name: St Leonards (Buckinghamshire) - Uphill and general area
notes: The sound of running boars is reported here, snorting loudly as they go.

title: This Little Piggy...
location_name: St Pierre du Bois (Guernsey) - House at Le Lorier
notes: A sow and her litter were said to pass through the house, in protest of food to the poor being withheld.

title: The Birds
location_name: St Pierre du Bois (Guernsey) - Les Jaonnets Lane
notes: A flock of screeching invisible phantom birds apparently attacked a witness walking along this lane.

title: Horse and Cart
location_name: St Saviour (Guernsey) - De Garis house (no longer present)
notes: The sounds of a horse and cart unloading stone has been heard around this former house. Another story says it is haunted by a sow and her piglets.

title: Running Dog
notes: Upset by the continued yapping of the gravedigger's dog, the bell ringer poisoned the pet pooch. The animal died an agonised death, and some people now claim the dog runs around the church, floating slightly above the ground.
address: Pankhurst Cres, Chells, Stevenage SG2 0QF, UK

title: The White Rabbit of Etruria
notes: This ghostly white rabbit appeared after the phantom cries and scream of a young boy. It was thought that the rabbit was the ghost of a fourteen year old lad murdered by another teenager while arguing over a wager.

title: Piano Cat
location_name: Stoke on Trent (Staffordshire) - New Inn, Basford (no longer open)
notes: When the piano keys could be heard striking without human intervention, a ghost cat would be blamed. The phantom feline was said to be appeased when a small amount of beer would be left out for him.

title: Early Morning Rider
location_name: Stoke Trister (Somerset) - Field in the village
notes: While walking his dog, this witness heard horse's hooves walking alongside him for several minutes, never becoming and louder or quieter, or changing pace. The witness thought the sound may have been coming from behind a nearby hedge, though there was nothing visible.

title: Horseman
notes: A horseman with his caparisoned horse was said to travel over this bridge, the sound of his jangling armour causing unease to anyone unfortunate enough to be nearby.

title: Flaming Carriage
notes: Surrounded by fields, this narrow path would have been quite spooky, especially if one encountered the entity which was said to haunt it. A phantom flaming carriage contained a headless woman would move at breakneck speeds, drawn by a team of six greyhounds. Why? No one knows.

title: Dog That Still isn't There
location_name: Stourpaine (Dorset) - Village square, heading out towards the hills
notes: Heard dragging chains as it ran, this phantom hound is never seen. It is thought the dog died while escaping from a master who mistreated it. A similar story is told in Pimperne, Dorset.

title: Old Woman
notes: This house is reputedly haunted by the ghost of an elderly lady and her black dog.

title: Ranulph
location_name: Stratton (Cornwall) - Blanchminster Castle (were ruins in a field, current status not known), located at Binhamy grounds
notes: When this knight returned from the crusades, he discovered his wife had thought him dead, and had remarried. He spent his remaining days living rough in the land around the castle, and his ghost can be seen in the former moat. One version of the story says that his ghost takes the form of a hare.

title: Pale Donkey
notes: A small white donkey haunts the area where its master was robbed and murdered while travelling across the heathland. While the owner's body was found, the donkey vanished without trace, now only appearing briefly before Christmas once a year.

title: Bjorn
location_name: Styhead (aka Sty Head) Pass (Cumbria) - General area
notes: Said to be a thirteenth century outlaw, the ghost of Bjorn wanders the area carrying a bag of living cats. He is easily recognisable, having lost his head.

title: Old Man Marriott
location_name: Sutton Lane End (Cheshire) - Rossendale wood (known locally as Marriott`s Wood)
datetime: Unknown, said to occur around midnight
notes: A ghostly figure of Old Man Marriott is said to walk down the road with his sheep dog and his gun.

title: Jeremiah Abershaw
notes: No wombles here, only a ghostly padfoot looking for victims after being hanged for his crimes two hundred years ago. On two occasions the ghostly entity has been seen wearing convicts clothing. Abershaw is sometimes heard on horseback, hooves heard thumping on the ground, yet the animal never appears.
address: Wimbledon and Putney Commons, Windmill Rd, Wimbledon Common, London SW19 5NR, UK

title: Cat
notes: A cat has been seen in the building running across the bar area. A member of staff who spotted the animal in July 2007 mistook it for a real creature, only to be told otherwise when she mentioned her encounter to others. There are also stories of a phantom man who sits by the fireplace with a beer.

title: Black Cat
notes: A ghostly black cat was spotted by the occupants of this property.

title: Mary on Piggyback
notes: Mary killed her husband's prized pig, so he drowned her in return. Mary is now said to ride the hog around the area of the river where her husband murdered her.

title: Horses
notes: There is said to be a secret passage connected this site and Dally Castle, with phantom horses heard running the route at midnight.

title: Headless Group
location_name: Tenby (Dyfed) - Between Tenby and Sampson Cross
notes: A headless team of horses driven by a headless coachman carries a headless lady in a coach along this road every night. As they reach the crossroads known as Sampson Cross, some ten miles from their starting point, they all disappear in a large explosion. Some people believe the entities have been laid, and now rest in a nearby lake.

title: House with Glowing Windows
datetime: 31 October (reoccurring), Wedding party occurs. Lights seen May 2013
notes: After a murder of a newlywed bride, this house was left to fall into ruin. Some people reported that a glowing candle could be seen moving past various windows, while others claimed a ghostly wedding party occurred. The Hall is now lost below the waters of the flooded valley of Thirlmere, which is home to a phantom dog which swims through the water. In 2013 a witness camping in the area became unnerved after witnessing two silently moving lights along the road.
address: 4 Brotto Cottages, Thirlmere, Keswick CA12 4TN, UK

title: White Lass Beck
location_name: Thirsk (North Yorkshire) - Stream in the area
notes: The woman in white who haunted this body of water was thought to be a maid murdered in the area, her body discovered buried in a gravel pit. As well as manifesting as a woman, White Lass Beck would also take the form of a white dog or white heifer.

title: Two Monks
location_name: Thornham Parva (Suffolk) - Road heading towards the village
notes: A male witness reported cycling home towards the village at one night when he passed by two hooded figures in monks' garb and a white dog. The two figures raised a hand and wished the man goodnight, but when he directly looked at the pair, the witness realised the inside of the hoods were empty - the monks had no heads.

title: Beaten Donkey
notes: The area around the inn is haunted by a donkey that was beaten to death by its owner.

title: Horse
notes: One unnamed lane in the village is the haunt of a headless horse. The village must attract decapitated entities, as a headless pig is also said to haunt the general area.

title: Headless Dog
notes: A ghost of a little headless dog wagging its tail was taken at a tea party in the village early in the twentieth century. The photographer, an ex-Scotland Yard Inspector, claimed he saw nothing when taking the image.

title: Garden Monks
notes: Several shades have been reported here, including monks walking about the garden, a small dog that runs around the same area, a grey lady that stands by a fireplace and another female who haunts a bedroom. A phantom coach can only be seen or heard by members of the family and is an omen of impending death.

title: White Rabbit
notes: The white rabbit is said to appear at different times of the year, running about the churchyard.

title: Last Wolf
location_name: Trillick (County Tyrone) - Forest and mountain in the area
notes: A phantom white wolf, thought to have been the last one killed in the area, haunts the mountain where she was born. The creature has reportedly been seen leaping out at a car as it passed some bushes and also observed by a family out picnicking.

title: Headless Rider
notes: A beautiful white horse carrying a headless rider gallops across the valley when the night sky is right. The phantom is either a peddler or a soldier, depending on the legend.

title: Pet Duck
notes: This tame bird was killed by a landlord after it took to attacking patrons. Unfortunately, death failed to prevent the ankle pecker continuing its work, and it fell on members of the clergy to exorcise the spirit.

title: Horses
location_name: Twechar (Dumbartonshire) - Antonine Wall - Bar Hill Fort
notes: While picnicking with her daughter, this witness heard a stampede of horses close by, but no animals could be seen.

title: Man in Black Cape
notes: More frequently seen in the nineteenth century, this ghostly gentleman, complete with cape and cane, could be seen walking his dog around the estate. It was thought that he had suffered a heart attack and broken his neck while falling down a set of steps. There is also a local story that a death of an heir of the Shrewsbury family is always preceded by the appearance of a ghostly old gypsy on the estate.

title: Glowing Dachshund
notes: A pet belonging to two women, the dachshund disappeared one day, though its phantom manifested several times along the road for a period of weeks afterwards.

title: Giant Black Bird
location_name: W1 (Greater London) - Unknown house, Dean Street
notes: Elliott O'Donnell wrote (albeit briefly) about a large, phantom black bird which appeared so frequently that the residents grew accustom to it.

title: Old Yella
location_name: W2 (Greater London) - The Motley Gentleman's Club
notes: This establishment was haunted by a large mongrel with a shaggy yellow coat which lay asleep on top of a staircase. The creature was mistaken for a real dog by one member who tried to tap it with a stick - the stick passed through the dog and the entity faded away.

title: White Rabbit
notes: When the moon is brightest, the ghostly rabbit bolts across the churchyard and vanishes into the boundary wall. Another entity that haunts the churchyard is that of a man who tried to shoot the rabbit with a shotgun but managed to kill himself with the weapon instead!

title: Fallen Horses
location_name: Wakefield (West Yorkshire) - Denby Dale Road bridge, and river below
notes: Walking her dog across the bridge, Amanda West was frozen with fear when the sound of horses passed her - with no horses to be seen. Without warning, she then heard a loud splash. Looking into the river below, the water was calm, but the sound of panicking horses could now be heard coming from below. She later found out that the original bridge had collapsed in 1836 and a team of horses pulling a cart had drowned.

title: Terrier
notes: This small, yapping dog has attacked and scared creatures many times larger than itself - when touched the phantom pooch fades from view.
address: Manygates Ln, Sandal, Wakefield WF2 7DS, UK

title: Equine Entities
notes: Said to be heard only during the day, phantom horses gallop across the common. In addition, reports of a phantom man have been frequent since the 1940s, appearing both on the common and in the church ruins.

title: Patter of Doggy Footsteps
location_name: Walsall (West Midlands) - Private building along Lysways Street
notes: A woman living here at the time reported twice hearing what sounded like a small dog following her down the staircase, late at night. On both occasions, when she turned around nothing could be seen.

title: Snowy the Cat
location_name: Wansford (Cambridgeshire) - Yarwell tunnel, Nene Valley Railway (13m west of Peterborough)
notes: Snowy's owner, the stationmaster, was killed by a train while out looking for his pet. The cat is doomed to haunt the area in which it disappeared until found by the stationmaster.
address: Wansford Station, Nene Valley Railway Ltd, Stibbington, Peterborough PE8 6LR, UK

title: White Stag
notes: A white stag, which gained protection from the king who spent a day attempting to hunt the creature, remained to run through the area after its death.

title: The Dun Cow
location_name: Warwick (Warwickshire) - Warwick Castle and surrounding area
datetime: Prior to death in the Warwick family
notes: Appearing at least once at the castle prior to a death in the family, the phantom 'Dun Cow of Warwick' appears a few weeks before a death in the family. Way back in history, a member of the Warwick family killed the giant cow when it roamed as a living creature that terrorised people across the land.

title: Magpie
location_name: WC1 (Greater London) - Residence along Great Russell Street
notes: A phantom magpie is said to tap on the window of a house along this street before manifesting inside. The bird then perches on a floating stick, both bird and baton vanishing the moment a sound is made.

title: Fading Lady
location_name: WC2 (Greater London) - Peacock Theatre (was Royalty Theatre), Dean Street
notes: A woman clothed in an outfit dating from the Queen Anne period is reported to descend the staircase here, slowly fading before she reaches the bottom. A short scream is said to signify her complete disappearance. The female ghost has not recently manifested and appears to have been replaced by the sound of a phantom dolphin which, during life, was kept in a small tank under the stage in the 1930s.

title: Headless Horse
notes: The headless horse which runs along this road was said to have been a casualty during the civil war.

title: Hare
notes: The hare that haunts this area is thought to be the ghost of a witch. In life, she enjoyed turning herself into a lepus. Another ghost at the gate was said to be a farmer murdered by a woman. For the crime she used a dagger, from which the gate takes its name.
address: Old Daggers Gate Rd, United Kingdom

title: Dog in Cellar
notes: Delivery men at the White Hart Inn have complained of a dog darting under their feet as they worked in the cellar, though the landlords have always insisted that the area is devoid of animals.
address: 1 High St, West Mersea, Colchester CO5 8QD, UK

title: Pitch Forked Dog
notes: A farmhand discovered a dog laying in the hay - he poked it with his pitchfork, causing the entity to vanish. The sound of a coach has also been heard as it stops by the front of the house, though nothing is seen.

title: Black Bird
location_name: Whirlow (South Yorkshire) - Lane near Whirlow Hall (no longer standing)
notes: A member of the family which owned Whirlow Hall was said to haunt the lane, taking the form for a black bird with a white tail (or wings). It was capable of paralysing people; in one instance the entity froze the rider of a mule in the air as his mount continued walking along oblivious to its master's predicament.

title: Horse?
notes: While staying in a house along this road, a witness heard a horse walking outside on the gravel - when they looked from the window, nothing could be seen.

title: Footsteps
notes: Sounding like someone moving across flagstones, the echoing footfalls may belong to the owner of a ghostly dog also seen in the premises.

title: Woman Wearing Sackcloth
location_name: Wickham Market (Suffolk) - The White Hart (currently a collection of shops and flats)
notes: The ghost is said to be that of one Betty Price, executed for being a witch. The old room 14 of the Inn was also reportedly haunted, by a cat. Only one person has reported spooky occurrences since the pub has been converted, and that relates to an answer machine that switches itself on.

title: Padding Dog
notes: This invisible hound has been heard walking around the building. It is said to leave a tranquil air in its wake.

title: Old Jeffrey
location_name: Willington (Northumberland) - Old Mill (no longer intact, though some of the Rope Works still use the old mill buildings)
notes: Once said to be the most haunted site in the north of England, the mill was haunted by several ghosts. While the most well known was 'Old Jeffrey', a bald man who floated a metre off the ground, other ghosts included a lady in lavender, another woman in grey, and a third woman with empty eye sockets. Phantom animals spotted on site included a rabbit, cat, and sheep.

title: White Lady
location_name: Woburn (Bedfordshire) - The Inn (was the Bedford Arms Inn)
notes: The misty white lady is not alone in her haunting of this building. A ghostly figure is also reported to sit by the fireplace smoking a pipe, a little phantom dog by his side.

title: Cavalier
location_name: Woodborough (Wiltshire) - Road between village and Allington
notes: Returning home one night, a driver encountered a man wearing Cavalier-style clothing. The figure was accompanied by a large black dog. The driver sped away, rather than investigate further.

title: Grey Cat
location_name: Worcester (Hereford & Worcester) - Old Talbot public house
notes: Perceived by both man and animal, this ghostly cat has been known to brush up against people, only to vanish once their attention has been attracted.

title: Bear
location_name: Worcester (Hereford & Worcester) - Worcester Cathedral and College Green
notes: Ever since the Civil War there have been reports of a phantom bear that rises as if to attack. One guard is said to have taken a shot at the creature, which of course had no effect.

title: Little Girl
location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - Snickleway Inn, Goodramgate (was the Angler's Arms public house)
notes: Seen as she waits on the staircase, this entity is said to radiate peace and love. She met her death when a horse and cart delivering beer ran her over just outside the pub. The ghostly presence reported down in the basement is much more sinister and has been called evil by previous landlords. Finally, a phantom cat may rub itself against the legs of visitors.

title: Rabbit
location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - St George's Roman Catholic Church
notes: Seen moving around the aisles of the church, the ghostly rabbit always vanishes before it is caught. Some believed it to be the ghost of Dick Turpin who is buried nearby.

title: Screams
location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - The Olde Starr Inn, Stonegate
notes: Another building which once housed injured Civil War troops, the basement is said to be the place where amputations occurred, which explains the sound of cries and screams. Two black cats are also reported to haunt the pub, and an old woman has been seen on the stairs.

title: Roman Legion
location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - Treasurer's House, Minster Yard
notes: Harry Martindale was working on a ladder in the cellar of the building when a group of Roman soldiers walked past him along the old Roman Road which ran through the basement. Harry was so shocked that he fell from his ladder, and despite his terror, noted that the figures disappeared into the floor at knee height and was also later able to describe the uniforms and equipment of the Romans. At the time Harry was told that his description of the soldiers was wrong, and the uniforms and equipment did not match that which was used by the invaders, although later local archaeological discoveries resulted in Harry's observations being proved correct. Harry died in 2015 and never changed his story, nor made any money from sharing it. The building also claims to be home to several other ghosts, including a dog, a black cat, George Aislaby (killed in a duel), and Frank Green, the man who converted the interior into what it is today. The Tapestry Room is reputed to have an oppressive atmosphere and is where the wife of a former owner murdered him after he conducted one extra-marital affair too many (so the story goes).

title: Dean Gale
notes: This gentleman loved the Minster so much that in death he refused to leave and has been seen sitting while listening to sermons. A crisis apparition has also manifested here; the ghost of an officer appeared to his sister as she prayed in the church in the 1820s - at the exact moment he had died on far away shores. Also reputed to haunt the building is the sound of a dog barking.

title: Wellington
location_name: Aberdare (South Glamorgan) - Sky over Cwmbach estate
notes: A witness outside their home heard engines but could see anything. They stepped inside their doorway but as the sound of the engines grew louder the witness stepped outside again. Looking up, the witness spotted a twin engine, dark coloured Wellington bomber fly over and past, as 'real looking and sounding as any solid aircraft'. At the time, the witness thought nothing was strange about the encounter as they were unaware that there were no flying airworthy examples of a Wellington that existed.

title: RF398
notes: The last remaining Avro Lincoln Bomber situated here is said to be haunted by a pilot seen in the cockpit, while a tape recorder left on board overnight picked up the sounds of a busy airport, though the hangar and the area were devoid of life.

title: Landing Craft
notes: This ufo report was based on a story made up by two young people returning late from a leave pass. As time went on, the story grew, and it eventually became that an UFO had touched down near a hangar on the base, and was recovered and removed for reverse engineering.

title: Run and Shout
notes: Having just locked the main hangar, the key orderly heard a shout and the sound of running from within. He unlocked the hangar, but no one was could be seen.

title: Pilot
notes: A figure wearing a Second World War pilot's suit walked past one witness as he stood by a photocopier. The figure then passed through a pair of closed doors leading outside and disappeared in a field. The pilot is rumoured to be a Polish airman who hanged himself.

title: Lancaster Bomber
location_name: Barnoldswick (Lancashire) - Area near Rolls Royce's Bankfield factory, and area towards Craven
notes: Around thirty witnesses claimed to have seen a silent, grey coloured aircraft resembling a Lancaster Bomber moving silently through the sky. The accounts were virtually all isolated and spanned the month.

title: Young Men
location_name: Barton under Needwood (Staffordshire) - Former RAF Tatenhill, now a business park
notes: A witness who works on the site has reported the smell of old aftershave in the corner of a warehouse which is only detectible at 20:30h, and the sounds of young men in disused buildings. A loud crash and the shaking of a concrete floor resulting in one employee running to a warehouse, convinced that racking units must have collapsed, but nothing was disturbed. On one summer night, the sound of young children playing and singing could be heard by the main gate at 23:00h, a location many miles from the nearest residential site.
address: R67M+P2, Burton upon Trent, Burton-on-Trent DE13 9PD, UK

title: RAF Pilot
location_name: Belsay (Northumberland) - A696, just outside of village
notes: Media outlets reported that Rob Davies and Chris Felton had filmed a figure which resembled a 'RAF pilot' standing on the roadside late at night. Although the footage was poor, the witnesses said the figure was dressed all in beige and held his arm out as if hitchhiking. When they turned the car around and drove back for another look, the figure had vanished.

title: Spitfire
location_name: Biggin Hill (Kent) - Biggin Hill Airport & village
notes: Observed flying overhead, and sometimes heard, a phantom Spitfire haunts the skies of Biggin Hill - January would appear to have become its favourite haunting month. Airmen dressed in trench coats have been reported in the village; they stop people and ask directions before disappearing.
address: London Biggin Hill Airport, Main Rd, Biggin Hill TN16 3BH, UK

title: Clubfoot
notes: The nickname given to an Australian worker on the base who blew himself up trying to sabotage a Lancaster bomber during the Second World War was maybe a bit too unkind, and he was seen for years after his death walking around on the perimeter road. A ghostly NCO also made a comeback at the former base. Sergeant Sinclair managed the loading of aircraft bombs during the Second World War; a mistake caused a bomber to explode on take-off, killing Sinclair while he was trying to flag the aircraft down to alert them to the error. For a short while his ghost was seen on the runway, arms waving wildly in the air.

title: Three Airmen Playing Squash
location_name: Bircham Newton (Norfolk) - Wartime Airfield (owned by Construction Industry Training Board)
notes: Many ghosts are said to exist here, mostly coming into being during the Second World War. During the war, a car full of 'merry' (ie, very drunk) pilots crashed into a hangar, killing the driver and all the passengers; the scene is now re-enacted at irregular periods. Another group of ghosts were called into being when a bomber that crashed on the landing strip lost three of its crew - they now return to play their favourite racquet game! The sound of a squash ball echoing in the deserted building, and that of soft footfalls are more common than seeing the airmen, though one man in officer's clothing has been seen a few times.
address: Bircham Newton, King's Lynn PE31 6QR, UK

title: Man in Flying Kit
location_name: Bitteswell (Leicestershire) - Asda distribution warehouse, Magna Park (site of former airbase)
notes: The warehouse was constructed at the end of the runway at Bitteswell. It is reported that in the toilets is a permanent stain on the floor in the shape of a man, and an airman in flying kit has been repeatedly seen in there.
address: Lutterworth Rd, Bitteswell, Lutterworth LE17 4YZ, UK

title: Trolley Lady
notes: Throughout the 1950s, patients would report a ghostly woman in a green apron pushing a trolley through the sick quarters, disappearing through a door. In 2008, during a visit to Bletchley Park, four visitors sitting in a car heard a disembodied voice giving instructions to fill in log sheets.

title: Avro Lancaster
notes: A large aircraft moved silently over the treetops, so low that the witness thought it would land or crash into a field. It was later identified by the witness as an Avro Lancaster.

title: Wet Footprints
notes: A pilot killed when his new aircraft crashed into the sea was heard walking about his hut at night, his wet footfalls slopping on the floor.
address: Boscombe Down, Amesbury, Salisbury SP4, UK

title: Manchester
notes: It is thought that the sound of droning sometimes reported over the former airbase is that of a Manchester bomber - a high loss ratio ensured that these aircraft were later replaced with Lancasters. A phantom light source has also been reported moving in the tower, and a pilot in full kit was seen once.

title: Hazy Airman
notes: A man and his twelve year old son watched a hazy figure dressed in a Second World War uniform walking towards them, even though the figure did not actually move closer. The uniformed man then reached down to the ground as if to pick something up and slowly faded away. Other people have seen an airman near the former Hangar One and have also heard an air raid siren. A photograph taken in 2012 is said to show a phantom figure.

title: Airman
datetime: Unknown, but said to be most likely to appear on Mother's Day
notes: This pub is said to be home to a phantom airman wearing a great coat, most likely to be seen on the fourth Sunday of Lent.
address: Main Road, Burn, Selby YO8 8LJ, UK

title: Pipe Smoking Man
notes: When farmers worked the land where a Second World War airfield once stood, it was reported that they would occasionally be approached by a young man smoking a pipe. This figure would wish them a good evening before vanishing. It was said that this ghost was once a pilot who worked here, though no one can be quite sure of his true past.

title: Headless Airman
notes: An airman has been observed standing around with no head - it is thought that he was decapitated as he tried to bail prior to crash landing.

title: Helicopter
location_name: Buxton (Derbyshire) - Skies above Harpur Hill area
notes: A phantom helicopter was reported flying around the area at night several times over a two year period. Press and police were unable to discover who or what was behind the mysterious craft.

title: Second World War Pilot
notes: A few buildings on this site have reports of phantom pilots in their flying gear or RAF uniforms. Sounds of footsteps in empty areas are not uncommon, and there is also a story of ghostly singing.
address: Cambridge City Airport (CBG), Newmarket Rd, Cambridge CB5 8RX, UK

title: Wellington
notes: A man sitting in his living room at home heard an old fashioned aircraft approaching. The sound grew louder and louder, finally deafening and items within the house began to shake. The man went out to investigate, and found other people outside trying to spot the aircraft. Despite the weather being clear, no one could see the aircraft, although one old man was convinced that the sound was of a wellington bomber.

title: Sounds of Driven Coach
location_name: Castle Donington (Leicestershire) - Donington Hall (currently Nottingham East Midlands Airport HQ)
datetime: Prior to death (coach), man and woman in January and November 2020
notes: It was once said that when the head of the household hears a phantom coach approach but is unable to see it, a member of the family will die within a year. The hall is also reported to be haunted by a hand, possibly belonging to Lady Catherine Hungerford. During 2020 a figure was spotted on the road outside, vanishing when approached, and an air hostess wearing an old fashioned hairstyle silently moved along a hallway.
address: East Midlands Airport (EMA), Castle Donington, Derby DE74 2SA, UK

title: Officer
notes: A defunct airliner once stood at the perimeter of the airfield and was used for training. An engineer spotted lights on and returned to the flight deck where he saw a figure in the co-pilot seat who vanished in an instant. The figure was thought to be the first officer who died in a crash.
address: East Midlands Airport (EMA), Castle Donington, Derby DE74 2SA, UK

title: German Pilot
location_name: Chalvington with Ripe (Sussex) - Ripe Lane, heading towards village
notes: This pilot is said to have died during the Second World War when his aircraft crashed near the village. He now makes the occasional appearance in the field where he died. He was last seen by a woman driving towards the village.

title: Silent Bomber
location_name: Church Broughton (Derbyshire) - Skies over old air force base
notes: A bomber thought to be from the Second World War has been observed moving silently over the skies in this area.

title: USAF Pilot
location_name: Cirencester (Gloucestershire) - Queen Anne's Column, in the park
notes: Standing against the monument, this young man has been known to talk to members of the public, only to vanish after his first sentence.
address: Queen Anne's Rd, Cirencester GL7, UK

title: Three Pilots with Pale Faces
notes: Seen by two children, the three pilots are believed to be German, the sandpit being a crash site for a Messerschmitt 110 shot down during the Second World War. The area is currently a car park for a nearby factory.

title: Droning of Aircraft
notes: Since the city was levelled by German bombers during the Second World War, some people have reported hearing 'old' aircraft over the ruins of the cathedral. In 1893, witnesses spotted a human arm holding a blue torch in the north aisle. The arm vanished when one man grabbed a poker and approached it, although manifested again a short time later.

title: Passenger
notes: Workers on the 007 film Casino Royale reported seeing the ghost of a woman moving along the aisles of a 747 they used as a set. It is believed the phantom is of a female passenger who died from a heart attack at least thirty years previous.

title: Freddy's Photograph
location_name: Cranwell (Lincolnshire) - Runway of post-First World War Naval Airport
notes: A group photograph taken here in 1919 shows the face of an engineer who died three days before; Freddy Jackson had tripped and fallen into the blades of an aircraft ready for take-off. Issue 91 of Fortean Times has a good copy of the photograph.

title: Bomber
notes: Looking out from their kitchen window, two witnesses watched a large grey aircraft pass silently overhead. As the aircraft was low, the witnesses thought it was going to crash, and stepped outside to investigate, although nothing could be heard. They later found out that a bomber had crashed nearby during the Second World War.

title: Pilot
location_name: Croydon (Surrey) - Area around New Addington, and Queen Elizabeth's Drive
notes: The ghost of a German pilot shot down over the area during the Second World War is said to remain in the neighbourhood. One story says that a man looking for his lost wallet along Queen Elizabeth's Drive had the object handed back to him by the pilot, who appeared quite solid.

title: Pilots
notes: It was said a chambermaid working at this hotel quit after spotting a phantom RAF pilot. A guest also reported seeing a group of pilots sitting and singing around a fireplace in the restaurant.

title: Mynarski
notes: Mynarski was awarded the VC for trying to save a rear gunner when the bomber they were in caught fire. His ghost dressed in flying kit has been seen around the airport's hotel.
address: Teesside International Airport (MME), Tees Valley, Darlington DL2 1LU, UK

title: War Staff
location_name: Derry / Londonderry (County Derry / County Londonderry) - City of Derry Airport (formally Eglinton air base)
notes: Staff have reported seeing figures dressed in Second World War clothing around the airport.
address: City of Derry Airport, Airport Roundabout, Eglinton, Londonderry BT47 3GY, UK

title: Officer on Bike
notes: Cycling along on an old bike, this phantom RAF officer once stopped and asked two guards for the control tower to be opened for him. When they agreed, the officer vanished. On other occasions, lights were reported moving around the control tower, though it would be locked and empty.
address: Cuckoo Ln, Digby, Lincoln LN4 3LH, UK

title: Aircraft of the Future
notes: A lost pilot recalled flying over disused airfield in the 1930s and saw it operational, with yellow RAF aircraft on the ground - these did not exist at the time. He later checked and found the airfield really was unused. A few years later, the airfield was reopened for training purposes, and aircraft based there painted yellow.

title: B17 Crew
notes: The control tower is haunted by crew from a B17 which hit the building while attempting a landing. Other witnesses report hearing aircraft flying low overhead, though nothing can be seen, while hangar 5 is another paranormal hotspot.

title: Headless Airman
location_name: East Cowes (Isle of Wight) - Skies above area
notes: Seen parachuting down from the skies, this decapitated airman is thought to date from the Second World War.

title: Tall American
location_name: East Kirkby (Lincolnshire) - East Kirkby Watch Tower (now an aviation heritage centre)
notes: This USAAF officer has been seen several times in the old watch tower. He is thought to have been killed when a Flying Fortress crash landed near the site in 1944. The same figure is thought to have been seen on the runway - dragging his parachute behind him, he slowly moves towards the control tower.

title: Fred
notes: During the 1980s and 1990s, workers at the MOD transport section would say 'hello' to Fred, the resident ghost. It was said that if Fred were not acknowledged, the phantom would open lockers and move things around. It was thought that Fred was a worker at the site during the Second World War; he died after walking into a propeller. The ghost of a young man in a grey RAF uniform was encountered in 1974, as it walked over to a witness and touched their forehead before vanishing.

title: Morse Code
location_name: Elsham Wold (Lincolnshire) - Former RAF base and flight tower (no longer standing)
