notes: Riding headless on a black horse, the Dullahan are said to be bearers of ill-omen. The entity carries his severed, decaying head in his right hand. Wherever the Dullahan stops, it is said that a mortal will shortly die on that spot.
address: Bryansford Rd & Moneyscalp Rd, Newcastle BT33 0PX, UK

title: Aunt Charlotte
location_name: Bryanston (Dorset) - Portman (aka Bryanston) House, currently the school
notes: The shade of Aunt Charlotte was so prevalent the first building here was pulled down and rebuilt solely to rid the living of her ghost. Charlotte may never have been seen again, but a phantom horse and carriage now haunt the drive.
address: Bryanston St, Blandford Forum DT11 0PX, UK

title: Phantom Coach
location_name: Buckland Newton (Dorset) - Park Lane, between village and Glanvilles Wootton (aka Wootton Glanville)
notes: The origins of this phantom coach and team of horses is unclear.

title: Lady Bolingbroke
location_name: Bucklebury (Berkshire) - Manor House, and general area
notes: After her husband escaped to France with another woman, Lady Bolingbroke (nee Frances Winchcombe) died of a broken heart. Her ghost has been reported at the manor house, while the streets of the village are home to her coach.

title: Riders
location_name: Buckstones (West Yorkshire) - A640, near the Nont Sarah public house
notes: A policeman returning home after a late shift spotted a small group of horsemen dressed in Cavalier-style clothing riding along this road. He drove past the figures three times to prove to himself that they were not a figment of his imagination.

title: Grizelda the Highway Woman
datetime: At the end of every month (reoccurring), last seen 1987
notes: Dressed as a male highwayman, this girl has been seen still riding her midnight black horse. She reportedly held up a stagecoach carrying an execution order condemning a friend of hers, which bought him enough time to escape the noose.

title: Horseman
notes: A ghostly man on horseback is said to cross the lane before vanishing.
address: College Ln, Bunbury, Tarporley CW6, UK

title: Horses and Coach
location_name: Bungay (Suffolk) - Road between Ditchingham and Bungay at Lions Grave
notes: Reported by at least one driver, a coach with a team of horses haunt the road, seemingly charging at incoming traffic and turning away at the last possible moment. One version of the tale says the coach is either seen or heard, but never both.

title: Horses
location_name: Burbage Bridge (Derbyshire) - Layby along Ringinglow Road
notes: Two witnesses in the back of a camper van heard horse's hooves outside their vehicle (even though they did not hear the creature approach). They heard the creature depart before it returned soon after, this time brushing against the rear window. The witnesses drove away, unable to see anything in the darkness. The driver turned the van around a few hundred metres down the road and returned to see what had been harassing them, but nothing was found.

title: Sir Thomas
notes: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven (or twelve, or forty) bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.

title: White Horses
notes: Galloping around the neighbourhood at night, these creatures are thought to be connected to a battle fought more than a thousand years ago.
address: Burnham Green, Datchworth, Welwyn AL6 0SY, UK

title: Headless Horse
location_name: Burnham Green (Hertfordshire) - Roads in the area
notes: The result of a Danish skirmish over a thousand years ago, this decapitated horse runs along the lanes surrounding the area.
address: Burnham Green, Datchworth, Welwyn AL6 0SY, UK

title: Lady O'Hagan
location_name: Burnley (Lancashire) - Summit area of Manchester Road
notes: The last owner of nearby Towneley Hall, the woman now drives her horse and trap in the area. It is said you can hear the hooves pounding on the cobbled streets, even though the stones have been replaced with modern materials.

title: Phantom Coach
notes: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.

title: King Arthur
location_name: Cadbury (Somerset) - Cadbury Castle, and road between North Barrow and South Barrow villages
notes: Escorted by mounted knights carrying lances, King Arthur of the Britons traverses this route back to Camelot (as Cadbury is thought to be) on this day in June. One witness said that on 23 June 1995 they encountered an armoured man (more dark age than medieval) holding a standard on horseback - the figure disappeared around a corner, though there was no place that he could have gone. One legend says a door opens every seven years in the hillside so that Arthur and his company can leave to feed their horses.

title: Coach Circling the Courtyard
location_name: Caister-On-Sea (Norfolk) - Caister Castle, one mile west of Caister-On-Sea
datetime: Warning of a death in the family
notes: Warning about the encroaching death of a family member, a horse drawn hearse driven by a headless horseman passes through the gates of the castle and circles the courtyard seven times.

title: Coach
notes: This coach, pulled by a team of four horses, crosses a bridge close to Calcutt.

title: Walter Calverley
location_name: Calverley (West Yorkshire) - Calverley House (currently called Old Hall?), surrounding countryside
notes: A temporary fit of madness resulted in this Elizabethan gentleman killing two of his children and stabbing his wife (who escaped by feigning death). His regretful spirit rides the countryside on a headless horse, although one source says that it had been laid. Poltergeist-like activities have also been reported at the house.
address: 1 Back Ln, Farsley, Pudsey LS28 5EU, UK

title: Headless Horseman
notes: Galloping across the parkland under the light of the moon, this rider could be searching for his missing appendage. Some also say that the area is haunted by a coven of witches.

title: Headless Horseman
notes: Emerging from a deep pit near to Cantley, this headless spirit on horseback rides off towards the nearby village.

title: Horace Wimpole
notes: A former owner of the property wrote that he saw the ghost of Horace riding on a black horse several times, always just prior to the death of a loved one.

title: Knight in White
notes: The knight has been observed standing by the keep, while his riderless horse trots around nearby. Banging and clanking has been reported in the castle after dark, with searches revealing no apparent source.
address: Market Pl, Castleton, Hope Valley S33 8WQ, UK

title: Black Dog
notes: A shuck is said to walk the area near the castle, while a phantasmic knight stands motionless on the ramparts. Author Wayne Anthony reports that an old lady and a phantom horse have also been reported in the area.
address: Market Pl, Castleton, Hope Valley S33 8WQ, UK

title: Silent Stagecoach
location_name: Chalfont St Peter (Buckinghamshire) - Amersham Road and Greyhound Inn
notes: An old fashioned stagecoach pulled by a team of ghostly horses passes along this road and vanishes as it pulls up at the Greyhound Inn.
address: Amersham Rd, Chalfont St Peter, UK

title: Horses
location_name: Challacombe (Devon) - Broken Barrow (exact location not known)
notes: A man who tried to break into this barrow was driven mad by the sound of horses - each time the robber reached into the hole he had made, the sound of horses could be heard, though the creatures could not be seen. The man eventually lost his sight and hearing, dying three months later.

title: Horse and Cart
notes: A phantom horse pulling a cart is said to haunt this road.

title: White Stallion
notes: Roaming riderless, this white (or grey, according to other reports) horse jumps large hedges and runs wild in the area.

title: Royalist
notes: The ghost of a Royalist on horseback is said to ride silently around the lanes leading to this village.

title: Galloping Horses
notes: A pair of galloping horses are said to charge down the hill, vanishing as they approach the bottom.

title: Ann
notes: This female ghost is rumoured to ride her horse along the lanes that lead to the castle, recognisable by her tricorne hat.
address: Chiddingstone Castle, Hill Hoath Rd, Chiddingstone, Edenbridge TN8 7AD, UK

title: Footsteps
notes: People have reported the sounds of footsteps walking on the grass behind them - though no-one is present when the witnesses look around. A spectral man dressed in black rides on horseback between the graves, occasionally dismounting before vanishing.
address: 121 Old Church Rd, London E4 6ST, UK

title: Headless Horse
notes: A woman and her daughter reported seeing a headless horse in a field close to Junction 29.

title: Major Smalman
location_name: Church Stretton (Shropshire) - Area around Wilderhope Manor
notes: A diehard Royalist, Smalman's home was once besieged by Roundheads during the latter stages of the Civil War. The Major managed to escape on horseback, his ghost now seen galloping away from the building. The manor itself is also reported to be haunted by both Smalman and the shade of a young girl who smiles at any witnesses before screaming horribly.
address: Pilgrim Cottages, Longville in the Dale, Much Wenlock TF13 6EG, UK

title: Trotting
notes: The clip-clop of phantom hooves has been reported in this area, normally accompanied by a strange pale mist which (it is said) sometimes transforms into a horse with a headless rider.

title: Horsemen
notes: While on his way home with his dog, Mr Sampson watched two men on horseback riding around the fort. His dog immediately ran home, leaving Sampson to watch the figures as they dismounted and vanish (special thanks to the Churchtown Village Renewal Trust, from their 'The Annals of Churchtown').

title: Riderless Horse
location_name: Clacton-on-Sea (Essex) - Junction of London Road and St Johns Road, Great Clacton
notes: Before traffic in the area increased considerably, there were several reports of a ghostly horse without rider which would cross the roundabout before vanishing into a now demolished barn.
address: St John's Rd, Great Clacton, Clacton-on-Sea, UK

title: Horse and Cart
notes: Walking his dog one early morning, a man watched a horse and cart appear from nowhere. The man was frozen to the spot. The cart was driven by a weathered man, and appeared quite solid, although old fashioned. After the cart had passed the witness, he composed himself and turned to follow the vehicle, but it had vanished.
address: Aynsley Ave, Newcastle-under-Lyme, Newcastle ST5 4ET, UK

title: Royalist
notes: This phantom Royalist soldier died on the site from his injuries and has been seen near the bar. A ghostly coach and horses also haunts the area. As a side note, a secret tunnel is said to run from the pub to a nearby house.

title: Puca
location_name: Clonlara (County Clare) - Bridge (may no longer be standing), two miles south west of village
notes: A dark shadow-like horse was named by some to be a puca, although it would appear no one stayed around long enough to find out for sure.

title: Sound of a Horse
notes: The driver of a horse and cart and a passenger were left shaken after encountering the sounds of a horse which passed them, and although nothing could be seen, the sounds were accompanied by a cold gust of wind. A murder had occurred along the road a few years previous, where a woman on horseback was decapitated by a former lover.

title: Old Man
location_name: Coate (Wiltshire) - Path across field heading towards Pond House
notes: This pathway is reputedly haunted by a little old man dressed in old fashioned clothing.
address: Wiltshire Path, Heritage Park QLD 4118, Australia

title: Horse in Chains
location_name: Cockpole Green (Berkshire) - Area around Putters Farm
notes: An old work horse still draped in the chains it used to drag a plough around the fields still manifests and scares the unwary.

title: Charlotte in White Robe
notes: Located in a hidden part of the grounds, Charlotte's Well was built for a woman after which it took its name. Charlotte is said to have loved the well so much that she could never leave and is said to have been seen since her death around the bathing pool. She may have last been seen walking around a wooded area in a white dress during the winter of 1977, although there are stories that a phantom woman still appears in the area. A phantom coach is also said to ride around the area.
address: Berechurch Hall, 115 Berechurch Hall Rd, Colchester CO2 9NW, UK

title: Simon Cunliffe
datetime: Once a year, middle of winter (reoccurring), sound recorded 26/07/1996
notes: Simon rides the area where he once lived, blowing his horn prior to a tragedy. It is said that he scared his wife to death by riding up the staircase of their home, chasing a fox that had ran inside to escape the hunt (ghost hunters Colin Veacock and Peter Crawley recorded a sound like a riding crop here in 1996). Another tale states that the horseman is not Simon but an assassin, killed by a manservant before he could murder the master of the hall. The young daughter of a family living here during the twentieth century told her mother that a 'white woman' would visit her during the night - the sightings stopped when the child changed bedrooms.

title: Phantom Coach
notes: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution. A headless Black Shuck is also reputed to cross this bridge once per night.

title: Monks with Body
location_name: Colwall (Hereford & Worcester) - Road near village green known as 'Haunted Lane'
notes: A woman cycling along the lane during the Second World War encountered a procession of cowled monks carrying the body of a young man draped in black. Pedalling away from the scene, the woman was overtaken by a ghostly horseman also galloping from the monks. Man and horse vanished as they reached the railway bridge in the village. Many years later two children walking home encountered a tall, hooded monk along a country lane. They quickly ran past.

title: Sir George Sydenham
notes: Sir George is reported to ride his headless horse up and down the hills near the village. Combe Sydenham is also a place where the Wild Hunt has been reported, always heard as locals were too afraid to look (and rightly so!).

title: Marbury Lady
location_name: Comberbatch (Cheshire) - Marbury Park and former hall (no longer standing)
datetime: Exact date unknown, but reported to be around 22:30h (female ghost)
notes: An Egyptian maid was pushed down the staircase of the old manor house, dying in the fall. Her ghost was said to haunt the old hall, though when it was demolished, the spirit changed location and took up residence in the park. A phantom mare named Marbury Dunne is also said to haunt the park.
address: Marbury Country Park, Northwich CW9 6AT, UK

title: Rider
notes: A man on horseback, considered to be a highwayman, reportedly haunts this building. It may have been this entity which created the loud heavy footsteps from above which followed one witness along the corridor to the back rooms; when asked, the owners said no one else was updates at the time.
address: Puesdown Inn, Compton Abdale, Cheltenham GL54 4DN, UK

title: White Rider
notes: This pallid figure on horseback has been reported in the village.

title: Headless Woman
datetime: Woman and horse unknown, male figure seen 15 May 2009, around 01:30h
notes: The terrifying shade of the headless woman walks the hill near the gateway of where this castle stands. The same ghost may also be responsible for the lights that flit across the ruins at night, or they could be caused by phantasmal Royalists that used the castle in 1643. The sound of a galloping phantom horse has been heard at the bottom of the castle hill. A cloaked figure with a brown floppy hat was observed by a local man in 2009 while he was picking worms for fishing. The figure was in the middle of a field but vanished after the witness took his eyes off the man for a few seconds.

title: Peg Sneddle
notes: Peg Sneddle, or Elizabeth Sleddall as she was known in life, would manifest prior to a death in the Machell family (she was married to a Machell, but used her maiden name after his premature death). This ceased when her body was hidden under a large rock in the nearby river, though it would reappear every year in September. A phantom coach and four also haunts the same area.

title: Soldiers
location_name: Cregneish (Isle of Man) - Meayll Stone Circle
notes: A phantom army comprising of soldiers and their horses are said to haunt the area round these ancient burial chambers.
address: Mull Hill, Isle of Man IM9, Isle of Man

title: Horseman
notes: This horse backed rider enters the building though the original gate, that is now walled up. The ghost of Sir William Crichton is also said to haunt the site, walking towards castle tower, manifesting on the date of his death (although that date is not certain, only being recorded as prior to July 1454).

title: Horse
notes: A headless horse is reputed to gallop along in this area.

title: Cromwellian Rider
notes: Cromwellian troops have been seen near the local church, while another soldier rides a horse along the road; he dismounts and vanishes through the church door.
address: Croft Ln, Crondall, Farnham GU10 5QF, UK

title: WW2 Soldiers
notes: Soldiers dressed in Second World War uniform have been seen around the castle area, and the site is also said to be haunted by a horse and carriage. As a side note, Bram Stoker is said to have been partly inspired to write Dracula after seeing the castle while staying at Cruden Bay.
address: A975, Cruden Bay, Peterhead AB42 0NE, UK

title: Coach & Horses
notes: Once upon a time, the coach and its team of horses arrived too late to save a girl who committed suicide in the local pond - out of guilt it now makes its return journey.
address: Butlers Green House, Butler's Green Rd, Haywards Heath RH16 4BH, UK

title: Hearse
location_name: Cutcombe (Somerset) - A396, between village and Timberscombe
notes: Some accidents occurring along this road have been blamed on the phantom black coach pulled by headless horses that runs wild and driverless.

title: Monk
notes: A ghostly figure in robes was seen walking towards the gatehouse before vanishing into a wall. It is said the monk sometimes appears on horseback, and the site is also haunted by a white woman.

title: The Black Prince
location_name: Dartford (Kent) - A2 road between Dartford and Bexley
notes: Dressed in black armour, this ghostly figure on horseback was observed several times walking along the road during the Second World War.

title: Sam Hunnaford
notes: This local farmer hanged himself out of shame after been conned at a fair - his shade has been seen making his last journey on horseback, though instead of hanging itself, the ghost continues towards his former home.

title: Benjamin Gear
notes: A former mayor of Okehampton, the figure is said to haunt the waters of the pool. He also takes the form of a black horse. There are reports of an exorcism attempt which may have failed.

title: Old Crockern
notes: Riding on the back of a skeletal horse, Crockern insists on moving down this path waving a sword in his dead hand.

title: Yeth Hounds
location_name: Dartmoor (Devon) - General area of the River Tavy
notes: These variants of the shuck, complete with hunters on horseback, have been heard running in this area after dark. Part of the Tavy is also said to be haunted by the ghost of Sir John Fitz, whose body was found in the river.

title: Limpety
location_name: Dartmoor (Devon) - Green Hill & Black Lane area
notes: Lost on the moor during a snowstorm, a master of the Dartmoor foxhunt claimed that a petite man on a grey horse came from nowhere and led him to safety. He later saw a painting of a gypsy called Limpety and named him as his saviour, only to be told that he had died on the moor 50 years previous.

title: People on Horseback
notes: Wearing clothing from the middle ages, witnesses observed several horsemen galloping across this Dartmoor area. Another party of witnesses saw what looked like cavemen moving around the stone circle at the Cleave.
address: The Cleave, Lustleigh, Newton Abbot TQ13 9TJ, UK

title: Military Horseman
notes: This figure wearing a grey greatcoat has been seen riding along near the ruins of this building.

title: Coach Arriving
location_name: Dartmouth (Devon) - The Royal Castle Hotel, The Quay
notes: Early morning, during the time of golden leaves, the sound of a horse and coach can be heard coming to a stop outside the hotel. There are sounds of someone jumping on board before the hooves can be heard pulling away. One of the bedrooms may be haunted by a robed figure, and the sounds of two people fighting can be heard in the courtyard.

title: Clibbon
notes: Found guilty of highway robbery, Clibbon was tied to the back of a horse and dragged around the town until he was dead. His screams are sometimes heard. The same area is also home to a headless horse, though it may not be the same creature associated with the highwayman.

title: Small Carriage
location_name: Delamere Forest (Cheshire) - Road by the Station Cafe
notes: Returning from a late shift at work, this witness drove past a horse pulling a small, two wheeled carriage that was dimly lit by lanterns. The driver of the carriage remained motionless as the witness's car went by.

title: Horse and Rider
location_name: Denbury Down (Devon) - Woodland in the area
notes: A figure on horseback has been observed in the distance, holding a shield and sword. Perhaps connected to this entity, the sounds of a horse charging through woodland have been reported. There may be an additional relationship to the nearby Denbury Hill Iron Age hill fort which folklore says contains golden treasure.
address: Denbury Down Ln, Denbury, Newton Abbot TQ12, UK

title: Runaway Horse
notes: While driving into Denchworth, two people spotted a panicked horse, which galloped from a side road in front of their car. The driver immediately slammed on the brakes and turned the headlights down so as not to startle the creature further. Not quite believing what they had both seen, they turned the headlights back up only to see the horse galloping in front of the car for a few more seconds before vanishing.

title: Wild Men
notes: Dressed in animal skins and on horseback, the dozens of phantom, wild-looking figures are reportedly Viking raiders.

title: Sounds of a Running Battle
location_name: Derby (Derbyshire) - Alvaston area, near the castle
notes: Three girls reported listening to the sounds of a small battle here in the early twentieth century - gunfire, galloping horses, and men shouting. Four people also heard the sounds while out late at night during the 1980s, and two brothers passing through the area in 2009.

title: Old Soldiers
notes: Squadies tell tales of seeing strange people in the newer buildings who vanish if approached, while outside a phantom horse and cart are occasionally seen.

title: Death Coach
notes: As the owner of the house lay dying, the other family members around his bed heard a horse drawn coach pull up outside the house. Believing it to be the doctor, they rushed downstairs, but nothing could be seen on the gravel driveway. The owner died soon after.
address: Derrymore West, Derrymore, Co. Kerry, Ireland

title: Darrie Loughlin
location_name: Derryveagh Mountains (County Donegal) - Area around Lough Dheerig (possibly Lough Derg?)
notes: Around eighteen months after his death, Darrie Loughlin's ghost started to appear in the area, riding a white horse towards the lough.
address: Derryveagh Mountains, Staghall Mountain West, Co. Donegal, Ireland

title: Runaway Horses
location_name: Devizes (Wiltshire) - Oliver's Castle, aka Roundway Castle, aka Rundaway Castle - Bloody Ditch
notes: It is said eight hundred men died at the Battle of Roundway Hill in 1643, after a battlefield manoeuvre which trapped cavalry in a slopped area. The men and horses were slaughtered, and since the battle ghostly horses have been seen galloping around the area. A headless ghost was once also said to walk the area, possibly linked to a barrow on the site, as is the phantom hound which drags a chain from its neck.

title: Grey Pony
notes: The churchyard is haunted by a man who rides around on a grey (or white) pony. A local superstition says that anyone who runs backwards around the tomb of Robert Cooke will hear crockery rattling in the grave.

title: Kitchen Horse
notes: The kitchen was built over the old stables, and the sound of a horse kicking a stable door is said to be occasionally heard. Horseshoes belonging to Shetland ponies have been found in the garden.

title: Black Coach
location_name: Doneraile (County Cork) - Area outside Doneraile Court
notes: A farmer returning home encountered a coach pulled by four headless horses, which pulled away from the Court's driveway at breakneck speed. The man later learned that Viscount Doneraile died just before his encounter. The ghost of Lady Casteltown has been reported within the building, as has her husband.
address: Doneraile Estate, Demesne, Doneraile, Co. Cork, P51 XR66, Ireland

title: Coach in the House
notes: One owner of the property won a large amount of money when he drove a horse and coach up the stairs inside the house - his victorious ghost reportedly re-enacts the antic. A separate entity is that of a headless grey woman, a member of the Trenchard family who slit her own throat, while a priest is said to haunt the gatehouse (where he was held prisoner).
address: Wolfeton House, Wolfeton, Charminster, Dorchester DT2 9QN, UK

title: Horses
notes: The sounds of horses dragging prisoners to their execution site are said to be heard along this road.

title: Rider
notes: A decapitated rider on horseback follows a road that no longer exists - the figure crosses the gravestones and vanishes through a hedge.

title: Horse
location_name: Dover (Kent) - Common Lane and another unnamed lane
notes: Police were called after a riderless horse was spotted cantering in the area. No horse could be found, and in one area the creature had been reported, the snow showed no hoof marks.

title: House Party
location_name: Downend (Isle of Wight) - Area near Knighton Gorges
datetime: 31 December knight seen (reoccurring), house reported in the twentieth century
notes: A manor house has been seen in the area, a party in full flow visible through its windows. However, the house was demolished many years previous. Even when not seen, locals have reported hearing music coming from the site. A large black knight on horseback is still occasionally seen, once a year.
address: Knighton Gorges Manor, 8 Winford Rd, Newchurch, Sandown PO36 0FB, UK

title: Coach & Four
notes: A phantom coach drawn by a team of four horses travel around this area. They are said to look quite solid, though is illuminated by an unknown source.

title: Headless Horseman
location_name: Dublin (County Dublin) - Roper's Rest, leading to Love Lane
notes: This decapitated figure rides along the road once darkness falls, and is thought to be an ill omen for any witness, or one of their family.

title: Buck Jones
notes: Riding headless on the back of a white horse, this phantom is named as Buck Jones, former City Sheriff (though the man died in a debtor's prison).

title: Shadowy Coaches
location_name: Dublin Mountains (County Dublin) - Montpelier Hill - ruins of the Hell Fire Club
notes: Founded in the 1730s, the Hell Fire Club quickly became notorious for its black antics. The former meeting ground is said to be haunted by some of these dark deeds, and by phantom horse and coaches which rush past the building, containing the souls of former Hell Fire Club members.
address: Mountpelier Hill, Mountpelier, Co. Dublin, Ireland

title: Headless Rider
notes: This decapitated male wearing a flowing cape passes through the village on an off-white horse.

title: Horseman
notes: This figure rides the area in search of his head that was removed as he hit a low branch while galloping at fantastic speed, escaping from his girlfriend's overprotective brothers. Two more ghosts have been reported in the same area, both running away from a murder site.

title: Squire Fulford
notes: Driving a coach pulled by four headless horsemen, the squire passes though the village only at certain times of the year.

title: Squire on Horseback
notes: Riding his fastest racehorse, this phantom landowner charges around the heathland worrying the locals.

title: Coach
notes: Seen galloping towards the hall, this coach is pulled by four black, headless horses. One legend says the coach contains a coffin.

title: Phantom Horse
location_name: Durnford (Wiltshire) - Salisbury Road between Durnford & Amesbury, near Chalk Pit Hill
notes: This creature, only heard and not seen, would gallop on the road and in the fields in this area.
address: Salisbury Rd, Amesbury, Salisbury SP4, UK

title: Horse & Cart
notes: The phantom horse and cart that haunts this area came into being after falling into the river and vanishing without trace.

title: Man on Horseback
location_name: E4 (Greater London) - Chingford Mount Cemetery, Old Church Road
notes: The ghostly galloping rider seen crossing this area of land must at times dismount - disembodied human footsteps have been heard on the paths, as have strange voices.
address: 121 Old Church Rd, London E4 6ST, UK

title: Colt Pixie
location_name: East Boldre (Hampshire) - Cold Pixie's Cave (Bronze Age barrow), Beaulieu Heath
notes: An equine pixie, this fairy entity would play mild pranks and call out to horses, the distraction causing the creatures to become lost.

title: Horse
location_name: East Chinnock (Somerset) - Main road in the village
notes: The sounds of a horse have been heard as it gallops down the street. Some believe it carries two eloping lovers from the rectory.

title: Taxi Service
notes: A phantom horse and carriage arrive in the courtyard, picking up an unidentifiable person before once again vanishing to the ether.

title: Rushing Horseman
notes: This figure has been both seen and heard as he darts around trees and over roads. One witness said the entity charged towards their car, disappearing on impact.
address: Barming Woodlands, 33 Rede Wood Rd, Maidstone ME16 9HR, UK

title: Duke of Monmouth
notes: The Duke is said to return to the place where he rested while escaping to the coast after the Battle of Sedgemoor. The grounds of the inn are also said to be haunted by the disembodied head of a white horse.

title: White Rider
location_name: Eckington (Derbyshire) - Moss valley, between town and Ridgeway
notes: Two women out walking their dogs watched a white female figure on horseback in the woods. The entity vanished as it rounded a non-existent corner. A local piece of folklore says the area is haunted by a white horse, although traditionally riderless. In 2022, witnesses spotted a hooded grey lady carrying a basket who disappeared into bushes.
address: Gosber Rd, Eckington, Sheffield S21 4BZ, UK

title: Runaway Horses
location_name: Edale (Derbyshire) - Crossroads between church and railway station
notes: Three farmers walking home from a village meeting heard horses running towards them. The men made ready to stop the runaway but were amazed when the sounds past straight through where they stood, and they could see nothing.

title: Grizel
notes: The face of this lady, heavily scarred by fire, has been seen around the area of the Grassmarket - her and her brother were killed by the local authorities for sex crimes and black magic. Some people have also reported seeing her in a phantom coach being pulled by a team of six horses.

title: Deacon Brodie
location_name: Edinburgh (Lothian) - Castle Hill and Royal Mile
notes: Brodie is reputed to walk this area, carrying a lantern. A coach pulled by a team of black horses (which sometimes breath fire) s also said to start its journey here before travelling along the royal mile. The coach is filled with people in black garb. Another phantom, General Dalzell, rides the same route, but this time on a white horse.

title: Riders
notes: As Bessie Dunlop tied her horse to the edge of the loch, she heard dozens of people on horseback dash past her into the water, although nothing could be seen.

title: Driverless Coach
notes: The driverless phantom coach is said to contain Lady Alice Lisle, one of three locations where her ghost haunts. Though the coach and horses have not been seen for many years, it has been said that the sound of a carriage can still be heard.

title: Alice Lisle
notes: For assisting two fugitives, Alice Lisle was hanged in 1685 - though her defence argued that Alice did what any good Christian would do. The rustling of Alice's silken dress and footfalls could be heard echoing down the corridors for many years after her death. Alice also haunted the road outside the hall, appearing in a coach pulled by headless horses.
address: Moyles Court, Rockford, Ringwood BH24 3NF, UK

title: Dark Horseman
notes: Possessing a grinning skull barren of flesh, this dark horseman and his black steed gallop furiously down muddy paths towards the nearby waterfalls.

title: Headless Horseman
location_name: Elsing (Norfolk) - Belaugh Wall, between Elsing and Bawdswell
notes: This decapitated phantom on his steed is said to ride along the wall before leaping over the stonework on the stroke of midnight.

title: Seventeen Century Boy
location_name: Enchmarsh (Shropshire) - Road leading through the village
notes: Two men driving through the village encountered a 'colourless' horse and rider standing in the middle of the road. The rider appeared to be dressed in 17th century clothing. The horse then galloped away across a nearby field.

title: Headless Monk
notes: The monk was caught with a woman and killed for his crime. The entity now returns once a year. He may have last been seen in 1981 by a teenager who would search the woods near the abbey for old pots and pipes. After returning home one night from the woods, the teenager reportedly spotted a monk in a brown robe holding a lantern on a hooked pole outside his window. The abbey is also home to phantom horsemen, and Roesia of Dover who is looking for her heart (it was buried here by her father).
address: Lesnes Abbey, Abbey Rd, London SE2 0AX, UK

title: Lady Charlotte Watson-Taylor
notes: Both prison workers and villagers have reported seeing this phantom lady inspecting the prison grounds, and even driving her horse and carriage over a road close by where a bridge once stood.
address: Erlestoke House, Erlestoke, Devizes SN10 5TU, UK

title: Plague Victims
notes: Several graves belonging to plague victims are thought to be haunted by their occupiers.
address: London Rd, Ewell, Epsom KT17 2AY, UK

title: Horse and Coach
notes: The sounds of a horse pulling a coach have been heard outside this inn, though never seen.

title: Horses
notes: The sound of neighing and hoof thuds can be heard in the area, even when there are no wild horses present.

title: Horses
location_name: Exmouth (Devon) - Town end of St Andrews Road
notes: Thought to be the site of a former stables or old coaching house, the sounds of hooves, whinnying and soft talking have been heard in the area.
address: St Andrew's Rd, Exmouth EX8, UK

title: Drakes
notes: While the house is haunted by Sir Francis Drake, the road outside is also populated by the ghosts of a horse and rider. Eliot Drake broke his neck in a fall while racing, but now continues to ride.

title: Old Man
notes: Said to haunt the upper area of the building, the old man could be one of the many plague victims from 1665. A white horse also haunts the grounds.
address: Eyam Hall Craft Centre, Eyam Hall Craft Centre, Main Rd, Eyam, Hope Valley S32 5QW, UK

title: Headless Horseman
location_name: Farley (Staffordshire) - Road between Farley and Alton
notes: Riding a white horse and donned in a full suit of armour, his knight can also be recognised by his lack of head.

title: Sounds of a Coach
location_name: Farnham (Surrey) - The Hop Bag Hotel, Downing Street
notes: The sounds of a coach pulling into the courtyard of this hotel have been heard, complete with whinnying horses. A ghostly girl in grey has also been seen waiting for the return of a lost lover killed on the outskirts of the town.

title: Horse and Coach
notes: An old coaching route, some still say that phantom horses and coaches can still be heard passing along the road.
address: Main Rd, Farningham, Dartford DA4, UK

title: Horses Crossing Drawbridge that No Longer Exists
notes: A ghostly coach being pulled by a team of horses is said to cross a ditch next to the fort, where once a drawbridge lay. The fort is also the site of an alleged suicide which is now re-enacted; the ghostly white figure is still seen dangling from the roof beams where he hanged himself.
address: View Point Rd, Felixstowe IP11 3TW, UK

title: Pale Rider
notes: Passing through the castle park, a tall man riding a white horse has been seen. The castle itself is home to a green lady who is said to have committed suicide after her lover ran away.
address: Fillingham Castle, Fillingham, Gainsborough DN21 5BX, UK

title: Distressed Horse
notes: Said to have been slaughtered in the abattoir, this horse is said to be heard every six months. There are also reports of the creature being heard galloping away from the area.

title: Obrick's Colt
location_name: Fitz (Shropshire) - Area was known as Cuthery or Cutberry Hollow
notes: Robbed of her jewels after death, a wealthy local woman came back post-mortem, taking the form of a white horse. The phantom horse was reputed to be so bold that it would occasionally manifest during the daytime.

title: Mad Horse
notes: The hall and surrounding area were haunted by a wild mare.

title: Helpful Horse
notes: This boggart took the form of a horse. The entity would plough fields and work around the farm in return for a warm place to spend the night (or a pie, depending on the story).

title: Little Folk
notes: The area around the lake is reportedly home to boggarts and hobs. There are also stories of ghostly Roundheads moving around the area, some on horseback and others wearily walking holding pikes.

title: The Spittal Hill Tut
notes: Nicknamed the Spittal Hill Tut, this ghostly entity would take the form of a small horse. The entity grabbed one man passing through the area on horseback, almost crushing him to death. Some believed the Tut existed to protect some treasure concealed beneath the hill.
address: Spittal Hill Rd, Boston PE22, UK

title: Horse and Carriage
notes: A man out cycling at night reported hearing a horse galloping along - he heard it pass him by, but nothing could be seen. It is reported that a ghostly horse pulling a carriage heads towards the house, but never departs.

title: Horse and Cart
location_name: Fulmer (Buckinghamshire) - Ford running through the village
notes: Heard near the ford, several people have reported the clip clop of hooves and the grind of an old cart.

title: Phantom Coach
location_name: Geldeston (Norfolk) - Road to Bungay from Geldeston
notes: A coach drawn by four horses and driven by a headless coachman is supposed to travel from Bungay to Geldeston before returning to its departure point.

title: Armed Rider
location_name: Gerrards Cross (Buckinghamshire) - Area around the Bull Hotel
notes: While walking home from the Bull Hotel, Dave Robson heard the thumping of hooves approach him. Within moments, a dark cloaked figure wearing a big hat and holding a gun dashed by on horseback before vanishing near a group of trees.
address: Oxford Rd, Gerrards Cross SL9 7PA, UK

title: Alexander MacAllister
notes: The site of a battle in 1594 between the troops of George Gordon, 1st Marquess of Huntly and Francis Hay, 9th Earl of Erroll, is haunted by the headless ghost of MacAllister riding on horseback - he fought briefly, although was decapitated by the first cannon shot.

title: White Horse
notes: It was once said that a phantom white horse haunted a spot at the hall where a former owner had concealed a cache of riches.

title: Drowning Lovers
notes: A failed escape from Cromwellian soldiers resulted in a young officer and his lover drowning in the river Wye. Their ghosts, mounted on horseback, have been reported struggling in the river, standing in the ruins of the nearby castle, or heard screaming during storms. In addition, an imprisoned Irish chieftain who died trying to escape is said to haunt the Macbeth tower.

title: Civil War Skirmish
location_name: Grafton Regis (Northamptonshire) - Exact location not known
notes: Six workers claimed they heard charging horses, the clash of swords, and gunfire, though could see nothing strange. A local historian said that a small fight had happened nearby, between Roundheads and Cavaliers.

title: Captain Blundell
notes: Blundell, a British officer, rides decapitated upon horseback in this area. His headless body was found in a nearby barracks, and an investigation ruled that the officer had killed himself. Some people thought this to be unlikely and believed his ghost had returned to prove that a murder had really occurred.

title: Poisoned Horses
location_name: Great Ayton (North Yorkshire) - Area near the river
notes: The sounds of poisoned horses neighing haunts this area - a story tells how thirty men and over a dozen horses died after drinking poisoned water from this river. The river itself is said to reflect the dead soldiers, if one examines it for long enough.

title: Kate Slough
notes: The apparition of blonde-haired Kate on horseback has been observed near the area where she once drowned. Her white horse has also been observed riderless.
address: Pork Ln, Great Holland, Frinton-on-Sea CO13, UK

title: Woman in a Crinoline Gown
location_name: Great Holland (Essex) - Treasure Holt - farmhouse in marshes
notes: The history of this building goes back to 1138, and several ghosts have attached themselves to it and the surrounding area. The woman dressed in a crinoline gown drifts silently through the house, while a woman with blonde hair sits in front of the fireplace. This may be the same ghost who rides a white horse down the lane outside. A cavalier has been observed in the lounge, and a phantom monk walks a foot above the ground outside in the garden. Finally, just around the corner of the building the spectre of a highwayman still hangs from a tree.

title: Sir John
notes: Upon his death, Sir John de Plessis reportedly requested that he be buried upright, sitting on top of his favourite steed. The executors of his will made it so, and Sir John has since been seen riding along the hillside. The same story is attributed to a Captain Backhouse, though his haunting stopped after being dug up and laid out traditionally. A female phantom rider has also been reported haunting the area, her head sitting on her lap.

title: Coach
notes: The site was said to be haunted by a phantom coach and horses that were heard coming to a halt on the gravel driveway but were never seen.

title: Earl of Shaftesbury
location_name: Gussage All Saints (Dorset) - Lanes in the area
notes: This Earl of Shaftesbury, who died in 1683, is said to drive his coach around the lanes in the area, looking for souls to take.
address: Gussage All Saints, Wimborne BH21, UK

title: Abigail Carstair
notes: Carstair was on the run after being accused of witchcraft - she leapt from the cliff rather than being tortured by the religious fanatics. Her shade is sometimes seen trying to hide in the area, while the ghostly horseman also reported in the same place may be one of her hunters.

title: Coach Sounds
notes: An invisible, phantom coach is driven through the area by a team of horses.

title: Waiting on the Platform
notes: When overgrown and unloved, the station was said by some to be haunted by a figure. Some assumed the ghost was a woman thrown from her horse and trap into the path of an oncoming train. The area was also said to be home to a phantom carriage.

title: Coach
notes: The coach, pulled by a team of horses, belts down the driveway, enters the house, and disappears while ascending the staircase. In 2017 a visitor to the house spotted a man in Stuart attire reading a book while sitting by a window. The witness, believing the figure to be a reenactor, pulled out their phone to take a photograph, but the figure had vanished. The witness asked around and staff said there were no costumed actors in that day (nor any scheduled for the near future).
address: Hatfield House, Park and Gardens, Hatfield AL9 5HX, UK

title: Pale Horse
location_name: Hathersage (Derbyshire) - Sicklehome Hollow, between Hathersage and Bamford
notes: A phantom white horse is said to roam the area but only visible to those with second sight.

title: Rider
notes: A phantom man upon a shaggy horse was once said to haunt this hill, manifesting to other riders and causing their mounts to bolt.

title: Sir Thomas
notes: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.
address: 5 Great Hautbois Rd, Coltishall, Norwich NR12 7JN, UK

title: Cavalier
notes: A fisherman encountered a spectral Cavalier passing over the bridge on horseback. The entity faded away.

title: Men and Horses
notes: The vicar called the police after hearing many men and horses traversing the nearby downs. Police found nothing.

title: Hedley Kow
notes: A spirit that plagued many households in the village, the Hedley Kow would take the form of human or beast, sometimes luring the living into marshy areas. The entity finally disappeared after it manifested as a horse and ran off, escaping the fury of a victim of one of its 'jokes'.
address: Hedley on the Hill, Stocksfield NE43, UK

title: White Horse of Clumly
notes: Both horse and rider were killed in an accident as they galloped back home, the rider having just disposed of a body. Now the pair only appear when the environment is right.

title: Headless Highwayman
location_name: Hickleton (South Yorkshire) - Area near the church
notes: This ghostly figure still rides his horse, still seeking his head that was taken from him as punishment for his crimes.

title: Lady on Horseback
notes: This ghostly woman is reported to ride her mare along the lane where she fell from her horse and died.
address: Loakes Rd, High Wycombe HP11, UK

title: Old Coker
location_name: Hill Deverill (Wiltshire) - Gun's Church (or Gunschurch) barrow
notes: Even though he died in 1730, Henry Coker is said to still ride his horse, follow his hounds, rattle his chains and blow his hunting horn in the area.

title: Horse Drawn Coach
location_name: Hindon (Wiltshire) - Junction of the A303 and the A350
notes: This ghoulish coach is drawn by a team of four grey spectral horses.

title: Waving White Lady in a Coach
notes: After losing control of the horses, a coach driver and his female passenger died. Now, the phantom vehicle is seen outside the church; the white woman in the carriage waves at witnesses before the coach pulls away and the accident is repeated.

title: Horses' Hooves
notes: The hill is apparently home to the sound of horses moving around, the noise providing an uneasy feeling to any witness.
address: Stutton Rd, Brantham, Manningtree CO11, UK

title: Coach
notes: Appearing on the stroke of midnight, this coach pulled by a team of black horses circles the driveway before mysteriously vanishing again.

title: Galloping
location_name: Hollingbourne (Kent) - Hollingbourne House, north of village
notes: Heard only at night (and it is said seen only during the day) a ghostly horseman haunts the north part of the village, near where he failed to jump a gate, breaking his neck in the process.
address: Hollingbourne House, Hollingbourne Hill, Hollingbourne, Maidstone ME17 1QJ, UK

title: Sounds of Fighting
location_name: Holme Hale (Norfolk) - High Street, near the public house
notes: A couple of witnesses reported hearing horses and fighting which moved through the village street and over a field in the early hours of the morning.

title: Grey Woman
notes: Seen carrying a small baby, this woman dressed in grey was often heard walking about just prior to manifesting. The sounds of a horse passing outside have also been reported.
address: Moneyrow Grn, Moneyrow Green, Holyport, Maidenhead SL6 2ND, UK

title: Coach
notes: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven (or twelve) bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.
address: Mayton Bridge, Unit L/Bridge Farm, Little Hautbois, Buxton, Norwich NR12 7JR, UK

title: Horseman
notes: A wild horseman travels the desolate moors, looking for souls to take.

title: Lady in Brown Dress
datetime: Mostly unknown, tap felt in October 1995
notes: Local legend says this ghost is a serving girl whose child was killed when the lord of the manor threw it from an attic window. A pair of disembodied hands have been reported floating around the building, also said to belong to the former servant, while on the odd occasion, a male figure in spurs has been observed. A visitor to the manor in 1995 felt a strong tap on her shoulder. Expecting it to be her boyfriend, she turned, but was alone in the room. There are several other local legends about the manor, including hauntings by a horse and carriage, a phantom woman, and ghostly birds.
address: Hangleton Valley Dr, Brighton and Hove, Hove BN3 8AN, UK

title: Lady on White Stallion
notes: Appearing during the day, this female rider has been observed galloping across the road only to disappear once reaching the other side. The same area is haunted by a spectral coach being pulled at breakneck speed by four horses, and by a hooded man riding a bicycle.

title: Horse Worrier
location_name: Icklingham (Suffolk) - Hill near Deadman's Grave (now an SSSI)
notes: The ghost of a man and his horse, supposedly buried under the hill, were blamed for worrying other horses after dark, preventing them from using tracks through the area.
address: Deadman's Grave, Icklingham, Bury Saint Edmunds IP28 6PY, UK

title: Headless Shape on Horseback
notes: The Archbishop of Sudbury (or another senior religious figure) was decapitated near to this point by his enemies, the man now returns on dark nights to retrace the route of his death. Another story names the headless horseman as a highwayman, or one of three rebels executed nearby for the murder of the Archbishop.
address: Icklingham, Bury Saint Edmunds IP28, UK

title: Horseman
location_name: Ightfield (Shropshire) - Path between Ightfield Hall and village
notes: A phantom horseman is said to appear and charge at pedestrians before vanishing.
address: Ightfield Hall, Ightfield Hall Cottages, Ightfield, Whitchurch SY13 4BN, UK

title: Screaming
notes: The cries for help heard only on Halloween emerge from the staircase. They may belong to a maid once caught in a fire which engulfed the building - she fell to her death trying to escape. A ghostly carriage pulled by a single horse haunts the driveway, while another spook has been reported in the library.
address: Chapel of Garioch, Inverurie AB51 5HS, UK

title: Pale Figure
location_name: Ipswich (Suffolk) - Pipers Vale, aka The Lairs
notes: This pallid person spotted standing around the area could be the phantom of one of many suicides which have occurred on the nearby Orwell Bridge. At least one witness has heard an invisible horse.
address: Pipers Vale, Gainsborough Ln, Ipswich IP3 0ER, UK

title: Ewen of the Small Head
location_name: Isle of Mull (Argyll and Bute) - Duart Castle
datetime: Prior to a death in the MacLean family
notes: This decapitated figure on horseback is seen as a member of the family who killed him lies dying.

title: White Horse
location_name: Ivinghoe (Buckinghamshire) - Ivinghoe Aston to Slapton Road
notes: While travelling along this road during heavy rain at night, a taxi driver encountered someone riding a white horse. The driver had to stop abruptly to avoid the rider, and when the driver looked up, man and horse had vanished. When the witness later mentioned the encounter to other people, they said it was not the first time the entity had been seen.
address: Slapton Rd, Leighton Buzzard LU7, UK

title: James II
notes: James II was killed storming the castle, and it is reportedly his ghost on horseback that still charges around the area.

title: Sound of Hooves & Screams
notes: Once a family home, local legend has it that a child ran out of the manor to meet his mother and father who were returning for Christmas in a horse-drawn coach. The child was hit by the horses and died of his injuries. The anniversary of the event is marked by the reoccurring sounds of the tragedy.

title: Farmer
notes: This entity rides on horseback pulling a roller across the fields.

title: Sounds of a Coach
notes: A cyclist who paused on the road to relight his lamp heard the thump of hooves and the wheels of a coach pass him, even though nothing was visible. A local legend says that the phantom sounds belong to a couple who ran away to wed, but one angry parent followed and shot them both.

title: Headless Horseman
location_name: Kilcummer (County Cork) - Hill in the area
notes: This area was the haunt of a headless horseman who died while racing the country roads. His ghost was put to rest after a local man was brave enough to let the decapitated figure ride by his side.
address: Kilcummer Upper, Kilcummer, Co. Cork, Ireland

title: Horseman
notes: A grisly headless horseman rides around this area of the village.

title: Prince Rupert's Horse
location_name: Kineton (Warwickshire) - Marlborough Farm Camp (Second World War PoW camp, exact location not known), and Graveyard Spinney
notes: Two sentries reported seeing something white, resembling a horse, jump a hedge and disappear close to their camp. A police officer who patrolled the area encountered the ghost so frequently that he ignored the entity. Local folklore claimed a phantom horse foaming at the mouth, thought to have belonged to Prince Rupert, haunted the area.

title: Squire Surtees
location_name: Kingston St Mary (Somerset) - Tainfield House (no longer a single house)
notes: Riding a mottled grey horse, the squire rides circles on the driveway, rattling a handful of chains.

title: Sir George Blount
notes: Angered to such a degree by his daughter eloping with a man he hated, Sir George Blount gained his revenge by coming back from the dead and terrorising this village. He was seen on horseback and would scare locals washing their clothes in a long gone pond. A group of clergymen finally bottled the ghost and cast it into the sea (or buried it in the local churchyard).

title: Horses
notes: Tourists staying in this cottage were said to have been awaken as the building shook due to a stampede of phantom horses. Footsteps have also been heard outside, and the face of a quickly vanishing sailor observed looking in through windows.

title: Grey Horses
notes: A man walking his dog reported seeing a grey coach being pulled by two grey horses. Two grey men sat driving the ghostly vehicle.
address: The Dumbles Bungalow, Doles Ln, Kirkby in Ashfield, Nottingham NG17 8GX, UK

title: Copeman
location_name: Kirton in Lindsey (Lincolnshire) - Road between Kirton and Grayingham
notes: A man named Copeman murdered along the road may still haunt the area on horseback. Two men cycling back from Kirton spotted the ghostly horseman.

title: Edward Higgins
notes: A former robber and burglar, Higgins was caught, killed, and now haunts the hotel where he made his base of operations. A phantom coach has been seen and heard moving over cobbles outside the building.
address: The Royal George Building, Knutsford WA16, UK

title: Horse & Cart
notes: A motorcyclist encountered a horse pulling a cart with a high back and sides, being led by a man holding a large whip. As the cyclist slowed to overtake the cart, he was momentarily blinded by an approaching car. When his vision had returned to normal after a few seconds, the cart had vanished.

title: White Horse
notes: A legend says that when evil or misfortune is en route to villages alongside the lake, a white horse appears and crosses the water.

title: Glowing Horseman
location_name: Lamarsh (Essex) - Road between Lamarsh Hills and Bures St Mary
notes: Terrorising all who travelled the road between dusk and dawn, it took the combined efforts of all the local priests and their staff to exorcise this ghost.

title: Horse & Rider
location_name: Langford Budville (Somerset) - Area near the army base
notes: A horse and rider were killed by the Wild Hunt when they failed to hide themselves in time from the rabid hounds of hell. Their souls remain trapped in the area.

title: Raided Building
notes: Over the past two hundred years, there have been several reports of this building seen to be on fire with soldiers running around its base - the vision vanishes if investigated. Another tale of the area tells of a headless coachman who drives a phantom coach.

title: Funeral Coach
notes: A funeral coach pulled by headless horses reputedly terrified people in the area. The locals summoned Rev Richard Dodge to banish the entity. Searching for the apparition, he found the coach parked on the moor, a phantom coachman standing beside it, and by the phantom's feet, the local vicar lay unconscious. Dodge said a prayer, the coachman fled in his vehicle and the vicar recovered (after a few days in bed). The coach never returned to Lanreath.

title: Charging Horses
notes: A Civil War battle site, the sounds of horses have been reported charging around the vicinity, though the creatures cannot be seen. Old fashioned figures, two women and a man, are also said to haunt the area.

title: St Thomas of Canterbury
notes: The ghost of Thomas Beckett appears on the anniversary of his death, passing through the village on horseback.

title: Coach
notes: Two women returning home to Kingstanding heard the approach of a fast moving horse-drawn coach behind them so climbed up a bank to let the vehicle pass. The coach, too wide for the road on which it travelled, passed at speed and left the road, falling into a ditch. The coach then promptly vanished.

title: Headless Coachman
notes: A groom and his assistant heard galloping approaching Leam Hall. Expecting the lord and lady of the hall, the pair stood outside awaiting the coach. However, instead of the master's coach, the approaching vehicle was of a 'strange design' and was driven by a headless man. The phantom coach was driven straight past the witnesses and vanished into a closed gate.

title: Carriage
notes: Not reported for years, the ghostly carriage was supposed to be pulled by a team of four horses.

title: Coach
notes: A phantom coach can be heard travelling the area.

title: Headless Women
location_name: Leigh (Dorset) - Pool in the area, exact location unknown
notes: Having drowned in the pool when their horse and coach fell in, these four headless women (still in their coach) rise from the water at midnight and travel around the area.

title: Danes
location_name: Leith Hill (Surrey) - Area around Anstiebury hillfort
notes: The site of a battle where the Saxons engaged the Danes is said to be haunted by matching soldiers from both camps.

title: Coach
location_name: Lelant (Cornwall) - Lane off the road between village and Chy-An-Gweal (Carbis Bay)
notes: This phantom coach was driven along the dark lane by a ghostly woman.
address: 1 Chy An Gweal Cottages, Carbis Bay, Saint Ives TR26 2PR, UK

title: Dench Family Coach
location_name: Lenchwick (Hereford & Worcester) - Road between Lenchwick and Norton
notes: Belonging to the Dench family, a phantom coach is said to run along this stretch of road.
address: Norton and Lenchwick, Evesham WR11, UK

title: Coach
location_name: Letton (Norfolk) - Road through village (now the road between Letton hall and Cranworth, the Shipdham Road)
notes: A female witness recalled seeing a phantom coach being pulled by invisible horses along this road, so bright it lit the night up as if day.

title: Open the Gates!
location_name: Lincoln (Lincolnshire) - Flat area at top of Steep Hill, outside castle gate
notes: A night worker returning home early in the morning was unnerved as a man riding a black horse charged by him. As the rider approached the locked castle gates, he screamed 'Open the gates! Open the gates!' before vanishing near the still closed entrance. Another witness reported feeling breathing on the back of the neck, hearing strange footsteps and seeing shadows.

title: General Dalyell
notes: A one-time owner of the building, General Dalyell has been seen on horseback on the estate. The seventeenth century commander may return to gloat over his constant poker victories, in which he even beat the Devil himself! A phantom monk has also been reported on site.
address: House of the Binns (National Trust for Scotland), Binns View, Linlithgow EH49 7NA, UK

title: Headless Horseman
location_name: Litchfield (Hampshire) - Angledown Barrow (aka Angle Down Barrow)
notes: Holding his head under one arm, this horseman was said to have manifested when a small group of men attempted to dig up the barrow while looking for treasure (reputed to be a golden image).

title: Lady Lightfoot
notes: This ghostly woman drove her coach and horses around inside the courthouse, screaming in protest of her murder while in prison.

title: Solid Gold
location_name: Littlebredy (aka Little Bredy) (Dorset) - The Grey Mare & Her Colts (long barrow)
notes: Hidden under the Grey Mare is reputedly a coffin made from solid gold. There is also a local legend that connects the barrow to a phantom horse that runs around the area.

title: White Hound of Feens
notes: A legend says this hound, which can be heard howling in the night, is sometimes accompanied by a young lady in grey on horseback. She, in turn, has been known to ride with a wild hunt called the Dorcas Noble, while trying to win the heart of a former boyfriend. A large white hound was seen in the area during the 1980s - the size of an Irish Wolfhound, during one encounter the creature vanished as if running behind a black sheet.

title: Horseless Horse Rider
location_name: Llanerchymedd (Llannerch-y-medd) (Gwynedd) - Road between Llangefni and Benllech, near the California Inn
notes: Thrown off her mount and killed, this shade now stands around the place where she broke her neck.

title: White Horse
location_name: Llangynwyd (Mid Glamorgan) - Roads in the area
notes: Even the most fearless residents on the village felt terror when they encountered this phantom, snow-white mare along the roads at night.

title: Fantastic Horse
location_name: Loch Borralan (Highland) - Waters of the loch
notes: This loch monster will disguise itself as a radiant white horse, enabling it to walk up to victims before pulling them into the loch's murky waters.

title: Kelpie
location_name: Loch Coruisk (Highland) - Waters of the loch
notes: This lake monster would take the form of a beautiful horse which would try to drown anyone who tried to mount it.
address: Loch Coruisk, Isle of Skye, UK

title: White Horse
location_name: Loch Dornoch (Highland) - Straight piece of road lined with trees on both sides
notes: Witness Mr Morrison stopped his car suddenly when he encountered a white horse ridden by a Victorian man in the middle of the road. Both entities vanished quickly into the distance.

title: Water Horse
location_name: Loch Shin (Highland) - Waters of the loch
notes: This creature was friendlier than many of the other Scottish water horses and assisted in the construction of a local church. It may have been yellow in colour.

title: Alex Skene
notes: A local villain, Alex was drowned by Old Nick as he tried to cross the waters of the loch in his horse-drawn carriage. The same coach still travels the surrounding countryside, driven by its dead master. The same area is also 'haunted' by a large hairy monster, apparently once photographed from a distance.
address: Loch of Skene, Westhill AB32, UK

title: Headless Horseman
datetime: Prior to tragedy in the Ewen family
notes: This decapitated ghostly figure was once Ewen of the Little Head. Ewen was killed in battle with a rogue laird and now appears in the area around the house to warn the Ewen family of impending doom. Another animal, a large black dog, is reported to patrol the grounds.
address: Lochbuie House, Lochbuie, Isle of Mull PA62 6AA, UK

title: Bean-nighe
location_name: Lochbuie, Isle of Mull (Argyll and Bute) - River by Moy Castle
notes: The Bean-nighe is a Scottish spirit, an ill bringer who would be seen washing the clothes of those soon to die. This creature has long breasts, slung over her shoulders, and if she were grabbed from behind, the Bean-nighe would say whom the clothes belonged; if yours, she could change fate. The owner of the castle, upon seeing the creature, tried to grab her but failed - he died in battle the following day. As if this entity were not enough to contend with, the area is haunted by a headless horseman.
address: Lochbuie House, Isle of Mull, Lochbuie PA62 6AA, UK

title: White Horse
location_name: Lochgelly (Fife) - Old house (may no longer stand) on the banks of Loch Gelly, Powguild
notes: A white horse once haunted the area around the house. Another animal ghost was reputedly a mole - a former resident declared that she would haunt the garden she loved in the form of the furry mammal, and so she did.

title: Headless Horseman
location_name: Loddington (Leicestershire) - Road on a steep hill in the area
notes: The horseman, holding his head under one arm, gallops at breakneck speed down the hill, with anyone who witnesses him doomed to suffer a family bereavement.

title: Coach
notes: The driveway of the house is said to be haunted by a coach containing a headless footman and four headless passengers, driven by a headless coachman, pulled by a team of four headless horses.
address: Kingston Russell House, Long Bredy, Dorchester DT2 9HR, UK

title: Headless Horses
notes: A coach pulled by six headless horses is said to travel up the hill. There are also vague reports of a shuck-like dog that runs in the same area. Another ghost, that of a scary old woman with matted black hair, was also reported here in 1973.
address: Compton Ct, Long Compton, Shipston-on-Stour CV36, UK

title: Horse & Cart
notes: While overtaking a slow moving lorry, a driver spotted a grey horse and cart to the side of the bridge ahead of him. When the driver reached the bridge, the horse had vanished.

title: Horses
notes: The site of a Cromwellian defeat, the anniversary of which is marked by phantom hoof falls.

title: Coach & Horses
location_name: Louth (Lincolnshire) - Cadeby Hall and Barton Street
notes: The evening prior to a death in the owner's family a team of four horses would pull a coach up the driveway before fading from view. The nearby Barton Street is also haunted by a phantom horse that throws its rider into a ditch.

title: Galloping Horse
location_name: Lower Bebington (Lancashire) - Orchard Cottage (no longer standing)
notes: Demolished in 1934, the surrounding land of the cottage is haunted by a horse which could be heard quite distinctly, though never seen.

title: Tommy
notes: Seen on marsh fields with another horse at night, Tommy died several years previous, though still enjoys grazing alongside his former friends.

title: Headless Man
location_name: Lulsley (Hereford & Worcester) - Sandy Lane (between Osebury Rock and the river)
notes: One folk story says this road is home to several entities that appear to travellers, including a headless man, a white horse, a black dog, and a sad woman with flowing hair who wears a white robe.

title: Duke of Monmouth
notes: The ghostly figure of the Duke of Monmouth, upon a white horse, is said to ride inland.

title: Coach and Horses
location_name: Lyme Regis (Dorset) - Road near Trent Manor House
notes: This piece of road is haunted by a coach that vanished without trace many years ago.

title: Coach and Four
notes: This team of horses and the coach all fell into the pit and drowned. Their ghosts run along the fateful route and the accident is periodically replayed.

title: Winner
notes: A group of miners playing cards invited a passing stranger to join their game. He sat with them, quickly winning all hands, although the game quickly came to an end when the miners fled, having realised their guest had cloven feet.
address: Mid Glamorgan, 107 Llwydarth Rd, Maesteg CF34 9EU, UK

title: Horses
notes: A legend once said that horses were driven around the area of the church by an invisible entity. Fire emerging from the horses' nostrils would be blamed for darkened grass and the flaking paint on fencing. In the same area, a phantom donkey was said to have been banished after a man kicked the creature.

title: Sounds of Battle
location_name: Maghull (Lancashire) - Exact place not known, but south of the area
notes: One would have hoped that this Jacobite battle would have finished by now, but it is not the case. The occasional headless horseman is reported in the area. Image (c) Peter Crawley.

title: Headless Coachman
notes: A headless coachman, his missing cranium in a box besides him, drives his coach and four along this lane.

title: Blazing Eyed Dog
notes: A large black hound with burning red eyes was said to lurk in this area. A phantom grey horse is also reported, as is an old woman laden down with a bundle of faggots, drifting towards the Holy Well.
address: Feltrim Rd, Malahide, Co. Dublin, Ireland

title: Horse
notes: Walking her dogs, this witness heard and felt a large horse trotting towards her. She called the dogs to one side, to let the rider pass, but upon turning around, she could she nothing there.

title: Pounding Hooves
location_name: Manifold Valley (Staffordshire) - Road leading to Throwley Hall (no longer standing)
notes: The local stories state that many years ago, a coach returning guests to the hall crashed along the road, killing all on board. The horses (and sometimes the wheels of the coach) can still be heard on occasion, thundering along the road.
address: Throwley Hall Farm Far Wall Lane, Ilam, Ashbourne DE6 2BB, UK

title: Golden Coach
notes: Pulled by a team of four headless horses, this golden coach appears at The Hatches.

title: Georgian Coach
location_name: Market Harborough (Leicestershire) - Unidentified long road in the area
notes: This Georgian-era coach pulled by four ghostly horses is supposed to be connected to a well-known house in the area. One folktale says a young person mistook the phantom coach as 'real' and asked the driver for directions; coach and horses vanished, leaving only the smell of brimstone.

title: Roman on Horseback
notes: This Roman centurion passes through the area on horseback.
address: Ridgeway Cl, Market Lavington, Devizes SN10, UK

title: Catherine Ferrers
location_name: Markyate Cell (Hertfordshire) - Area around the priory
notes: Seen riding her horse topless, and swinging around tree branches without a stitch on, the ghost of the highwaywoman Ferrers continues to create the same amount of havoc that she did whilst living.

title: Coach
datetime: Last sighting unknown, but said to appear at midnight
notes: This coach, driven by a decapitated horseman, manifests on the stroke of midnight and travels through a tiny gap in a hedge.

title: Racing Driver
notes: A phantom coach driver takes is said to dash through the wood, whipping his horses to breakneck speed.

title: Lord on Horseback
notes: A former owner of the hall is now said to ride around the area on horseback. A lion was seen by several people just after the Second World War, though no tangible evidence was ever found - it is unclear whether is qualifies as an ABC or a feline phantom.

title: Funeral Procession
location_name: Marnhull (Dorset) - Area near quarry, and/or Sackmore Lane
datetime: Always at midnight, thought the date changes
notes: This line of mourners following a black horse drawn carriage has been reported travelling the roads in Marnhull. Another report states that there are only two pallbearers, and they are more likely to be seen along Sackmore Lane.
address: Sackmore Ln, Marnhull, Sturminster Newton DT10, UK

title: Phantom Coach and Horses
notes: Witnessed by the wife of a landlord, a phantom coach pulled by a team of horses entered the courtyard, quickly fading from view.

title: Horseman
location_name: Matlock (Derbyshire) - Bakewell Road, outside of the Railway Hotel
notes: One woman being driven along this street reported seeing a dark man on horseback. He wore a long cape and a large floppy hat partly covered his face.
address: Railway Hotel, 7 Bakewell Rd, Matlock DE4 3AU, UK

title: Laird on Horseback
notes: Killed by a local witch, this former owner of the tower can be seen riding his mare, lost on the barren land. Another ghost story from the area says a woman returned from the grave to ensure that her hidden treasure was given fairly to the landowner and a kind serving girl.

title: Woman riding Horse towards Clouds
notes: This sight could only be seen while looking through the branches of a certain tree in the area at night. However, the tree has since been cut down...

title: Mr Baker
location_name: Melbury Bubb (Dorset) - Dirt track along Bubdown Hill, known as Murderers Lane?
notes: Mr Baker was a farmer murdered on the hill in 1694. His ghost returns on the anniversary of the crime, possibly driving a horse and carriage.

title: Arthur Bryant Haig
notes: Former owner of the house, Arthur Bryant Haig, is still said to mount his (now phantom) chestnut horse and ride through his lands towards a nearby river.

title: Coach and Four
notes: This team of horses, together with their ghostly coach, have been seen approaching the front door to this building.

title: Danish Brothers
notes: Many moons ago, two Danish brothers fought to discover who should marry a beautiful Saxon girl. They managed to kill each other, and their ghosts continue to fight to this day. Other stories say the island is haunted by the sounds of a horse, the shouts of an abusive man who drowned chasing his wife and daughter, and a Roman centurion.

title: Red Stagecoach
location_name: Mevagissey (Cornwall) - Road between Mevagissey and Truro
notes: A driver along this road closed his eyes and prepared for a crash when this eighteenth century coach driven by four horses appeared in front of him - but the accident never happened, as the coach vanished into thin air. The witness later said he had also seen the driver and a guard on the coach.

title: Invisible Carriage
location_name: Michelmersh (Hampshire) - Between Manor Farm & Dower House
notes: Heard but not seen, a horse drawn carriage moves between these two buildings, driven by Lord Ogle.
address: Manor Farm Ln, Michelmersh, Romsey SO51 0NT, UK

title: Gentle Music
notes: Three children reported hearing a battle coming from outside the castle and seeing a knight on horseback who charged at them. Inside the castle, muffled sixteenth century music has been heard, while there are persistent rumours of buried treasure somewhere in the grounds (which can only be found after running nonstop around the fort nine time and quickly entering the mysterious door which magically opens!).
address: Castle Hill Middleham, North Yorkshire, Middleham, Leyburn DL8 4QG, UK

title: Grey Horse
notes: Author Alasdair Alpin MacGregor reported encountering the phantom of a grey horse at an overgrown water trough near Kirkandrews upon Eden. MacGregor relayed his sighting to a local postmaster, who told him reports of the apparition were not uncommon.

title: Headless Horseman
notes: This phantom rider is named as local highwayman Galloping Dick.

title: Heavy Horse
location_name: Milton Ernest (Bedfordshire) - Field along Thurleigh Road
notes: A hunter with a torch looking for rabbits heard and felt a heavy horse running toward and then past him, but nothing could be seen in any direction.
address: Thurleigh Rd, Milton Ernest, Bedford MK44, UK

title: Black Bess
location_name: Milton Keynes (Buckinghamshire) - Woughton on the Green, general area
notes: A former hideout of the highwayman Dick Turpin, both he and his dark horse were once seen in the area - even after his death.

title: Grey Lady
location_name: Moneyrow Green (Berkshire) - White Hart public house
notes: This phantom woman is sometimes seen along with her young child. The road outside the pub is haunted by a racehorse which occasionally gallops by, more often heard than seen.
address: Moneyrow Grn, Holyport, Maidenhead SL6, UK

title: Horse & Cart
location_name: Mosbrough (South Yorkshire) - The Crofts public house, road leading to
notes: Patrons of this pub reported seeing a ghostly cart being pulled by a horse, in turn being led by a farmer. The apparition would disappear at a nearby pond.

title: Lucky Venture?
notes: The stadium was briefly haunted by a horse named Lucky Venture which died on the track. The animal's shade was exorcised by a couple of local university students.

title: Faceless Woman in Carriage
notes: While moth spotting, a woman watched as three men on horseback pulled up beside her, escorting a coach. The witness reports seeing a woman inside, wearing a cloak, and with feathers in her hair, but her face consisted of smooth flesh - no eyes, nose or mouth were visible. After the witness screamed, the coach and horses rode off.
address: Mutford Big Wood, Lowestoft NR33, UK

title: Juan White
notes: A local witch, Juan White is thought to now haunt this location. She was seen in 2004 by a couple of people who also heard galloping horses just prior to their sighting, though they could not see any of the creatures.
address: Milfraen Ave & Gwent Terrace, Nantyglo, Ebbw Vale NP23 4PJ, UK

title: Smith on Horseback
notes: An owner of a horse once hanged a blacksmith for failing to complete a piece of work within a tight time limit - the date of the death is marked by the appearance of a large white horse carrying the dead smith (or the phantom smith opening the gates, depending on the source). Another report says that the ghost of the smith does not actually appear but unlocks and opens the gates. A spirit in the house itself, that of a grey lady, has been seen several times, and there are unsubstantiated reports that it has been exorcised.
address: Nether Lypiatt Manor, Lypiatt, Stroud GL6 7LS, UK

title: Phantom Horse and Rider
location_name: Newmarket (Suffolk) - Hamilton Stud Lane, and Newmarket Racecourse
notes: Fred Archer, a famed jockey from the late nineteenth century, has been blamed for accidents on the Newmarket Course. He has also been seen riding on the back of his favourite mount along Hamilton Stud Lane and the heath.
address: Rowley Mile Racecourse, Cambridge Road, Newmarket CB8 0TF, UK

title: Flying Horse
notes: This barrow is home to a headless horseman who travels several metres above the ground.
address: Barrowfields, The Barrowfields, Narrowcliff, Newquay TR7 2RZ, UK

title: Lady Jane Grey
