notes: Raised here as a child, Lady Jane's tormented shade now haunts both the mansion and the grounds surrounding the building. One ghost story says that she arrives in a coach pulled by four black headless horses at the ruins of Bradgate on Christmas Eve.
address: Bradgate Park, Bradgate Park, Newtown Linford, Leicester LE6 0HE, UK

title: Horses & Coach
location_name: North Craven (North Yorkshire) - Road outside Brunton House
notes: A team of four horses pulling a spectral coach reportedly passes the house.
address: Brunton House, Austwick, Lancaster LA2 8DE, UK

title: Roundheads
notes: At least one witness has reported seeing spectral Roundheads on horseback ride through this area.
address: Belgrave Rd, Bulwell, Nottingham NG6, UK

title: Children
notes: The ghosts of a white woman and of two children have been reported in this wooded area. While the woman in white has been named as Mary Chaworth Musters, the children are thought to have been murdered by their father William Saville, hanged for the crime in 1844. The sound of horses has also been reported.
address: Colwick Woods Ct, Sneinton, Nottingham NG2, UK

title: Trotting
location_name: NW1 (Greater London) - Camden Market (Stables Market section)
notes: Traders have reported the sounds of horses trotting on the cobbles, and occasionally cries for help from long forgotten accidents.
address: The Stables Market, 711 Chalk Farm Rd, Chalk Farm, London NW1 8AH, UK

title: Trotting
datetime: Horses reported in the 1930s, other events in 2004
notes: Sounds of a horse moving around this area were frequently reported prior to the Second World War. More recently, one witness reported her dog taking bites at an invisible presence within the public house.

title: Coach
location_name: Oakley Down (Dorset) - Area near the barrow
notes: A coach pulled by two headless horses was spotted by a boy transporting wheat during the early hours of the morning.
address: Wimborne St Giles, Salisbury SP5 5QP, UK

title: Headless Driver
notes: A coach and four driven by a headless figure is said to travel along this road.

title: Horse
location_name: Odstock (Wiltshire) - Lane adjoining St Marys Church
notes: A woman in the sitting in the passenger side of a car opened her window as the vehicle left the church. Suddenly both the woman and driver heard a horse trotting alongside the vehicle, even though no animal could be seen. As the vehicle reached the main road, the trotting ceased.

title: Floating Lights
notes: Paranormal occurrences have happened here, including lights that have appeared and floated around the house, sounds of horses trotting up to the back door (no such creature being found on investigation), and the roof glowing so violently, witnesses outside thought the building was on fire.

title: Headless Horseman
location_name: Onecote (Staffordshire) - Road from Leek to Onecote, and Butterton Moor
notes: This headless horseman is said to either be a murdered pedlar or a knight killed in combat with the Scots. Either way, the figure is said to be unlucky (with witnesses either dying or losing livestock) and an exorcism once performed on the phantom horseman by seven preachers failed.

title: Sir Geoffrey de Mandeville
location_name: Outer London: East Barnet (Greater London) - Oak Hill Park
datetime: 24 December 2026, 23:30h or 00:00h next due, then every sixth year (reoccurring)
notes: Sir Geoffrey, complete with red cloak and jangling spurs, appears here on horseback and heads towards the church to disappear through its wall. Even though the ghost is said to appear on Christmas Eve, the last verified sighting of Sir Geoffrey was on 17 December 1932.
address: Oak Hill Park, Parkside Gardens, London, Barnet EN4 8JP, UK

title: Turpin and Bess
location_name: Outer London: Enfield (Greater London) - Rose & Crown public house, Clay Hill
notes: Reportedly once owned by Dick Turpin's grandfather, the highwayman would visit the family when he needed to hide out. His ghostly form has been seen both inside and out, sometimes accompanied by his horse.

title: Heavy Breathing
location_name: Outer London: Heathrow Airport (Greater London) - General area
notes: This ghost was heard several times, breathing heavily around female members of staff, but never being seen. A second entity wears a bowler hat and has been known to ask about the whereabouts of his briefcase - both case and man were in an aircraft that crashed in the late 1940s, killing all on board. A phantom Dick Turpin rides a brown horse across the runways (or cargo sheds), while cleaning staff during the 1970s reported a presence which held them down, applying pressure around their throats. Finally, prior to the airport being built, a phantom Saxon prince reputedly haunted the area close to a pond where he drowned.

title: Invisible Mare
location_name: Outer London: Lower Bedington (Greater London) - Orchard House (no longer standing)
notes: Heard but never seen, a horse would gallop up to the gates of the house before stopping.

title: Queen Elizabeth
location_name: Outer London: Richmond (Greater London) - Richmond Palace
notes: As she lay dying elsewhere, Elizabeth was observed standing at a window of the palace. A few reports say she still haunts the building. The palace may have been the location where Edward Seymour, Lord Protector of England, and Anne Seymour witnessed a 'hand with a bloodied sword' emerge from a wall. Edward was beheaded a short time later. There are also more recent reports of phantom horses' hooves heard clopping around the courtyard.

title: Horse Drawn Carriage
location_name: Outer London: Slade Green (Greater London) - Maisonettes, Slade Green
notes: While sitting in bed in his maisonette on the fourth floor, this witness was startled to see the tail end of a black liveried horse drawn carriage fade through his room and wall. He later learnt that a neighbour had died at exactly the time of his sighting. Soon after this, the now empty flat in which the neighbour had died became the scene of strong paranormal disturbances. Though the windows and doors sealed, items in the building were smashed and thrown around, and it was not until an exorcism was carried out that the apartment fell silent.

title: Phantom Coach
notes: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.
address: Oxnead Bridge, Brampton, Norwich NR10 5HN, UK

title: White Horse Upright
notes: This ghostly creature walks around on hind legs, ending the jaunt as it vanishes down an ancient well.

title: Bobby Bannister the Bastard Landlord
notes: Being dragged by a ghostly horse, this tragic figure bounces around the roads, his neck broken whilst hunting. One report says that the ghost was laid by a team of holy men who banished it to the bottom of the millstream. Screaming sometimes comes from the millstream, the sounds belonging to a girl who fell and was crushed by the wheel.

title: Coach
notes: The ghostly team of horses pulling a coach travel aimlessly in the neighbourhood.

title: Young Horseman
notes: Often heard and sometimes even seen, the horseman is thought to have been a young lad who stole a horse from his master to speed up the journey to meet his secret lover. In his haste, he galloped past the scene of an accident involving a cart - he could have saved the life of the driver who was trapped underneath, but lust took priority and the rider left the site without offering assistance. The victim of the accident later died but managed to curse the lad who rode on by; hence, the boy's ghost now roams.

title: Headless Horses
notes: Pulling a coach, this team of headless horses manifests around the season of goodwill. Legend states if stared at, it will whisk the witness away to the nether realm.

title: Horse & Dog
location_name: Penselwood (Wiltshire) - Sloane Track - between Penselwood and Stourton
notes: A man who wagered that he could cross the track in record time fell from his horse and snapped his neck. The man on horseback, now minus a head, with a hound in tow, tries again and again to make the journey once a year.

title: Smoking Hooves
notes: After an earthquake here, an area of the beach was discovered to be covered in small hoof prints, some smoking, and others spouting water. Some blamed the Devil, others a phantom horse and carriage that travels through the town.

title: Horse-Drawn Coach
notes: A woman heard a horse drawn coach outside her place of employment, but when she looked out of the window, nothing could be seen.

title: Hounds All Black and Wide-Eyed
location_name: Peterborough (Cambridgeshire) - In all the woods between Stamford and Peterborough
datetime: Between Lent and Easter (reoccurring), but last reported 1100s
notes: These woods were the scene of a wild hunt, which included large black hounds and loathsome riders on the backs of mares and goats.

title: Headless Horseman
notes: Witnessed by at least two different people since 1990, a headless horseman has been seen trotting down the high street during the early hours.

title: Monk
location_name: Pewsey (Wiltshire) - Fyfield Manor, the Monk's Tower
datetime: Monk in mid twentieth century, coach unknown
notes: One man was told by a gardener that a phantom monk could be heard ascending the staircase in the tower. The man went on to spend three years living in the tower and never heard a thing. Another local story states that the driveway of the building is haunted by a phantom coach and four.

title: Horses & Carriage
notes: Seen trotting down the main street, the history of the carriage pulled by two horses (or four, depending on the source) is unknown.

title: Horse and Coach
notes: Mrs Hackford was driving her car at around 4pm when a horse and carriage carrying an old lady and two men in Victorian clothing rode past her in the opposite direction. Because of the weirdness of the situation, the witness looked behind her, only to realise the coach was no longer there.

title: Hanging Judge Jeffreys
notes: As well as the notorious judge's ghost drifting around the building, one witness has also reported seeing the shade of a young blonde girl. She also haunts the road outside the building, causing passing motorists to stop sharply. The road in turn is also haunted by three horsemen.
address: Plas Teg, Mold Road,Pontblyddyn,Mold, Flintshire, Mold CH7 4HP, UK

title: Bloody Horse
location_name: Pool (Cornwall) - Road and tunnel close to South Crofty mine
notes: Driving past the mine in bad weather, a driver and his passenger felt a strange negative sensation wash over them. The driver slammed on the brakes and as the car stopped, they spotted a bloody horse standing in the lights. The driver started to climb out of his car to move the horse out of the road, but the creature had gone - there was a steep drop to one side of the road and a wire fence to the other side.
address: South Crofty Mine, Dudnance Ln, Pool, Redruth TR15 3QT, UK

title: Runaway Horses
location_name: Porlock (Somerset) - Road leading downhill to the town
notes: These horses were all killed when they broke away from their coach on the hill and lost control on a tight bend. They are heard re-enacting their final moments.

title: Tall, White Figure
notes: The pale figure most commonly seen is thought to be Charlotte (see Portchester church entry), though there are other reports of a monk who walks about outside the front of the castle, a Roman soldier, and a lady on the back of a white horse.
address: Church Ln, Portchester, Fareham PO16 9QW, UK

title: Lord Rokeby
notes: Lord Rokeby races around the area, minus his head, being drawn by a team of four horses.

title: Lady Carew in a Coach Pulled by Four Black Horses
notes: Occurring on the stroke of midnight, the phantom coach driven by a skeleton is seen to crash into the side of the bridge and fall into the river below. After selling her soul to the Devil, Lady Carew was taken away by two skeletons driving a coach pulled by four black horses on her wedding day. The coach disappeared on the bridge over the River Thurne, and the scene is repeated every 31 May. Another version of the story says the phantom is Lady Evelyn, who died when her coach crashed in 1741.
address: Potter Heigham, Great Yarmouth NR29, UK

title: Charging Horseman
notes: Killed by a Lancastrian arrow in the 1400's, this glowing messenger is doomed to carry his cargo forevermore. Another story says he was decapitated by a thin wire that was placed across the road.
address: Shaw Green Ln, Prestbury, Cheltenham GL52, UK

title: Headless Horseman
notes: A phantom coach and horses is driven down this lane by a headless horseman. The horseman's whip sparks at each crack.
address: Mill Ln, Pulham Market, Diss IP21, UK

title: Horse & Chain
location_name: Purse Caundle (Dorset) - Lane between village and Stourton Caundle
notes: An invisible team of horses pull an equally invisible coach along this lane. The only indication of their passing is the noise of the horses and the jangling of a chain. The sounds are said to reach the top of Stourton Caundle before ceasing.
address: Stourton Caundle, Sturminster Newton DT10, UK

title: Horseman
notes: A ghostly horseman has been heard and occasionally observed heading out of the village.

title: Tom King
notes: The sound of hooves heard several times from outside the public house are thought to belong to the horse of Tom King, a local highwayman who once frequented the bar.
address: Town Ln, Rackenford, Tiverton EX16 8DT, UK

title: Christopher Bloor
location_name: Rainham (Kent) - Lane leading to Bloor Place (no longer standing) and near church
notes: Christopher Bloor had a reputation for being a seducer of women. This proved to be Christopher's undoing - he was decapitated by a posse of local men with wives with whom he had slept. The lane was haunted by the headless Bloor, who reputedly drove his coach pulled by headless horses, and ghostly hoofbeats heard around the church.

title: Man on Black Horse
notes: Locals tell of an unknown man on a black horse that is believed to haunt the area. The devil is seen riding across the broad every 31 December; under his arm is the soul of a Colonel who had dared hunt on the Sabbath.

title: Earl Gerald
notes: The earl is said to walk under Lough Gur, emerging on the back of a black horse every seven years to visit his former home. Another former earl, James, is also said to haunt the castle - he was murdered by his servants.

title: Something
notes: A vague entry in the Laois Burial Grounds Survey 2011 says that the word Dullow is used to describe a phantom, although details of the haunting are not given.

title: Fast Moving Procession
location_name: Ratlinghope (Shropshire) - Road towards village, along the Long Mynd
notes: A large group of people following a horse drawn hearse have been seen on several occasions, moving much more swiftly than one should expect.
address: National Trust - Carding Mill Valley and the Long Mynd, Church Stretton SY6 6JG, UK

title: Sound of Horses
location_name: Redditch (Hereford & Worcester) - The Lane, Church Hill South
notes: A local legend says that the phantom horses heard in this area belonged to Roman riders who fell into a large pit trap dug by their enemies.

title: Horse with Trap
location_name: Rettendon (Essex) - Road leading to East Hannington
notes: The ghost haunting this road is said to be that of a farmer riding his horse and cart.
address: Rettendon, Rettendon Common, Chelmsford CM3 8EL, UK

title: Dog under Horses' Hooves
notes: A couple in a trap encountered the Shuck, and it ran under their horses. The driver tried to hit the black dog with his whip, but it passed straight through the creature. The same area is also haunted by a phantom coach, being driven at breakneck speed.

title: Coach & Horses
notes: The ghost of a smuggler driving his coach runs around the sandy area of this building, while on some nights a macabre entity is seen climbing out of the sea and enters the rectory. This may be the same entity which asks (in a gruff voice) 'why don't you turn around and look at me?' - those who do so have never seen anything.

title: Coach
notes: This coach pulled by a team of four horses is driven over a piece of private land, where a county lane once ran.

title: Horse
notes: A farmer heard a galloping horse rapidly approaching him, even though he could not see the creature. Moments later, there was a crashing sound, and a large gust of wind passed the man, even though the creature remained invisible.

title: White Horse
notes: Together with a fairy, this pale horse rises from the dangerous waters once a year. It has been known to drown people who come too close.
address: The Strid, River Wharfe, Skipton BD23 6AN, UK

title: Horses
notes: This lane is reputed to be haunted by the phantom sounds of horses.

title: Coach Crash
notes: A phantom coach and horses is seen to crash before vanishing into a wall somewhere in this area.
address: Ancre Hill Estates, Rockfield Rd, Monmouth NP25 5HS, UK

title: Coach and Horses
location_name: Rockfield (Gwent) - Road between Rockfield and Monmouth (Ancre Lane?)
notes: A coach pulled by a team of frantic horses reputedly vanishes into a field after crashing through a wall on the roadside.

title: Alice Lisle
location_name: Rockford (Hampshire) - Area around Alice Lisle public house
notes: Alice Lisle was the last woman to be executed by decapitation, at the hands of Judge Jeffreys. Her ghost rides around the area in a driverless coach, pulled by headless horses.
address: The Alice Lisle, Rockford, Ringwood BH24 3NA, UK

title: Headless Horsemen
notes: This church was said to have been relocated due to the intervention of headless horsemen.

title: White Woman & Coach
notes: It is said that a phantom white woman haunts this area, sometimes seen climbing into a ghostly horse-drawn coach which then drives off.

title: Mary Jenning
location_name: Royal Tunbridge Wells (Kent) - Common land opposite The Pantiles
notes: Mary Jenning, a rather large drunken woman, is said to haunt this area, and was observed by two women sheltering under a tree from a sudden downpour. In addition, a phantom female voice can be heard calling out the name 'Daniel'; the woman is never seen but is thought to be a lady who fell to her death from the nearby Wellington Rocks. Finally, a horseman dressed in mauve on the back of a black horse also travels the area.
address: London Rd, Royal Tunbridge Wells, Tunbridge Wells TN2 5TD, UK

title: Guarded Hoard
notes: Magically concealed, the treasure hidden here only stands a chance of discovery when the moon is full. However, if anyone comes to close to discovering the hoard, a phantom horse appears and charges the treasure hunter.

title: Bottled Squire
notes: This ghost of a squire driving his coach and horses raised hell in the neighbourhood until exorcised; trapped in a bottle now hidden within the walls of the hotel, he now waits until released again. A recent email received suggests that late night drivers in the area still encounter the phantom coach along Leicester Road.
address: Brownsover Hall, Brownsover Ln, Rugby CV21 1HU, UK

title: Sister Lucy
notes: Sister Lucy ran away from her chapel, but realising she had made a mistake, tried to return. She slipped as she passed the well and drowned in its waters. Her ghost is now said to haunt the area. The same location is also said to be home to a phantom horse.

title: Thundering Hooves
notes: Three witnesses standing outside watching the lunar eclipse heard horses loudly galloping across the field ahead of them, although the field was empty. One of the witnesses kept horses of their own and was familiar with the sound.

title: Hunting Party
notes: A rider on horseback and his hunting dog are reported to haunt the area around the hall, where their bodies were once discovered while the house was extended. Thomas Tresham, who constructed the building, wrote circa 1584 that he and two servants had heard three loud knocks (as if made from a hammer) emerge from a table.
address: Desborough Rd, Rushton, Kettering NN14 1RR, UK

title: White Horses
notes: A pair of white horses would occasionally appear along this road.
address: Sleaford Rd, Ruskington, Sleaford NG34, UK

title: Misty Horseman
notes: Sometimes, around midnight, a thick mist rolls into the churchyard. Soon after, a headless horseman rides in, vanishing into the mist, heading towards an old castle keep.

title: Heavy Breathing
location_name: Salcombe (Devon) - B3181 Salcombe to Malborough road
notes: A witness travelling this road reported hearing the heavy breathing of galloping horses along this road, but not accompanied by hoof falls. The hedge from which the sound could be heard moving past glowed briefly as the eerie sound climaxed.

title: Black Hounds
notes: The forest is reported to be home of several black dogs which bring ill fortune to any witnesses. There are also stories of a ghostly white deer which darts between the trees, a headless woman riding on horseback, and a coach and horses which leaves the forest and heads towards a nearby farm.

title: Coach and Horses
location_name: Scawby (Lincolnshire) - Road north of village, just before the crossroads (geography may have changed since story first published)
notes: A phantom horseman can be heard laughing as he drives his team of horses around this area. He was blamed when a local man drowned in a lake that no longer exists. Another phantom male was said to haunt this lake, but what has become of him is not known.

title: Lord John Angerstein
location_name: SE10 (Greater London) - Trafalgar Road to Vanbrugh Hill
notes: Lord John's coach, pulled by four headless horses and driven by the lord himself, is said to leave Trafalgar Road and return to his former home on Vanbrugh Road.

title: Fox Hunt
notes: A local legend says that the five trees are where an old witch coven would meet. One of the witches took the form of a fox and lead the local hunt over the edge of a cliff - the scene is repeated if the weather conditions are right.

title: Two Men with Pack Horses
notes: Two men leading pack horses escort the body of Edward the Martyr Prince to his final resting place.

title: Cavalier on Horseback
datetime: Prior to tragedy in the family
notes: The family which once lived in this house were haunted by a Cavalier who would appear galloping towards the building just prior to a death or bad news.

title: Uther Pendragon
notes: A large man on horseback seen galloping around on winter nights is said to be Uther Pendragon, though his motivation for being here is unclear.

title: Arthur's Victory
datetime: Battle in sixth century(?), entities in late twentieth century
notes: This hillfort has been named by some as the location where King Arthur defeated the Saxons. Several phantasmic events also surround the Rings, including the sighting of a ghostly warrior on horseback, a spectral woman wearing a black dress, and an ugly dwarf-like entity which peers into the windows of parked cars. Finally, there are stories of a golden coffin buried in the area.
address: Blandford Rd, Shapwick, Blandford Forum DT11 9JL, UK

title: Horse and Cart
location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - The Crofts public house, Mosborough
notes: This phantom horse and cart would be seen in the car park and were frequently mistaken as being 'real'. When a pond on the grounds was filled in, the apparition never returned.
address: The Croft, Hillside, Mosborough, Sheffield S20 5AL, UK

title: Coach and Four
location_name: Shepley (South Yorkshire) - Roads around the Shepley War Memorial
notes: Appearing at night, this phantom coach pulled by four horses would move at breakneck speed along the roads in and around the village.

title: Hooves on Gravel
notes: While walking home through the nearby countryside, a woman reported hearing the overworked horses and the sound of crunching gravel. The site of the occurrence, upon investigation, proved once to be a brick yard.

title: Shadows
notes: A former occupier of this property moved out after his dog chased a shadow out of the living room and up the stairs. They also reported hearing horse's hooves and experiencing poltergeist-like behaviour.

title: Peasant Girl
location_name: Silverstone (Northamptonshire) - Whittlebury Forest (and site of the former woodland)
notes: A knight took his own life after having his love rejected by a peasant girl. Lying in his own blood, the knight cursed her, and she died soon after. The peasant's ghost remains in the forest. Another entity reported to haunt the area is the wild hunt, complete with headless horseman (thought by some to be the aforementioned knight).

title: Horseman
notes: This figure on horseback is supposed to date from the late Bronze Age, which probably makes him the UK's oldest ghost. When a nearby barrow was excavated, it revealed the bones of a man and horse. The area is also said to be home to fairies.

title: Headless on Headless
location_name: Skipsea (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Roads around town
notes: The headless highwayman seen around this area rides a headless horse. A road on a hill south of the town is haunted by a woman (who may also be headless) wearing black, who quickly vanishes just before reaching a hill.

title: Clifford's Coach
datetime: Midnight, prior to a death in the family (reoccurring)
notes: The appearance of Lady Ann Clifford in her horse drawn coach was said to herald the death of the owner of the local castle.

title: Young Girl on Horseback
location_name: Slapton (Buckinghamshire) - Road from Slaptonbury Mill (no longer standing) to farm on other side of the village
notes: Legend says that the girl and her horse were both drowned when the mill stream flooded; the sound of the horse's hooves can still be heard. One driver reported seeing a well-dressed person upon a white horse as they travelled between Ivinghoe Aston and Slapton. They feared that they had hit the horse, so stopped, only to find no one there.

title: White Horse
location_name: Slindon (Sussex) - Area around village and Fontwell Park
notes: Often seen early in the morning, this riderless phantom steed moves towards witnesses and dissolves away seconds before reaching them. The entity may be related to the ghostly carriage pulled by a team of horses which was said to pass through the village along the lane close to the old butcher's shop.
address: Fontwell Park, Fontwell Ave, Arundel BN18 0SY, UK

title: One Man and his Dog
notes: Two men witnessed a man with a hound, pursuing several horses along a steep rocky ridge. Man and beasts suddenly vanished - the witnesses were at first concerned that they had all fallen over the edge of a cliff, though were unable to find any bodies.

title: Army Marching On
location_name: Souter Fell (Cumbria) - Side of Mountain visible from Wilton Hall
notes: Over two dozen witnesses saw a mass of troops ascending the mountain on horseback, though there were no real soldiers in the area. The ghostly army had been seen twice before, always on the same date.

title: Horses
notes: Ghostly horses and their riders are reported to haunt this region.

title: Judge Peck
notes: Perhaps connected to the Spixworth Manor ghost, a local tale warns of the phantom Judge Peck who travels the area in a coach.

title: Horse Drawn Hearse
notes: Several spooky manifestations were claimed by the manor house, included a spectral hearse driven up the driveway, a piano which played itself, a man bearing a lantern who resembled a night watchman, and a ghostly woman holding a candle.

title: Sobbing
location_name: St Albans (Hertfordshire) - Wicked Lady public house
notes: The sound of a lady's gentle sobs has been heard diffusing through the fabric of the pub. The common outside the pub is home to a highway woman on horseback, after whom the building is named.

title: Coach
location_name: St Andrews Bay (Fife) - West Sands Road, and general area
notes: This spectral coach and four is seen passing through the area, vanishing in a puff of blue smoke and leaving only the reek of sulphur.
address: W Sands Rd, St Andrews KY16, UK

title: Galloping
location_name: St Just (Cornwall) - Road between St Just & Tregeseal
notes: The sounds of horses pulling a carriage have been reported coming from this stretch of road during stormy nights.

title: Three Horsemen
notes: Three figures riding white horses were said to gallop loudly down the hill.

title: Lady Matthias
location_name: Stackpole Elidor (Dyfed) - Stackpole Court (no longer standing)
notes: A headless coachman driving headless horses pulling a headless lady in a coach would travel around the former court building. The woman in the coach, named as Lady Matthias, was exorcised many years prior to the demolition of the building.
address: National Trust - Stackpole, 88A Main St, Pembroke SA71 4HH, UK

title: Bloody Horseman
location_name: Staindrop (Durham) - A688 between town and Raby Castle
notes: This phantom rider on a white horse has his face covered in blood. The entities are said to slowly descend into the ground.
address: Raby Castle, Park and Gardens, Staindrop, Darlington DL2 3AH, UK

title: The Baron on Horseback
datetime: Winter nights (reoccurring), but last seen nineteenth century?
notes: A baron who took over the land on which a monastery stood had the building dismantled and the stones used to construct his manor house. He was cursed by monk, however, and fell off his horse and broke his neck before the house was finished. His shade still rides around the neighbourhood.

title: Turpin
notes: It was once believed that the ghost of Dick Turpin passed by this inn on Wednesday nights - the thud of horse's hooves could be heard, and some people reported seeing a man on horseback just outside of the building.

title: Scarlet Rider
location_name: Stockport (Greater Manchester) - Bramall (also known as Bramhall) Hall
notes: This figure on horseback, dressed in an unmistakable red, appears once a year - in 1630 he is said to have taken the life of the owner of the hall.
address: Bramhall Hall, Bramhall, Stockport SK7 3EW, UK

title: Wild Hunt
notes: Hounds, horses and hunters all pass through the village while chasing the souls of the damned. It is not known for sure whether the hunt last passed through this way in 1960 as no one dared peek out from their windows.

title: Coach
location_name: Stoke Abbott (Dorset) - Between Stoke Abbott and Beaminster
notes: Driving a phantom coach over road and field, an angry ghost of a former squire is doomed to relive his journey over and over.

title: Horseman
notes: A horseman with his caparisoned horse was said to travel over this bridge, the sound of his jangling armour causing unease to anyone unfortunate enough to be nearby.

title: Horseman
location_name: Stow on the Wold (Gloucestershire) - A429 Fosse Way, heading towards Warwick
notes: A driver reported glancing at her rear view mirror and seeing a horseman wearing a long black cape leap out into the road. The horseman was so close, the driver thought the rider would hit the car, but he vanished.
address: A429 & Fosse Way, Cheltenham GL54 2EZ, UK

title: Outline of Coach
location_name: Stroud (Gloucestershire) - Road running alongside Rodborough Common, and apartments built near Rodborough Manor
notes: A couple out walking in the 1970s watched in amazement as the ghostly outline of a horseless coach passed them. In 2013, a group of friends watched as an old fashioned car silently moved by. No driver could be seen and as they tried to follow, it vanished. Other witnesses have seen the phantom, and others have reported headless horsemen or of hearing galloping.

title: Coach & Horses
notes: A local legend says a phantom coach haunts this area.
address: Stock Gaylard House, Stock Gaylard, Sturminster Newton DT10 2BG, UK

title: Danny Grimshaw
location_name: Sutton Courtenay (Oxfordshire) - Hobbyhorse Road and near the local power plant
notes: This ghostly figure, charging around the roads on the back of a white horse whilst waving a bloody, straight edged razor, has survived at least one exorcism attempt.
address: Hobbyhorse Ln, Sutton Courtenay, Abingdon OX14, UK

title: Jeremiah Abershaw
notes: No wombles here, only a ghostly padfoot looking for victims after being hanged for his crimes two hundred years ago. On two occasions the ghostly entity has been seen wearing convicts clothing. Abershaw is sometimes heard on horseback, hooves heard thumping on the ground, yet the animal never appears.
address: Wimbledon and Putney Commons, Windmill Rd, Wimbledon Common, London SW19 5NR, UK

title: Brown Bear
location_name: SW3 (Greater London) - Glebe Place, Chelsea (aka Henry VIII hunting lodge)
notes: Bears, brown, white and black in colour, have all been reported in this area - a once popular place for bear baiting. A man on horseback has also been sited, riding in the area, and an unidentified home along the street was reputedly haunted by the sounds of choking.

title: Highwayman with Horse
notes: Appearing under the light of the full moon, the ghostly highwayman on horseback is reported to say, 'Your money or your life', and other such cliche expressions.

title: Running Rein
notes: Racehorse Running Rein (or Running Rain) was put down on the property when he became part of a legal dispute (possibly over the horse's true age) and his owner became unwilling to hand the mare to another trainer. The creature could be heard at night, galloping around the site.
address: Sywell House, 21 Church Ln, Sywell, Northampton NN6 0FD, UK

title: Battle of Bramham
notes: The sound of horses galloping on the field is thought to be those forever fleeing the nearby site of battle, which took several hundred lives.
address: Bramham Park, Bramham Ln, Wetherby LS23 6ND, UK

title: Horse
location_name: Talke (Staffordshire) - Road just outside the village
notes: Drivers reported hearing the sound like a hammer striking an anvil before seeing a phantom horse being led across the road, both man and beast cut off at the knees as if walking a much older track since built upon.

title: Black Coach
notes: This area of moorland was haunted by a black carriage pulled by two black, headless mares. The entity was never seen again after the Reverend Doidge exorcised the entity.

title: William Doggett
notes: Having borrowed large amounts of money to build the house, William Doggett committed suicide when he realised he could never pay off the loan. Doggett's ghost was heard walking around the house and blamed for opening and closing doors. It was also seen, headless, driving a coach in the grounds.
address: Tarrant Gunville, Blandford Forum DT11, UK

title: Horses
notes: There is said to be a secret passage connected this site and Dally Castle, with phantom horses heard running the route at midnight.

title: Plotter
notes: A horseman who rides through the village on this date was considered to be a member of the Gunpowder Plot escaping from London.

title: Drake
location_name: Tavistock (Devon) - Road between town and Plymouth
notes: Pulled by black headless horses, Sir Frances Drake drives a dark hearse down these roads whenever his drum is heard to beat (see Yelverton (Devon) entry).

title: Headless Group
location_name: Tenby (Dyfed) - Between Tenby and Sampson Cross
notes: A headless team of horses driven by a headless coachman carries a headless lady in a coach along this road every night. As they reach the crossroads known as Sampson Cross, some ten miles from their starting point, they all disappear in a large explosion. Some people believe the entities have been laid, and now rest in a nearby lake.

title: Heinous Horse
location_name: Termonfeckin (County Louth) - Road passing Rath House
notes: Two young adults driving along this road witnessed an otherworldly horse with the face of a man, the creature's eyes bulging from their sockets. The manifestation stood in the road, blocking their path for two minutes before vanishing.
address: Rath House, Dardisrath, Termonfeckin, Co. Louth, Ireland

title: Headless Horseman
notes: A local farmer told his friends that he watched a headless horseman sitting upon a white steed pass by this crossroads.

title: Funeral Procession
location_name: The Wansdyke (Wiltshire) - Various places along the post Roman dyke
notes: A wooden wagon bearing a coffin, pulled by jet black horses, has been spotted along this old dyke. The spectacle vanishes if witnesses become too close.

title: Little George
location_name: Thetford (Norfolk) - St George's Nunnery - Nun's bridges
notes: The seven year old George fell from a wooden horse in May 1559, cracking his skull on the wall of the bridge and dying from his injury. The young ghost haunted the area for many years (riding around on a headless wooded horse) before being exorcised.
address: Nuns' Bridges Rd & Nunnery Dr, Thetford IP24 3EL, UK

title: Mustard Coloured Coach
notes: On the stroke of midnight, a mustard coloured coach is believed to drive down the driveway of the ruined building. Its owner is thought to be Duke de Morrow, who lived in the building at the beginning of the twentieth century.

title: Radiant Boy
location_name: Thirsk (North Yorkshire) - Road to the town from Topcliffe, exact location unknown
notes: Lighting up the night with his brilliance, this lad rode past the witness on horseback, disappearing across a field.

title: Horse & Cart on Driveway
notes: The present Lord's father told stories of hearing horses and wheels on the gravel driveway; on investigation, there was nothing ever seen.

title: Horse
notes: One unnamed lane in the village is the haunt of a headless horse. The village must attract decapitated entities, as a headless pig is also said to haunt the general area.

title: Merlin
notes: A place featuring in Arthurian legend (Arthur is said to have been born here), the cave under the castle is said to be the one time home of Merlin. His spirit now shows a reluctance to leave the place. A ghostly horseman was reported by one witness, who promptly named the spirit as King Arthur.

title: Galloping Horses
notes: A ghostly herd of horses is said to haunt this area.

title: White Lady
datetime: Mostly unknown, but cloaked figure October 1976 and circa March 1995
notes: This pale figure is said to be one of a handful of ghosts reported here over the years, normally seen in the garden. A woman who starved to death in a bedroom and an old nurse also continue to reside here, while a headless horseman has been reported just outside the grounds of the building. In 1995 a member of staff spotted a glowing man wearing a cloak who faded away, remarkably like an event in 1976 where a young man watched a cloaked figure wearing a tricorn hat glide silently across the road outside the hall before passing effortlessly over the hedgerow.
address: Dartington Trust, Dartington Hall, Totnes TQ9 6EL, UK

title: Coach
notes: A phantom coach pulled by ghostly horses was spotted as it passed through the village.

title: Trotting Horse
notes: A newspaper article from 1956 suggested that the phantom house which would trot across the mountain was only the sound of distant (real) horses that drifted in on the wind.

title: Galloping Horsemen
location_name: Tremadog (Gwynedd) - Bypass close to the Tremadog roundabout
notes: A group of four or six horsemen wearing brown leather were seen charging across this bypass.

title: Bolting Horses
notes: The pool was once the scene of a tragedy when a horse drawn carriage crashed into it, drowning all on board. This mishap can still occasionally be heard. The pool was once believed to be so deep that it could never be measured.

title: Red Coach
location_name: Truro (Cornwall) - Road between Truro and Mevagissey
notes: This large red coach, drawn by four horses, charged at a motorist driving the opposite way down the narrow road - the apparition vanished just before impact.

title: Headless Rider
notes: A beautiful white horse carrying a headless rider gallops across the valley when the night sky is right. The phantom is either a peddler or a soldier, depending on the legend.

title: Horses
location_name: Twechar (Dumbartonshire) - Antonine Wall - Bar Hill Fort
notes: While picnicking with her daughter, this witness heard a stampede of horses close by, but no animals could be seen.

title: Young Male Rider
notes: The area around the castle is haunted by a decapitated horseman. The ghost is thought to be a man who tried to marry a girl residing at the castle, but her brothers chased him away. During the pursuit, the young man fell from his horse and lost his head.

title: White Horse
notes: The famous white horse seen on the hill from the village is reported to leave once a year (or every 100 years, depending on the storyteller) to have its feet shod at the nearby Wayland's Smithy. Another local legend states that the horse is not really a horse, but the dragon slain by St George on that very hill.

title: Coach
notes: Heard but not seen, a phantom coach occasionally crosses the bridge.

title: Stagecoach
notes: This black and red coach is still drawn along the roads, pulled by four phantom horses.

title: Horse and Pedestrian
location_name: Ventnor (Isle of Wight) - Old Park Road
notes: One local reported witnessing a phantom horse pass by, closely followed by the sounds of footsteps in pursuit of the creature. During the 1920s, two skeletons were discovered close to site of the haunting.
address: Old Park Rd, Ventnor PO38, UK

title: Carriage
location_name: W2 (Greater London) - Bayswater Road, area near Hyde Park
datetime: Coach unknown, woman possibly early twentieth century
notes: A nineteenth century coach and horses are said to still run along this stretch of road. Another ghost reported was a shadowy woman in shabby clothing with a face which resembled death incarnate.

title: Coach and Horses
datetime: Once per year, on a night of a full moon
notes: Moving at breakneck speed, a phantom team of horses pulling a coach race across the bridge before vanishing on the other side.

title: Fallen Horses
location_name: Wakefield (West Yorkshire) - Denby Dale Road bridge, and river below
notes: Walking her dog across the bridge, Amanda West was frozen with fear when the sound of horses passed her - with no horses to be seen. Without warning, she then heard a loud splash. Looking into the river below, the water was calm, but the sound of panicking horses could now be heard coming from below. She later found out that the original bridge had collapsed in 1836 and a team of horses pulling a cart had drowned.

title: Equine Entities
notes: Said to be heard only during the day, phantom horses gallop across the common. In addition, reports of a phantom man have been frequent since the 1940s, appearing both on the common and in the church ruins.

title: White Horse
notes: This pale riderless horse gallops over the gardens, twice a decade. The hotel itself is haunted by a young man, who some speculate to be the former rider of the horse.
address: Walton Hall & Hotel, Warwick CV35 9HU, UK

title: Phantom Coach
location_name: Wangford - SW of Thetford (Suffolk) - Raydon Hall, Quay Lane
notes: A coach pulled by a headless horse runs down Quay Lane on dark nights, believed to be driven by a former owner of Raydon Hall.
address: Quay Ln, Reydon, Southwold IP18, UK

title: Rider
notes: The threat of spotting a phantom woman on horseback ensured locals would not use this route after dark.

title: Phantom Horseman
notes: A phantom horseman, perhaps connected to the nearby battle site of Edge Hill, visits this area twice a year. One local man claimed to have seen a lantern being swung along a road, although no one held it.

title: Caesar
notes: Several ghosts have been reported here, including Caesar (and an accompanying army). Other people have reported an elderly man with a long white beard who moves between the trees, and the pounding hooves of invisible horses. Phantom druids (or the Devil himself, depending on the source) appear if you run around the fort seven times at midnight.

title: Force
location_name: Waterford (County Waterford) - By the old infirmary, towards John's Park / Polberry Cross Road
notes: A witness walking home in the early hours of the morning was pushed two metres by an unseen force. No one else could be seen. The witness said the sensation resembled a push from a horse and counted it as one of their worst experiences.

title: Headless Horse
notes: The headless horse which runs along this road was said to have been a casualty during the civil war.

title: Horse and Half A Rider
location_name: Wendover (Buckinghamshire) - Steep road off the M40, though Wendover Woods
notes: Recorded by the local press, a man reported that his wife had seen a horse pass in front of the car in which she was travelling, and upon the creature was the bottom half of a rider - the figure was not visible above legs. The driver of the car did not spot anything.

title: Speeding Horseman
location_name: West Auckland (Durham) - Route between town and Hamsterley Forest
notes: Seen galloping at the speed of an arrow, this horseman vanishes once he enters the dense woodland.
address: West Auckland, Bishop Auckland DL14, UK

title: Coach
location_name: West Kennett (Wiltshire) - Road, exact area unknown
notes: Pulled by a team of four horses, a spectral coach haunts the area.

title: Headless Driver
location_name: West Lulworth (Dorset) - Lane along Coach Lane Gates (gates may no longer stand)
notes: A ghostly coach pulled by horses and driven by a headless coachman reportedly travels along this lane. The coachman was said to have been decapitated during a highway robbery.

title: Pitch Forked Dog
notes: A farmhand discovered a dog laying in the hay - he poked it with his pitchfork, causing the entity to vanish. The sound of a coach has also been heard as it stops by the front of the house, though nothing is seen.

title: Mad Maude
location_name: Weston on the Green (Oxfordshire) - Weston Manor Hotel
notes: Burnt at the stake for having an affair with a monk, Maude wanders the hotel, which was once a convent. The eerie sounds of an invisible horse and coach moving around the courtyard have been reported by more than one visitor.
address: Northampton Rd, Weston-on-the-Green, Bicester OX25 3QL, UK

title: Old Soldiers
notes: The spirits here reportedly include voices that call out to witnesses over the nearby River Carey, and horsemen who gallop silently along. People have also reported pockets of cold air, and watching a white woman glide over the marsh, near where her lover was murdered by Royalists. On the anniversary of the battle, sounds of fighting have been heard.

title: Coach and Four
notes: It was once said that a ghostly coach pulled by four horses and driven by a headless figure would pass through the village and vanish into the church wall.

title: Officer on Horseback
notes: Two hunters reported seeing a locally known Captain riding his horse over the warren, even though several months had passed since the officer's death.

title: Coach
notes: This ghostly coach is pulled by a team of six grey horses.

title: Horse?
notes: While staying in a house along this road, a witness heard a horse walking outside on the gravel - when they looked from the window, nothing could be seen.

title: Sir Laurence Tanfield
notes: A former landowner in the area, Sir Laurence returned after dying and can be seen driving a coach pulled by four black horses. It is said that bad luck falls on any witnesses.

title: Coach and Horses
notes: After a spectral horse and carriage stops at the top of the driveway, a woman climbs out and walks to the hall's door to be met by a long dead butler. A similar story says that Anne Boleyn travels in a coach along the roads surrounding the Hall around the same time of year.

title: Horse
notes: A driver spotted a white horse crossing the road ahead and commented to her passenger, who said she could not see anything. As the driver reached the spot the horse crossed, she realised there was nowhere the horse could have gone.

title: Coffin
location_name: Wimborne (Dorset) - B3078, close to Clapgate Junction
notes: A coffin has been spotted on the roadside here, though it disappears if looked at closely. Some believe it belongs to someone who committed suicide, denying them the right to be buried in a churchyard, being buried close to this spot instead. A phantom black horse is also said to haunt this area.

title: Sounds of Assault
notes: The sounds of several miners attacking and murdering Clara and Allan, a young eloping couple, can be heard in this Derbyshire area. One version of the story the miners died or went mad one-by-one, with the final miner confessing to the crime and claiming the ghost of Clara followed him everywhere. Clara may also be seen riding her horse through the area
address: Winnats Pass, Hope Valley S33 8WA, UK

title: Coach
location_name: Winsley (Wiltshire) - Converted coach house at Winsley Manor
notes: It was said that horse and carriage could sometimes be heard outside this coach house, even though they could never be seen.
address: Winsley Mnr, Winsley, Bradford-on-Avon BA15, UK

title: Horse and Cart
notes: This spectral horse and cart continue to retrace the final journey which abruptly ended when the creature fell into the pond and drowned.

title: Coach and Horses
notes: Appearing at midnight on Christmas Eve, a phantom coach pulled by four ghostly horses gallop along this road. Another story (or a variant of the first) states a spectral horse would charge along the road dragging chains which bounced along the ground.

title: Headless Horseman
location_name: Woodhead (Derbyshire) - Area where Crowden Hall once stood (demolished 1937)
notes: This decapitated figure on the back of a stallion still rides around the area where Crowden Hall once stood.

title: Headless Horses
location_name: Woodmanton (Wiltshire) - Location known as Patty's Bottom
notes: Rumoured to be a battleground between Britons and Romans, the sounds of battle, accompanied by the site of headless horses, have been reported. The battle was said to be so intense, the valley filled with blood.

title: Coach
location_name: Wool (Dorset) - Hethfelton House and/or stone bridge close to Bindon Abbey
notes: This horse drawn coach drawn by four horses drives by the house, heading towards the River Frome (or Bindon Abbey passing over the fifteenth century stone bridge over the river. Only those who have Turbeville blood can view the phantom.

title: Woman
notes: This phantom woman wearing a flowing grey dress is said to be quite beautiful and walks from the ruins to the nearby River Frome.
address: MQJR+GP, Wareham, Wool, Wareham BH20 6BN, UK

title: Guarded Secret Tunnel
notes: Leading to an untold amount of wealth, the tunnel hidden in this hillfort is guarded by a pair of snake-like dragons. The ghost of a druid is reported to haunt the site looking for this treasure, and the sound of phantom galloping horses have also been reported. Finally, another legend says the structure was formed by earth falling off the Devil's shovel as he tried to dig a hole in the South Downs.
address: National Trust - Cissbury Ring, Findon, Valley, Worthing BN14 0HT, UK

title: Galloping
location_name: Wrington (Somerset) - Lane through woodland between village and Cleeve
notes: Reports slightly differed regarding this phantom horse. Although never seen, the creature would either be heard galloping, or a slower steady clop like a shire horse pulling a heavy load.

title: Phantom Coach
notes: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.

title: Horsemen
location_name: Wyke (West Yorkshire) - A58/A641 junction, also known as Hell Fire Corner
notes: Both ghostly horsemen and a phantom car have reported around this junction. The area is considered an accident black spot, begging the question whether ghosts cause the accidents or are they the products?

title: Screaming Horses
notes: Locals say that the horses belonged to a Roman chariot; the driver lost control in the ice and snow, and the entire team fell into a deep ditch and died.

title: Nobleman
notes: Having lost an illegal duel, this figure died; his ghost now staggers around the area, fooling people into believing he is still alive prior to vanishing. A man on horseback also appears in the park and is named as Dick Turpin.
address: St George's Fld, York YO10, UK

title: Bathing Mermaid
notes: Twelve people watched a beautiful pale woman washing herself in the sea, with what appeared to be a black tail splashing around behind her.

title: Shape Changers
notes: The mermaids said to live in this area would be capable of changing their shape as they came on to land. They would also be seen sitting on the rocks playing harps.

title: Women with Child
location_name: Barra (Outer Hebrides) - Reef in Caolas Cumhan
notes: Crofter Colin Campbell raised his rifle to fire at what he thought was an otter eating a fish but stopped when he realised the creature was a mermaid holding a child. The creature dived into the sea after Campbell made a noise.
address: Caolas, Isle of Barra HS9 5YL, UK

title: Swan Eater
notes: An unknown large creature was seen to attack several swans from beneath the water of the lake - it is believed to be a type of large catfish, which can grow up to sixteen feet in length. There has also been at least one mermaid sighting in the lake, and Sir Lancelot's body is supposed to be buried nearby.
address: Martin Mere, Burscough, Ormskirk L40 0TA, UK

title: Mer Children
location_name: Calf of Man (Isle of Man) - Rocky area, exact location not known
notes: Three men from Douglas found two merchildren on the rocks. One was already dead, but they saved the second child and took it to their town. The creature was described as around 60.3 centimetres in length and brown in colour, although the scales around its tail were slightly violet, and the hair on its head light green.
address: Calf of Man, Isle of Man

title: Shorted Armed Mermaid
notes: Watched by a farmer for two hours, this mermaid had a pale upper body and a red/grey tail covered in hair. Its face was human, with deep-set eyes and short neck. The creature combed the hair on its head with what looked like short, stubby arms before diving into the water and washing its torso.

title: Teenager
location_name: Castlemartin (Dyfed) - Off the coast in the area
notes: Henry Reynolds spotted a merman off the coast which he initially believed to be a pale skinned teenager before noticing its brown tail which waved about. While the body and arms looked human, the arms and hands looked too short and thick, while the creature's nose was long and sharp. Reynolds went to find other witnesses, but by the time he returned, the merman had gone.

title: Mermaid
notes: This mermaid was so upset at the way she was treated by onlookers, she drove to the bottom of the pool and never returned. It was thought that she guarded a mass of treasure.
address: Child's Ercall, Market Drayton TF9, UK

title: Mermaid Curse
notes: The mistress of the castle ordered the destruction of a black rock which stood just a short distance away, to prevent a mermaid sitting upon it and singing. As revenge, the mermaid cursed the family, ensuring they all died without leaving an heir for the estate.

title: Macphie's Captor
location_name: Colonsay (Argyll and Bute) - Unknown cave along the coast
notes: A mermaid kidnapped a man by the name of Macphie and held him in a cave along the island's shore. Macphie was given any item he wished for but remained unhappy until he managed to escape. The mermaid gave chase, but Macphie tossed his dog into the sea. Dog and mermaid fought until they both died from their injuries.

title: Llys Helig
notes: Llys Helig was a mythical palace, which was sunk to punish its evil owner. The ruins of the walls can sometimes be seen at low tide, though in recent years it has been discovered that the rocks are a natural formation. Mermaids have also been seen in these waters, and one was said to have cursed the town of Conway after she was washed up on the beach and no one offered to help her back into the sea.

title: Mermaids
notes: These women of the water were seen dozens of times during the last twenty years of the nineteenth century. One was said to have been shot, but the body was not recovered.

title: Treasure Guardians
location_name: Duncansby Head (Highland) - Cave system off coast
notes: A team of mermaids and mermen are reported to guard a hidden cave full of treasure.

title: Captured Fisherman
notes: A good looking fisherman once caught the eye of a mermaid who fell in love with him - she kidnapped the fellow and held him in a secret cave under the sea, where he remains to this day...

title: Wish Granter
notes: A local man caught a mermaid on the rocks, only releasing the creature after she agreed to grant three wishes. One of the wishes ensured the man became a skilled doctor, another granted the gift of prophecy, but the third wish, to become a great singer, apparently failed.
address: Eilean-Anabuich, Isle of Harris HS3 3AG, UK

title: Vanishing Twitchers
notes: Two birdwatchers that vanished without trace on the island after arriving with 86 other people were thought by locals to have been taken by merfolk. An extensive search by the coastguard could find no trace of the missing people.

title: Well Dweller
location_name: Fornham All Saints (Suffolk) - Well in the village (exact location unknown)
notes: An old story, most likely told to prevent children playing too close to the well, said a mermaid waited in the water at the bottom. She was ready to drown children who touched the water's edge.
address: Fornham All Saints, Bury Saint Edmunds IP28 6LA, UK

title: Mermaid
location_name: Hayfield (Derbyshire) - Mermaid's Pool, near Kinder Reservoir
notes: Anyone who sees the creature is said to gain immortality. One man who came here once a year lived to be 104 (so the story goes)!

title: Sea Folk
notes: The area around the island was said to be home to many mermaids and kelpies.

title: White Figure
location_name: Isle of Canna (Highland) - Sea, about quarter of the way between island and Skye
notes: Neil McIntosh, steering a boat between the two islands in stormy weather, glimpsed a white human figure in the sea. McIntosh thought the creature to be a mermaid, although admitted he did not have much of an opportunity to examine it.

title: Mermaid
location_name: Isle of Sanday (Highland) - Beach on north end of island
notes: A mermaid perched on a rock threw herself into the sea when approached by two local men walking along the beach. After the mermaid entered the sea, the witnesses could see the creature's upper body resembled a woman.

title: Hybrid
location_name: Isle of Sanday (Highland) - Exact area not known
notes: Folklorist Walter Traill Dennison recorded in 1893 that there were decedents of a union between a male selkie and a woman living on the island - you could tell who the offspring were because they had webbed hands and feet.

title: Halibut-Tailed creature
location_name: Isle of Yell (Shetland) - Thirty miles off the coast
notes: Three (or six) fishermen claimed to have caught a ninety centimetre long mermaid while at sea. The creature had arms around 23 centimetres in length with webbed fingers, and while it had blue eyes and nostrils, no ears or chin could be seen. It possessed a tail similar to a halibut and had two fins on its shoulders. After listening to the creature wail for three hours, the fishermen threw it back into the sea.

title: Mermaid?
notes: A mermaid which was spotted several times in this area was described as a 'normal' woman, leading one to conclude that maybe she was..?
address: Kilconly North, Kilconly, Co. Kerry, Ireland

title: Bloodied mermaid
location_name: Killone Lake (County Clare) - Waters of the lake
datetime: Every forty years, exact date unknown
notes: A mermaid who frequented the lake would often steal wine from the crypt beneath the church (or the cellar of Newhall, depending on the storyteller). She was stabbed (or shot) when caught but managed to drag herself back to the lake before dying. The waters are said to turn red with her blood every forty years.

title: Mermaid
notes: A report exists of a seven foot long mer-creature being seen here, bathing on the rocks.

title: Sailor
notes: Leaving no footprints as he crosses the sand, this bearded sailor is one of the many who lost their lives in the sea nearby. He is particularly noted for the large brass buttons on his jacket and for shouting 'All on this beach is mine!' at some visitors during the 1940s. A red-headed mermaid has also been reported in the area.

title: Killer Mermaid
notes: The fish woman surfaces to comb her hair at midnight - anyone approaching too close are dragged down into the deep waters with her. One legend says that the mermaid last appeared in the nineteenth century to prevent her pool being drained, threatening to flood the nearby town of Leek if people proceeded to dig away at her home.

title: Singing
notes: Although this creature has not been seen, it can be heard singing - a ship will sink nearby exactly seven days later.

title: Seaton
notes: Upset with the residents of the village Seaton, a mermaid caused all the buildings to be buried by sand.

title: Angry Mermaid
location_name: Lornty (Perth and Kinross) - Waters between town and Loch Benachally
notes: These waters were once the swimming area of a mermaid renowned for her bad temperament.

title: Abducted Woman
location_name: Lough Sheelin (County Meath) - Waters of the lough
notes: A farmer spotted a beautiful woman leave the lough and forced her to return to his farmhouse. Eventually she agreed to stay with him, on the condition that she never glazed upon the lake again. Twenty years passed and the farmer and the mermaid had several children. While waiting for the farmer to return from the fields on a clear day, the mermaid spotted the lough in the distance and fled towards it, never to be seen again.

title: Thieving Mermaid
notes: An accident sent a church bell into the waters of the local river - a passing mermaid took the opportunity to steal the bell and can sometimes be heard ringing it. As a sidenote, a bell was recovered from the water in 1848.

title: Mermaid
notes: A sailor once fell in love with a mermaid here. The woman gave him a golden ring, informing the sailor that she would be back in five days to meet him. Five days later, the man died.

title: Mermaid
location_name: Morridge Moor (Staffordshire) - The Black Mere, also known as the Blake Mere
notes: This fishy female comes to the surface of the pool at night, ready to pull any witnesses down to the dark waters where they remain forever.

title: Upset Mermaid
notes: The last time this area of water was cleaned, a mermaid popped up out of the water and threatened to destroy Newport and Meretown if the pool was emptied.

title: Therianthropy
location_name: North Ronaldsay (Orkney) - Exact location not known
notes: The Goodman of Wastness, a farmer by trade, stole a selkie's seal skin and hid it, forcing the woman to become his wife. After many years of marriage and a house full of children the selkie found her seal skin and ran off back to the sea, never to be seen again.

title: Mermaid's Grave
location_name: Nunton, Benbecula (Outer Hebrides) - Exact location not known
notes: The area reportedly contains the grave of a mermaid killed in the early nineteenth century. The mer-creature had been seen for a couple of days, before a teenage boy threw a rock at it, killing the entity. Accounts stated that the upper part of the creature was the size of an infant, while the bottom resembled a salmon.
address: Baile nan Cailleach, Isle of Benbecula HS7 5LU, UK

title: Mermaid's Stone
location_name: Old Cambus (Borders) - Road leading to Church of St Helen
notes: The concave nature of a stone on the road leading to the church was attributed to the foot of a mermaid (an untraditional untailed one) escaping from her jailor.

title: Merman
location_name: Orford (Suffolk) - Coast near to Orford Castle
notes: Fishermen caught a naked, hairy man just off the coast. The man did not speak, even when hung upside down and tortured. His diet considered of fish, which if given to him raw, he squeezed with his hands and drank only the liquid. He was held at the castle for a while (some sources say six months) before being allowed to swim in the sea, albeit guarded in a netted enclosure. The wild man managed to escape the enclosure and played in the waves, occasionally disappearing under the sea. Just as the guards had given up of hope of recapturing him, the wild man returned and was taken back to the castle. As he had returned voluntarily, the wild man was not guarded as intensely as previous and stayed at the castle for another couple of months before once again escaping into the sea, but this time never returning.
address: Orford Road, Orford, Woodbridge IP12 2ND, UK

title: Angry Mermaid
notes: A local tale states that the harbour here was silted up by a mermaid, more than a little upset after she was shot at by a fisherman. The area has also been home to sea serpents.
address: Harbor, West Quay, Padstow PL28 8AQ, UK

title: Selkie Prince
location_name: Papa Stour (Shetland) - Exact location not known
notes: A group of hunters who arrived at the island killed and skinned many seals before a violent storm began to batter the island - unbeknown to the hunters, a selkie prince was amongst the seals. The hunters finally returned the skins to the remaining seals, after which the storm ceased.

title: No Mermaid Here
location_name: Peel (Isle of Man) - Sea off Peel
notes: A Canadian newspaper reported that the Mayor of Peel and others had seen a mermaid off the coast and were offering a reward for the creature's capture. The story turned out to be a fake.
address: Peel, Isle of Man IM5, Isle of Man

title: Mermaids
location_name: Perranuthnoe (Cornwall) - Cudden Point, off the coast
notes: This area is another haunt for the Cornish merfolk.
address: National Trust - Cudden Point, St Hilary, Penzance TR20 9BB, UK

title: Merman
notes: This figure normally warned of ill fortune at sea - if the crew of a fishing vessel spotted him, they would return to port for the day. Two fishermen spotted two of the creatures in 1814 - one they described as black in colour, with curly hair and long arms, while the other was female with long hair.

title: Mermaid
notes: One story says that a mermaid came ashore here but died soon after being discovered by local people heading to church.
address: Pennsylvania Rd, Isle of Portland, Portland DT5 1HT, UK

title: Rake
notes: This freshwater mermaid was reputed to be armed with a rake, ready to drag in any children who strayed too close.

title: Mermaid
location_name: Rinns of Islay (Argyll and Bute) - Small island off Rinns of Islay
notes: There are several entities said to haunt this small island, including a mermaid, the sound of rattling chains, and water horses. A woman supervising cattle on the island was attacked by something tall and hairy, with no skin upon its face. She managed to survive by throwing boiling water over the entity and creating a magic circle, remaining inside it.
address: Rinns of Islay, Isle of Islay PA47 7SZ, UK

title: Gipping Girl
notes: The mermaid that frequented the Gipping would prey on children who played near the deeper waters of the river.

title: Mermaid
notes: Her reasons unknown, this mermaid returns once a year to ring a bell hidden deep underwater and sing.

title: Rothesay
location_name: Rothesay (Argyll and Bute) - Shoreline within a mile of town
notes: Three men spotted a mermaid carefully combing her long black hair along the shoreline. One of the witnesses also reported a large sea monster nearby, which had the body of a man 'but with the head of a brute'.
address: Rothesay, Isle of Bute PA20, UK

title: Bench End
notes: A carved wooden bench arm is in the shape of a mermaid. A notice in the church said that, in days of olde, a mermaid tried to enter but was turned away by a mortified crowd. She later snuck in undetected and remains to this day.

title: Cursed
notes: A fisherman who caught a mermaid while out at sea killed the creature with a knife. It was said that from that moment, he was cursed.

title: Noggle
notes: The Noggle was a type of water horse that could be found stopping water wheels. When a miller appeared to chase the beast off, he would often be tricked into mounting it - being taken to deep water and his doom.

title: A Sea Man
notes: A man was caught in the sea by fishermen and kept for many weeks in a cottage where he lived on a diet of fish. After a while, the 'sea man' returned to the sea, waving goodbye to people standing on the coast before diving down and never returning.

title: Dark Haired Mermaid
notes: Little is known about the sighting of a mermaid with long dark hair once spotted here.

title: Glassy Eyed Mermaid
location_name: Sound of Mull (Argyll and Bute) - Sea
notes: Becalmed in their skiff after setting out from the Sound of Mull, two men spotted a creature in the water which had the head, neck and upper torso of a woman, although the hair was coarser and the eyes glassy.

title: Blocked Door
notes: Having just dropped anchor in the bay, the captain of a ship was surprised when a mermaid stuck her head out of the sea and asked if the anchor could be moved, as it had landed on the entrance to her home. The captain was happy to comply.

title: Naked Mermaid?
notes: William Munro, a schoolmaster from Thurso, watched a naked woman combing her hair on a rock in the bay. After a few minutes she dived under the water and vanished.

title: Woman in the Sea
location_name: Uist (Outer Hebrides) - Sea south of islands, heading towards Skye
notes: Caught in a storm at sea, Lachlan McArthur watched a creature resembling 'a woman in upper parts' rise from the water, leaving him terrified.
address: Uist, Isle of Benbecula HS7 5QA, UK

title: Saxi and Herman
notes: Saxi and Herman, two giants who were brothers, once lived on the island. They both fell in love with a mermaid who said she would marry whoever would swim to the North Pole with her. Both brothers began the trip, but neither returned.

title: Mathew Trewhella
notes: A son of a church warden, Mathew Trewhella one day disappeared without trace. A sea captain later told the village folk that Mathew was with a mermaid, living under the sea.

title: Bushy Bearded Man
notes: This cloaked, bearded gentleman was seen lurking around the attic and one of the bathrooms. A few unearthly notes from a non-existent music box have been heard in the bar area.

title: Blind George
location_name: Anstey (Hertfordshire) - Fields around the village, and Devil's Hole cave
notes: Said to still be playing his fiddle in tunnels under the village, Blind George ventured into the Devil's Hole cave with his trusted dog - the hound ran out, and was found with its hair burnt away, and George was never seen again.

title: Lost Piper
notes: Emanating from deep underneath the farmhouse, piping can be heard - a musician named Tam once hid in a nearby cave complex, found himself lost, and is still looking for a way out.

title: Piper
notes: What is it with inquisitive pipers and tunnels? Playing his pipe while investigating this secret passageway, a man vanished without trace (though, of course, he is now heard).
address: Gight, Castle Car Park, Ellon AB41 7JA, UK

title: Loud Heart Beats
notes: A family fled the building after being subjected to strange sounds, including deafening heart beats and mournful music played by an unplugged organ, and furniture which would move itself around. When a priest tried to perform an exorcism, his holy water threw itself on him. After the family left the property, they asked the council to rehouse them and were refused.

title: Bagpipes
location_name: Athgreany (County Wicklow) - Stone circle, aka Piper's Stones
notes: Played by the fairy folk, the sound of bagpipes could once be heard emerging from the stones. A tree in the centre of the stone circle is considered to belong to the fairies.

title: Organ Plays
notes: Several times, late at night, the church organ was heard to burst into tune, stopping as investigators reach the churchyard.

title: Piper Piping
location_name: Ballintra (County Donegal) - Cave system known as Pullans
notes: The sound of pipes echoes throughout this cave structure on certain nights, as the ghost of a piper tries to find his way out.
address: Lismintan Or Ballyruddelly, Ballintra, Co. Donegal, Ireland

title: Band
notes: On two different occasions, villagers heard drums, brass, and a cornet being played in the village hall at midnight, although the building would be locked and unlit.

title: Scottish Piper
notes: The ghostly Scot plays his pipes while pacing up and down one of the ruined battlements.

title: Talking
notes: While checking out this site, investigators heard a piano and children playing in an empty area.

title: Pianist
notes: The owners of this pub heard the piano playing in the back room as they were tidying up. When investigated, no one could be found, and the piano keyboard remained covered. The event occurred a day after two other nearby pubs reported poltergeist-like incidents.

title: Lady in Red
datetime: Unknown, singing heard on Easter Sunday (reoccurring)
notes: This spectre stands on the top of a tower, illuminated by bright moonlight, as if waiting for someone. A child haunts the bridge - the young boy disappears when he reaches the halfway point. More stories tell of phantom singing, music, and words spoken in an unknown tongue.

title: Moving Coffins
notes: Music, crashes, thumps, bell ringing and footsteps have all been reported emerging from the church after it has been locked up for the night. A local tale involves the coffins in the Waldegrave family crypt mysteriously moving on their own accord between the times the tomb was opened.

title: Phantom Organ Music
notes: A witness reported hearing the organ playing, though they were alone in the church at the time.
address: St. Margaret's Church Old, Church Rd, Bowers Gifford, Benfleet SS13 2HG, UK

title: Woman's Voice
notes: A couple awoke to hear their baby gripe in the adjoining room. Before they could climb out of bed, they heard a strange woman's voice say 'hello' in a maternal fashion, followed by a 'Shhh...'. Bounding into the baby's room to challenge the woman, all that could be seen was the sleeping child. A couple of days later, toys could be heard playing tunes in the loft, although the music promptly ceased when the loft hatch was opened.
address: Bradfield St George, Bury Saint Edmunds IP30 0BG, UK

title: Tinkling the Ivory
location_name: Bradford on Avon (Wiltshire) - Fat Fowl restaurant
notes: The piano in the restaurant is said to occasionally play a note or two under its own accord, while cutlery is moved, and doors close by unseen hands.

title: Secret Door
location_name: Brecon Beacons (Powys) - Llyn Cwm Llwch (somewhere around the area)
notes: Once a year, a secret door opens here which will take mortals to the fairy realm. The door was once always open, but after a man stole a fairy flower, access was restricted. The area is also home to an old woman who uses music to lure the weak willed into the lake waters where they drown - once she has killed nine hundred people, she will regain her youth and become mortal.

title: Music
location_name: Breedon on the Hill (Leicestershire) - Near the church
notes: A couple sitting close to the church heard loud music, sounding like an ice cream van tune, although nothing was in sight. When the music started a second time, they left the area.
address: Breedon on the Hill, Derby DE73, UK

title: Music in the Air
notes: Fairy music was once not uncommon in this area. While last heard in 1939 by a group of school children, one hundred years previous it was said a young girl danced herself to death to the tunes.

title: Victorians
notes: While walking along the seafront, two sisters watched Victorian figures walking around and listened to the sound of music and laughter. The seafront was lit up as if daytime, and a young man and a boy walked out to sea along a rope bridge. As the sisters walked away, they realised the scene they had watched was impossible, and turning back they saw only the darkness over the sea. One sister later discovered that there was a chain bridge on the spot which led to the pier, but it was destroyed in 1896.
address: Brighton Beach, 4 Madeira Dr, Brighton BN2 1ET, UK

title: Piano
notes: A former member of staff reported that they and other staff have heard a piano being played when the club is quiet and no one else around.

title: Piano
notes: A friend of the owner was awoken to see the owner's deceased husband standing over the bed. The site was also home to phantom piano music and poltergeist activity in a bedroom.
address: Tidy St, Brighton and Hove, Brighton BN1, UK

title: Fairground Organ
location_name: Bristol (Somerset) - Gardiner Haskins store (former soap factory)
notes: A few spooky events were documented in the local press, including the music from a fairground organ, a disembodied male voice, and the fleeting sighting of a Victorian woman dressed in white. A poltergeist was blamed for moving pots of paint around the site.

title: Piano Man
notes: A piano in a lecture theatre here was seen to play itself and has been also heard several times since.

title: Pam the Fiddler
notes: Pam, the local schoolmaster, was killed by the village vicar after refusing to stop fiddling around the area after dark. The vicar covered up the crime by secretly burying Pam's body; within a few weeks villagers began to report hearing Pam playing the fiddle, and some claimed to have seen him, though his head had changed into that of a goat.

title: Pharmacist
location_name: Bury St Edmunds (Suffolk) - British Gas Showroom (was next door to Cupola House)
notes: Phantom music could be heard coming from the basement where a pharmacist had taken his own life in 1913. A broom had also been spotted moving unaided. The showroom briefly opened the basement to the public in 1993 to raise money for Comic Relief.
address: Bury St Edmunds, Bury Saint Edmunds, UK

title: Music
notes: Fairies could once be heard making music under this artificial hill.

title: Famh
notes: This author is not sure if this is a joke, but... the Famh is said to be a strange dog/mole hybrid (Dole? Mog?), which possesses a vicious set of teeth and a disproportionately sized mouth. It lurks in the mountain range, shying away from human contact. The area is also haunted by a piper, who is seen standing on the tallest peaks, and is heard playing at night.
address: Cairngorm Ski Area, Aviemore PH22 1RB, UK

title: Lady in White
notes: A young lady dressed completely in white is said to haunt this building. The ghost would descend the main staircase. Phantom music has also been reported in the house.

title: Piping
notes: Something strange lured a piper into this cave from which he never returned. Hundreds of years later, the piper could still be heard playing his instrument deep below the earth.

title: Rustling
location_name: Castle Combe (Wiltshire) - Salutation Inn (was The Gibb Inn)
