notes: One of several ghosts reported here, Lady Constance killed herself after watching her husband die when a stag gored him. Her pale white shade now frequents the grounds. Lady Limerick reported seeing the ghostly Edward, the Black Prince during the First World War, and he popped up again in 1931. The spirit of a servant haunts the loft, while a poltergeist outbreak in 1950 resulted in furniture being tossed around.
address: Hall Place, Bourne Rd, Dartford, bexley DA5 1PQ, UK

title: Queen Elizabeth
location_name: Outer London: Richmond (Greater London) - Richmond Palace
notes: As she lay dying elsewhere, Elizabeth was observed standing at a window of the palace. A few reports say she still haunts the building. The palace may have been the location where Edward Seymour, Lord Protector of England, and Anne Seymour witnessed a 'hand with a bloodied sword' emerge from a wall. Edward was beheaded a short time later. There are also more recent reports of phantom horses' hooves heard clopping around the courtyard.

title: Walter & Liz
location_name: Outer London: Wallington (Greater London) - Path between Croydon Road and London Road, Beddington Park
notes: Local legend has the ghosts of Elizabeth I and Sir Walter Raleigh frequenting this area together, dating from when the latter lived at nearby Carew Manor.

title: Queen Margaret of Anjou
notes: Last seen by a group of children during the Second World War (who mistook the phantom for someone in fancy dress), the wife of Henry VI pines for her dead son Edward. More recently, poltergeist like effects and the sounds of phantom footsteps have been reported, as have the sounds of phantom children.
address: Owlpen Manor, Uley, Dursley GL11 5BZ, UK

title: Jane Seymour
notes: The Seymour family once owned this property, and Jane is thought to haunt the surrounding grounds. The shade of Anne Boleyn has also been reported loitering near a row of nearby yew trees. Finally, the hall is reputedly the location where the Mistletoe Bough bride story originated, with the chest still (?) being present today.

title: Charles I
notes: The former king has been observed around the college grounds both with and without his head. A shade belonging to a former royalist commander also lurks here - he was shot for treason on the site.

title: Phantom Coach
notes: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.
address: Oxnead Bridge, Brampton, Norwich NR10 5HN, UK

title: Red Water
notes: The waters of the loch are said to turn red just prior to news that will greatly affect the royal family.

title: Lost Princess
notes: This wandering shade, thought to be Princess Nest, known as the Helen of Wales, travels around the castle grounds as if looking for something. Another legend says that the great hall is haunted by a former owner and his pet ape, both of whom were found dead and on fire.
address: Castle Ln, Carew, Tenby SA70 8SL, UK

title: Messy Bed
location_name: Pencaitland (Lothian) - Penkaet (or Pencaet, or Pencait) Castle, also known as Fountainhall House
notes: The ghost of Charles I is blamed for disrupting the bedding in the room that contains his death mask. John Cockburn, who committed murder here, can be heard dragging the body along various parts of the building. Finally, the castle is also haunted by a beggar, falsely accused of using witchcraft against another former owner.
address: Fountainhall House, Pencaitland, Tranent EH34 5EY, UK

title: Nell
location_name: Pewsey (Wiltshire) - The Phoenix Inn (original building no longer stands)
notes: The king's mistress would stay at the inn when the King travelled and resided in a nearby manor house. Her spirit remained until the inn was demolished.

title: Eleanor of Aquitaine
location_name: Quarr Hill (Isle of Wight) - Quarr Abbey
notes: The wife of Henry II is reported to haunt these ruins, appearing as if she regretted plotting against her husband. However, there is no evidence that Eleanor of Aquitaine visited the area in her lifetime.

title: Boadicea
location_name: Quidenham (Norfolk) - Low mound, around 300 metres from the church, known as Viking's Mound
notes: This site has been named as another contender for the final resting place of Queen Boudicca.

title: Elizabeth Hesketh
notes: The lady now manifests herself as a grey shade, waiting for her husband to return from a war with Scotland. She may have last been seen by a curator as he sat playing the piano. A shade resembling Queen Elizabeth has also been reported, though her link with the hall is unknown. Ghostly music is also reputed to occasionally be heard.
address: 200 Liverpool Rd, Rufford, Ormskirk L40 1SG, UK

title: Hooded Figure
notes: This figure, covered so no flesh is visible, is sometimes seen darting between the trees. It could be a lover of Nell Gwynn, who the king had murdered.

title: Nellie & Charlie
notes: The figures of Nell Gwynn and King Charles II have been seen on the property, though never together.
address: Salcey Lawn, Hartwell, Northampton NN7 2HA, UK

title: White Lady
notes: Some people believe that this phantom figure is Margaret Radclyffe, Queen Elizabeth's favourite Maid of Honour. She died of a broken heart in 1559 shortly after the death of her brother.

title: Anne Boleyn
notes: The former queen sometimes returns to this church, where local legend says she was laid to rest after her body was clandestinely removed from the Tower of London.

title: Queen Maude
notes: The queen is said to walk around the depression in the land that bears her name. Some others name her Queen Mab or Empress Maude.

title: Queen Mary, and Girls Laughter
notes: Queen Mary rebuilt the Hall after it was burnt down by troops searching for the monarch. Seen in the room in which she slept, the Queen's ghost appears to always smile, and wears the same dress which appears in her portrait located elsewhere in the building. An explanation for the laughter which sometimes echoes through the Hall has never been discovered, although another royal, Lady Jane Grey, is still seen in her former bedroom and walking down corridors - it is said this was the only place that she felt happy.
address: Sawston Hall, Church Ln, Sawston, Cambridge CB22 3JR, UK

title: Footsteps
location_name: Scone (Perth and Kinross) - Scone Palace, South Passage
notes: The ghost here is said to constantly walk up and down the passage, always at the same constant speed. One witness reported feeling something hit her arm three times while passing along the corridor.

title: Anne Boleyn
location_name: SE1 (Greater London) - Lambeth Palace, & River Thames near the building
notes: Carried in a Thames barge crewed by faded grey figures, the shade of Boleyn is taken to the Tower of London where she met her maker. The palace itself sometimes echoes with Anne's voice, pleading and crying for her life to be saved.

title: Bodiless Hand
location_name: SE10 (Greater London) - Greenwich Palace (currently Royal Naval property), & the Queen's House - Tulip Staircase
notes: The phantom hand photographed on the Tulip Staircase is one of the most frequent images to crop up in books about ghosts and hauntings, though recently the effect has been duplicated with a long exposure time. The other ghost in the former palace resembles Queen Elizabeth, complete with period costume and red hair. The Queen Anne block is haunted by Admiral Byng, executed for treason in the 1700s.

title: Monk
notes: When this man died, he was the only one to know the true location of the Abbey's hidden treasure. He now returns to ensure it remains hidden. It is worth noting that the ghost 'walks' on his knees - one assumes this is because of a change in ground level. Several people claimed to have seen a phantom man running around the ruins at night; some say that this was the ghost of King Edward.
address: Shaftesbury Abbey, Museum and Garden Trust, Park Walk, Shaftesbury SP7 8JR, UK

title: Mary Queen of Scots
location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - Manor Lodge (aka Manor Castle)
notes: Folklore says that Mary, wearing a long black dress, appears in the Turret House and glides along corridors and passes through walls. In 2015, a visitor to the site watched a glowing white figure of a woman wearing a long dress run towards and vanish behind a building.

title: Mary with her Jailor
notes: Mary Queen of Scots was held here for many years, and on occasion her ghost is seen looking out from a window. Her male jailor is sometimes seen standing guard on the stairwell.

title: Isabella of Valois
location_name: Sonning (Berkshire) - Site of Bishop's Palace (no longer standing)
datetime: Summer evenings (last seen late eighteenth/early nineteen century?)
notes: Wife of Richard II, Queen Isabella' drifts around the field where the palace once stood. As the phantom grey woman moves, ghostly music emerges from the ground. George III's organist lived nearby and may have been the last person to witness the phantom.

title: King Charles I
notes: King Charles I is said to have used this building as a hideout whilst on the run, and his phantom now appears in the King Charles Suite. Another man, sporting a powdered wig, also haunts the building, as does a woman in eighteenth century dress.

title: Mary
notes: The Queen of Scots has put in at least one appearance here, praying at the altar.

title: King Harold's Army
location_name: Stamford Bridge (North Yorkshire) - Area over battlefield
notes: The re-enactment of Harold's 1066 battle has been observed in the skies over the field where much blood was shed.

title: Princess Cicely
location_name: Standen (Isle of Wight) - Great East Standen Lodge
notes: Never seen, but her presence is announced at night when her invisible form is felt sitting on the end of a bed.

title: Man in Highland Dress
notes: A photograph taken in the 1970s shows what could be a spectral figure walking along the cobbled path. Reports of phantom footsteps echoing down corridors have also been reported at the castle, and a green lady has been observed. Not to be forgotten, some also say a woman in a pink silken dress stalks the area outside the fort, and she is named as Mary Queen of Scots.

title: Chained Monk
notes: Said to predate the construction of the palace, this monk appears bound in chains once a year on Christmas Day. The sound of a suicide (the single shot of a pistol) is occasionally heard on the first floor - the private secretary to King Edward VII ended his life here.

title: Misty Shape
notes: A misty shape scared a worker in the building, causing her to flee. The visitation had been preceded by much banging and crashing.

title: Animated Queen Anne
location_name: SW1 (Greater London) - Queen Anne's Gate, Westminster
notes: Once a year the statue of the Queen becomes animated and walks around the neighbourhood. Until recently, the statue was believed to be Bloody Mary, until a clean-up operation revealed an inscription pointing out the error.
address: Queen Anne's Gate, London SW1, UK

title: Madame de Beauclair
notes: Madame de Beauclair was a good friend of the Duchess of Mazarine, and both lived in the palace grounds. They made a pact in which the first to die would appear to the other. The Duchess was first to die, and months passed without her ghost appearing. Suddenly one day the ghost appeared to Madame de Beauclair with the warning that the Madame would be dead by the following day. Sure enough, at one in the morning, Madame de Beauclair died. Both women are said to have been seen since, the Duchess of Mazarine by a servant.
address: St James's Palace, Marlborough Rd, London SW1A 1BQ, UK

title: Queen Elizabeth
notes: Said to be home to several shades, one of the figures that haunts the fort is reportedly Elizabeth I.

title: Ghost Hunt
notes: This building broke the record for having the world's largest ghost hunt, with 700 people looking for the phantom monk, a grey lady, a drummer boy and Mary Queen of Scots, amongst other shades...
address: Castle St, Burton upon Trent, Tutbury DE13 9JF, UK

title: Eleanor of Castille
location_name: W4 (Greater London) - Blackfriar's Priory, Queen Victoria Street
notes: The ruined building houses this former Queen's heart, and it is she who is said to now walk the area.

title: Barbara Villiers
location_name: W5 (Greater London) - Walpole House, Chiswick Mall
notes: Barbara, mistress to King Charles II, was once a beautiful young lady, until she contracted a disease that swelled her body to a huge size - her bitter, misty shade walks the house, acting threateningly towards any who see her.
address: Walpole House, Chiswick Mall, Chiswick, London W4 2PS, UK

title: George II
notes: George II has been seen looking out of a palace window at the weathervane, waiting for a message from Hanover. Another ghost, thought to be Sophia (aunt of Queen Victoria), can be heard operating an old spinning wheel late at night and the final entity takes the form of a man wearing breeches and walks around the courtyard.

title: Blue Lady
notes: Her origin unknown, one story says that the phantom blue lady walked straight through a van while crossing the bridge. The driver was convinced he had hit someone until he spotted the figure continuing to walk unimpeded across the bridge.

title: King's Mistress
notes: Room 3 was reportedly the home of a ghostly mistress of Charles I, who cried herself to death on hearing of his execution. Room 5 appears to be the haunt of two children, who stand by the wash basin.
address: Bistro George @ George Hotel The George Hotel, Wallingford OX10 0BS, UK

title: Mary Queen of Scots
notes: Mary was held here briefly en route to Fotheringhay castle, and after her execution, it is said her shade returned here (as well as many other places throughout the UK).

title: Man in Grey
location_name: WC2 (Greater London) - Drury Lane Theatre Royal
datetime: Prior to a long running show (grey man)
notes: Seeing this ghost, dressed in a long grey coat with a Tricorn hat, is considered a good omen; if it appears either in the morning or afternoon during rehearsals, the performance will go on to be a great success. King Charles II is also supposed to haunt the building (he was a great theatre lover), as does the shade of Joe Grimaldi who helps anyone struggling with their lines. The backstage corridors are home to the wraith of Charles Macklin, a murderer never found guilty, and now walks the scene of his crime. Finally, actors have been known to experience a chill and simultaneously catching a whiff of lavender while on stage, the sensations blamed on a ghost.

title: Anne Boleyn
location_name: WC2 (Greater London) - Durham House (no longer standing) Durham House Street
notes: This building held Anne during her last days, and while the house no longer stands, its basement remains, and it is here the ghost still resides.
address: Durham House St, London WC2N, UK

title: Jailed Mary
notes: Imprisoned by the English, Mary Queen of Scots spent some time at this castle - one story has it that her shade walks around the courtyard. One person walking through the main hall spotted, out of the corner of their eye, a woman in Tudor clothing, sitting and watching the outside world through a bay window.

title: Mary Rose Tudor
notes: This building may contain the ghost of Henry VIII's sister.

title: Coach and Horses
notes: After a spectral horse and carriage stops at the top of the driveway, a woman climbs out and walks to the hall's door to be met by a long dead butler. A similar story says that Anne Boleyn travels in a coach along the roads surrounding the Hall around the same time of year.

title: Anne Boleyn
location_name: Wilmslow (Cheshire) - Bollin Hall (demolished nineteenth century, likely a railway viaduct now stands on the site)
notes: It was claimed that the hall was both the birthplace of Anne Boleyn and where her ghost returned.

title: King Alfred
notes: King Alfred's remains were originally buried at the Old Minster, but the monks claimed Alfred's ghost would walk around at night - the King was moved to the New Minster.

title: Four Royals and a Hunt
notes: Henry VIII, Queen Elizabeth, Charles I & George III have all reportedly been seen here after their natural lives ended. Elizabeth frequents the library, Charles the Canon's House (traditionally walking on the night of 26 June), while George haunts the rooms in which he was bound after being declared mad. Henry VIII can only be heard, moving slowly down the corridors, dragging his bad leg. The great park attached to the castle is home to Herne the Hunter, who together with his devil dogs, stalk the woodlands. In 1906 a guard was investigated after he fired five rounds at a white figure - he claimed the bullets passed straight through the figure. Finally (for the time being), the park is also haunted by the ghost of a Grenadier Guard who killed himself while on duty.

title: Child in Yellow Dress
location_name: Windsor Forest (Berkshire) - Callins Bridge, near Shurlock Row
notes: This young girl with raven hair is reported to lead anyone who follows her to a mass grave of soldiers killed in the Civil War.

title: Mary's Lights
notes: The spirit of Mary Queen of Scots is reported to have taken a liking to the ballroom, while it is in debate whether the spheres of light seen floating around the site after dark are part of the same entity.

title: Phantom Coach
notes: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.

title: Thomas Percy
location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - Holy Trinity Church, Goodramgate
notes: Local folklore says that a decapitated Thomas Percy, the Seventh Earl of Northumberland, fumbles around the churchyard searching for his severed head. Thomas and head separated after upsetting Elizabeth I; the earl attempted and failed to raise an army against his queen and was executed for this error of judgement. More recently, a ghostly figure in a long gown (possibly a nun) has been observed on the site.

title: Lady in Green
location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - Principal's House and the King's Manor, Exhibition Square
notes: The female ghost which haunts the Principal's House is said to be either Anne Boleyn or Catherine Howard, dressed in a green dress and carrying roses. The ghost of a monk frequents the King's Manor. The sounds of wounded men can sometimes be heard here - the building was used as a makeshift hospital during the Civil War.

title: Headless Hound
notes: This headless dog was said to have once belonged to a young giant who ran so fast that he decapitated the dog by pulling too sharply on the leash.

title: Red Eyed Dog
notes: This huge hound possesses eyes that burn as bright as the sun, making the creature easy to spot if he tries to sneak up on you.

title: Dreadful Eyes
notes: A few entities were said to have made their homes here, including a black dog with dreadful eyes, a ghostly horseman, and a wagon containing a coffin pulled by four phantom horses.

title: Running Hound
location_name: Alfriston (Sussex) - Between the Downs and Town Fields
notes: A large black dog is said to run from the Downs to Town Fields - here it stares over a flint wall before retreating the way it came.

title: White Dog
location_name: Alfriston (Sussex) - The White Way, road leading from the town towards Seaford
notes: This creature is reportedly the dog of a young man murdered while out walking during the 1700s - because the pitiful hound sat by the victim's shallow grave, the criminals killed it also. The haunting is said to have ceased after the bodies of dog and owner were discovered when the road was widened.

title: Long Necked Dog
location_name: Algarkirk (Lincolnshire) - Area around the village church
notes: This black dog was described by a witness as being tall and lean, with a long neck and a protruding muzzle. The hound is not regarded here as an ill omen.

title: Dog in Trees
notes: This large black hound was seen between three trees that grow close to the religious house.

title: Hound
location_name: Allington (Wiltshire) - Area between village and All Cannings
notes: The story of the black dog was used to frighten children in this area. One man may have been chased by the creature (though it was dark and stated it might have been a pony), while another couple who spotted the hound said it stood almost as tall as their pony.

title: Grey Greyhound
notes: After seeing a creature in his chicken coop, a farmer fired a shotgun at the creature - it ran through the wire netting and escaped. Examining the scene in the morning, the farmer could find no holes in the netting or footprints of the dog.

title: Black Dog
notes: Charles Walton (the man murdered in Lower Quinton (see Quinton database entry for further information)) claimed to have seen a phantom black dog for several successive nights when he was younger - on the last night, instead of the dog, he saw a ghostly headless woman. He soon heard that his sister had died at roughly the same time as the last sighting.

title: White Dog
notes: Murdered alongside his peddler master, this glowing white hound was said to haunt the lanes close to Alveston.

title: Greyhound
location_name: Amlwch (Gwynedd) - Stone circle between village & St Ellian Church
notes: A phantom hound was said to haunt this prehistoric site - it beat up a local preacher a couple of times before moving on to a further plane of existence.

title: Barguest
notes: Possessing glowing red eyes and a yellow tint to its fur, the dog-like barguest is a harbinger of doom.
address: Troller's Gill, Appletreewick, Harrogate BD23 6DE, UK

title: Death Prediction
location_name: Ardura, Isle of Mull (Argyll and Bute) - Area around Lochbuie House
notes: The black dog around this area would warn of impending death. One version of the story says that dog carried a puppy on the back of its head. In 1909, Dr MacDonald spotted the dog while treating chief Murdoch Gillian MacLaine - his patient died soon after.
address: Lochbuie House, Lochbuie, Isle of Mull PA62 6AA, UK

title: Shaggy Dog
notes: It has been reported that this house is haunted by a sheepdog-looking entity, which has also been heard barking. Folklore said a man in a red coat also haunted the site.
address: Old Huntsmans House, Kennel Ave, Ascot SL5 7NZ, UK

title: Vanishing Hound
location_name: Aylesbury (Buckinghamshire) - Unnamed field in a village close to the area
notes: For several nights, a man walking to milk his cows in the early hours of the morning encountered a large black dog which blocked his path. He finally grew tired of having to walk around the hound and, while walking with a friend, struck the creature - the dog vanished but the man fell down paralysed, never to be able to move or speak again.

title: Black Dog
notes: Mistakenly blamed for killing a child, it was later discovered that the dog had tried to defend the infant from a wolf (a la Gelert). However, this hound was less forgiving for its former master's murderous action and now runs around the dense woodland.

title: Webbed Footed Hound
notes: A large dog with eyes the size of saucers once haunted this area.

title: Yellow Eyed Dog
notes: A witness watched a large black dog with yellow eyes cover eighteen metres in just three strides. Over the following two months the witness lost two members of their family.
address: Taylor Hill Way, Clonard Or Folkstown Great, Balbriggan, Co. Dublin, Ireland

title: Lofo
notes: Nicknamed Lofo by the local population, this black dog would grow to several times its original size if challenged or acknowledged. A group of men returning from a wake fled from the hound in terror after one man tried to kick the creature, only for his foot to pass through the dog.
address: Ballintubbrid West, Ballintubber West, Co. Cork, Ireland

title: Moddra-na-Craeuv
location_name: Ballyandrew (County Cork) - Glen of Croke, which stretched from Saffron Hill to Pinegrove
notes: The phantom woman who once appeared here was said to be a Creagh girl who died shortly after her lover was killed by her father. The Moddra-na-Craeuv, also known as the dog of the Creaghs, also haunts the area.
address: Saffron Hill, Ballyandrew, Co. Cork, Ireland

title: Trotting Dog
location_name: Ballygar (County Galway) - Lane in the area
notes: Potentially last seen by a cyclist, the black dog which haunted this area would vanish into thin air as witnesses looked on.

title: Shug Monkey
notes: For reasons unknown, the shuck which has been observed in this area is believed to have a bald head and wide eyes, like that of a monkey, while the body is that of a large black shaggy dog. It leaps out in front of cars travelling along this road, only to dart out of the way at the last possible second, either on all four legs or standing upright.
address: W Wratting Rd, Balsham, Cambridge CB21, UK

title: Black Collie
notes: This phantom dog stands as tall as a very large collie and is said to belong to the fairies.
address: Barber Booth, Hope Valley S33 7ZL, UK

title: Rough Coated Dog with Large Yellow Eyes
location_name: Barham (Suffolk) - Along the main Norwich Road, Barham Church Lane, just past Barham Hall gates.
notes: The dog harassed a couple of men walking back from work - one of them reported trying to hit the creature with a stick, but the piece of wood passed straight through the dog's body. Another version of the story states the dog disappears into a solid wall.
address: Barham Hall Cottage, 10 Church Ln, Barham, Ipswich IP6 0PT, UK

title: Shuck Haunt
notes: Legend states the stream to be the haunt of a Shuck, though no further information could be found.

title: Shag Foal
location_name: Barnoldby le Beck (Lincolnshire) - Church of St Helen's
notes: A black dog the size of a donkey, this entity was said to let loose the most unearthly cries.
address: Barnoldby le Beck, Grimsby DN37, UK

title: Chained Hound
notes: Buried in nearby woodland by an owner, this phantom hound is now reputed to roam the area, a large chain around its neck.
address: Gold Hill, Batcombe, Shepton Mallet BA4 6HF, UK

title: White Dog
notes: A woman passing through the cemetery watched a large white dog as it faded away in front of her.

title: Red Eyed Hound
notes: This large black dog with glowing eyes is said to have driven a local farmer mad after the man tried to block the creature's path. The farmer was also left almost completely paralysed. The hound is said to walk from the churchyard to nearby marshland before fading from view.

title: Headless Hound
location_name: Beetham (Cumbria) - Between village and Milnthrope, and surrounding countryside
notes: It is said that if this large creature follows you, death is not far behind.

title: Capelthwaite
location_name: Beetham (Cumbria) - General area near the river Bela
notes: Also known as a Cappel, this spirit is said to be able to transform into any four legged creature, though it prefers the form of a dog. A local vicar is said to have banished the Capelthwaite into the River Bela, even though it would help some locals drive their sheep.

title: Black Dog
location_name: Belchalwell (Dorset) - Unnamed gate in the area (still standing?)
notes: A particular gate in the area was said to be home to a black dog once the sun went down.

title: Sluagh
notes: The Sluagh are a fairy host that travel across the sky to the land of the ever young, Tir-nan-Og. A couple of hunting hounds belonging to the Sluagh are said to have landed in Benbecula briefly before heading off on their travels.

title: Glowing Mouthed Hounds
location_name: Birstall (Leicestershire) - Pit (no longer exists) near Kilby Avenue, and general area
notes: The locals here say their devil dogs, known as Shag Dogs, have a glow that emanates from the inside of their mouths. A pit, long since filled in, was said to be the creatures' home.
address: Kilby Ave, Birstall, Leicester LE4, UK

title: Headless Hound
notes: Dragging a chain, this decapitated dog crosses the churchyard.

title: Large Hound
location_name: Blairgowrie (Perth and Kinross) - A93 north of the town
notes: This phantom dog, said to be huge, patrols the road. Its presence makes normal dogs nervous and horses shy. One story says to stare into the creature's eyes three times will result in death.

title: Black Dog
notes: This lane is a favoured haunt for the black shuck. The same lane is reportedly haunted by a ghostly horse and carriage.
address: Little Ln, Blakeney, Holt NR25, UK

title: Old Shuck
notes: This lane is another site where the black shuck was said to wander after dark.

title: Hunt
location_name: Blandford Forum (Dorset) - Woods in the area
notes: Thought to be part of a phantom hunt, these ghostly dogs have been heard running and yelping through woodland. It is also thought that a headless shuck haunts the area, though it is less sure whether the entity is connected to the hunt pack.

title: Following Dog
location_name: Blyborough (Lincolnshire) - Road by village pond, leading to Grayingham
notes: As this creature followed a woman, she turned and lashed out with her umbrella - only for it to pass straight through the creature.

title: Shuck
notes: During a church service in 1577, a large black dog burst in and created havoc. Blackened scorch marks said to be caused by the creature still exist today on the church door. Many years later, in 1973, a man working in the marshes near the church reporting hearing a panting dog standing beside him, though nothing could be seen.

title: Watcher Shuck
notes: The large black dog here would observe people while they were in the wood, though the creature would never leave the protective tree line. The site may also be the haunt of a grey woman and a grey man.

title: Shadows
notes: A shadowy hound, said to be slightly larger than a Saint Bernard dog, was reported to haunt this area. The creature has never seen directly, but only from the corner of the eye. Scraping sounds resembling claws on concrete can also be heard, always behind the witness.

title: Country Dog
notes: The area between these two villages is reportedly patrolled by a shuck.

title: Grave Hound
notes: The site where a Jacobite was murdered by a companion is now haunted by a phantom black dog.

title: Invisible to Some
notes: Only one of the two lead miners playing cards at the gates to the town could see a large black dog as it approached them. The man tried to point the creature out to his co-worker, but his friend was completely obvious to the phantom hound which then disappeared. Shaken, the witness refused to go to work the following day, and later heard his co-worker and several others had been killed when a shaft collapsed.
address: Town Gate, Bradwell, Hope Valley S33 9JX, UK

title: Bedroom Shuck
location_name: Bredon (Hereford & Worcester) - Cottage, exact location not known
notes: Appearing in a bedroom, this dog had glowing red eyes and warm breath - it disappeared as it approached the bedroom door to leave.

title: Hound with Pointed Ears
location_name: Brereton (Staffordshire) - Coal Pit Lane, and roads leading to Brereton
notes: This large black dog, with pointed ears and glowing eyes, is said to haunt the area. In 1972, one witness watched a large ball of light crash into the ground, and the hound appeared soon after.
address: Coalpit Ln, Brereton, Rugeley WS15, UK

title: Trotting Dog
location_name: Bridgend (Mid Glamorgan) - Crossroads between Bridgend and Laleston
datetime: Said to manifest every night at midnight (reoccurring)
notes: The phantom hound haunting this crossroads would appear nightly and trot off along a road before vanishing. It was said several people had tried to follow the creature but would always lose sight of it.

title: Sitting Shuck
notes: This shuck is said to sit inside the church, and to see it will quickly bring ill-fortune.
address: St Marys Church, Bridport DT6 3NZ, UK

title: One of Three Shucks
notes: One of three bridges in Lincolnshire that is said to be a haunt of the black shuck, this one normally manifesting on a Saturday night.

title: Willie Sled's Dog
location_name: Brigham (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Brigham Lane, at a crossroads
notes: The crossroads was home to a phantom white hound known as Willie Sled's dog, named after a local sandpit worker.

title: Large White Dog
datetime: Last dog sighting unknown, tall man seen in Spring 1977
notes: Reportedly the size of a small horse, this phantom dog will follow lonely walkers on the beach for short distances before vanishing. A witness reported seeing a tall man in old fashioned clothing and a tall hat walking along the sidewalk during the night in 1977 - the figure vanished into the mist.
address: Brighton Beach, 4 Madeira Dr, Brighton BN2 1ET, UK

title: Morphing Shuck
location_name: Bristol (Somerset) - Dead end lane in Hallen Bristol, next to the M49 bridge
notes: The large black dog seen here in 1908 was seen to climb out from a hedge, before transforming into a donkey and standing on its hind legs. A very similar creature was seen in 2004, as it passed through a car, shaking the occupant.

title: Shaggy Dog
notes: This road is another haunt of the black shuck in Dorset.
address: Common Water Ln, Broadwindsor, Beaminster DT8 3QR, UK

title: Black Dog
notes: A gentleman who committed suicide in the village is said to have returned in the form of a large black dog.

title: Cwn Annwn
datetime: 1961 onwards (hound), face sighting possibly mid twentieth century
notes: A driver passing through Buckholt in 1961 reported seeing a large black hound, believed to be Cwn Annwn, a spectral hound of Annwn, although some people believe it to be a ghostly donkey. More recently, although no date has been mentioned, the hound has been seen in a nearby cottage which is also haunted by two phantom people (one short, one headless). Finally, a witness encountered the face of a beautiful woman along the road. The witness apologised and stepped to one side before realising that there was no one else present.

title: Leaping Dog
location_name: Buckhurst Hill (Essex) - Area around local graveyard
notes: After scaring three teenagers walking through the graveyard, this shuck went on to unnerve a passing motorist as the creature leapt over the churchyard wall and landed on the bonnet of a car.

title: Horseman
notes: A ghostly man on horseback is said to cross the lane before vanishing.
address: College Ln, Bunbury, Tarporley CW6, UK

title: Strange Hound
notes: A phantom dog has been spotted in an area near the school.
address: School Ln, Bunbury, Tarporley CW6, UK

title: Shuck
notes: Believed by some to be the headquarters of Old Shuck, the ruined castle is still avoided at night.

title: Lightning Dog
notes: Appearing on 4 August 1577, the same date as the Blythburgh report, a black dog entered the church during a storm and killed several people. Some believe the dog still patrols the area.

title: White Shuck
location_name: Burgh (Suffolk) - Bath Slough, a boggy pool near the town
notes: A white dog the size of a bullock frequented this area.

title: Old Scarfe
notes: The shuck is known locally as Old Scarfe, named after the Baron Rudolf Scarfe who was a thirteenth century villain who lived near the castle. Old Scarfe haunts the castle grounds and the Yarmouth Road, his single eye glowing red and yellow.
address: Burgh Castle, Great Yarmouth NR31, UK

title: Trash
location_name: Burnley (Lancashire) - General area, though near the church a favourite haunt
notes: A local name for their version of the shuck, Trash was said to be very hairy, with feet that created splashing noises no matter what surface it stepped on. Death would follow a sighting.

title: Ghostly Black Dog
location_name: Burwell (Cambridgeshire) - Burwell to Snailwell - path southeast, by a belt of trees leading to the A11
notes: Another shuck legend, though details are sketchy.

title: Patting the Fading Dog
notes: While passing the church, a man saw a large black dog. He reached out to pat the hound's head, but the creature disappeared into thin air. The man later learnt that his brother had died at the exact moment he saw the dog.

title: Black Dog
location_name: Cadley (Wiltshire) - Fields between village and Marlborough
notes: A black dog may haunt this area.

title: Black Dog
notes: Very little is known about the hound that is said to have frequented this area, other than appearing black and large.

title: Unlucky Shuck
notes: This road, and area, are said to be frequented by a shuck that brings ill luck to all that see it.

title: Gabriel Hounds
location_name: Cannock Chase (Staffordshire) - The Chase and surrounding moorlands
notes: A pack of demonic dogs glide (or fly) across the lands, their eyes burning with fire and mouths covered in blood, with their howls carried on the wind.

title: Robin
location_name: Carrigaholt (County Clare) - Road between Carrigaholt and Ross (R487?)
notes: The phantom hound which haunts the road connecting these two locations was thought to be the ghost of Robin of Ross in animal form.
address: Rahona East, Carrigaholt, Co. Clare, Ireland

title: Powerful Dog
location_name: Carrigaline (County Cork) - Lanes in the area
notes: Said to have made its home in the ruins of a big house, this hound left its dwelling at midnight. Most witnesses were said to have been drunk men, although on one occasion several sober gentlemen chased the creature and had it cornered - the dog vanished in front of their eyes.

title: Black Dog
notes: A shuck is said to walk the area near the castle, while a phantasmic knight stands motionless on the ramparts. Author Wayne Anthony reports that an old lady and a phantom horse have also been reported in the area.
address: Market Pl, Castleton, Hope Valley S33 8WQ, UK

title: Sitting Hound
notes: A phantom dog was once a common sight here, as it sat on the corner of the road near a railway bridge waiting for an owner which never came.
address: Horderns Rd, Chapel-en-le-Frith, High Peak SK23, UK

title: Fiery Eyed Hound
notes: As the name of the location suggests, this wooded area is home to a black dog whose eyes glow as red as hot coals. To see him means death within the year. One story says the hound once belonged to a man killed in a duel; the dog tore out the throat of the surviving fighter.

title: Great Dane
notes: This dog leapt out in front of an incoming car - the driver thought he hit the creature but could not find any evidence of an accident when he stopped.

title: Grave Dog
location_name: Chideock (Dorset) - Road from Morcombelake, & local graveyard
notes: This creature followed a couple of witnesses as they walked home from a nearby village. The creature turned away near the graveyard, and they watched it disappear next to a gravestone. A phantom priest is also said to haunt the same area.

title: Barrow Dog
location_name: Chilham (Kent) - Pilgrim's Way, opposite the Woolpack Inn
notes: A witness watched this large black creature bounding towards the barrow, where it promptly disappeared.
address: Woolpack Inn, The St, Chilham, Canterbury CT4 8DL, UK

title: Wild Edric
location_name: Church Stretton (Shropshire) - Hills around the town
notes: The large hound with eyes that glow red like hot coals is said to be a manifestation of Wild Edric, local hero and protector of Britain.

title: Mean Old Dog
notes: This phantom hound is said to harass those using the road late at night. It can easily be recognised by its pair of horns.

title: Big Eyed Dog
notes: The phantom hound with glowing eyes which walked the corridors at night was said by students to be the manifestation of a hanged murderer. The building was also once witness to a Crisis Manifestation, when the ghost of a Marshall appeared to two sisters - they identified him as their brother, who, it was later discovered, was killed in action at the time of the visitation.
address: Kilcock Road, Castlebrown Or Clongowes, Clane, Co. Kildare, W91 DN40, Ireland

title: Earless Dog
location_name: Clashmealcon (County Kerry) - Area around Brown's Castle
notes: The ghostly, earless dog that haunted this area was supposed to emerge from the castle's moat at night before wandering off to hunt rabbits.
address: Clashmealcon E, Clashmelcon, Co. Kerry, Ireland

title: Black Dog
notes: The outside of this public house is said to be haunted by a ghostly black dog. The inn took the name 'Kremlin' after people reported hearing Russian voices and music emerge from the jukebox during the Cold War.
address: Clay Hill, Cleehill, Ludlow SY8 3NZ, UK

title: Snapping Dog
location_name: Clonakilty (County Cork) - Road east of town
notes: Three men travelling along this road found themselves followed by a hound which stayed a reasonable distance before vanishing at a roadside cross. When one man headed away from the other two, the hound appeared again, chasing and nipping at the man. After sharing their experience with a priest, the area was blessed and the dog never returned.

title: Tchen Bodu
notes: This black hound, known locally as tchen Bodu, is seen as a death omen for either the witness or someone close to them.
address: Clos du Valle, Guernsey GY3, Guernsey

title: Wide-Eyed Dog
notes: A large dog with saucer sized eyes would sit on the roadside. No one would see it move (it would just vanish from view), although if stones were thrown at the creature, the eyes would grow even larger. It once appeared to one man who discovered the time of the sighting matched the time that his father died. The same area is also haunted by a phantom white donkey.

title: Barghest
notes: A hound-like creature with eyes as large as saucers was once said to lurk in this area, which was once considered a place of gambling and other morally dubious activities.

title: Black Dog
location_name: Cold Knap (South Glamorgan) - Beach, between cave and mini golf course
notes: This witness, who would often sit on the pebbles with their two dogs, watched another dog, black in colour, move silently across the beach some fifty metres away. The witness's curiosity was raised by the fact that despite the rocks and stones underfoot, the hound moved smoothly, and their own dogs remained oblivious to the strange canine. Sixteen years later the same witness reported their second paranormal event on the beach - a nebulous black cloud which formed around their feet. After tolerating the cloud for several minutes, the witness left the area.

title: Dog of Justice
notes: This shuck appeared to two men who had just committed a murder, chasing them back to the village from where they were trying to escape. The creature vanished once they were arrested.

title: Headless Hound
notes: The black shuck which walks this area is instantly recognisable as the creature is missing a head.

title: Screaming
datetime: August, first Monday of the month (reoccurring)
notes: Cries and screams can be heard from a man torn into small pieces by hell hounds. The echoes can be heard once a year.

title: Small Black Dog
location_name: Combs (Derbyshire) - Road between Combs and Cockyard
notes: Smaller than the average shuck, this phantom dog was reported to silently follow people down a hill before vanishing.

title: Pongay
location_name: Coppull (Lancashire) - Darkland Bridge and surrounding area
notes: Part dog and part ghost, Pongay was once often blamed for accidents and mishaps in the area.

title: Escort
notes: This shuck was regarded as the traveller's friend, walking alongside anyone traversing the road on their lonesome. It would vanish into thin air if touched.

title: Great Shaggy Beast
notes: Standing almost six foot tall, this black dog made itself known to a couple of water workers along Watery Lane (who wisely ran away). Prior to this, the creature had appeared in the middle of the eighteenth century and was said to denote a death in the witness's family.

title: Portent of Doom
notes: To be followed or escorted by this black hound would result in misfortune soon after the encounter.

title: Human-faced Dog
location_name: Creag an Ordain (Highland) - B869, between Stoer and Lochinver
notes: On closer examination, this shuck has the horrible face of a person, with little devil-like horns. If it barks more than two times, disaster will follow shortly.

title: Phantom Dog
notes: A Woman's Institute member encountered a ghost dog here, terrifying her.

title: Fire Breather
location_name: Crockerton (Wiltshire) - Unnamed road in the area
notes: This ghostly hound was said to be able to blow fire from its nose.

title: Shuck
notes: Many people have reported seeing the black shuck in this area of Norfolk, with the local populace claiming the creature is the ghost of a dog whose master drowned just off the Cromer coast.

title: Black Retriever Drowning Child
location_name: Cromer (Norfolk) - Off the coast of Cromer
notes: A local child befriended a 'stray' black dog and took the creature swimming in the ocean - while out over deep water the dog changed into a human and tried to drown the youngster. Another version of the tale states the dog does not transform, by merely barks and growls, preventing the child from swimming back to land. In both versions, the child is saved by the intervention of sailors.

title: Black Hound
notes: A local legend says that this area was home to a large black dog which hunted souls. A local minister faced the hound and banished it after smacking the creature between the eyes with a bible. The story found renewed interest in 2022 after the chapel sold at auctions for fifty-three thousand pounds.

title: Changing Entity
location_name: Darlington (Durham) - Glassensikes (stream or river) and Harewood Hill area
notes: The large black dog once said to haunt this area could also take the form of a flaming headless man, a headless woman, a white cat, or a rabbit.

title: Dewer
notes: A Wisht huntsman, Dewer would chase people up the highest crag in the area and leave them to fall to their deaths - if the drop did not kill them, the unfortunates would be consumed by hell hounds waiting at the bottom.

title: Large Black Hound
notes: Said to have inspired the famous 'Hound of the Baskervilles', several phantoms are held to blame for this creature (including Drake (for his dealings with the devil) and Richard Cabell (a man so evil that he now hunts children with his devil dogs!)).

title: Yeth Hounds
location_name: Dartmoor (Devon) - General area of the River Tavy
notes: These variants of the shuck, complete with hunters on horseback, have been heard running in this area after dark. Part of the Tavy is also said to be haunted by the ghost of Sir John Fitz, whose body was found in the river.

title: Wish Pack
notes: Led by Drake, this pack of hounds patrol the area.

title: Wish Hounds
location_name: Dartmoor (Devon) - Wistman's Wood, one mile north of Two Bridges
notes: Large black dogs are rumoured to hunt in this area of the moors - they once killed the son of a farmer. In the same area, a procession of white clad figures could be seen walking through the wood, following the path of what was once a well-used funeral route.
address: Wistman's Wood, Two Bridges, Princetown PL20, UK

title: Black Dog
notes: The ghost of a local weaver metamorphosed into a great black dog when the village vicar threw consecrated earth into his face - the hound was then taken to a pool by the waterfall and instructed to only harass people once it had emptied the water using a small, cracked shell.

title: Huge Hound
notes: The phantom black dog that haunted this area was believed by some to be a former murderer, or the Devil. The dog was said to have been banished in 1931, after a local Reverend buried some bones in the churchyard which had been discovered in a cottage, although it has been seen since, most recently in 2008 by a witness who described it as having an abnormally shiny coat and a spiked collar.

title: Weaver
datetime: Said to briefly appear at midday and midnight (reoccurring)
notes: A weaver returned to continue work after he died, refusing to leave until a vicar convinced the weaver he was dead. The weaver transformed into a hound and was taken to a pool of water by the vicar, who issued the dog with a broken shell and told the creature he could only leave when the pool was emptied.

title: White Spotted Dog
notes: A secret room in the moat was said to contain nine kegs of gold protected by a large black dog with a white spot. The only way to escape with the gold was to stab the dog three times with a dagger to the white spot.

title: Dog with Long Shaggy Coat
location_name: Ditchingham (Norfolk) - Halfway between Ditchingham and the Maltings
notes: While returning home late one night, a male witness saw a large black dog walking along the same path towards him. He stepped into the middle of the road to let it pass, only to see it disappear once close to him.

title: Wish Hounds
location_name: Ditchling (Sussex) - Road between town & Westmeston, and nearby downs
notes: The local shucks, known as wish hounds, reportedly stalk these roads. One of them is said to be headless.

title: Pack of Hounds
notes: The lands around Doneraile are home to a phantom pack of hounds, sometimes heard but not seen and at other times observed in silence. On one occasion, a witness spotted a rider leading the pack, leading to speculation that a former lord of the manor had not yet realised he should have passed over.

title: Black Dog
notes: The hound that haunted this road was not thought to be harmful, though the locals were said to be afraid of travelling here at night.

title: Hound
notes: A black dog haunted this stretch of road, although there have been no sightings for at least one hundred years.

title: Black Hound
notes: This dark hound reportedly patrols the village from dusk until dawn.
address: Down St Mary, Crediton EX17 6DX, UK

title: Pack of Hounds
notes: A hunting pack of black dogs are reported to chase the unwary in this area of Lancashire.

title: Black Dog
location_name: Drumcliff (aka Drumcliffe) (County Clare) - Exact location not known
notes: Once thought to haunt this area, the phantom black hound would appear to be mostly forgotten about.

title: Chained Hound
location_name: Dublin (County Dublin) - Pussy's Leap, Templeogue area
notes: The large black canine which walked his area created the sound of jangling chains with each step it took.

title: Lord Norbury
notes: The otherworldly black hound which stalks the area is said to be the ghost of Lord Norbury, a harsh judge renowned for his overuse of the hangman's noose.

title: Devil in Disguise
location_name: Dublin (County Dublin) - Grand canal near Harcourt Bridge (aka Rialto Bridge)
notes: The devil would take the form of a black dog and stalk the area around the bridge.
address: Rialto Bridge, Saint James, Dublin, Ireland

title: Glowing Eyes
notes: A witness recounted how friends were terrified by a strange black dog with glowing red eyes which appeared and disappeared around them. The sightings were accompanied by high pitched screeching.

title: Stalking Hound
location_name: East Dereham (Norfolk) - Between village and (former) RAF Swanton Morley
notes: Cycling from his home towards the RAF base, John Harries was stalked by a black dog. The hound stopped when Harries stopped, although it vanished when Harries reached the base.

title: Headless Shuck
location_name: East Runton (Norfolk) - Runton Heath, between East Runton and Aylmerton
notes: Manifesting on dark stormy nights, this shuck brings death to those who see him within a year.

title: Three Hounds
notes: Seeing one shuck is often thought of as an ill omen - the three which seem to haunt this area in a pack are a guaranteed bad portent.

title: Big Animal
notes: A large, black ghostly dog is reputed to walk around The Tips area.

title: Headless Hound
notes: The large black dog which brings death and misfortune in this neighbourhood is reportedly headless.

title: Barguest
location_name: Egton (North Yorkshire) - Area around the Esk Valley
notes: This northern variant of the shuck is seen just prior to the death of a local man or woman.

title: Black Hound
location_name: Ennistymon (aka Ennistimon) (County Clare) - Ennistymon House (now Falls Hotel)
notes: The grounds around this house were said to be home to a black dog, which despite its size, was quite harmless.
address: Glencree, Castlequarter, Ennistymon, Co. Clare, V95 D2PC, Ireland

title: Postman's Dog
location_name: Ewenny (Mid Glamorgan) - Crossroads where one road leads to Ogmore
notes: A postman reported that he would see a large phantom black dog every night, watching the creature move silently as if on a mission.

title: Black Dog
notes: This port town is rumoured to be patrolled by the black shuck, though there are no dated reports of the entity being seen here.

title: Black Animal
location_name: Fingest (Buckinghamshire) - Road between Fingest and Skirmett
notes: A large black dog-like animal was once said to stand under a tree along this road, snarling at travellers.
address: Fingest Ln & Skirmett Rd, Skirmett, Henley-on-Thames RG9 6TD, UK

title: Dog with Glowing Eyes
notes: Locally known as 'Old Trash', this black dog bounds along the dunes leaving no prints or marks in the soft sand. He was last seen by two journalists and a photographer, though it is unclear whether an image was caught of the entity.

title: Labrador
notes: Author Hugh Brawn encountered a black Labrador-like shuck here. The thin creature crossed the road in front of him but vanished before reaching the other side.

title: Devil & Shuck
notes: Believed to have occurred in 1602, a miller was harassed by the Devil. The Fallen Angel tried to talk to the miller, who refused to stop working. The Devil gave up, turning into a large black dog before running away.

title: Black Dog
notes: This phantom hound is thought to relate to a murder which once took place close to the church, although further details are fleeting.

title: Black Dog
notes: One witness claimed to have encountered a black dog while walking along the road. The witness tried to stroke it, but his hand passed through the creature. The dog then walked alongside the man until he reached Coombs Cross, when it jumped eight foot (2.4 metres) into the air and disappeared into the cemetery.
address: Varteg Rd, Garndiffaith, Abersychan, Pontypool NP4, UK

title: Odin
notes: The large phantom dog reputed to haunt this royal household is named the 'Hound of Odin'.

title: Running Dog
location_name: Geldeston (Norfolk) - Lover's Lane, leading to the churchyard
notes: The small lane leading to the local churchyard is reputedly home to a phantom hound - the creature has been spotted running along the lane. It is thought that the location that the hound appears will soon be the site of a crime. A ghostly donkey seen here may be the same entity. The same area is also haunted by a haunted coach as it travels towards Bungay Castle.

title: Wicked Parson
location_name: Gillingham (Norfolk) - Dogs Lane and All Saints church
notes: Last seen sitting on a verge along Dogs Lane with glowing eyes, this phantom hound is thought to be a holy man with less than holy tendencies. The entity was also reported outside the church in 1913.

title: Brown Dog
notes: The size of a cow, this large dog was mistaken for an escaped lion until it vanished into thin air.

title: Dog with Long Legs
location_name: Gorleston (Norfolk) - Suffield Road and High Street
notes: A woman reported seeing a strange dog-like creature with long legs on two different occasions around Gorleston. When she spotted the creature the second time running down the High Street, it vanished without trace.
address: Suffield Rd, Great Yarmouth NR31, UK

title: Large Running Dog
location_name: Gorleston-on-Sea (Norfolk) - Along beach, half a mile from the Gorleston Rescue Headquarters
notes: While on duty at the HQ, Graham Grant watched a large black dog running in the sand, pausing every now and then to look around. It vanished without warning, in the middle of the beach.

title: Pony Sized Dog
location_name: Grantown (Moray) - A939, normally around the Glenlivet forest area
notes: This strange hound stands as tall as a pony, has green tinted fur, and either has a very small head or no head at all.

title: Rainbow Eyed Barguest
location_name: Grassington (North Yorkshire) - Area near the village
notes: This shuck had rings of colour in its eyes and would rattle before making itself visible.

title: Hairy Jack
notes: This large black dog was said to have a particularly shaggy coat.

title: Shaggy Brown Creature
location_name: Great Bookham (Surrey) - Between Polesden Lane and Admiral Walk (before the crossroads with Crabtree Lane)
notes: While walking her dog with her father, this witness observed a large dog-shaped figure in the middle of the track about 20 metres away. It was around a metre high, very shaggy looking and had brown fur. It was also 'blurry'. The entity vanished as the witness approached, although dematerialised as the witness walked away.

title: Black Hound
location_name: Great Durnford (Wiltshire) - Chalk pit between village and Netton
notes: A local man tried to hit this phantom dog with a stick while returning from the pub. The stick passed straight through the creature.

title: Phantom Dog
location_name: Great Massingham (Norfolk) - Peddar's Way & Massingham Heath
notes: The shucks around this area bring misfortune to any who see them, one witness having a serious road accident exactly one year after seeing a ghostly dog. Reports of a grey phantom dog have also been made, quite rare in East Anglian Shuck mythology.
address: Great Massingham, King's Lynn PE32, UK

title: Phantom White Dog
location_name: Great Snoring (Norfolk) - Road South of Great Snoring
notes: Just prior to the Second World War, a farmer reported driving straight through a phantom white dog. He was so shocked he stopped his car and ran. Many cyclists in the area had also reported the creature.

title: Large Black Dog
notes: This area lays claim to be one of the many places in East Anglia where the shuck has visited.

title: Black Hound
notes: This stretch of road is said to be home to Old Scarf, a black dog that haunts the area.

title: Running Dog
location_name: Great Yarmouth (Norfolk) - Suffield Road, near the police station
notes: An animal resembling a dog with long legs was seen running along the path, vanishing within seconds of being spotted. The same entity was also observed along the High Street by the same witness, though years previous.
address: Suffield Rd, Great Yarmouth NR31, UK

title: Bear Sized Hound
location_name: Great Yarmouth (Norfolk) - Yarmouth South Station (no longer standing)
notes: This creature was described as white and the size of a polar bear by two witnesses as they watched it leap over engines.

title: Scared Shuck
notes: While this shuck is said to be just as terrifying as his brethren in other parts of the country, a good loud shout is reportedly enough to drive him away with his tail between his legs.

title: Thomas Colley
notes: The shuck spoken about around these parts is thought to be the spirit of a particularly nasty man who drowned two people thought to be witches - he was executed for his crime and his body left on a gibbet. The large black dog with red eyes was last seen sinking into the earth by the village headmaster.

title: Large Black Dog
notes: A local legend says that the area where the Saxon church once stood is home to a large black dog which glides around. Its howls are prevalent throughout August.

title: Two Dogs
notes: Twice over a period of two years one witness watched two phantom dogs run along this lane. A black hound is chased by another dog - both a slightly out of focus and move thirty centimetres above the ground.
address: Greens Ln, Haslingden, Rossendale BB4, UK

title: Gabriel Hounds
notes: To hear the howl of this demonic dog indicated the imminent death of a family member, although on one occasion, the howling came from a farmer's son who then eloped with a woman in a Scooby-Doo-que ruse that is too convoluted to summarise here.

title: Snarling unlike any Dog
location_name: Hemby (Norfolk) - Lane leading to a Caravan Park, behind a fence in the garden of a house
notes: When walking back from the arcades, a couple and their nineteen year old daughter witnessed a large dog with glowing red eyes, growling 'unlike any dog... ever heard before'. The family reported a run of bad luck ever since.

title: Vanishing Dog
notes: The dog in question ran in front of a cyclist - the man braced for impact, only to see it vanish. A quick search revealed no sign of the creature.

title: Black Dogs
location_name: Hemswell (Lincolnshire) - Area between village and Grayingham
notes: These calf-sized dogs patrol the roads connecting the two villages. A woman who encountered one of the hounds is said to have lashed out at it with her umbrella, only for the object to pass straight through the creature.

title: Fiery Eyed
notes: The large demonic dog of this area is said to be the size of a calf with the fires of hell burning within its eyes.

title: Devil Hound
location_name: Hergest (Hereford & Worcester) - Hergust Court, now a farmhouse
notes: This hound would appear to members of the Vaughan family before one of their number died. It is said that when Black Vaughan was beheaded in 1483, his faithful hound seized and ran off with the head. The creature is said to have inspired Sir Arthur Conan Doyle to write 'Hound of the Baskervilles'.
address: Hergest Court, Hergest Rd, Hergest HR5 3EL, UK

title: Faines
location_name: Hethersett (Norfolk) - Mill Road and Gravel Pit Lane
notes: Faines were creatures that stood as tall as a calf, with large eyes. One account has the entity possess the ability to blow anyone nearby away which a large gust of wind.
address: Mill Rd, Hethersett, Norwich NR9, UK

title: Vanishing Hound
notes: A policeman reported an encounter with a black dog which suddenly vanished without trace along this road.
address: Taplin Rd, Hillsborough, Sheffield S6, UK

title: Loping Phantom Dog
location_name: Hockley (Essex) - Road between Hockley and parish church
notes: Appearing on misty nights, those who see the dog are said to die before the end of the year. One witness walking home on a dark night fainted after a large black dog appeared by her side, disappearing into thin air several minutes later. It is not known if she suffered the fate that legend dictates.

title: Invisible to Some
location_name: Holmesfield (Derbyshire) - Bury Hill ('three lane ends'), although cannot find on modern maps
notes: The barghast, a demonic dog with saucer-sized eyes, was considered a death omen. The creature which lurked here was spotted by a woman, but remained invisible to her sister who walked besides her. The sister died a month later.

title: Padfoot
notes: A man returning home spotted a white dog in the hedgerow and hit out at it with a stick. The stick passed straight through the creature, which then turned and stared at its attacker. The man ran home, fell ill and died soon after. The shuck is said to sometimes run around on two legs and is considered a harbinger of death.
address: Jenkin Rd, Horbury, Wakefield WF4, UK

title: Red Eyed Dog
location_name: Horton (Dorset) - Bagman's Lane and Pot Lane
notes: This lane is home to a local shuck; with red eyes and fur black as coal, his passing is to be taken as an ill-omen.

title: Subduing Hound
notes: This black dog attacked one man walking by the hall, pushing him to the ground but not causing any harm. Coincidently, at the same time, the Hall's owner died.

title: Black Hound
notes: This devil dog is more likely to be heard baying at the moon, its howl freezing the blood of witnesses.

title: Dog of Ill Omen
notes: Banished by an unknown holy man, this creature would once walk through the town warning the inhabitants of misfortune.

title: Rooftop Hounds
location_name: Hynish (Argyll and Bute) - Fort at foot of hill in the area
notes: This old fort was used by two sons of a local farmer to watch over the family cattle overnight, as the pasture was far from the family home. One evening, the children's mother heard a voice warning her that her sons were in danger. The mother ran to the fort and found her sons terrified, claiming that they had heard fairy hounds stomping and howling on the roof of the fort.
address: Hynish, Isle of Tiree PA77 6UD, UK

title: Barguest
location_name: Ilkley (West Yorkshire) - Moorland to the north of the village, and of Otley
notes: The large black hound was believed to lurk on the moors in this area.

title: White Hound
location_name: Imogane (County Cork) - Jer. Sullivan's Cross, travelling from Liscarroll
notes: While walking to Imogane from Liscarroll, a white dog appeared next to Matt MacMahon and accompanied him home. When Matt opened the gate leading to his house, the hound vanished (special thanks to the Churchtown Village Renewal Trust, from their 'The Annals of Churchtown').

title: The Devil Dog
location_name: Ingatestone (Essex) - Area around the Star Inn
notes: Although initially sounding like a shuck, the Devil Dog in this area is said to be the spirit of a canine that died around 1914. Small and black, the hound was reputedly seen several times in the twentieth century, trying to block pedestrians walking along the lane.

title: Big Black Dog Saves Bishop Petre
notes: A mugging was foiled when Bishop Petre's attackers were scared off by a large black hound which came from nowhere. As he brushed himself down, Petre realised the dog had disappeared as quickly as it had appeared.

title: Farm Dog
location_name: Ipstones (Staffordshire) - Area around Hermitage Farm, and Indefont Well
notes: The lane outside of the farm was once the haunt of a phantom black hound. The same dog (or maybe another) also haunted the area around Indefont Well.
address: Hermitage Working Farm, Froghall Rd, Froghall, Stoke-on-Trent ST10 2HQ, UK

title: Burning Dog
location_name: Islay (Argyll and Bute) - Bothy on Beinn na Caillich
notes: While visiting the island with his hunting dog, Macphie came across a mountainside bothy and decided to rest. A dark entity appeared in the doorway and the dog attacked it. Macphie noticed his dog's fur was smouldering, so fled back to his boat. The dog followed but had changing - the creature was now mad and breathed green flame. Macphie killed the dog with two sixpences fired from a shotgun.
address: Beinn na Caillich, Isle of Islay PA44 7PZ, UK

title: Potato Eater
notes: A crofter noticed his potato store was raided each night. One morning he discovered the tooth of a Cu Sith stuck in a potato - looks like this Cu Sith liked a meal of spuds!

title: Tow Dog
location_name: Isle of Portland (Dorset) - All over Island
notes: This shaggy dog that stands six foot tall has an unusual trait for a shuck - it only blocks people from passing, never injuring them. The hound is also known as the Row Dog.
address: Isle of Portland, Portland DT5, UK

title: Black Dog
notes: This black dog vanishes just as quickly as it appears.

title: Tail Touch
notes: Mr Foley was said to have encountered a phantom hound while passing through this area. The dog's tail brushed against him, and later that night Foley was unable to sleep. He died a few days later.

title: Elfin Hound
location_name: Kennavarra, Tiree (Argyll and Bute) - General area
notes: A large black fairy hound would walk the sandbanks during storms, the creature's howl carrying in the wind. The same area was also home to bean-nighe, who could be heard scrubbing the clothing of those soon to die.

title: Paws the Size of Hands
location_name: Kennavarra, Tiree (Argyll and Bute) - Grassy sandy area close to a hill
notes: A man walking along the links changed direction after spotting a huge dog asleep on a sand dune. Returning to the place the following day, the man found the dog's pawprints in the sand, each the size of his hand.

title: One Eye
location_name: Kenworth (Bedfordshire) - Footpath between Common Road and Hollicks Lane
notes: The phantom hound that haunts this area is said to be large, black, and in possession of only one eye.
address: Hollick's Ln & Common Rd, Kensworth, Dunstable LU6 2PJ, UK

title: Black Dog
location_name: Keresley (Warwickshire) - Watery Lane and area around Hound's Hill
notes: In 1949 a water worker walking along the lane early one morning encountered a large black dog with glowing eyes. The creature sat on its hind legs and watched as the worker crept around it. Other encounters are not dated. One states that a horse rider struck the demonic hound with his crop after the creature blocked his path. The dog exploded, throwing the rider from his horse.

title: Angry Dog
location_name: Kilbrittain (County Cork) - Road two miles west from the village, and continuing east of village
notes: Three men heading towards Kilbrittain from Clonakilty encountered a large black dog which followed them until they reached a cross in the road, where the hound disappeared. The group split up at this point, but one man reported that the dog reappeared behind him, snapping at him until he arrived home at Ballinspittle. A priest blessed the area where the creature first appeared, and there were no more reported sightings.

title: Two Headed Dog
notes: A pool in the area is said to contain treasure, though unfortunately it is guarded by a large hound with two heads. A man who managed to drain the pool of all its contents was visited by the hound, resulting in him reversing his actions and replacing the water.

title: Large Dog
location_name: Kimberley (Nottinghamshire) - Field close to the water tower
notes: Pushing their bike up the field, this witness paused to take in the view. Looking back the way they were walking, they spotted a horse-sized black dog ahead. The creature ran along a hedge at the top of the hill. The witness took their eyes from the dog for a moment, during which it vanished.

title: Shuck Home
location_name: Kirton (Lincolnshire) - Belle Hole Farm, and nearby stream
notes: A shuck is said to have set up home in a hole in the stream bank near this farmhouse. The farmer's wife reported seeing the creature enter the kitchen - she dared not move until the shuck left on its own accord.

title: Lady-faced Dog
location_name: Knaith (Lincolnshire) - Area around cottage, no longer standing
notes: A story here tells of shuck that possessed the face of a woman, or sometimes a woman who had the face of a dog. The hound would emerge from the hills close to where the lady lived. The entity is thought to have once been a normal woman who underwent the transformation after being murdered.

title: Hound
location_name: Knighton Down (Wiltshire) - Barrow in the area
notes: A phantom hound is said to lurk around a long barrow in this area.

title: Hound
notes: This dark dog is said to be extremely unlucky for all those who see it, for the creature's temper is reportedly quite heinous.

title: Retriever
notes: This black hound is said to resemble a retriever. The creature is said to 'accompany' anyone who walks through the area. Another phantom (or perhaps the same entity in different form) is that of a figure who materialises in the back seat of cars passing through the village, visible only in the rear view mirror.

title: Black Hound
notes: Fortunately for any witnesses, this member of the Cwn Annwn is reported to be harmless as it runs between castle and town, although on one occasion it screamed so loudly that a witness thought the ground was moving beneath her feet.

title: Padfoot
location_name: Leeds (West Yorkshire) - Area between Wreghorn (?) and Headingly Hill
notes: This creature, a midnight black dog the size of a donkey, would bring bad luck and misfortune to all who saw it. It was also always heard when a local person of some importance died - his howls and barks would cause all the mortal dogs in the neighbourhood to also howl.

title: Gabble Retchets
notes: These local devil dogs were believed to be the souls of those who were not baptised as children and have returned to haunt their parents.

title: Black Hound
notes: This park, like many in Yorkshire, is reputedly home to a large demonic black hound. A photographed was published by the local press in 2019 which claimed to show the entity but was more likely to be a dog being walked by its owner.
address: Roundhay Park, Mansion Ln, Roundhay, Leeds LS8 2HH, UK

title: Shag Dog
location_name: Leicester (Leicestershire) - Kilby Road (was once Black Lane)
notes: Black lane was patrolled by a large black dog with bright eyes and a large, fanged mouth which glowed like burning coals. It once saved a local girl from being attacked by a robber.

title: Hound
notes: This is another site in Dorset reported to be home to the large, black devil dog.

title: Shuck Skeleton?
notes: The remains of a hound seven foot (2 metres) long were unearthed on the abbey grounds. Because of the proximity to the village of Bungay, some media outlets claimed the remains belonged to the Black Shuck. An old local legend says the area was home to the large black dog.
address: Abbey, Abbey Rd, Theberton, Leiston IP16 4TD, UK

title: Gallytrot Leaping over Wall
notes: Seen by Lady Rendlesham and Lady Walsingham, the shuck appeared at midnight, leapt over the low churchyard wall, and disappeared towards the sand hills.

title: Calf Sized Dog
location_name: Leverton (Lincolnshire) - Road between village and Wrangle
notes: An early twentieth century witness reported seeing this creature several times over the years, always when cycling at night between the two villages. A more recent witness heard howling and spotted two red eyes - after the witness's uncle died a short time later, they believed what they had encountered had been a shuck.

title: Headless Hound
location_name: Leweston (Dorset) - Junction north of Stokebridge Farm
notes: A phantom dog with no head is reputed to cross a country lane close to the farm.
address: Lower Stockbridge Farm, Sherborne DT9 6EP, UK

title: White Dog
notes: This large hound is said to jump from the castle ruins at people, before slinking back from where it came.

title: Black Hound
location_name: Lismire (County Cork) - Road between village and Bawnmore
notes: A man cutting turf along this road just after dusk was approached by a large phantom hound. The presence worried the man, so he quickly left. It was speculated he was digging close to buried treasure, and phantom animal protecting the hoard.

title: White Hound of Feens
notes: A legend says this hound, which can be heard howling in the night, is sometimes accompanied by a young lady in grey on horseback. She, in turn, has been known to ride with a wild hunt called the Dorcas Noble, while trying to win the heart of a former boyfriend. A large white hound was seen in the area during the 1980s - the size of an Irish Wolfhound, during one encounter the creature vanished as if running behind a black sheet.

title: Cwn Annwn
location_name: Llanhilleth (Gwent) - Unidentified road in the area
notes: The Cwn Annwn, a pack of hell hounds, were heard as they passed a local man as he walked home. The invisible dogs were heading towards the nearby river.

title: Grey Hound
notes: Said to be of immense size, a large grey hound was believed to haunt the area after dark.
address: National Trust - Carding Mill Valley and the Long Mynd, Church Stretton SY6 6JG, UK

title: Coach Crash
location_name: Long Newnton? (Gloucestershire) - Jackament's Bottom - was a farm between Kemble and Badminton
notes: A coach crash which occurred here killing the driver was reported to have been caused when a phantom dog appeared; its presence made the horses bolt which upset the carriage.
address: Jackaments Bottom Farmhouse, Farm, Rodmarton, Cirencester GL7 6PJ, UK

title: Devil Dog
notes: This hound, believed by some to be the Devil, appeared during one annual festival. The terrified attendees fled the scene, and the party was never held again.

title: Fire Breathing Hound
notes: This abomination with cloven hooves protects a hidden hoard of treasure somewhere near this house.

title: Hound
notes: A phantom dog is said to haunt this wooded area, though no witnesses exist.

title: Dark Hound
notes: A phantom black dog, possibly a shuck, is said to haunt this area.

title: Faery Dog
notes: This hound has been known to leave oversized paw prints on the damp sand which vanish suddenly halfway across the beach.
address: Luskentyre, Isle of Harris HS3 3HL, UK

title: Dog on Fire
notes: During a storm, the gallows that once stood here were hit by lightning - a large fire quickly took hold around the area, and the witnesses who quickly arrived on the scene reported seeing a large black dog in the middle of the blaze running around the flames. The entity suddenly vanished.
address: Galley Hill, Hemel Hempstead HP1, UK

title: Missing Corner
location_name: Lyme Regis (Dorset) - Old Black Dog Hotel (was the Black Dog public house)
datetime: Corner incident unknown, phantom last seen 1959
notes: The shuck which lives here is reported to have knocked off the corner of the pub while passing by one night. The shuck was last seen in 1959 and was described as the size of a calf with glowing red eyes.
address: Lyme Rd, Lyme Regis DT7 3TJ, UK

title: White Dog
notes: Departing the station, this train driver reported seeing a white misty dog move quickly across the track from left to right. One side of the railway stands a six metre high wall, while on the other a 3.5 metre high mesh fence - nowhere where the dog could have come from or disappeared to.

title: Black Dog
datetime: Prior to a death or grave misfortune
notes: This shuck is only an ill omen for those who see him and live at the castle.
address: Leeds Castle, Broomfield, Maidstone ME17 1PL, UK

title: Hound
location_name: Mainsriddle (Dumfries and Galloway) - Road between village and Prestonmill, area once known as Dairymaid's Hollow
notes: A black dog which was said to haunt this area was once heard invisibly passing by three women, although one of them speculated the sound may have been a peacock. A woman in white was also seen by a doctor using the road.

title: Blazing Eyed Dog
notes: A large black hound with burning red eyes was said to lurk in this area. A phantom grey horse is also reported, as is an old woman laden down with a bundle of faggots, drifting towards the Holy Well.
address: Feltrim Rd, Malahide, Co. Dublin, Ireland

title: Headless Hound
location_name: Manchester (Greater Manchester) - Cathedral area, & bridge over the Irwell towards Salford
notes: A tradesman spotted the spectral dog outside the cathedral and the creature jumped up at him. The phantom creature was finally exorcised under the bridge over the Irwell.

title: Yellow Dog
notes: This large hound of ill fortune is yellow in colour - one story states it was once mistaken for a lion.

title: One of Three Shucks
notes: One of three bridges in Lincolnshire that is said to be a haunt of the black shuck.

title: Headless Dog
location_name: Manton (Wiltshire) - House (since demolished) along Preshute Lane
notes: This headless dog would have been silent if not for the chain dragging on the floor which dangled from its neck. The owner of the property was never afraid of the entity.

title: Happy Puppy
notes: This shuck is most likely the friendliest on record - it would run around the children's bedroom, hide under beds, and was also once found in the attic.

title: Not Boxer
