location_name: Mawbeg (County Cork) - Bridge over the River Bandon
notes: Mick Donovan spotted this phantom hound and mistook it for his own dog 'Boxer', calling out to the creature until he realised that the entity was a ghost.

title: White Dogs
location_name: Meenogahane (County Kerry) - Area once known as Laghtnegunbane
notes: An area around here was known as 'grave of the white dogs' and thought to be haunted by the creatures.
address: Meenogahane, Main Street, Co. Kerry, Ireland

title: Donkey Faced Dog
notes: The shuck that was once seen around Melton possessed a donkey's head and a smooth coat. The creature bit one man on the head as he tried to capture it, before vanishing.

title: Sometimes Headless
notes: The shuck which haunted this churchyard sometimes appeared headless.

title: Large Dog
notes: This dog was thought to contain the soul of a man named Blomeley, who shot several people on the site. Accompanying poltergeist like activities made it difficult for anyone to live in the house.

title: Black Dog
location_name: Mildenhall (Wiltshire) - Earthworks around Folly Farm (a former Roman camp), and surrounding villages
notes: This dark hound is believed to be connected to the Roman fort.

title: Scared to Death
notes: One report of a shuck in this village states that the female witness who encountered the demonic dog with eyes like red hot coals died of shock three days later.

title: Bulging Eyes
location_name: Milton (Ayrshire) - Unnamed fields in the area
notes: A large black dog with bulging eyes, moving faster than a normal hound, was spotted by a local farmer. The farmer followed the dog as it crossed a field and reached the farmhouse of a neighbour. As the dog entered the farmhouse a firearm was heard to discharge - upon investigation the body of the neighbour was found shot dead.

title: Transparent Hound
location_name: Minchinhampton (Gloucestershire) - Area of Woefuldane Bottom, aka Woeful Danes' Bottom, aka Woeful Dane Bottom
notes: The phantom hound which stalks the road here is said to be semi-transparent. Phantom horsemen are also said to ride through the area.
address: Woefuldane Btm, Minchinhampton, Stroud GL6, UK

title: White Dog
notes: Folklore says the white dog's appearance is a prelude to a death in the Norman family.

title: Vanishing Dane
location_name: Molland (Devon) - Road leading into the village
notes: While heading towards the village, a driver was forced to break when a Great Dane ran into the road. As the car stopped, the hound walked up to the car and then vanished.

title: Large Dog
notes: The large hound which was reputed to haunt the area may have been mistaken for a ghostly donkey which quickly dissipated into mist when kicked by a passerby.

title: Black Dog
location_name: Moortown (Lincolnshire) - Road leading to Moortown House
notes: This large hound was seen several times on the road, always vanishing at the same place. Other animals also felt the presence of the creature, with one pony refusing to travel down the road - the owner had to go the rest of the journey on foot. The same area was also haunted by a woman in a white apron.
address: Brigg Rd, Moortown, Market Rasen LN7 6JA, UK

title: Dark Hounds
location_name: Mount Schiehallion (Perth and Kinross) - General area
notes: These relatives of the shuck stalk the shady zones of the mountainside, while water horses patrol nearby lochs. Other pieces of folklore have Schiehallion the home of fairies and strange unearthly creatures.

title: Gwyllgi
location_name: Mousiad (South Glamorgan) - Path between village and Lisworney Crossways
notes: This Welsh shuck, with foul breath and a coat as dark as midnight, is said to haunt this piece of road. A servant who encountered the creature had to be helped home as the shock left him unable to walk. Another witness described the dog as having a human-shape, although the body and limbs were that of a large spotted hound.

title: White Hound
notes: The white phantom dog which lurked in this area was harmless unless provoked.

title: Death Harbinger
notes: This black dog of ill fortune can be fended off with a crucifix, though it is unsure whether this will stop the witness of dying shortly after viewing the hound.

title: Incorporeal Dog
location_name: Neatishead (Norfolk) - Unnamed road in the area
notes: While walking home, a man named Finch spotted what he thought was a fellow villager's dog in the road. The dog snarled at Finch, so he tried to kick it away. His foot passed straight through the creature.

title: Black Hound
notes: To see this huge black dog is normally a sign of ill fortune.

title: A Hound Follows
location_name: Newcastle upon Tyne (Tyne and Wear) - General area
notes: It was believed that a phantom dog would follow midwives as they travelled around the city. If the dog laughed as the midwife reached her destination, all would be well, but if the hound howled, then complications would follow. One report described the hound as Mastiff-like.

title: Black Dog
location_name: North Shields (Northumberland) - Milbourne Place, now Smith's Dockyards
notes: The area near the docks is haunted by the ghost of a sailor murdered by his parents; they failed to recognise their own son after his many years at sea and killed him for his money. He returns in the form of a large black dog, patrolling the area where the crime was committed.
address: Milbourne, Newcastle upon Tyne NE20 0EA, UK

title: Large Hound
location_name: Northallerton (North Yorkshire) - A684, between Northallerton and Leeming Bar
notes: A large black dog ran in front of two women travelling to Leeming Bar by car. The driver closed her eyes and braked hard, expecting to hit the creature. The passenger watched the hound pass through the bonnet, and noticed the creature had no facial features, floppy ears, and was shadow-like. A man who the women spoke to once they reached their destination later killed himself - was the hound a portent?

title: Large Dog
notes: A driver and his passenger spotted a large black shaggy dog with red eyes standing in the road ahead of their car. They drove slowly past the creature, which was tall enough to look down at the occupants through the driver side window. The driver then gazed into his mirror to see the dog vanish.

title: Lady Howard
notes: This large black dog is reportedly the ghost of Lady Howard, who walks from here to Tavistock. Some say it is a separate entity, and Lady Howard really travels by a coach made from the bones of her dead husbands.

title: Large Black Dog
location_name: Old Costessey (Norfolk) - Area around The Heart of Costessy Pub
notes: A strange black dog was reported in this area in 2005.
address: Town House Rd, Norwich, Costessey, Norwich NR8 5BS, UK

title: Shrieking Dog
notes: Shuck sightings were so abundant here that the parish named a lane after the beast - local legend also states when the creature disappears, it leaves the area charred and smelling of brimstone. The path leading to Cromer lighthouse is also rumoured to be popular with the creature.

title: Creature with One Yellow Eye
notes: A woman with her son reported seeing a creature with a single yellow eye that watched them from behind a bush. When the creature ran off, she could see that it was the size of a calf.

title: Vanishing Black Dog
location_name: Peacehaven (Sussex) - Steps on cliffs leading down to beach
notes: A jogger spotted a black dog just in front of him while moving down the steps. Concerned for the creature's welfare, as the owner could not be seen, the jogger followed the dog. No more than a couple of seconds behind the creature, it vanished - there was no sign of the dog in the sea, and nowhere else for it to have gone. Around a year later, the jogger was talking to another person who said that they had been followed by a black dog in the same area.

title: Moddey Dhoo
notes: The local shuck, the Moddey Dhoo, haunted the local guardroom - appearing at night to sleep by their fire. One guard grew tired of hiding away from it and went to face the creature alone; he returned soon after, silent and dying, but the creature was never seen again.
address: West Quay, Peel, IM5 1TB, Isle of Man

title: Black Dog with Drooping Ears
location_name: Peldon (Essex) - Wigborough Road between Guisnes Court and Sacott crossroads
notes: While with a friend driving a horse and trap, a man reported being followed by a dog the size of a calf, with drooping ears and eyes that glowed like bicycle lamps.

title: Phantom Hunt
location_name: Penbryn (Dyfed) - Unidentified farm in the parish
notes: Phantom hounds and hunters were once said to make an occasional appearance here.

title: Cwn Annwn
notes: This Welsh demonic hound has been reported in the area.

title: Black Dog
notes: This hound makes itself known only to those who will soon die.

title: Gytrack
location_name: Philpots Camp (Dorset) - The Big-Upon-Little Stone (aka Great-Upon-Little stone)
notes: This phantom black hound once kept the local populace away from the area at night.

title: Black Hound
location_name: Pontoon (County Mayo) - Crossing between Lough Conn and Lough Cullin
notes: This area is reputedly home to a black spectral hound. The ghostly creature is said to cause fear in living dogs.

title: Bad Omen
notes: A phantom hound is reputed to haunt the area around the monument, bringing bad luck or death to any witness.

title: Dog and Dragon
notes: A fearful dragon once made its lair near here but was driven off by a mysterious white shuck-like creature. The dog is still said to haunt the area.

title: Drowning
location_name: Portland (Dorset) - Cave Hole (or alternatively an unknown cave), close to the lighthouse
notes: The large, black dog that lives in an unknown cave grabs the unwary and drowns them.
address: Cave Hole, Portland, Portland DT5 2JT, UK

title: Large Black Dog
location_name: Portlaoise (County Laois) - Exact area unknown, possibly Ridge Road
notes: A black hound haunted the area where a man was once murdered.

title: Slender White Hound
notes: This phantom dog is said to resemble an albino wolfhound, though some say the entity is more likely to be a phantom deer.

title: Soul Gatherers
notes: The phantom dogs that frequent this area are reportedly sent to collect the souls of the recently dead, who in turn can sometimes be heard screaming and shouting for help. In 1953, a newspaper reported that a man was chased over the hill by what he claimed was the Devil, while the area also became a mini UFO hotspot after blue lights were seen and car engines cut out.

title: Headless Dog
notes: It is said that the town was once haunted by a headless black hound, appearing when danger threatened the town. Another ghost said to wander the general area of Preston was that of a white woman.

title: Black Dog Bounding
notes: The sight of a large ghostly black dog running in this area, heading towards Ely, is said to have quite common.

title: Black Dog
location_name: Princethorpe (Warwickshire) - Road known as Banbury Gap, between Princethorpe and Marton
notes: This black shuck would only to appear at midnight.

title: Searcher
location_name: Princetown (Devon) - Road between town and Plymouth
notes: This hound was once the pet of a traveller murdered in the area, and now continues to look for its owner. One man who reached out and tried to stroke the hound was struck by lightning.

title: Rattling Hound
location_name: Puncknowle (Dorset) - Road between village and Swyre, and area near the Old Rectory
notes: This large demonic dog is reputed to jump around, rattling a chain behind it.

title: King John's Hounds
datetime: Varies: 20 June or 23 June and 31 December (reoccurring)
notes: Twice a year, this pack of devil dogs is let loose in the neighbourhood. Sounds of ghostly plainchant have been heard coming from the manor house.
address: The Manor House, Purse Caundle, Sherborne DT9 5DY, UK

title: Chained Dog
location_name: Quemerford (Wiltshire) - Pound's Mill (no longer present), Marlborough Road, and fields in the area
notes: One witness at Pound's Mill spotted a black dog with a chain hanging around its neck. The hound vanished through a closed door. The same creature is also thought to haunt other areas in the hamlet.

title: Dark Hound
notes: A hound of hell was once said to lurk around the tower after dark. Some say it was the reincarnation of a murdered girl fed to her unsuspecting family by her stepmother.
address: Sandford St, Radcliffe, Manchester M26 2PG, UK

title: Searching Dog
location_name: Ramsbury (Wiltshire) - Area between village and Axford
notes: This phantom hound is said to be looking for its master who was killed while cutting down a tree. The hound's presence is denoted by the dragging of its chain.

title: Red Eyed Dog
location_name: Ramsey (Isle of Man) - Area around Milntown
notes: The large black dog reported here has glowing red eyes. It has walked past two witnesses without interfering with them, suggesting a benign entity.
address: Milntown Estate and Gardens, Lezayre Rd, Ramsey, Isle of Man IM8, Isle of Man

title: Haunt of Black Dog
location_name: Reach (Cambridgeshire) - The Devil's Ditch: defensive earthwork that ends close to Reach, crosses Newmarket Health, via A11 and A14
notes: One of the areas that the Black Shuck is said to run along, to see this creature will result in either the witness becoming insane within two months or indicate their death within a year.

title: Incorporeal Hound
location_name: Redcross (County Wicklow) - Country lane in the area
notes: A woman walking home tried to pat a black dog which appeared and padded along beside her. Her hand passed straight through the creature, and it vanished a few moments later.

title: Hound
notes: This location may have once been home to a ghostly hound.
address: Cloghkeating, Redhouse Hill, Co. Limerick, Ireland

title: Dog under Horses' Hooves
notes: A couple in a trap encountered the Shuck, and it ran under their horses. The driver tried to hit the black dog with his whip, but it passed straight through the creature. The same area is also haunted by a phantom coach, being driven at breakneck speed.

title: Combusting Dog
notes: A shuck which lurked here transformed itself into a raging fire, the size of a field, to scare a local man into respecting the Sabbath.

title: Skriker
notes: A man who encountered Skriker, the fiery-eyed black dog of doom, standing on the bridge expected his own life to shortly end; unfortunately, it was his eldest son and his wife who died within a few weeks of the incident.

title: Pack of Hounds
notes: This vicious pack of dogs belonged to a rogue priest, who allowed the hounds to freely hunt and kill whatever they found. Another legend says the Devil did not dare cross this river into Cornwall, as he feared the Cornish would turn him into a pie. Finally, a myth states that the river was a nymph named Tamara who was turned into a spring by her father, angered by her flirting with a couple of local giants.

title: Lightning Dog
datetime: December evening, possibly nineteenth century (if not, earlier)
notes: Walking home one night, a man encountered a large black dog. He tried to pat the creature, but his hand passed straight through the dog's head. The dog then yawned, and after smelling the sulphur-like breath, the witness fled, pursued by the dog. After a while, the man was thrown to the ground by a blinding flash, where he was found unconscious the next day. A legend says the phantom hound belonged to a murder victim, and now it tries to kill every man it encounters.

title: Large Hound
location_name: Rock of Dunamase (County Laois) - General area
notes: This area is reputedly haunted by a huge black hound which protects treasure buried somewhere under the structure. Another version of the legend says a swarm of rats and not a dog protect the treasure.
address: Park Or Dunamase, Dunamaise, Co. Laois, Ireland

title: The Hunt
notes: A wild hunt, complete with hell hounds, was once believed to be at home in this woodland.

title: Bright White Dog
notes: The witness to this entity crashed his car after swerving to avoid the partially transparent dog which ran out into the road.

title: Padfeet
location_name: Saddleworth (Greater London) - Gorge, was known as Hell Mouth
notes: A gorge or hillside chasm was home to the neighbourhood's demonic hounds.

title: Large Wolfhound
datetime: Seventeenth century onwards, last confirmed sighting early twentieth century
notes: Belonging to a drowned skipper, the dog failed to save his master's life, and now runs along the beach. His glowing eyes and black coat firmly place him in the shuck category. The creature was last seen by a man cycling in the area - he reported spotting a large shaggy dog which passed through a solid gate.

title: Trotting Dog
location_name: Sandwick (Orkney) - Road between Sandwick and Birsay
notes: Only appearing at night, a strange hound would trot alongside anyone walking between the two areas.

title: Black Hounds
notes: The forest is reported to be home of several black dogs which bring ill fortune to any witnesses. There are also stories of a ghostly white deer which darts between the trees, a headless woman riding on horseback, and a coach and horses which leaves the forest and heads towards a nearby farm.

title: Protective Shuck
location_name: Scunthorpe (Lincolnshire) - Road between Scunthorpe and Crosby
notes: This guardian dog appeared by the side of a woman, preventing a gang of labourers from attacking her. It vanished as she reached home.

title: Black Dog
location_name: Seatown (Dorset) - Old graveyard, where the A35 intersects the road leading away from village
notes: A large black hound is said to haunt this churchyard and has been seen vanishing into a large headstone.

title: Black Dog
location_name: Sedbergh (Cumbria) - Area around Capelthwaite farm (no longer present or renamed)
notes: This dog, known as the Capelthwaite, is the harbinger of death, and anyone who spots the creature will either lose their own life or that of someone close to them.

title: Shaggy Dog
notes: This shuck comes across as quite harmless and seems to appreciate the company of living animals.

title: Grey Dog
location_name: Selkirk (Borders) - B7009 between town and Langholm
notes: This phantom dog, slightly larger than a collie and in dire need of a groom, walks silently along this road at dawn and dusk.

title: Suicide Shuck
notes: This shuck is thought to contain the soul of a local man who committed suicide.

title: Expected Accident
notes: This large black dog would run across the road before leaping over a cliff that dropped several hundred feet. A few days later a serious accident would occur at that spot.

title: Gabriel Hounds
location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - Area around parish church
notes: These hounds are said to have the faces of men, and have been heard passing the church on dark, still nights.

title: Saucer Eyed Dog
location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - Bunting Nook, Graves Park
notes: This creature, with its unusually large eyes, has been known to turn itself into mist if threatened. A ghost hunter in the area reported to have spoken to a couple who witnessed a large black dog appear out of nowhere as they travelled down the lane.
address: Bunting Nook, Norton, Sheffield S8, UK

title: Scratching Hound
location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - Road outside the Botanical Gardens
notes: A phantom dog attacked one witness walking along this road - it bit his calf and clawed his back, leaving red weals. The man developed a high fever but later recovered after a spirit exorcism was performed.

title: Shock
notes: The black dog known as Shock to residents of the town would emerge from the sea and run up the hills. The entity was said to be headless, with white cloth covering the stump.

title: Vanishing Hound
notes: A dog was encountered by a local man as he walked home late one night. Wanting to move the creature from his path, the man threw a stick at it - the piece of wood passed straight through the hound, causing it to vanish.

title: Black Hound
notes: A priest was called in to rid a building of a large black dog that had taken up residence - the creature was banished to under the river Wyre, allowed only to come back when the river dried up.

title: The Padfoot
notes: This black, horse-sized hound unset the villagers when it paid them a brief visit.

title: Black Dog
location_name: Snitterfield (Warwickshire) - Brook House (AKA Bell Brook Inn)
notes: An unnaturally large dark hound ran across the lawn.

title: Disappearing Dog
notes: A large black dog here often escorted a local farmer as he drove his cart to market. When a girl tried to pat its head, it vanished.

title: Doggy Escort
location_name: South Muskham (Nottinghamshire) - Crow Lane, between village and Bathley
notes: A black dog would often appear along this road, trotting by the side of horse riders and motorcyclists.

title: Cu Sith
location_name: South Uist (Outer Hebrides) - Road alongside Lochnan Eilean?
notes: A woman walking with two friends in the pitch dark watched as a self-illuminating dog, the size of a collie but with a small head and no eyes, ran towards her. The creature vanished as it bounded past, but the two men with her saw nothing at all. Once she reached home and described what had happened to her aunt, the older woman told her it was a Cu Sith, a fairy dog.

title: Calf Sized Dog
notes: A calf-sized dog walking on the other side of the road was spotted by the passenger in a car (although not by the driver), unnerving the witness considerably.

title: Black Dog
location_name: St Audries (Somerset) - Road between village and Holford
notes: If it was not bad enough that this stretch of road was haunted by a shuck, some also say the area is also home to a phantom coffin that lies ominously in the middle of the lane and a grey misty shade of indeterminate origin.
address: St Audries park, West Quantoxhead TA4 4DS, UK

title: Noisy Dog
notes: This large black dog (some say it is a bear) is said to have sounded like a horse trotting, startling the witnesses who had expected to see a pony.

title: Black Dog
location_name: St Giles In the Wood (Devon) - Lanes in the area
notes: A large black dog was said to haunt the lanes and the farms in this area, as far down as Copplestone.
address: St Giles in the Wood, Torrington EX38 7JH, UK

title: Headless Dog
notes: A large black headless dog was said to patrol this road at night. Those brave enough to strike out at the hound hit nothing but air.
address: Ville au Roi, Guernsey GY1, Guernsey

title: Calf Sized Hound
location_name: St Pierre du Bois (Guernsey) - Road leading to St Saviours from L'Eree
notes: This large black dog was said to have caused one man to die of shock after it sat in his cart.

title: Shuck
notes: Large black dogs run about this area, a witness from the 1930s claiming to have seen one the size of a pony. A later witness said they had spotted the entity on more than one occasion during the early 2000s, and that the large hound was non-threatening.
address: Wandlebury Ring, Stapleford, Cambridge CB22 3AE, UK

title: Pack
notes: This ancient site is patrolled by a pack of large black shucks. The barrows are said to have been constructed by the devil.

title: Harriet
notes: The shuck which appears here is believed to contain the spirit of Harriet, once a local witch who remains much feared.

title: Phantom Pooch
notes: This ancient burial mound is reportedly haunted by a dog, which conceals itself beneath the hill.

title: Shuck with a Monk's Body and Dog's Head
notes: This strange entry in the 'shuck' category stands guard over a hidden hoard of gold. A legend states that St Felix instructed a monk and a dog to guard the buried treasure, and over the years the entities merged into a single presence.

title: Cat or Dog?
location_name: Sturminster Newton (Dorset) - Castle ruins and Newton Hill
notes: Reports vary on whether the creature that haunts this area is a large dog or very large cat. Either way, the entity was said to have large, saucer shaped eyes, and people went out of their way to avoid the area.

title: Headless Dog
location_name: Swaledale (North Yorkshire) - Humpbacked bridge leading to Ivelet
notes: The appearance of this headless hound denoted a forthcoming tragedy.

title: Cat Dog
notes: A large black dog was seen by two witnesses as it ran across the road ahead of them. It moved more like a cat than dog but did not look feline. One of the witnesses, a vet, was not able to identify it.

title: Black Dog
location_name: Swindon (Wiltshire) - Hill in Toothill area (was known as The Toot)
notes: This demonic hound once haunted the area before the area became a suburb of Swindon.

title: Large Dog
location_name: Swindon (Wiltshire) - Old canal bridge between Shrivenham Road and Drakes Way
notes: It was reported that a large dog with red eyes was seen by three people over a two day period. The hound appeared unable to cross an old metal bridge.

title: Black Dog
location_name: Swindon (Wiltshire) - Road between Moredon and Haydon Wick
notes: The road between these two locations was once believed to be the haunt of a shuck.

title: Guard Dog
notes: The site where three Jacobites are buried is haunted by a large black hound that ensures their graves are not disturbed.

title: Velvet Chain
notes: An invisible dog runs around this area, with a chain as soft as velvet. One would imagine this makes it one of the hardest entities to detect in the whole of the UK. A legend says that a clump of yew trees in the region of the village conceal a silver table.
address: Tarrant Gunville, Blandford Forum DT11, UK

title: Lady Howard
location_name: Tavistock (Devon) - Fitzford House driveway, heading towards Okehampton
notes: Lady Howard is said to have poisoned two of her four husbands, and now takes the form of a large black dog with a single eye which departs the driveway of Fitzford House, followed by a carriage pulled by headless horses, heading towards Okehampton Castle. Once they reach their destination, Lady Howard plucks a single blade of grass and then returns home, doomed to repeat the action until there is no grass left and she is released.

title: Black Dog Lies near Bed
notes: Never returning since the destruction of the building, a large black dog was seen on several occasions, lying across the beds.

title: Banishing the Dog
notes: The ancient custom of 'clipping the church' once preceded the chasing of the black dog, which was thought to be Satan, out of the village's boundary.
address: Thorne St Margaret, Wellington TA21 0EQ, UK

title: Horse sized Dog
notes: This great dog is said to be the size of a small horse, also sharing a few other equestrian characteristics.

title: Bloody Tongue
location_name: Thornton, Bradford (West Yorkshire) - Jim Craven Well
notes: Though the exact location of the well has now been lost, it was guarded by a large black hound with glowing red eyes, known as 'Bloody Tongue'. The beast was sometimes accompanied by the ghost of an old hag with a storm lantern.
address: Craven Ave, Thornton, Bradford BD13, UK

title: Barguest
notes: Little is known about the Throstlenest shuck, which patrols the area between Darlington and Houghton.

title: Vanishing Shuck
location_name: Thurton (Norfolk) - Near the village, precise details unknown
notes: While cycling home, a witness reported that a large black dog had leapt out in front of him, before disappearing through a solid wall.

title: Barking Hound
location_name: Tiree (Argyll and Bute) - Cladach a Chrogain beach
notes: This harbinger of doom, known locally as a Cusith, was renowned for its barking - if it yapped once or twice, it was a sign everything was okay, but three times warned of incoming danger for the listener or their family. Some people believe that the creature can become semi-human in appearance.

title: Cu Sith
notes: Two boys spending the night in a shieling hut were awoken by the sound of a large creature on the roof howling. When dawn came and they ventured out, they discovered the large prints of a Cu Sith on the turf roof.

title: Pack of Hounds
location_name: Todmorden (West Yorkshire) - Area between Todmorden and Mankinholes
notes: Wailing at the moon, this pack of devil dogs, known locally as Gabriel Ratchets' run over both land and water looking for people to terrify.

title: Dog as Large as a Bike
location_name: Tollesbury (Essex) - On the route to Jordan's Green, along the lane that leads up to Gorwell Hall.
notes: Shuck sightings appear quite common in this area. This encounter involved a woman coming face to face with a black dog with a bright red tongue, which was as large as her bicycle. It chased her (or ran alongside her, according to other stories) as she peddled down the road, disappearing soon after.

title: Snapping and Snarling Black Dog
notes: A cyclist reported being attacked by a large black dog while travelling down the coast road. Leaping off his bike to scare the animal, it disappeared before his eyes.

title: Black Dog
notes: This wish hound has been seen running along this stretch of road.

title: Lean Hound
location_name: Tring (Hertfordshire) - Area where gibbet once stood
notes: A black, lean dog with fiery red eyes would stand in the middle of the road in his village, sinking into the ground if approached. It was thought to be the spirit of a chimneysweep executed for murder.

title: Le Tchan de Bouole
datetime: Weather Dependent: Prior to a storm (last seen 2008)
notes: This dog, sometimes dragging a large chain, is said to appear before the area is hit by bad weather. Writer Linda Corby claimed that three of her friends had seen this large black hound a few hours before the Great Storm of 1987 (although there may be a more mundane explanation), while in 2008 a car full of people were chased by a black shadow accompanied by the sound of dragging chains.

title: Huge Dog
notes: A very large black hound is said to stalk the village, and may have attacked two locals in 1745, killing one and severely injuring the other.
address: Pilgrims Way, Trottiscliffe, West Malling ME19, UK

title: Large Dog
location_name: Turnditch (Derbyshire) - Unnamed lane, and Carsington Water
notes: During a thunderstorm, a driver spotted a large black dog cross the road ahead and move down a residential lane. The driver stopped to look for the hound, but it had vanished. A phantom black dog is said to haunt the nearby Carsington Water.

title: Black Dog
notes: A demonic black dog once haunted this area.

title: Hound
location_name: Tyneham (Dorset) - Boatswain's Coppice, between village and Lulworth
notes: This small piece of woodland was the home of a phantom dog that would physically prevent anyone from walking between the two villages.

title: Growing Hound
location_name: Uplyme (Devon) - Lane near Black Dog public house
notes: The black dog here increases in size until it matches the height of the local trees, after which it turns into vapour and vanishes. A local tale reports it haunted a farmhouse that once stood nearby - the farmer once chased the dog away, and it vanished into the ceiling of one of the rooms; on examination, he found much gold hidden in the roof where it disappeared.

title: Intangible Hound
location_name: Upper Booth (Derbyshire) - Exact area not known
notes: Out walking during the evening, a girl watched a phantom black dog pass through a wire fence.
address: Upper Booth Farm Campsite, Edale, Hope Valley S33 7ZJ, UK

title: Hound
notes: The creature known as the Upware Hound would walk the lanes at night.

title: Black Dog
location_name: Urchfont (Wiltshire) - Unspecified footpath that crosses fields and allotment
notes: This hound is thought to be a harbinger of misfortune.

title: Phantom Dog, Calf Size
location_name: Walberswick (Suffolk) - Road between the Bell Hotel and the Vicarage, and the beach leading to Dunwich
notes: For four hundred years, a black shuck has been reported in the area, local legend stating that it is the Devil incarnate. The creature has most been recently witnessed by two women during the 1980s; they reported seeing a large phantom dog, black in colour and the size of a calf.

title: Cursed Castle
notes: After being caught stealing from her customers, an old servant at the castle lost her job. In revenge, she bewitched the place - a large black dog appeared and created havoc. The creature was defeated by quotes from the bible read aloud by a local holy man.

title: Hunting Pack
notes: These Yeth hounds stalk as a pack after dark on the hill side.

title: Gurt Dog
notes: This shuck has proved quite helpful when encountered, appearing to lost walkers and leading them to safety. However, another entity in the same area known as the Wild Rider is considered fatal if met.

title: Gabriel Hounds
notes: Gabriel Hounds, demonic dogs that could fly, would often be heard in this region. Other witnesses thought they were geese.

title: Fire Breathing Hounds
notes: This pack of albino dogs run while breathing flame from their mouths.

title: Shuck Attack
location_name: Wells-next-the-Sea (Norfolk) - Lane leading to marshes between Wells & Stiffkey
notes: Local legend says a man was attacked by a shuck down a lane between Wells and Stiffkey. Author Christopher Marlowe was allegedly chased by the same creature a little short time later, while out looking for the beast.

title: White Dog
datetime: 23 June (or Midsummer Eve), sunrise (reoccurring). Encounters in 1995 and 1996
notes: Appearing to mark the start of the longest day of the year, this pale hound has been observed at sunrise. Some reports say the dog is accompanied by a ghostly druid. In 1995 a witness heard an almost unbearable high-pitched sound before observing a Neolithic man emerge from the barrow and walk away. The witness was terrified and fled. An experience in 1996 resulted in a witness feeling as if they had hit an invisible wall and something was trying to push them out, and they later read of someone four years earlier who had been grabbed by invisible hands.

title: Dog with Large Eyes
notes: This canine entity with eyes as large as saucers is said to roam the fields around the lane.

title: Pitch Forked Dog
notes: A farmhand discovered a dog laying in the hay - he poked it with his pitchfork, causing the entity to vanish. The sound of a coach has also been heard as it stops by the front of the house, though nothing is seen.

title: Cerberus
notes: This three-headed hound stands guard over a hoard of hidden treasure somewhere on this site.

title: Barghest
notes: This demonic dog is the size of a small bear, with yellow eyes as large as saucers. To stare the beast in one of these eyes will result in the death of the onlooker within days.
address: Wharfedale, Burley in Wharfedale, Ilkley LS29 6AH, UK

title: Former Knight
notes: One local legend says that a knight was driven to madness and suicide after having his love rejected by a ranger's daughter. After she died, the knight's ghost returned in the form of the shuck, to hunt and chase the spirit of the woman. Other local legends say that the shuck is just part of a larger phantom hunt.

title: White Dog
location_name: Wick St Lawrence (Somerset) - Back lanes around the village
notes: A young man driving his car was said to have been chased by a large white dog which reached up to speeds of thirty miles per hour for prolonged periods.
address: Wick St Lawrence, Weston-super-Mare BS22 7YP, UK

title: Shuck
notes: A calf-sized dog bringing death to all that see it runs this area of the Fen, which is currently a nature reserve.
address: National Trust - Wicken Fen Nature Reserve, 34 Lode Ln, Wicken, ELY CB7 5XP, UK

title: One of Three Shucks
notes: One of three bridges in Lincolnshire that is said to be a haunt of the black shuck.
address: Willingham by Stow, Gainsborough DN21, UK

title: Hairy Jack
notes: Known locally as Hairy Jack, the black dog which haunts this area is said to be covered in pigs' hair. A local man was said to have been pushed into a gatepost by an invisible dog after it placed its paws against his shoulders.

title: Panting Dog
location_name: Wilton (Wiltshire) - Field known locally as Severals (path of old Roman road)
notes: The phantom hound found here would be completely silent, though the dog's panting gives away its location. The Roman road is also haunted by a woman in black, said to move so quickly that she is impossible to catch.

title: Dog with Glowing Eyes
location_name: Winsford (Somerset) - Winsford Hill, and surrounding Exmoor area
notes: The devil dog found in Exmoor has been known to dematerialise slowly, leaving just a pair of red glowing eyes behind.

title: Bridge Dogs
notes: Two bridges along this stretch of road are the haunt of the black shuck.
address: Mill Rd, Wissett, Halesworth IP19, UK

title: Long Legged Dog
notes: A dark dog with long legs and drop ears suddenly appeared in front of a car. As the driver passed, the dog vanished as quickly as it had arrived.

title: Galley Trot
datetime: Unknown, but prior to twentieth century
notes: The large demonic dog once reported around Woodbridge was white and as large as a cow. The creature was said to give chase if people fled from it.

title: Ghostly Dog
notes: A large black hound has been observed here several times, recognisable from its lack of ears and saucer-sized eyes.

title: Talking Shuck
notes: The shuck in this yarn caught a man and spoke, informing the unfortunate gentleman that he would be dead within a week. Not surprisingly, he died the following night.
address: Woolpit, Bury Saint Edmunds IP30, UK

title: Black Dog
notes: Unsurprisingly, it is a black dog that haunts the road which has taken its name. It is believed to only reveal itself to those soon to die.

title: Huge Dog
location_name: Worksop (Nottinghamshire) - Blyth Road, near Hodsock Priory
notes: A black dog with glowing red eyes standing taller than a Great Dane was encountered by a woman driving home. The creature appeared to be dragging something large across the road, although the witness was not sure what it was. Distracted by something else for a few moments, the woman turned back to look at the hound, only to find it had gone.

title: Headless Dog
location_name: Yeaton (Shropshire) - Road between village and Baschurch
notes: The site of a murder which took place many years ago was said to have become home to a headless black dog.

title: Barghest
location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - Side roads and snickleways in city
notes: This large black hound with red glowing eyes, known as Barghest, Barguist or Barguest, is said to prey on humans alone along the snickleways in the city. The creature has also been spotted in the old castle area.

title: Pensioner
notes: The remains of an aging soldier were discovered in a barn along this road; much of the body had been reduced to ash, though the face could be recognised. The bales of straw surrounding him failed to catch fire.

title: Thomas Williams
notes: Human combustion, but in this case not so spontaneous. A sailor who liked his rum, Thomas Williams fell unconscious after a drinking session. His friend summoned a doctor who arrived after dark. The attending physician held a candle close to Thomas, who promptly caught light, bathed in blue flame. A bucket of water thrown over the fire only made the flames larger. The witnesses all left (for reasons not fully explained) and returned the following morning to find Thomas's torso completely consumed, but his shirt only charred. Thomas's friends said that he had been wicked and that the devil had come for him.

title: First Irish Case?
location_name: Ballybane (County Galway) - Private residence, Clareview Park
notes: The BBC reported that Dr Ciaran McLoughlin, West Galway coroner, had recorded a verdict of spontaneous combustion in a case he had investigated. The body of Michael Faherty had been found in his living room, totally burnt, although damage to the rest of the room was minimal.

title: Fire Dancer
notes: As Phyllis Newcombe (22) left the dance floor, her dress burst into flame, and she died a few hours later in hospital. Tests showed that her clothing could not be ignited with a cigarette.

title: Soldier
notes: The body of a soldier was discovered in a hay loft, much of his corpse charred by fire, but the surrounding area untouched by the flames. His legs and boots were also undamaged.

title: Mrs Stout
notes: Mrs Stout was seen going to bed, apparently fine, but found dead the following morning. Her body was heavily burnt, with parts not altered by fire crumbling when touched. Her nightdress remained undamaged by the fire. An inquest determined she had 'died by the visitation of God'.

title: Untouched Armchair
location_name: Ebbw Vale (Gwent) - Council house, exact location unknown
notes: The body of a seventy two year old was found reduced to ash, leaving only a pair of feet in socks, a soot covered skull, and an undamaged armchair in which most of the remains sat...
address: The General Offices, Steel Works Rd, Ebbw Vale NP23 6DN, UK

title: Barry Soudaine
notes: Barry Soudaine was found his kitchen, only a foot and a trainer remaining amongst a pile of ash. Some plastic fittings in the kitchen had melted, but there was not serious fire damage. The coroner recorded an open verdict regarding the death.

title: Elizabeth Clark
location_name: Hull (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Trinity Almshouse
notes: Elizabeth Clark spent the night here and was discovered in the morning covered in heinous burns which later claimed her life. No one heard any noises during the night, and although covered in the ash from her nightdress, the bed itself was not scorched.

title: Torso Reduced to Ash
notes: Grace Pett's burnt body was discovered in her house; other people living there were not aware of the fire that had killed her during the night.

title: Blackened Remains
location_name: Nairn (Highland) - Area close to Hardmuir Wood
notes: The charred remains of John Anderson could only be recognised by the horses and cart found by his body. One witness said that John had fallen from his cart while burning, his horses remaining still, and that the fire had been extinguish with buckets of water within fifteen minutes of starting. The doctor who performed the autopsy believed the fire to be 'no ordinary combustion'.

title: Annie Webb
location_name: Newport (Gwent) - Private house along Corporation Street
notes: When Annie was found, only a pair of legs, piece of arm and skull remained. A white tablecloth lay untouched nearby.

title: Mrs Carpenter Bursts into Flame
notes: While enjoying a boating holiday, Mrs Carpenter burst into flame and was reduced to a charred corpse.

title: Combusting Cat
location_name: Outer London: Stanmore (Greater London) - Anmer Lodge nursing home
notes: Peppi the cat enjoyed life at the nursing home, before exploding into blue flame with a bang and a flash. If the story is true, then Peppi could be a rare example of Spontaneous Feline Combustion.
address: Anmer Lodge, Coverdale Cl, Stanmore HA7 3DJ, UK

title: Body in the Basement
location_name: Reading (Berkshire) - Private house along Baker Street
notes: A young daughter found her mother burnt to death in the basement - some witnesses described the body as charred, while the room itself was barely touched.

title: Robert Francis Bailey
notes: This homeless man's burning body was found in an empty building, blue flame still coming from his charred body. It was though Robert was still alive when he combusted, as his teeth were dug deep into a wooden stair banister pole.

title: Human Fireball
notes: A girl walking down the stairs with a group of friends suddenly burst into flame. They quickly beat the flames out, but she later died of her injuries.

title: Woman's Skull
notes: This item is said to be impossible to remove from the building - it always teleports back!
address: Skull House Mews, Appley Bridge, Wigan WN6, UK

title: Chatty Fellows
notes: A local legend tells of talking skulls found in this graveyard, though what became of them is not known.

title: Bony Guardian
notes: Home to the skull of a slave, the object is said to scream if removed from the house. One former owner threw the skull into a pond, only to fish it out two days later after being kept awake at night by supernatural 'sounds'. The site is also home to phantom coach and horses, and/or a phantom funeral procession.

title: Woman in Black
notes: When the skull of a local girl was displayed here, several people reported seeing a spectral female figure standing by it. The skull's owner was convinced the item was cursed and died a week after finally giving it away.
address: Ocean Boulevard, Promenade, Blackpool FY4 1EZ, UK

title: Footfalls
notes: Loud footsteps can be heard traversing empty parts of the building - it may be the last remnants of the haunted skulls that once were on display here, since relocated to Turton Tower.

title: Cursed Cranium
notes: The cursed skull created havoc whenever removed from the property, so a solution-focused individual finally walled it up in a room of the hall.
address: Brougham Hall, Brougham, Penrith CA10 2DE, UK

title: Cursed Skull
location_name: Browsholme (Lancashire) - Browsholme Hall (referred to as Bowland Hall by Eric Maple)
notes: When the skull was last removed from the hall (as a joke), it was blamed for spontaneous fires and family deaths. The skull was quickly returned and has not left since.

title: Anne
location_name: Burton Agnes (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Burton Agnes Hall
notes: Falling in love with the hall, Anne decreed that upon death her head should be severed and stored within the house. Immediately after she was murdered, the house was plagued by crashes and bangs, which ceased when her head was removed from the coffin and brought inside. Her skull has since been bricked up to ensure it never accidentally leaves the homestead.
address: Rudston Rd, Burton Agnes, Driffield YO25 4NB, UK

title: George Whewell
location_name: Bury (Greater Manchester) - The Pack Horse Inn, Affetside
notes: If removed from the public house, this discoloured skull which once belonged to executioner George Whewell is said to scream until returned.
address: 52 Watling St, Affetside, Tottington, Bury BL8 3QW, UK

title: Screaming Skull
location_name: Bury St Edmunds (Suffolk) - Old Bury Gaol (possibly)
notes: The skull of William Corder, infamous for the Red Barn Murder, was stored in the prison - it was thought to be possessed by evil, and strange sounds and spectral figures were seen around it. The skull was finally removed and buried in an unknown local country graveyard.
address: Old Bury Rd, Bury Saint Edmunds IP28, UK

title: Duellists
notes: Two skulls are currently stored in the Towers, that when stored in previous places, have been blamed for causing two ghostly men to appear and begin fighting. Poltergeist activity is also attached to the stories, and a woman apparently dressed in mourning costume haunts the staircase of the tower (and possibly an old path outside the tower). The area outside the tower is haunted by a phantom coach.
address: Chapeltown Rd, Chapeltown, Bolton BL7 0HG, UK

title: Theophilu Brome
notes: Local legend says that when the skull of Theophilu Brome (or Broome) has been removed in the past, the house has been besieged by howls and wails. When the tomb of Theophilus Brome was opened in the local church, the skull was found to be missing.

title: Drink Obsessed
location_name: Derby (Derbyshire) - O'lafferty's public house (once known as The George), Sadlergate
notes: This entity, which enjoys switching the barrels off and causing general mischief, could be related to the thousand year old skull discovered in the basement.

title: Drifting Nun
notes: Appearing at dusk, the figure of a nun has been reported floating down this lane. Her skull is housed at Pennis Farm and it is said if the object is ever removed a great curse will befall the valley.

title: Livvy
location_name: Great Urswick (Cumbria) - General Burgoyne public house
notes: The bar manager watched a faceless girl in a white nightgown walk through the closed front door. The ghost is thought to be Livvy, who drowned in the Urswick Tarn. A skull nicknamed 'Jimmy', believed to be bad luck if touched, is kept in a cupboard that sometimes opens on its own.
address: Church Rd, Great Urswick, Ulverston LA12 0SZ, UK

title: Dafydd
notes: The skull of Dafydd, a Welsh Prince, can be found here. The last time it was removed from the building, the person responsible was plagued by shadowy figures and screams - these stopped once the skull was returned.

title: Unhappy Skull
notes: The skull (or skulls - one version of the story has two) causes trouble whenever removed from the site - it is said that to prevent 'accidental removal', the skull is now part of the brickwork.

title: Dafydd
notes: The skull of Dafydd Prince of Wales is housed in a box at the hall. A curse is said to befall the owners of the building if it is ever removed from the property.

title: Freda
location_name: Hull (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Ye Old White Hart public house
notes: It is said that if the Freda's skull located behind the bar of this pub is moved, the former owner returns and haunts the building until the cranium is replaced.

title: Reappearing Skulls
notes: Belonging to a married farming couple, the skulls came back to haunt the person who framed them for a crime that resulted in their execution. No matter how they were destroyed, the grinning skulls always returned to the hall, until the death of the man they cursed.

title: Talking Skulls
notes: A local legend speaks of a pair of skulls which once resided in the churchyard which would chatter away after dark.

title: Walled Up Skull
location_name: Lund (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Manor house
notes: Not much is known about the skull and its surrounding legend, other than to prevent the removal of the object, it was bricked up in the attic.

title: Francis Downes
notes: This building is reported to have had a skull bricked up within its walls, to prevent it from accidentally being removed and condemning the house's occupiers to misfortune or death. The macabre relic is thought to belong to Edward Barlow, a Catholic executed by Protestants in 1640.
address: Wardley Hall, Wardley, Worsley, Manchester M28 2ND, UK

title: Sister
notes: Two sisters once lived here and fell in love with the same man. One sister murdered the other, but before dying, she said that she would never rest in any grave. The dead sister's bones were stored in a container on the property and if moved, strange noises could be heard at night and the farm's cattle would die.

title: Headless Sister
notes: The house connected to the park is home to a skull of a woman - it is believed to belong to the ghost of a young girl who carries her head under her arm, killed by her own sister. Another legend connected to the park is the 'Elm-Ash' tree, a single tree comprising of both types of wood given in its name - it was said to have grown from the grave of a male suicide into which two wooden stakes were thrust to ensure he did not return.

title: Teleporting Relic
notes: The home of a skull with history unknown, the relic returned home despite several attempts by the property owners to dispose of it. They finally stopped trying and bricked up the skull behind a wall.

title: Homely Skull
notes: The home of another screaming skull, the building would echo with the sounds of cries and moans if the morbid item were removed.
address: Tunstead Milton, High Peak SK23, UK

title: Dicky of Tunstead
location_name: Whaley Bridge (Derbyshire) - Tunstead Farm, located at Combs Edge, between Whaley Bridge and Chapel-en-le-Frith
notes: Dicky is the name given to the skull that resided in the farmhouse (now thought to be buried on the grounds). The ghost of a girl that appeared in the 1880's was blamed on Dicky, as was the large black dog that followed locals around the farmland after dark.
address: Whaley Bridge, High Peak SK23, UK

title: Skeleton
notes: These prehistoric barrows were destroyed to make room for farmland, though one story states that one farmhand found and removed a human skull from the site, only to have the rest of the skeleton visit him during the following night, demanding the return of its head.

title: Orange Lights
notes: A handful of reports concerning strange lights were recorded during 2006. One family observed six orange lights hovering in the sky before appearing to disintegrate.

title: Green Light
location_name: Aberystwyth (Dyfed) - Sky above St James Square
notes: A witness spotted a green glowing bell-shaped object hovering above the square. The witness followed the object as it moved towards the pier and finally watched as the green light vanished out to sea. The witness later discovered the same object had been seen by a policeman who had been ridiculed at work after reporting the UFO.
address: St James's Square, Aberystwyth SY23, UK

title: Landing Craft
notes: This ufo report was based on a story made up by two young people returning late from a leave pass. As time went on, the story grew, and it eventually became that an UFO had touched down near a hangar on the base, and was recovered and removed for reverse engineering.

title: Round Platform with Handrail around Edge
notes: A female witness watched for 5 minutes as this disk carrying a dozen men dressed like sailors flew overhead, at a height of about 30 feet.

title: Little Green Men
notes: An elderly man who set up here for a spot of night fishing was invited into a UFO by two little men in tight green outfits, with visors covering their faces. They shone a light at him before saying that he was 'too old', promptly returning him to his fishing rod.

title: Golden Men
location_name: Anlaby (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Anlaby school, playing ground
notes: A youngster playing in the grounds of the school watched a round craft land and three figures dressed in golden clothing climb out. The child ran off to find friends to tell, and when he returned with someone the craft was already back in the sky - it quickly flew off.

title: Man with No Face
location_name: Anstey (Leicestershire) - Fields along Anstey Road, leading to Leicester
notes: Thought to be an early alien encounter (though it can also be interpreted as ghostly in nature), a woman playing in the fields came across a figure without a face dressed in black. Behind him, she could see a circular 'hut'. By the time she awoke her father, who was sleeping in a nearby field, both hut and man had gone.

title: Floating Lights
location_name: Ashburton (Devon) - Skies north of the village
notes: Admiralty Intelligence Officers investigated a series of strange lights which were seen in the skies over the area by multiple witnesses. The investigating officers also watched the lights rise into the air and vanish. The officers' report suggested that the lights may have been attached to a balloon used to lift wireless equipment to enable German spies to contact the mainland, although no evidence could be found.

title: Triangle
notes: Three large orange lights which formed triangular patterns were observed in the sky to the north-east.
address: Ashdown Forest, Wych Cross, Forest Row RH18 5JP, UK

title: Mystery Lights
notes: Some say that UFO sightings here go back at least three hundred years, though only more recently have crop circles and photographs of glowing balls of light been documented.

title: Hairy Creatures with Large Pointed Ears
location_name: Aveley (Essex) - Road in the area, exact location not known
notes: While driving home, a family of four were abducted by a blue UFO; the occupants described as four foot high hairy beings, with large eyes and pointed ears. After 3 hours, the family were returned to their car.

title: Saucer
location_name: Bagshot (Surrey) - Two miles past Junction 3, heading towards London
notes: Watched for between five and eight minutes, this saucer shaped craft had 'fifty pence piece type edges'. White lights could be seen at the front of the object, and blue lights at the rear.
address: Unit 4 Swift Ln, Bagshot GU19 5NJ, UK

title: Orange Globes
location_name: Bangor (County Down) - Skies around the area
notes: Several media companies and air traffic control were contacted after witnesses watched three orange globes fly across the night sky. The objects were visible for around five minutes before disappearing.

title: Purple Object
notes: A purple pear-shaped object was seen by Kris Reed, from his home. The ufo hovered before moving away, creating a noise like a washing machine. On the same night, another witness reported seeing a large red ball moving slowly across the sky.

title: UFO Technology under R&D
notes: A relatively new myth, this location is said to house downed UFO technology, under reverse engineering by UK scientists.

title: Green Triangle
location_name: Belfast (County Antrim) - Above Yorkgate shopping centre
notes: Shoppers at the centre reported seeing a green triangular ufo cross the sky above them.
address: York Gate Shopping Centre, Belfast BT15 1WA, UK

title: Inverted Mushroom
location_name: Bircham Newton (Norfolk) - Skies over Bircham Newton
notes: This strange flying machine passed overhead here.
address: Bircham Newton, King's Lynn PE31 6QR, UK

title: Mystery Rockets
location_name: Birmingham (West Midlands) - Skies above the area
notes: Fast moving objects seen during the day at low altitude and moving from east to west leaving a trail of sparks were identified as 'probably' shooting stars by the Edgbaston Observatory. One witness said that he thought the object he witnessed resembled a rocket.

title: Silver Saucer
location_name: Birmingham (West Midlands) - Skies over the area
notes: Four witnesses watched a silver object in the sky during a storm. One of the witnesses claimed the object would periodically pause in its movements before continuing.

title: Fifty Pence
notes: A nightclub manager reported watching a heptagon shaped object hovering before moving away at speed. The ufo projected two beams of light from the front and two weaker beams from the rear, and had clusters of red, yellow and green lights on the underside.

title: Men with Light Bulb Heads
location_name: Bolton (Greater Manchester) - Alleyway in town, exact location unknown
notes: A local lad escaped one night from his house to play hide and seek with his friends. While searching for them, he observed three figures in grey rubber suits and fishbowl helmets in a back yard; they also possessed pallid faces shaped like upside-down pears, with slits for eyes and mouth. When they moved towards the boy, he ran home.

title: Murphy Incident
notes: After spotting a ufo hovering over his field, a farmer reported the sighting to the Manchester Aerial Phenomena Investigation Team. The team reported by were followed by an unidentified man, while the farmer was warned to stop talking about the incident by the Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food, although the government agency denied they had spoken to the farmer.
address: Coal Pit Rd, Bolton BL1 7PD, UK

title: Mysterious Lights
notes: Sightings began at this UFO hotspot around 1977, though it was not until the 1990s that the popular media began to take an interest. Though media coverage has died down as of late, it has been reported that many locals still witness strange lights but are now less likely to talk about their encounters.

title: Almost a Disc
notes: A UFO videotaped over the village was circular with a semicircle missing from an edge, rather like a digestive biscuit with a bite taken from it.

title: Missing Time
location_name: Bordon (Hampshire) - Forest Road / Hendon Road area
notes: Two friends driving from the hospital to one of their parent's homes, a two minute trip, lost one hour forty minutes of their lives. Neither was aware of any periods of unconsciousness or 'jumps' in time. Two years later, another report came in of a missing time episode in the area; this time the witness lost three hours during a five minute walk.
address: Hendon Rd & Forest Rd, Bordon GU35 0BX, UK

title: Round Object
notes: A brother and sister watched a large round metal looking object with blue and red lights around the sides hovering over the field opposite their home. It was stationery for around three minutes, before disappearing in a flash.

title: Red and Orange Spheres
location_name: Bridlington (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Dotterel junction area
notes: Witness David Hinde reported two spherical lights in the sky, glowing red and orange. He watched them for ten minutes while they darted around, defying the movements of conventional aircraft.

title: Drone-Like UFO
location_name: Bridlington (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Off South Beach
notes: A witness watched a small drone-like UFO above the ocean before it vanished without trace.

title: Domed Craft
notes: This craft hovered over the town for around an hour before disappearing.

title: Saucer Occupants
location_name: Broad Haven (Dyfed) - Broad Haven County Primary School
notes: A large disc was seen landing near the school by fourteen of its pupils, some of whom also said men with pointed ears and dressed in silver clothing had climbed out. For several months after the event, sightings continued in the area.

title: Silver Suited Figure
notes: A seven foot (2.13 metres) tall silver clad entity was seen by Billy and Susan Coombs from the window of their farm. The family were plagued by other ufo related experiences around this time, although in 1996 businessman Glyn Edwards claimed he had wandered the area in a silver suit as a joke.

title: Flashing Orange
notes: A couple in their car thought a falling flashing object was going to hit them. They reported the object as being half the size of their Vauxhall Corsa, flashing orange, and being a dinnerplate shape. The ufo landed in nearby fields, but what became of it is unknown.
address: Stoneleigh Rd, Bubbenhall, Coventry CV8, UK

title: Plasma Ball
notes: Six young people watched a glowing ball hovering above the park. They originally believed the light to be from a police helicopter's searchlight before realising there was no rotor blade sounds. The ball remained for five minutes before shooting upwards and disappearing.
address: Grove Rd, Burbage, Hinckley LE10, UK

title: Snaking Lights
notes: A married couple, along with other witnesses, observed five circular lights in the sky. The lights were different colours and 'snaked' for a few minutes before all turning white and shooting off in different directions. Twenty-two years later a witness heard a 'TV white noise' sound emerging from the sky which lasted around 20 minutes.

title: Boomerang
notes: While outside, a male witness heard aircraft engines and looked to the sky. He watched a 737 appear briefly in the gap of the clouds, closely followed by another craft which was twice the size of the recognisable airplane. The latter craft was boomerang shaped with squared wing tips and dark grey or black in colour. After disappearing into the clouds, the witness did not see either craft again.
address: Royal George Rd, Burgess Hill RH15, UK

title: Flying Arrow
location_name: Burnley (Lancashire) - Over Rowley lake, Brunshaw road
notes: A night fisherman spotted an arrow shaped object travelling at a fantastic speed above him. The craft was silent, had a light at each corner and left no contrails. The witness believed it could have been manmade. And possibly connected to a nearby military base.
address: Brunshaw Rd & Rowley Ln, Burnley BB10 3HX, UK

title: Ball Lightning?
notes: A van driver reported an extremely bright ball of light the size of a steering wheel hovering above the road. After around three seconds it darted past the van, the driver estimating it moving between 80 and 90 mph.
address: A29 & Bury Hill, Pulborough RH20 1NP, UK

title: Four Spheres
location_name: Bury St Edmunds (Suffolk) - Howard estate, heading towards Fornham St Martin
notes: The Ministry of Defence confirmed that Suffolk Police had handed over a report of four black spheres which one person had seen moving across the sky in formation.
address: Howard Estate, 7 St Olaves Rd, Bury St Edmunds, Precinct IP32 6SP, UK

title: Revolving Lights
location_name: Bury St Edmunds (Suffolk) - Sky over the Cathedral
notes: Several people, including a member of staff at the Cathedral, reported observing a luminous grey UFO over the area. One witness contacted the police.
address: 5 Angel Hill, Bury St Edmunds, Bury Saint Edmunds IP33 1UZ, UK

title: Helicopter
location_name: Buxton (Derbyshire) - Skies above Harpur Hill area
notes: A phantom helicopter was reported flying around the area at night several times over a two year period. Press and police were unable to discover who or what was behind the mysterious craft.

title: Flying Wedge
notes: Two pilots in a 737 reported narrowly avoiding a large black wedge shaped UFO while flying into the airport from Milan. The object passed so close to the passenger aircraft that one of the pilots ducked.

title: Silver Disc
notes: The occupants of the abbey were said to have been terrorised when a large radiant silver object flew overhead just as they sat down to eat. Many years later, a passing farmer listened to phantom plain chant while watching a ghostly form move around the gravestones.

title: Dark Disc
notes: A dark disc-like object was filmed flying over the castle.

title: Airship
notes: Two men speaking in a strange tongue were seen by a witness here - they made their escape in an airship. Though it is thought they were German, no country in the world had an airship capable of making that kind of flight at the time.
address: Mid Glamorgan, Cardiff Rd, Caerphilly CF83 1JP, UK

title: Rugby Ball
location_name: Caistor St Edmund (Norfolk) - Caistor Hall Country Club
notes: While stopping at the club to pick up a coat, Stanley Taylor watched an object hovering in the sky. He said it was the shape of a rugby ball and around the size of two double-decker buses. The dull silver object gave off a hum and, with a sudden 'whoosh' flew off, quickly becoming a dot in the clouds. Another person nearby was said to have heard the sound but did not witness the object.
address: Caistor St Edmund, Norwich NR14 8QN, UK

title: Spinning Balls
notes: Two witnesses watched three large white spinning balls holding equal distance from each other for around twelve minutes. The balls eventually moved at speed; one flew left, one right, and the third straight up, all vanishing from sight within a couple of seconds.

title: The British Roswell?
location_name: Caldbeck (Cumbria) - Cumbrian fells - exact area not known
notes: A disc shaped alien spacecraft crash landed in this area. Three people witnessed the event; a farmer and son who were held by military personnel for two weeks, and a poacher who claimed to have seen two bodies, with 'faces like the Devil's' (the poacher had a breakdown soon after). The RAF (?) removed all evidence of the incident from the area.

title: Circle of White Light
notes: Travelling along a busy road, one witness watched a circle of white light slowly cross the rooftops of four houses, as if a huge torch had been shone on the surfaces, before disappearing. Nothing could be seen in the sky, and there were no vehicles on the road that could have caused the light.

title: Solway Spaceman
location_name: Carlisle (Cumbria) - Coastal marshland NW of town
notes: Jim Templeton, while taking family photographs, caught on film a strange white figure wearing what appears to be a spacesuit floating behind his daughter. Jim stated that the figure was not visible at the time of taking the photograph. Author David Clarke wrote that the figure is likely to have been Templeton's wife Annie, the 'spaceman' illusion created by overexposure.

title: Bright Light
notes: Initially thought to be a star, a small bright ball of light started to follow this witness in her car as she returned from Christmas shopping. As the car reached the edge of Carnwath, the ball suddenly shot into the sky and disappeared.

title: Streaming Light
notes: Now thought likely to be Aurora Borealis, parish records mention a bright light over the skies of the village, changing from white to the colours of the rainbow. Occasionally flashes of light and smoke could be seen.

title: White Shape
notes: Filmed by a witness for several minutes, a round ball of white light darted around the sky. The light changed shape and size several times before finally disappearing behind a dark cloud.

title: Unidentified Snowy Object
notes: A witness here watched a bright object for around twenty seconds before it disappeared into a dark and heavy snow cloud.

title: Dumbbell Shaped Craft
notes: Elsie Oakensen saw this craft as she drove through the village, returning from work. Passing underneath, her car lost power, and she experienced fifteen minutes of 'missing time' - later hypnotic regression revealed she was taken on board the craft.

title: Downed Cigar
location_name: Cleveland (North Yorkshire) - Exact location not known
notes: A rumour floating around on the internet said that a cigar-shaped ufo, around six metres in length, was forced down by two RAF fighters. The crashed craft deeply embedded itself in mud, and rather move it straight away, authorities buried the ufo with the intent to move it once interest died down. Whether this happened is unknown.

title: Anchor
notes: During Mass, the congregation at this church were interrupted as an anchor dropped from the sky and caught on the church door. The people investigated and spotted a ship floating above them; one of the crew diving overboard and swimming towards them through the air towards the anchor. The congregation grabbed the sailor, but the bishop demanded that they let him go. As the group released the man, he floated upwards and once on board the ship, someone cut the rope attached to the anchor - the ship sailed off into the sky. The anchor was said to have been on display at the church.

title: Darting Light
notes: A group of children playing football watched a light dart around the sky at incredible speed. It momentarily stopped moving before darting off once again in the direction of the A12 road. The light later returned to the area and was seen by other witnesses.

title: Three Discs
notes: The famous black and white photograph of three discs flying over the town taken by a 15 year old schoolboy remains a mystery to this day.

title: White Light
notes: After a wind turbine was found damaged (one blade bent, another laying on the ground), several theories emerged to explain why the incident had happened. County councillor Robert Palmer reported seeing a round white light which hovered near the area, while the media reported that John Harrison had seen a massive ball of light with something akin to tentacles reaching for the ground. While the 'ufo hit our turbine' stories were popular with the media, there is a high chance that the blade just fell off, hitting the second blade as it fell.

title: Fog Bank with Flag
notes: In what could be an early UFO sighting, a man walking along the beach watched a localised 'fog bank' sitting on the sand, with a huge flagpole sticking out. After ten minutes, the object rose and faded from view.

title: Saucer
notes: While staying with relatives, a witness watched a saucer shaped object with blue lights. The saucer was bright metal, and the object hovered for a while before moving off at high speed. The witness ran downstairs to tell someone but was so shocked that they were unable to speak and soon realised that even although the sighting only appeared to last a few minutes, three hours had gone by.

title: Rockets
notes: Witnesses outside Crawley railway station watched two rockets heading west, with other reports coming from across Sussex during the same week.

title: Flying Saucer with Lights
notes: A mother thought her daughter had been attacked after hearing her screaming along the road. As she ran inside, the mother received a call from the mother of her daughter's friend who said that they had seen a flying saucer with coloured lights around it. The mother ran outside and spotted the object in the sky.

title: Fifty Pence
notes: A witness walking to the nearby college suddenly realised there was no traffic on the roads. Something made him look up only to see a large a fifty pence (heptagon) shaped craft hovering silently in the sky. It had a red underbelly, bright silver sides and moved from left to right. The witness looked down briefly, during which time the craft started to move away at incredible speed. The witness chased it across the road (traffic had appeared again) but it had disappeared. The witness later found out that he had lost an hour of time.

title: Silver Ball
notes: From the passenger seat, a witness watched a silver ball bearing-like object hovering in the sky. The large, stationary object hung quite low and did not move for two minutes, despite the windy conditions.

title: Four Balls
notes: Four orange balls of light were spotted silently floating in the sky, forming a pyramid. They remained for quite some time.

title: Glowing Saucer
notes: Driving home from his shop, as Robin Peck approached the village his van's engine cut out. He climbed out of the vehicle and as he lifted the car's bonnet, Robin realised that this hair was standing up. It was then Robin realised that a glowing, saucer shaped object was hovering around ninety metres away. After a short while, the object darted away, and the van started working again.

title: Orange Triangle
notes: Two children reported seeing a large orange triangular shaped object above them, making a funny noise.

title: Silver Cigar
notes: A silver cigar shaped aircraft was spotted flying high over East Dean. It left no vapour trail and moved steadily before darting off at an incredible speed.

title: Football Shaped Object
notes: Just down the road from the famous Bentwaters UFO incident, several villagers have reported other strange craft in the skies above the area.

title: Black Shape
notes: A group of eight people watched a perfectly round, dark object silently traverse the sky. No lights were visible from the object. A strange fiery UFO has also been spotted off the coast, though the date of this sighting is unknown.

title: Orange Orbs
location_name: Felixstowe (Suffolk) - Skies northwards at Old Felixstowe
notes: On a very hot summer evening two orange orbs were seen falling from the sky. Moving extremely fast, they appeared to chase each other. The objects were watched for around ten seconds before dropping and disappearing behind the horizon.

title: 2004 Hotspot
notes: Filey was named as UFO hotspot of the year, after local UFO groups received 48 reports of unidentified flying objects over a twelve month period.

title: Orange Energy
location_name: Flamborough (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Area near the lighthouse
notes: One witness spotted two red lights hovering silently hovering over the cliff before they descended and vanished. Sheep in a nearby field appeared destressed. The following night, the same witness spotted a small drone-like craft which transformed into a rectangular orange object, like a ball of energy, which crackled and buzzed. The object remained visible for a few minutes. Two people nearby were able to hear the crackling of the object but could not see it.

title: Rectangular Block
location_name: Flamborough (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Thornwick Bay
notes: One witness heard a quiet mechanical sound and could make out the faint shape of dark rectangular shape pass their caravan.
address: Haven Thornwick Bay Holiday Village, N Marine Rd, Flamborough, Bridlington YO15 1AU, UK

title: Dead Alien
location_name: Gateshead (Tyne and Wear) - Hedley Street and St Cuthbert's Church
notes: Five-year-old Robert Hall, together with other children, encountered several strange figures shortly after observing an egg-shaped flying object. One of the figures resembled a short bigfoot, while another had a skeletal body and bat wings. After taking a sample of Robert's blood, the figures left. The following day, a short 'grey'-like alien appeared to Robert. The boy's screams brought his uncle to the scene, who killed the alien with a shovel. A local story says the alien's body is buried in a nearby churchyard (or concealed within a secret room).

title: Washing Machine
location_name: Gilling West (North Yorkshire) - Area around Crabtree Farm
notes: During an overnight camping and fishing trip, Philip Cotton and Jean Clark watched a rotating orange and blue object that made a sound like a washing machine. They retreated to their car because the noise grew too loud, but reported the light was 'friendly' and they did not feel afraid of it.

title: Three Orange Globes
notes: An apprentice joiner spotted three bright orange globe-shaped lights in the sky while walking from work. He rushed home and told his parents, who laughed at his tale until a local radio broadcast mentioned several people had reported orange lights over the city.

title: Bright Ball
location_name: Glendale, Isle of Skye (Highland) - Exact location not known
notes: Three men watched a bright glowing ball, between four to five feet in diameter pass a few feet over a house and descend into a field where it disappeared in a small flash of light. The following day they went to check where the ball had vanished but found nothing. One witness said the ball had a white centre but glowed different colours at the edges.
address: Glendale, Isle of Skye IV55 8WW, UK

title: Sharp Turner
location_name: Glendale, Isle of Skye (Highland) - Skies in the area
notes: Two men walking dogs along the shore at dusk spotted a bright star-like light at high altitude. One witness said that he would have passed it off as a military jet, but it made an instant 140 degree turn before disappearing into clouds.
address: Glendale, Isle of Skye IV55 8WW, UK

title: Flying Square
location_name: Glossop (Derbyshire) - Road between Glossop and Chapel-en-le-Frith
notes: Teacher Michael Valance encountered a flying box hovering over the moors. He reported the UFO as glowing and having four bright windows. The craft vanished after several minutes.

title: Flying Wheelie Bin
location_name: Gorleston (Norfolk) - Skies over Brasenose Avenue area
notes: Several people spotted a strange wheelie bin-shaped object flying silently across the sky, fire seemingly coming from the bottom of the craft. One man photographed the object, and although the images were reasonably clear, the object remained unidentifiable until it was reported in the local press the 'craft' was a home-made hot air balloon.
address: Brasenose Ave, Great Yarmouth NR31, UK

title: Huge Shape
notes: A family driving home spotted a huge object in the sky, so large that it appeared to blot out the sky as it moved slowly across. When the family arrived home, one phoned the local radio station to report it and was told 'many' others had also reported it, and that the RAF had told the station that it was a crashing Russian spy satellite.

title: Circular Lights
location_name: Great Ryburgh (Norfolk) - Skies in the area
notes: At least three witnesses observed a very large circle of flashing lights for ten minutes as it crossed the sky. The lights moved both clockwise and anticlockwise, while another set pulsated.

title: Green Light
notes: This witness spotted a solid green glowing ufo with an orange trail as it crossed the sky heading east. The object generated no sound.

title: Abduction
