notes: This female abductee reported watching a male human vivisected while she was on board the alien craft. Later the same day, several witnesses reported lights and strange shapes in the sky.
address: Tetney Lock, Tetney, Grimsby DN36 5UW, UK

title: Triangular Craft
notes: Two police officers spotted a bright white light in the distance while driving along the A3. As they drove under the light at the flyover, they could make out three white lights on a black triangle shape with a dim pulsating red light in the centre. Other drivers had pulled over and watched the shape. Eight more lights were spotted drifting over Guildford.

title: Red Light & Blue Man
location_name: Hainault Forest Country Park (Essex) - Lake and Cabin Hill
notes: Two police officers watched a large red light moving by the lake - as they approached it vanished suddenly. The officers then spotted a strange white object; this also vanished. Three days later, two men walking their dogs encountered a blue figure that stood around 2.5 metres tall and 1.25 metres wide. Like the UFOs, this figure also vanished.
address: Hainault Forest Country Park, Chigwell IG7, UK

title: Red Lights
notes: Seven red lights were seen bobbing up and down as they slowly moved across the sky before fading away. The witness was told the lights were actually 'light reflecting off migrating birds', although they had not seen this effect prior to the sighting or since. The village was considered a hotspot for UFO sightings during the early 2000s.

title: CE III
location_name: Halkyn Mountain (Clwyd) - Exact location not known
notes: An alien contactee claimed to have witnessed a ufo landing on the mountain, where the craft left imprints. The witness was later contacted by a strange being with short curly hair who wore a blue jumpsuit.

title: Downed Craft Hoax
location_name: Haltwhistle (Northumberland) - Ten miles north of town in a marshy area
notes: A witness wrote to the RAF claiming he had seen a large silver disc crash and that he had investigated inside the craft before it had (mostly) sunk into the marsh. The RAF asked the police to investigate; the witness admitted that while he had seen the craft and thought it was going to land, he had fabricated the rest of the tale.

title: Hanbury Orbs
notes: A BBC cameraman caught three bright lights hovering over the Malverns on video. A local UFO group named them as the 'Hanbury Orbs', a group of lights commonly seen above the landscape in this region.

title: Black Cylinder
location_name: Harlow Hill (Northumberland) - West of Harlow Hill
notes: Four people watched a black cylindrical object, possibly rotating, for ten minutes as it crossed the sky. The object changed direction once, moving north to south before turning west.
address: Harlow Hill, Newcastle upon Tyne NE15 0QD, UK

title: Blue Spinner
notes: A witness watched a bright blue object 'like a spinning top' cross the sky and quickly fade into space.

title: Yellow Oval
notes: Two yellow, oval bright lights were seen silently moving over the moors from Haytor by people standing in a carpark. The lights moved in a swooping motion and would loop the loop.
address: Haytor Vale, Newton Abbot TQ13 9XS, UK

title: Flashing Lights
notes: Five people out metal detecting all watched the sky light up with flashing red, blue, purple and green lights which illuminated the field which they stood on. The lights stopped suddenly.

title: Pill-Shaped Craft
notes: One witness recalled hearing a strange noise from the sky and, looking up, spotted three metallic, pill-shaped containers flying through the air attached by a strong chord.

title: William Loosley's Abduction
location_name: High Wycombe (Buckinghamshire) - Woodland near the town, but exact location non-existent
notes: A hoax penned by David Langford, the premise being that the great, great granddaughter of William Loosley discovered a hidden account of his UFO sighting on the 4 October 1871, one day before William mysteriously vanished forever.
address: Woodlands, Bassetsbury Ln, High Wycombe HP11 1RB, UK

title: Flying Cross
notes: Two police officers chased a large cross-shaped UFO in this area as it flew low overhead. Another witness observed the craft later in the evening hovering and spinning on the spot.

title: Bright Lights
notes: While walking their dog, a witness watched several fast moving bright lights high in the sky performing irregular manoeuvres. The witness later checked for aircraft in the area, but the UFOs could not be explained by the few aircraft which were out on the night in question.
address: Windy Fields Rd, Holymoorside, Chesterfield S42, UK

title: Fast Ball
location_name: Hull (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Noddle Hill Playing Fields
notes: At least four people watched a fast moving ball, around the size of a car, pass overhead. After the object moved away, the witnesses were left with an odd static feeling.

title: Small or Far Away?
notes: A pilot and another witness stopped their car to have a better look at an orange oval ufo. The pilot did not recognise the oval as any aircraft that she was aware off, and because of the lack of clouds and landmarks could not determine whether the object was large and very far away or smaller and closer.

title: Abduction Photographed
notes: A former policeman photographed a strange creature running away from him on the moor - he gave chase, only to see a large silver disc take off and fly away at fantastic speed. The man soon realised that he had 'lost' almost two hours of the day; he was later hypnotically regressed and recalled being abducted. The area has a history of fairy sightings, which may be connected to this abduction story.
address: Moor, Wells Rd, Ilkley LS29 9JF, UK

title: Orange Lights
notes: A witness photographs six strange orange lights as they passed over the town at night.

title: Nocturnal Light
location_name: Ipswich (Suffolk) - A12 towards Woodbridge, after Orwell Bridge
notes: A family driving home one night watched as a bright light manoeuvred around the sky, before disappearing vertically into the clouds at a fantastic speed.

title: Spinning Lights
location_name: Ipswich (Suffolk) - Orwell Bridge and the A12 towards Felixstowe, and Nacton Road
notes: Two dull, spinning lights followed a teenager driving across the bridge for several miles. The lights always remained in front of the car, turning at the same time as the driver. Several other witnesses reported seeing similar lights around the Nacton Road area at roughly the same time.
address: The Quadrangle Centre The Drift, 21 Nacton Rd, Ipswich IP3 9QR, UK

title: Mushroom
location_name: Isle of Arran (Ayrshire) - Skies over area
notes: High in the sky, this mushroom shaped object was said by the witness to be bright blue underneath. The ufo was also seen be the witness's wife and two daughters.

title: Figure in Silver Suit
location_name: Isle of Sheppey (Kent) - Roadside of Sheppey Way
notes: A figure in a baggy silver suit climbed down a grassy verge by the roadside, watched by several motorists. It is thought this has a connection with a UFO seen a few hours earlier in the area.

title: Triangle
notes: A large triangular craft was watched as it moved irregularly across the sky. It made no sound and would occasionally disappear and reappear.

title: Triangle
location_name: Ixworth (Suffolk) - Road between Diss and Bury St Edmunds
notes: Two witnesses in a car spotted a triangular craft, around the size of a house, hovering above a field. Soft lights were visible under the ufo. The witnesses also noticed other road vehicles were slowing down to look at the object.
address: Ixworth, Bury Saint Edmunds IP31, UK

title: HALO
location_name: Kendal (Lancashire) - RAF Wharton, outside of the town
notes: RAF Wharton was believed to be the home of the UK stealth aircraft project, HALO (High Altitude, Low Observable) being the project name. Some believe this to be the result of back engineering a downed UFO.

title: Triangle
location_name: Kirkheaton (West Yorkshire) - Skies above the village
notes: A witness looked out the window at night and spotted a large triangular object, at least the size of a bus, hovering over the house and street. It had numerous lights around the edge, but the lights did not appear to cast any shadows. The object silently flew off in the direction of Holme Moss.

title: Tailed Sphere
notes: The Kirkwall coastguard reported a very bright spherical object with a tail, moving north west to the south east. The object then split in two. The Aberdeen coastguard reported seeing the same or similar two objects a little later.

title: Domed Craft with Flashing Lights
notes: During this sighting, the witness claims that the flashing lights became painful to look at, and he felt his body tingle, as if hit by a bolt of electricity.

title: Robot Attack
location_name: Langford Budville (Somerset) - Lane near the village, exact location unknown
notes: A woman driving near the village was attacked and abducted by a large robot after her car broke down - it took her to a domed craft where she was experimented on by three creatures.

title: Flying Loaf
notes: Several witnesses reported seeing a UFO hovering in the sky, resembling a glowing loaf of bread.

title: Balls of Fire
notes: These balls of flame where seen many times during the winter months, spinning like wheels.

title: Giant Pumpkin Smelling of Acid Drops
location_name: Leiston (Suffolk) - Beach north of Leiston, in the skies
notes: This strange UFO was reported over the sea.

title: Large Yellowish & Greenish Ball
notes: Observing the power station here (or maybe formed by it?), the unnatural ball only hung around briefly before leaving the scene.

title: Abductions
notes: Jenny Randles claims that there were two missing time episodes during these decades.

title: Mile Long Craft
notes: Lee Jones, his wife and her mother watched a huge saucer shaped craft hovering in the sky, clouds occasionally covering the object. A passenger jet that passed near the craft enabled Jones to estimate the ufo was about a mile long. Other witnesses reported seeing the craft throughout June and earlier in the year.

title: Anti-Ship Mine
location_name: Livingston (Lothian) - Dechmont Woods (a monument now marks the area)
notes: A forestry worker was attacked by 2 robotic creatures that resembled mines, with six protruding legs. They gave off a smell that knocked him out. Later, when the site was examined by police, they found evidence of a fight, and marks from the robot's 'legs'.

title: Cube With Arms
notes: A UAP was spotted by multiple witnesses. The object was difficult to make out, due to the piercing light it emitted, but was thought to be similar to a cube with 'arm light' appendages. A witness estimated the object to be between four and six times the size of an aircraft. The UAP was initially stationary but moved north over the Edinburgh flight path.

title: Downed Craft
notes: Some researchers believe that a UFO crashed on the mountain, the event covered up by the UK Government.

title: Downed Craft
location_name: Llandrillo (Clwyd) - Cader Bronwen hill, east of village
notes: A loud crashing sound was heard by many locals, coming from the nearby hill. They thought it was a light aircraft or military training jet, but RAF personal reported finding nothing, though they kept the area closed to the public for several days afterwards. Some now report that a small alien craft crashed there, and the wreckage recovered by the Government.

title: Alien Chase
notes: A couple in a car were reportedly pursued by a UFO. Both people claimed to have seen the occupant of the craft and described the figure as large with frizzy hair.
address: Myers Ln, Loxley, Sheffield S35 0AP, UK

title: Figures in Jumpsuits
location_name: Manchester (Greater Manchester) - Banks of the River Mersey, south Manchester
notes: Walking home with her two daughters, Lynda Jones and the children watched a twenty metre long orange/pink oval craft drop into a field. When they approached to take a better look, the ufo had changed to a grey colour and was hovering sixty centimetres above the ground. They all ran away after one daughter started screaming, and in later hypnotherapy Lynda recalled being abducted by almost human figures in jumpsuits.

title: Darting Lights
notes: Several circular bright lights were observed by a man walking his dog in the night sky. The balls were all different sizes and travelled very fast in a zigzag motion, creating no sound. The man was left shaken after watching the display but did not contact the authorities fearing they would think he was mad.

title: Dark Torpedo
notes: Reported as being over thirty metres long, this long, thin scareship of unknown origin passed over the town late at night.

title: Fast Moving Light
notes: Two people in a garden watched a light in the sky as it moved fast, suddenly stop, and move back on itself before disappearing.

title: Domed Object
location_name: Meriden (West Midlands) - Footpath in area, exact location not known
notes: While out walking, Cathie Connelly encountered an illuminated domed object surrounded by tall men wearing one-piece overalls. The men had strangely tall foreheads, and one man cleaned his hands using a duster smiled at the witness. As Connelly left the area, she turned back and realised they had gone.

title: Orange Lights
notes: It was reported that traffic was brought to a standstill when between 10 - 15 orange lights were seen moving in a southerly direction. The UFOs all moved at the same speed, before stopping and moving upwards. The sighting lasted for about ten minutes.

title: Triangular Object
notes: Two friends sitting in this car park watched as a large, brightly illuminated triangular craft pass overhead. After the sighting, as they tried to drive away, the car froze, as if held in place by an unseen force.

title: Aurora?
location_name: Ness (Outer Hebrides) - Skies over the area
notes: The strange doughnut-shaped exhaust trail left across the sky is said by some to have been created by the top secret US hypersonic Aurora spy plane.
address: Ness, Isle of Lewis HS2 0TA, UK

title: Soldier Shot by UFO
location_name: Newbiggon on Sea (Northumberland) - Old RAF base
notes: A soldier once based here alleged, in later life, to have been abducted while on guard duty at the base. Remembering the abduction from his dreams, the man claimed he fell unconscious after being hit by a thin beam of light.

title: Orange Ball
location_name: Newcastle Under Lyme (Staffordshire) - Over Wolstanton Marsh
notes: A police officer reported watching a spherical orange object pass directly overhead, moving from north to south. Three other witnesses were with the officer at the time.
address: Marsh Ave, Wolstanton, Newcastle-under-Lyme, Newcastle ST5, UK

title: Orange Lights
location_name: Northrepps (Norfolk) - Sky above North Walsham Road
notes: A witness reported watching dozens of bright orange lights high up in the air. The lights were visible for several minutes before fading out and were compared to missile countermeasures used by military aircraft.

title: Banana
notes: A flying banana shaped object was seen in the sky over the stadium a couple of times. It was described as bright and made up of several different colours.

title: Scareship
notes: A possible early ufo encounter. A married couple spotted two men wearing caps working on a cylinder-shaped craft perched on the ground. As the married couple approached for a better look, the men disappeared, and the craft took off.

title: Bus Sized UFO
location_name: Oldham (Greater Manchester) - Saddleworth Moor, near Dovestones Reservoir
notes: Saddleworth Moor has been home to many strange and curious sightings of UFOs over the years, the largest of which was the size of a British double decker bus seen in 1975. More recently, six lights spread out in a hexagon pattern which vanished after several seconds were seen in the sky, quickly followed by a sighting of a strange van which also vanished.

title: Pair of Lights
notes: A couple of men watched two lights hovering quite close to the water, which darted off at incredible speed as they approached. The area was home to Project Cobra Mist, which involved radar waves that followed the earth's curvature.

title: Power Cut
notes: A witness recalled how bright orange lights had moved across this area and cars stopped, their power drained. Fazakerley Hospital windows were damaged on the same night.

title: V
location_name: Outer London: Uxbridge (Greater London) - Skies over Hillingdon
notes: Larger than a jumbo jet, this V shaped craft was only spotted for a few seconds before disappearing.

title: Light Show
notes: Several witnesses in different areas of Paignton reported seeing up to one hundred orange, green and red lights in the sky. Witness accounts varied - one person said they moved in formation, while another reported some were moving to the left while others to the right. One person reported a blue light that the other ufos moved towards.

title: Yellow Lights
notes: A driver stopped his car to watch five large yellow lights hanging in the sky above the village. The balls disappeared after thirty seconds.

title: Green Flash
location_name: Pershore (Hereford & Worcester) - Skies above the area
notes: One witness reported seeing a green flash in the sky, followed by a green light 'swirling' before vanishing.

title: Scareship
notes: Seen by two police officers, this UFO resembled a large airship, travelling at a then unheard of speed.

title: Orange Lights
notes: Four orange lights were seen moving across the sky at night, their speed said to be several times quicker than other aircraft.

title: Ship
location_name: Portland (Dorset) - Exact location not known, was observed from Lyme
notes: A strange ship appeared in the skies over Portland for seven or eight minutes. Sails, rigging, and men armed with pikes could be observed on board, and the vessel was accompanied by stormy weather.
address: Isle of Portland, Portland DT5, UK

title: Silent Vulcan
location_name: Preston (Lancashire) - Sky near to the university campus
notes: Christened the 'Silent Vulcan' by students because of its similarity to a Vulcan bomber (but without any sound), this large aircraft was seen by a student in the skies of Preston. He stated the object shot off towards the dock area, moving at incredible speed.

title: Space Man
notes: Mark Taylor photographed a strange man floating high in the sky. After local press picked up the story it was revealed the sighting occurred on the same day a child had their life-sized stormtrooper balloon swept away by the wind.

title: Rugby Ball
location_name: Ramsden Heath (Essex) - Skies over Hanningfield Reservoir
notes: A zigzagging rugby ball shaped object, larger than an aircraft, was reported by a witness. The ufo was surrounded by red and orange lights.

title: Red Ball
location_name: Reading (Berkshire) - Skies above Basingstoke Road, Whitley
notes: Three teenagers spotted a strange red glowing orb flying overhead as they were out walking.

title: Spinning Saucer
notes: A schoolteacher and several pupils watched a white spinning disc with pulsating lights pass overhead.

title: Radar/Visual Sighting
notes: Several UFOs were detected by the base's radar, crossing the North Sea at speeds unmatched by any contemporary aircraft. One UFO was intercepted by a US fighter, but was soon lost.

title: CEII
location_name: Rendlesham (Suffolk) - Rendlesham Forest - near former USAF Base
notes: Once only famous in ufological circles, the story of the landing of an unknown craft at Rendlesham during the early 1980s still causes controversy today. Unrelated to the UFO sighting, the Big Cats of Suffolk website reported there had been a string of big cat sightings in the forest prior to 1995, with occasional sightings still reported.
address: Rendlesham Forest, Tangham, Woodbridge IP12 3NF, UK

title: Green Light
notes: Two police officers watched a bright green light quickly pass over the town and disappear over the horizon.

title: Diamond Formation
location_name: Ring's End (Cambridgeshire) - Skies above March Road
notes: Andrew Warren and his son watched a diamond formation of lights as they passed overhead. Though the source of the lights appeared close, there was no sound, and the pattern appeared to hover before moving away. They also saw two other patterns of light, one of which was a 'V' shape.

title: Black Oval
notes: One witness watched as a black oval shape unnervingly blocked out the night sky as it passed by.

title: Orange Jellyfish
notes: A family of four observed a set of three orange lights, 'undulating like a jellyfish', drifting over the town and heading upwards before they disappeared amongst the stars. The UFOs were also witnessed by two people on the outskirts of Romford.

title: Spaceship Alley
notes: This part of the county has so many reports of strange nocturnal lights that it justifies the dramatic nickname. However, cynics name British Aerospace as a possible source for the displays.

title: Alien Fairy
notes: Three figures, all less than four foot tall with tissue paper wings, drifted into a house in the town and interrogated a housewife for an hour. She said they looked like fairies, though they had laser beams on top of their heads and left via an eight foot long UFO.
address: Bluestone Walk, Rowley Regis B65, UK

title: Circular Object
location_name: Saxlingham (Norfolk) - Holt Road heading towards B1156
notes: A witness pulled their car over to have a better look at a circular object hovering about twenty metres above the road, as did another car behind them. The object did not make a sound and the witness estimated it was around five metres in diameter. The witness then lost their nerve and drove off, as did the occupant in the other car.

title: Glowing Light
location_name: Scarborough (North Yorkshire) - Reasty Hill, Silpho Moor
notes: A forty-six centimetre disc covered with strange hieroglyphics was found by three men who were investigating the sighting of a ufo which glowed eerily in the night sky.

title: Spherical Object
location_name: Sea between Isle of Man and Belfast (Isle of Man) - Sky
notes: A captain, first officer and flight engineer of an aircraft flying from Belfast to the Isle of Man reported a bright white spherical object that became an orange beam.

title: Black Object
notes: A large dark object was seen passing over the town, buzzing loudly.

title: Red Cloud
location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - Skies about Woodthorpe area
notes: A red cloud which covered the roof of a house was seen by three people. Different coloured beams of light, mainly green, pink and blue, emerged from the cloud for around fifteen minutes before vanishing.

title: Dark Red Cloud
notes: This 'cloud' which shot beams of light, heating up the cold winter night, hovered over the town before heading off towards Ireland.

title: Hotspot
notes: This area was claimed to be a UFO hotspot by enthusiasts.

title: Stretched Plectrum
notes: While watching some F15s over the Wash, a witness spotted a dark grey craft, the shape of a stretched plectrum, emerge from a cloud behind them. The ufo shimmered before entering a small cloud from which it did not reappear.

title: Low Flying Egg
location_name: Sopley (Hampshire) - A338 between Avon and Sopley, near bridge over the river Avon
notes: Two separate groups of witnesses watched as this craft, around 25 meters long, passed low over the road, melting the tarmac surface. Vehicles and lights nearby were disrupted.

title: Flying Disc
notes: A fighter pilot, while out on routine patrol, reported a near miss with a silver domed disc - the UFO travelled towards him at fantastic speed, veering off at the last possible second.

title: Radiant Ship
notes: A brightly coloured ship passed overhead, on an otherwise clear night.

title: Landing UFO
location_name: St Helens (Merseyside) - Field in Rainhill area
notes: Robert Bennett told the St Helens Star newspaper how he had worked with police and radio enthusiasts in chasing a strange craft across the St Helens sky. The pursuit ended after three police officers watched the ufo take off after landing in a field.

title: Bright Flashes
notes: A witness spotted two objects the shape of spinning tops which flashed brightly as they followed each other across the sky.

title: Scareship
notes: A dark cigar shaped object with a light was spotted moving above the grounds for several minutes before vanishing into cloud.
address: Burghley House, Burghley House, Peterborough, Stamford PE9 3JY, UK

title: Flashing Lights
notes: A low flying circular object was spotted by two friends as it moved across the sky towards them. As it approached, they noticed two small flashing lights on the side of the craft that grew in intensity until the glare became blinding. The witnesses shielded their eyes to avoid the light; as they did so the object made a strange sound and disappeared - no trace could be seen in the sky.
address: Stanton under Bardon, Markfield LE67, UK

title: Halo
notes: Driving back from work in the early hours of the morning, this witness spotted a halo shaped object hovering and darting around the sky before vanishing.

title: Flying Mushroom
notes: A woman, her husband and his mother watched glowing orange, mushroom shaped object for an hour as it hovered overhead, before moving away quite quickly towards the Stoke area.

title: Saucer Landing
notes: A saucer that changed colour from red to green to blue landed in an empty field on this estate, glowing brightly and seen by at least six people. The object took off, spinning, and headed off over the horizon.

title: Large Saucer
location_name: Stoney Stanton (Leicestershire) - Skies over the village
notes: With lights on either side and a flashing one on top, this large saucer shaped object was reported moving slowly across the sky.

title: Five Fliers
location_name: Stratford-upon-Avon (Warwickshire) - Skies over the town centre
notes: Over 100 people watched five objects moving over the town centre for a period of around thirty minutes. One witness said three of the lights formed a triangle, another hovered to one side, while the fifth light darted around. Another witness claimed they looked like little balls of flame.

title: Blond Haired Alien
location_name: Sudbury (Suffolk) - A1092 between Long Melford and Haverhill
notes: Rosalind Reynolds was abducted from her car by a tall alien with messy blond hair accompanied by several classic greys. She claims that after the event she developed a psychic talent for foreseeing disasters.

title: Hovering Craft
location_name: SW1 (Greater London) - Sky over Palace of Westminster
notes: Seen hovering silently in the sky, this UFO had white, red and green lights and remained visible for around 90 minutes.

title: Flashing Light
location_name: SW11 (Greater London) - Sky above Clapham Junction (also Wimbledon)
notes: A pilot waiting for a train at Clapham Junction observed a flashing light which would periodically disappear and reappear elsewhere in the sky in an instant. The object was still visible when the pilot reached his home in Wimbledon. Now a retired training captain, the man has never been able to explain the object.
address: Clapham Junction Station, Clapham Junction, London SW11, UK

title: Sky Ship
location_name: Swanscombe (Kent) - Church and nearby Blue Anchor public house
notes: A group here for Sunday service watched a strange ship drop anchor from the heavens, which became entangled around a gravestone. One of the crew jumped overboard and headed towards the anchor, moving as if swimming. Witnesses grabbed the stranger as he reached the ground; he struggled briefly before drowning, even though on dry land. The remaining crew in the ship severed the rope of the anchor and sailed away; the strange artefact was melted down and used to make the hinges on the local church doors.

title: Sky Ship
location_name: Tailltenn (now known as Teltown) (County Meath) - Exact area not known
notes: Conghalach Cnoghbha, High King of Ireland, and his men were said to have spotted a sailing ship in the sky here. A crew member of this ship threw a spear at a salmon, also in the sky, which missed and landed close to Conghalach. The crew member leapt from the ship and, swimming in the sky, came down to the ground to retrieve the spear. One of the King's men grabbed the swimmer, who screamed out 'I'm drowning!'. Conghalach told his man to release the crew member, who 'swam' back to the ship which then disappeared into the clouds.
address: Teltown, Bog Road, Co. Meath, Ireland

title: Large Eyed Aliens
location_name: Tarbrax (Lanarkshire) - A70 between Tarbrax and Balerno
notes: Ambulance mechanic Gary Wood and his friend Andrew encountered a black shiny UFO along the A70 - it had no lights and was silent. After realising that they had lost one and a half hours, they underwent hypnosis where they discovered they had been abducted by frail grey aliens with large eyes.

title: Ruggers Ball
location_name: Taverham (Norfolk) - Skies south of Red Woods
notes: A group of school children reported seeing a flat-bottomed UFO shaped like a ruggers ball hovering above woodland. The object had three bands of lights, hummed, and appears to disturb the tree line.

title: Flash
location_name: Thorpe Market (Norfolk) - South off the B1436
notes: A disc-like craft was observed by one witness. It had two lights which looked like windows, and a small red light towards the rear. The craft vanished in a flash of white light.

title: Abducted Policeman
notes: Alan Godfrey, a policeman on the Yorkshire force, reported seeing a large UFO along the road, and experiencing missing time. He was later hypnotised and 'discovered' that he had been taken inside the alien craft and subjected to experiments.

title: Flying Lights
location_name: Todmorden (West Yorkshire) - Skies in the area
notes: The skies between Todmorden and Bacup have been home to frequent reports of unidentified lights which have alarmed both people and animals alike.

title: Orange Lights
notes: A group of ten orange lights flying in a straight line were spotted by fishermen, a few forming triangular shapes. Changing position slightly, the witnesses could see around twenty more lights flying low behind the tree line, all moving in the same direction.

title: Flying Cigar
notes: Dozens of people contacted the local media with reports of a strange flying cigar which passed over the town. One witness managed to take a reasonably clear photograph of the UFO with his mobile phone.

title: Sixty Red Lights
notes: Sixty or more red lights were seen by several people over the skies of Uckfield. The lights moved in close formation and eventually moved away.

title: Brighter than Daylight
notes: Driving through the area, two people suddenly became aware of an unusual light coming from within a nearby barn. The people initially thought the light belonged to a man who would collect milk from farms in the area. As the people drove closer, three beams of brilliant light shone down from a disc shaped craft hovering above the field to the right of the car. As they passed the lane, the lights shut off and the craft flew northwest, leaving the area in complete darkness. The following day, the people returned to Uppertown and visited a farm. They noticed that all the produce had been dumped in the yard, as though the milk collector had quickly fled from something.

title: Moped
location_name: Upton Scudamore (Wiltshire) - Farm track, opposite a lane leading down to Upton Scudamore
notes: On a dense, foggy night, author Andrew Collins (pictured) and a team of four other paranormal researchers encountered a small light that moved a few feet off the ground, rather like the headlamp of a moped. The light vanished into an area blocked by low hedgerow, and when they examined the ground the following morning, there was no evidence of tread prints.

title: Burning Triangle
notes: Five office workers watched a triangular object with burning corners move across the sky from north to south. They only watched the ufo for a few seconds before it faded into the distance, moving at high speed.

title: UFO Capital
location_name: Warminster (Wiltshire) - Skies above town, normally near Cradle Hill
notes: To celebrate the countless UFO sightings over the town, the Marquis of Bath and the local tourist board officially called the town the UFO Capital of Britain.

title: Fireball
notes: A fireball with three points was seen speeding across the sky. The witness said that the object was like an aircraft.

title: Figure with Laser Eyes
location_name: Warrington (Cheshire) - Drayton Road, Risley exit from the motorway, and Warrington Road
notes: A driver pulled over to watch a two metre tall creature, dressed in silver and with thin beams of light coming from its large eyes, as it moved along the embankment by the side of the road. When the witness arrived home, he discovered that he had 'lost' almost an hour. Just prior to the driver's sighting, a local woman spotted the figure from her garden as it walked through the former munitions factory on Warrington Road.

title: Heptagon
notes: From their back garden, a witness watched a dark grey heptagon shape silently fly over their house.

title: Hovering Triangle
location_name: Welwyn Garden City (Hertfordshire) - Sky over city
notes: Two friends spotted a triangular shaped ufo hovering over a lake. The craft tilted upwards before simply vanishing, resulting in the friends running home without looking back.

title: Zigzagging Lights
location_name: West Bromwich (West Midlands) - Sky over Hateley Heath
notes: This witness observed between ten and twenty slow moving lights zigzagging across the sky in formation. Each light was semi-transparent, yellow with a red centre and moved silently from the south east, heading towards Walsall.
address: Huntingdon Rd, West Bromwich B71 2RP, UK

title: Giant Meteorite
notes: A large fireball was seen passing overhead. Several hours later there were a series of shockwaves that were attributed to an earth tremor.

title: Green Disk
notes: Several observers watched the fluorescent green ufo as it hovered over the area. A driver on a nearby road managed to photograph the object.

title: Greenish Grey Craft
notes: While patrolling the town centre, PC Colin Perks spotted a hovering craft that glowed a greenish grey colour. Colin said the UFO was around thirty foot long and that he could see portholes along the side. After several seconds it flew off.

title: Man in Silver Suit
location_name: Winchester (Hampshire) - Chilcomb Road, near the A272
notes: A couple out driving had to pull over as their car it began to shake out of control. They noticed a large cigar shaped object with three faces peering out nearby, and a bearded man dressed in a silver suit moving towards them. The strange entities then vanished, and the couple were able to drive away normally.

title: Ballerina Alien
notes: Councillor Adrian Hicks recalled in 2009 his encounter with an alien entity along a busy street. He described the creature as having large oval eyes, looking almost human, and wearing a tutu. The alien shuffled along the street like a penguin.

title: CE I
location_name: Woodbridge (Suffolk) - Road out to Martlesham from Woodbridge
notes: A triangular UFO was seen slowly floating towards the ground, several independent witnesses reporting it as a crashed aircraft. No aeroplanes were reported missing, nor any wreckage found.

title: Zig Zagging Light
notes: On two occasions, a witness travelling through the area observed a bright light flying low in a zig zag motion. During the second sighting, the witness checked their flight tracker app, but the craft did not appear. On both occasions, the witness found online comments that other people had seen the lights.

title: Foaming Wolf
location_name: A1067 (Norfolk) - Road between Fakenham and Norwich
notes: A driver on the way back from the cinema in Norwich encountered a large black wolf eating a carcass along this road. The creature was described as standing around a metre at the withers, with yellow eyes and black matted hair. The driver slowed and the wolf briefly looked up before continuing to eat. The witness continued home, shaken.

title: Creature from the Tomb
notes: The vampire that once frequented this castle, a former lord of the estate, lived under the site and would emerge at night to attack the local villagers. An outbreak of plague was also attributed to the unholy creature, and this resulted in the villagers digging the monster up from its shallow grave and burning it.

title: Female Spectre
notes: A local tale says that this old manor house is home to a female vampire, though many believe the entity to be ghostly in nature.

title: Walking Dead
location_name: Berwick on Tweed (Northumberland) - Exact area(s) unknown
notes: A former wealthy man who died was said to have returned from the grave and walked the streets at night. A pack of dogs were said to escort him. Finally, a group of local men dug up the grave and incinerated the body, though most of the men are said to have died soon after from an unknown disease.

title: Vampire Attacks
location_name: Birmingham (West Midlands) - Glen Park Road (Ward End) and Saltley, Small Heath and Alum Rock areas
notes: A gentleman, described as black and in his twenties, bit another man walking along the street before pouncing on neighbours who came to the victim's aid. One woman present was said to have had a chunk bitten out of her hand. Police, however, stated that they had received no reports of such an incident and dismissed the story as an urban myth.
address: Glenpark Rd, Saltley, Birmingham B8, UK

title: Blood Sucker
location_name: Blair Atholl (Perth and Kinross) - Glen Tilt, north of the town
notes: Two poachers shacking up in a bothy reported being attacked by a creature that drank blood from one of them. The pair managed to fight the creature off, and it flew away. The story is very similar to the vampire tale that happened at Fealaar, Aberdeenshire.

title: Doggett
location_name: Blandford Forum (Dorset) - Area near Eastbury Park (no longer standing)
notes: A corrupt manservant who stole thousands of pounds from his employer, Doggett finally killed himself, and now drives his phantom horse and carriage along this area. One local story says he returned as a vampire; after his body was exhumed many years after his death (from St Mary's Church in Tarrant Gunville) it was found to be uncorrupted, with a rosy tint to the cheeks.

title: Speed Wolf
notes: Although this creature is reported to resemble a normal wolf, it moves at fantastic speeds and covers great distances in a single bound. It is unclear whether the wolf is a physical entity, but the nearby village of Wormhill claims to be the location where the last wolf in England was killed in the sixteenth century.

title: Vampire
notes: One of the few vampire reports in the UK, this creature attacked a young girl in this building. One of her brothers witnessed the attack and shot the monster in the leg. The blood trail enabled them to track it to the village graveyard, where it was dug up and burnt.
address: Low Hall, Croglin, Armathwaite, Carlisle CA4 9SG, UK

title: Welsh Wolf
location_name: Denbigh (Clwyd) - Area between Denbigh and Wrexham
notes: Reported to be the size of a horse, this huge wolf would create mayhem in the region, feeding on livestock, dogs and the flesh of men.

title: Wolf-headed Skeleton
location_name: Dogdyke (Lincolnshire) - Langrick Fen, but exact location unknown
notes: A local archaeologist digging in the peat discovered a human skeleton but with a wolf's head. The man took his discovery home, but during the night he found his house besieged by a werewolf. The archaeologist spent the night barricaded in the kitchen as the beast tried to gain entrance to his house, and as the sun came up and the werewolf left, the man took the skeleton and reburied the bones where they were found.

title: Coffin Carrying Returners
notes: Several servants fell ill and died after taking seed corn from the Abbey barns. The servants soon returned, their coffins carried on their backs while running across fields and knocking on doors. More villagers fell sick, and the pestilence only ceased when the dead were dug up and burned. The survivors fled the village and left it to fall into ruin.

title: Suicidal Lycanthrope
notes: Andrew, a teenager from the village, was found dead at the crossroads having killed himself with a penknife. One of his friends reported that Andrew had called him just before midnight, saying that his skin was changing and that he was turning into a wolf.

title: Howl like a Foghorn
notes: A large black creature of unknown origin caused havoc here in the 1920s when it killed dozens of sheep. Though rarely seen, the creature was said to have a howl like a foghorn. Locals allocated the blame on a lycanthrope.

title: Werecat
notes: Sir Roger Smith and his two wives have their tomb within this church, with the effigy of Lady Ann possessing a dark mark on one whist. A local legend says that Ann was a witch who could change into a cat - the mark on her statue shows where a butler hit Ann in cat form with a small axe.

title: Wolf Man
notes: A woman walking home after dark reported seeing a grey man with a wolf's head, apparently stalking a large rabbit. The werewolf vanished when disturbed by a stag that ran out from a nearby wooded area.

title: Vampire
location_name: Fealaar (Aberdeenshire) - Hut hidden away in a wooded area
notes: Two hunters were said to have been attacked by a vampire while spending the night in the bothy. This story is very similar to the vampire tale that happened at Blair Atholl, Lowlands, and is likely to be derived from it.

title: Stinker
location_name: Flixton (East Riding of Yorkshire) - General area
notes: There are stories that the area around Flixton is home to a werewolf, which has glowing red eyes and a particularly bad body odour.

title: Gorbals Vampire
notes: An urban myth circled the area during the 1950s that the graveyard was home to a vampire with iron teeth. At the story's height, it is said that hundreds of children patrolled the site, looking for the monster which, they thought, had kidnapped and eaten two local children. Another story to emerge from the Necropolis is that of the white woman - a feminine statue that sits on top of the grave of three people killed in a tramcar accident is said to turns its head and watch visitors as they pass.

title: Abhartach
location_name: Glenullen (County Derry / County Londonderry) - General area, Abhartach's final resting place marked by a large stone with a thorn tree growing up from the side
notes: In a battle for the throne, the warrior Cathrain killed his rival Abhartach. Abhartach, however, returned from the grave two days later and preyed on the locals. Cathrain once again killed the undead fiend, but two days later, Abhartach came back and continued his attacks. Cathrain discussed the problem with druids, who told him to stab his adversary through the heart with a weapon made of yew. This he did, and Abhartach never returned.

title: Witch
location_name: Holywell (Clwyd) - Basingwerk Abbey, and forests of Longdendale
notes: A witch who could change into a wolf was unable to return to human form after the Abbot of Basingwerk asked heaven to stop the witch from killing innocents. The witch/wolf ran into King Henry II's hunting party and after a vicious fight, the party slayed the creature.

title: Wolf Men
location_name: Kingdom of Ossory (County Kilkenny) - Ossory was a sub-kingdom, most of which was in County Kilkenny
notes: It was said that men from this kingdom could transform into wolves whenever they pleased. However, while in wolf form their human bodies would be left at home. If moved, the person would be trapped as a wolf for the rest of their life.

title: Werewolves
location_name: Loch Langavat, Isle of Lewis (Outer Hebrides) - Exact area unknown
notes: The island was once home to a colony of wolfmen - even though they are extinct, disturbing their graves is reported to release their spirits.

title: Hooded Dead Figure
location_name: Lochmaben (Dumfries and Galloway) - Woodland around Lochmaben Castle
notes: Tom Robertson investigated the woods after hearing stories that animals had been found drained of their blood. He encountered a tall figure dressed in sacking with a hood over its head, which black eyes and grey face. The creature leapt into a tree and swung away. Eight years later Robertson went looking for the creature again, finding it and taking a couple of photographs.

title: Strange Wolf
notes: This area was reportedly the stomping ground for a werewolf - sightings were reported up until the 1990s.
address: Valley of Rocks, S W Coast Path, Lynton EX35 6JH, UK

title: Cormac Tadhg McCarthy
location_name: Macroom (County Cork) - Carrigaphoca (aka Carrigaphouka, aka Carrigaphooca) Castle
notes: Charged with tracking down Irish rebels, Cormac once ate the flesh of one man he caught. This may have damned him, and as such a few believe he still lurks the ruins of the castle. The site is also home to a Pooca, a ghastly grim entity which reputedly attacks visitors if the opportunity arises.

title: Evil Monk
notes: An evil monk returned from the dead as a vampire, leaving his grave at Melrose Abbey to feed at a local nunnery. When the other monks discovered this, the bravest stayed up one night with a large axe, and when the vampire climbed out of its tomb, it was beheaded (although the vampire's evil presence remains in the abbey). The building is also the final resting place of Michael Scot, a great wizard who discovered the secret of flying, and was locally named as building Hadrian's Wall single handed.

title: The Damned
notes: A sighting of a cloaked, vampire-like entity seen standing next to the cemetery in 1980 was explained 38 years later when Dave Vanian of The Damned admitted he went for a late night walk after recording at Rockfield Studios. As he walked through the cemetery dressed in a black cloak, he was spotted by the driver of a mini who almost crashed. The story of the cloaked figure appeared in the press a few days later, and Vanian decided not to say anything.

title: Smoky Vampire
notes: A plethora of sightings of a red eyed creature that could turn to smoke resulted in the cemetery becoming a second home to all manner of researchers, reporters, and fame seekers. The sightings died down after legal action against one of the vampire hunters. More recently, North London Paranormal Investigators encountered a pale figure in a suit, resembling an undertaker, while touring the area.

title: Church Dog
datetime: Circa early to mid nineteenth century
notes: This churchyard is rumoured to be the haunt of the black shuck, which may have been a shape-changing local wizard. Another tale says the dog's remains were bricked up in the church walls and he is nothing more than a folk memory of the interment.

title: Fox or Wolf?
notes: The area was once said to be the home to large grey creatures; thought by some to be wolf/fox hybrids, others believed them to be werewolves.

title: Modern Myth?
location_name: Sefton (Merseyside) - Church and roads in the area
notes: First-hand accounts appear to be rare, but one story says a nurse and her taxi driver both spotted a werewolf in this area.

title: Slain Dwarf
location_name: Slaghtaverty (County Derry / County Londonderry) - Abhartach's grave, aka Slaghtaverty Dolmen
notes: When a tyrannical dwarf was murdered by a local chieftain, his body was placed in the earth here. Less than a day later the dwarf returned, more vicious than before. He was killed again and reburied but came back and tormented the entire country. The chieftain consulted a druid, and following the wise man's instructions, slayed the dwarf for the final time, placing the body into the ground head first, robbing him of his magical powers.

title: Wolf on Hind Legs
location_name: Stafford (Staffordshire) - German War Cemetery, Camp Road, between Stafford and Cannock
notes: In two separate sightings, two men reported a large hairy creature standing on its hind legs, between 180 - 200 centimetres in height. One of the men approached it, but the creature ran off.

title: Hiding from Vampires
location_name: Stoke on Trent (Staffordshire) - Number 3, The Villas
notes: A body found in the building was surrounded by garlic, salt, crucifixes, and other items reported to scare off vampires - tragically, the dead man was found to have choked to death on a clove of garlic that lodged in his throat as he slept.

title: Vampire
location_name: Thornton Heath (Surrey) - Private house, Beverstone Road
notes: One piece of source material states that at three separate times over three months in 1938, a woman reported being attacked by a winged creature which bit her neck and drew blood. However, it is likely that the 'vampire' attacks occurred during the 1970s, and the 1938 incidents were more poltergeist is nature (contemporary newspaper reports support this). Even so, the 1970s incidents also involved poltergeist activity, and the woman at the centre of the claims alleged she endured a tiger attack.
address: Beverstone Rd, Thornton Heath CR7, UK

title: Box Carriage
location_name: A31 (Surrey) - Hogs Back, Guildford bound. 1 mile Northeast of Tongham junction
notes: Travelling home in 2007, a driver watched a horse drawn box carriage cross the road around 100 metres in front of him. The witness reported that he could not make out too many details as it was raining hard, though he was able to see a dim lantern towards the front of the carriage. As the driver reached the spot where he had seen the vehicle pass, he realised there was no side road where the coach and horses could have crossed. Another witness in the 1960s spotted a female figure with long hair, wearing a white dress, standing by the roadside. The witness stopped to see if she was lost or needed a lift, but she had vanished. Mentioning the encounter to a work colleague the following day, the colleague described the figure which was encountered and said the area was haunted.

title: White Lady
notes: The daughter of a previous owner, this shade is thought to have returned after the girl's suicide. She is said to walk down the staircase dressed in nightwear.
address: South, N Deeside Rd, Aberdeen AB12 5YP, UK

title: White Lady
notes: This pallid woman is said to leave the house at midnight and walk into a nearby wood. The White Room is said to be haunted by a little man with green eyes. It is not known for sure whether these ghosts are connected to a series of dark stains on the staircases of the building.
address: Llanvihangel Court, Llanvihangel Crucorney, Abergavenny NP7 8DH, UK

title: Coloured Women
notes: This castle is haunted by both a white woman and a lady in red, the latter thought to have been killed during a fox hunt, while the former returned after being buried in unconsecrated ground. Another story says that the white woman was the former owner of the castle (Winifred Cochrane, Countess of Dundonald) who returned after her bitter husband, written out of her will, purchased the site and destroyed the contents.
address: Gwrych Castle, Tan-Y-Gopa Rd, Abergele LL22 8ET, UK

title: White Lady
notes: The phantom white woman is said to make her way from the nearby church to a door on the east side of the hall.
address: Aberhafesp Hall, Aberhafesp, Newtown SY16 3HJ, UK

title: Girl in White Lace
notes: A ghostly girl dressed in white lace is said to haunt this ruined site. A student managed to photograph a strange misty 'face' in 2004 and reported hearing scratching noises. (Image copyright 2006 Lawson Clout)

title: Lady in White
notes: A woman in white is said to appear at midnight, sit for a while, and move off to a nearby stream to drink.
address: Ragley Hall, Park & Gardens, Ragley Hall, Alcester B49 5NJ, UK

title: Woman in White
location_name: Allington (Wiltshire) - Road to Horton, near Tan Hill
notes: Seen by two lay preachers driving a horse and trap, this phantom was mistaken for a real woman as she walked towards them in the rain. They could see she had auburn hair and a beautiful face, before she vanished without trace.

title: Burnt Dog
notes: A phantom dog reportedly haunts the hotel, where it was kicked to death by a drunken customer. Some say that a white (or grey) woman also glides around inside the building; the girl was a kitchen maid murdered in the hotel. Finally, the ghost of an old man haunts room number three.

title: White Lady
location_name: Altrincham (Greater Manchester) - Ashley Hall (no longer standing)
notes: This flitting entity was seen several times in a particular room of the hall, her large black eyes staring into the empty space ahead. The room was sealed up as the haunting presence was so strong.

title: Woman in White
notes: While camping close to this property, one traveller awoke to see a white female form standing at a gate. He woke one of his friends who also observed the ghostly figure. The following day the travellers moved on and never returned (special thanks to the Churchtown Village Renewal Trust, from their 'The Annals of Churchtown').
address: Annagh South, Annagh, Co. Cork, Ireland

title: White Lady
notes: Dying in childbirth, this mistress of a former owner still walks the walls. The ghost, or perhaps another, was seen in 1970 as it crossed the road outside the hall; the driver of a car drove straight through the figure. In October 2012, one team of ghost hunters recorded laughter in the stable when the area was empty - the area is reputed to be the location where a serving girl hanged herself - while another team in September spotted a black dog.

title: Woman in White
notes: During a fashion shoot in this former prison, studio photographer Alan Wells caught a strange white female apparition in the background of one of his images.

title: Swimming Horseman
location_name: Ashford (Kent) - Eastwell Park and Manor House
notes: Riding towards the park house, this phantom horseman veers off at the last minute and enters the nearby lake. A white lady haunts the house itself, seen by porters on the night shift. In the seventeenth century, the Earl of Winchelsea cut down several oak trees, bestowing a curse on his family which took the life of his wife and son shortly after.
address: Eastwell Manor Hotel, Boughton Lees, Ashford TN25 4HR, UK

title: Gliding Lady
notes: The phantom white lady here was reportedly seen by soldiers using the building during the Second World War and was thought to be the murdered wife of a former owner. Within the manor she would ascend the staircase holding a candle, while one person who observed her outside said she had no feet and had drifted across a nearby field. The kitchen area was also reputedly haunted by a ghostly monk.

title: White Woman
location_name: Ashmore (Dorset) - Ashmore - area between Washer's Pit and Spinney's Pond
notes: A local story says that a woman hanged over the pit was saved by a washer woman. This did not prevent the woman's ghost from returning to the area when she later died.

title: White Lady
notes: A white lady drifts around the grounds of the manor and sometimes the nearby henge and stone circles. The phantom woman's face is covered by a large hood. The inside of the manor house is said to be haunted by several other entities. One spirit would scatter rose petals across the floor of the Crimson Room, and a phantom monk was once spotted in the library. A ghostly Cavalier occasionally makes an appearance, and in 2014, a visitor was left with a sinking feeling after seeing (but not physically) two Victorian-era children swinging from the bannisters.
address: High St, Avebury, Marlborough SN8 1RF, UK

title: Lady in White
notes: A government worker walking from Avebury Manor reported encountering a ghostly woman in white lace and a white hood at an iron gate close to Truslow. The witness said the woman appeared from nowhere, took his shoulders, and spun him around before pushing him away.

title: Tabby Cat
notes: This feline apparition has been observed padding around on the first floor, vanishing when it sits down. A white Elizabethan lady is also reported to haunt the same floor, seen sitting in a chair.

title: White Lady
datetime: Late twentieth century, though sightings may go back to eighteenth century
notes: While the clothing of the woman varies (from wearing a raincoat to a wedding dress), the figure is always white. In true 'phantom hitchhiker' style, the woman had been picked up by passing drivers, only to vanish a few miles down the road.
address: Aycliffe Village, Newton Aycliffe DL5, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: Aylmerton (Norfolk) - Circular Hollows (local name Shrieking Pits)
notes: The White Lady has been observed looking into each pit, crying out in pity. Her origin is unknown, but she could just be a story concocted by local smugglers. The sound of the screams heard in the pits, which local folklore attributes to the woman's phantom, is more likely to be the wind.

title: White Woman
notes: A husband and wife encountered a white female phantom standing by the kitchen door, after which they had their bed covers pulled from them by an unseen hand on three consecutive nights.

title: White Woman
location_name: Ballygarvan (County Cork) - Small bridge over a stream
notes: A small bridge over a stream near Ballygarvan was home to a white woman. After appearing to one man who fell critically ill the following day, a priest went to the site and banished her.
address: Ballyduhig South, Ballygarvan, Co. Cork, Ireland

title: White Lady
location_name: Barnsdale Forest (South Yorkshire) - Robin Hood's Grave
notes: The area around the site thought to be Robin Hood's final resting place (one of them!) is said to be haunted by a white lady who drifts around the area.

title: White Woman
notes: A lady in white haunts this field, while the road nearby is home to a phantom dog.

title: Woman with Bright Pink Eyes
notes: The witness, a farmer, reported seeing two figures, one a young boy and the other a woman wearing a long white dress, standing in a field by his livestock. When he shone his flashlight at the people, he could see that the woman had eerie pink eyes. Filled with fear the farmer did not move until the figures disappeared towards the local graveyard, after which the witness ran home.

title: Gibbet and Those Hanged
notes: An oak tree standing in the park is said to be haunted by those once hanged there (including a woman in white), and that the Devil can be summoned by walking around it six times.

title: Water Fairies
notes: A witness watched the little people playing merrily in the water at the pass. Nearby is the grave of Gelert (the dog that was mistakenly killed by its master after saving a child from a wolf) where the hound's ghost has been reported. Finally, the ghostly woman in white who haunts the area is an ill omen, with death said to follow a sighting.

title: White Lady
location_name: Bedworth (Warwickshire) - Bedworth Road, aka Rabbit Lane
notes: A woman left traumatised after being attacked along the road later committed suicide. Her phantom is said to stand by the roadside, waiting for a lift. One witness who spotted her said she wore a long white dress, possibly a nightdress, and had long black matted hair. Another driver heading towards Astley in May 2012 said she spotted a figure on the roadside with her hands against her face which was holding a dreadful expression. Two more witnesses in December 2012 described a figure standing by the road with long black matted hair, although this time the figure was dressed in black.

title: The Running Lady
location_name: Beeford (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Road between Beeford and Brandesburton
notes: This woman in white is said to run along the road between the two villages, close to the North Frodingham junction.

title: Waiting Woman
notes: A woman in a white dress is said to wait for her Viking lover by the riverside.

title: Mrs Waggs
notes: A former housekeeper, Mrs Waggs is said to haunt this location. Another phantom, a woman in white still loiters in the garden, and finally an unseen coach pulls into the driveway.
address: Jenkyn Place Cottage, Hole Ln, Bentley, Farnham GU10 5LU, UK

title: Woman in White
notes: A phantom woman in white would drift around lanes near the grange.
address: Bidford Grange, Bidford-on-Avon, Alcester B50 4LY, UK

title: Anne Bruce
location_name: Biel (Lothian) - Lady's Walk, in grounds of the mansion
notes: Anne has been named as the white woman who walks along the path which bears her name. She spent many months walking this path after discovering her husband's infidelity after his death at sea.

title: Woman in White
location_name: Bilston (Staffordshire) - Greyhound and Punchbowl public house, was once Stow Heath Manor House
notes: Prior to becoming a public house, the staircase of the manor house was haunted by a woman in a white gown. While it is not clear whether the ghostly woman remains, more recently a baby has been heard crying and mild poltergeist activity reported.

title: Lady
location_name: Binfield (Berkshire) - Billingbear House, and hawthorn tree (which may no longer exist)
datetime: Pre-1924 (white lady), hawthorn tree on Christmas Eve (reoccurring)
notes: A ghostly white lady haunted this house before it burnt down. One room was transported to Pace College, Manhattan, and is said to contain a ghost (whether it is the white lady is unknown). Another piece of local folklore says a hawthorn tree on the grounds blossoms red flowers at midnight on Christmas Eve before quickly reverting to its previous state.
address: Billingbear House, Carters Hill, Binfield, Bracknell RG42 5PL, UK

title: Woman with Long Blonde Hair
notes: Said to be quite pleasant to watch, this beautiful wraith in her white dress disappears into a bedroom wall. The sounds of phantom footsteps have also been reported.
address: Court Mt, Canterbury Rd, Birchington-on-Sea, Birchington CT7 0BU, UK

title: White Lady
notes: Though seen wearing several different sets of clothing over the past fifty years, this young girl with blonde hair is thought to be Victorian and has a habit of passing through parked cars.

title: Loud Lady
notes: A white lady that manifested at this location would be accompanied by loud crashing sounds. Poltergeist activity was also reported. Twelve clerics finally exorcised the spirit.

title: Raped Nurse
notes: This figure dressed in white is reportedly a nurse raped and killed near the barracks - her shade has been observed floating around the area, her facial features almost blank.

title: Woman in White
notes: A child told her mother that she had seen a white woman drifting towards the house. Her mother dismissed the claim, until they received word that her father had drowned around the same time as the sighting.

title: White Lady
notes: This phantom lady is said to haunt the area surrounding the bar, although her presence is more likely to felt then seen.
address: Blane Valley Inn, 54 Glasgow Rd, Blanefield, Glasgow G63 9BP, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: Boltby (North Yorkshire) - Road leading into village
notes: One witness in a car reported seeing a white woman wearing an old fashioned party dress, although the woman's legs disappeared below the knee and the face was blurred. The witness said it was like watching a 'replay', and although the witness's car headlights did not illuminate the figure, the woman was visible against the darkness of the environment.

title: White Woman
notes: A phantom white lady is said to haunt the house, throwing herself from the roof and occasionally heard stomping on the staircase. She has been variously named as Lady Alice or Ailie, Lilyburn, Lilburne or Lillieburn.

title: White Lady
location_name: Bournemouth (Dorset) - Millhams Lane, and surrounding Longham area
datetime: February (reoccurring); last confirmed in 1970s, but legends say she continues
notes: The area around the A348 bridge is reportedly haunted by a ghostly white (or grey) woman, killed when hit by a horse and trap along the road. One story says she tries to entice men to jump off the bridge.

title: Woman in White
location_name: Bournemouth (Dorset) - Outside West Howe Baptist Church, on the corner of Holloway Avenue and Anstey Road
notes: A lady dressed in a white gown floated across the road before disappearing through a wall which led to a garden.
address: West Howe Baptist Church, Bournemouth BH11 9JS, UK

title: White Lady
notes: This incorporeal woman has been reported in various parts of the inn.

title: White Lady
datetime: Said to occur every seven years, but exact date unknown
notes: A ghostly, upset white lady is said to enter the house from the front door, drift up the stairs to the east window in the attic and throw herself out. The building is also reputedly haunted by an Elizabethan gentleman.
address: Braywick House, Windsor Rd, Maidenhead SL6 1DN, UK

title: Woman in White
notes: This woman in white was said to have been a princess - anyone who carried the ghost to a nearby churchyard could put her soul to rest.

title: Lady with Dog
notes: A male witness at the gate of this building spotted a lady in a white dress, walking her dog in the distance. The witness turned momentarily and when he looked back, the figure and dog had vanished - there was nowhere for them to have disappeared to. The same witness reported that several years previous he had heard someone whisper his name at the hall, even though the nearest person was some one hundred metres away.
address: Bank Hall, Liverpool Rd., Leyland PR26 9AT, UK

title: White Lady
notes: Seen by actors as they rehearse, the phantom lady is described as wearing a long white dress wearing bouffant style hair. A soldier dressed in a red tunic, the sound of disembodied footsteps and the rattling of chains also haunt the building.
address: 29 New Rd, Brighton and Hove, Brighton BN1 1UG, UK

title: White Lady
notes: Nothing is really known about this phantom white lady, and it is questionable whether she still exists.
address: 33-34 East St, Brighton and Hove, Brighton BN1 1HL, UK

title: Woman in White
location_name: Bristol (Somerset) - A38 near Barrow Gurney Reservoir
notes: A certain part of the road is said to be covered in skid marks where various cars have had to brake to avoid hitting a phantom woman dressed in a white coat. The figure always vanishes immediately afterwards.

title: Modern Man
notes: The shade of a man dressed in contemporary clothing was seen in the cellar before quickly vanishing, while the ghost of a woman with long dark hair and dressed in white has been reported in the bar.

title: White Lady
location_name: Broad Haven (Dyfed) - Haven Fort Hotel (aka Havens Hotel)
notes: A local legend claimed a white lady haunted the hotel grounds.

title: White Nurse?
location_name: Broadstairs (Kent) - Kingsgate Castle and beach below
notes: A photograph taken by artist Malcolm Baker appears to show the upper body of a white figure moving through foliage just outside the castle. Baker believes the figure to be a nurse in 1950s uniform, carrying a tray in front of her. A few months later musician Dan Smith reported spotting a nun dressed in white on the beach just after dawn, the figure quickly vanishing.
address: Kingsgate Castle, Joss Gap Rd, Broadstairs CT10 3PH, UK

title: Woman in White
notes: A lady in white is said to drift along this stretch of road.

title: White Lady
location_name: Bury St Edmunds (Suffolk) - Rushbrooke Hall (3.5 miles from Bury)
notes: Differing stories for the same myth - the first states that the White Lady was the wife of a former owner, murdered by her husband. She floats around the moat, the place she was drowned. The second tale places the murder in the hall, this time by persons unknown. The body was then thrown into the moat from the battlements.
address: Bury St Edmunds, Bury Saint Edmunds, UK

title: Black Monk
location_name: Caldey Island (Dyfed) - Priory and all over Island
notes: Standing taller than six foot, the monk dressed in black was said to rise from the graveyard at the old priory before drifting off. Though reported on the land immediately around the priory, some islanders encountered him further away, in their homes or on the roads. The ghosts of a white lady and of a madman were also said to haunt the island at some point in the dim and distant past.

title: Lady in White
notes: A young lady dressed completely in white is said to haunt this building. The ghost would descend the main staircase. Phantom music has also been reported in the house.

title: Ginger Bearded Man
notes: The main road leading through Calverton is said to be haunted by two ghosts - a man wearing a tweed suit and sporting a ginger beard walks along the path, while a phantom white lady waits patiently at a bus stop.
address: Main St, Calverton, Nottingham NG14, UK

title: A Nun and Brown Dog-Thing
notes: It is claimed that Abbey House is, or at least was, the most haunted house in Cambridge. While I'm not a fan of 'most haunted' titles, when you look at the number and type of the ghosts supposed to haunt the site (a poltergeist, ghostly echoes of chains, a butler, a woman in white, a grey lady, a squirrel, a dog, and a hare all amongst the entities which have popped up), one can understand why the title has been applied here. An exorcism was performed in the 1980s, which may have laid the entities.
address: Abbey House, 42 Abbey Rd, Cambridge CB5 8HQ, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: Cambridge (Cambridgeshire) - Sherratt & Hughes, Trinity Street (closed in 1991)
notes: Once said to be the oldest bookshop in the UK, the building claims to have a white lady who walks silently down the aisles. Her history is unknown. There are also reports of a man in Victorian evening dress.

title: The White Lady & The Black Man
notes: No recent sightings of these figures have been reported, and locals accept them as being little more than smuggler's stories told to inspire fear. The white lady is said to offer to dance with anyone she came upon during her walks, while the black man offered to buy souls in return for a song. The White Lady was seen around 1917 by a group of men out partying - one of them chased after the figure with a large stick, but the apparition disappeared before he reached it.
address: Hadleigh Castle, Castle Ln, Hadleigh, Benfleet SS7 2AP, UK

title: White Lady
notes: A 'new' building on the site of thirteenth century ruins, the White Lady once haunted the older area, and now has set up shop in the replacement castle. The woman is said to have died of a broken heart after her son drowned in a local pond. There is also reported to be a buried hoard of treasure here, zealously protected by either three eagles or an entity who confuses any potential gold thieves, while an ethereal cavalier walks the castle grounds.

title: White Woman
notes: A soldier on guard duty challenged this fabled phantom woman; she faded in front of him and he died of shock several hours later. It is believed the ghost may have belonged to a body found in 1835, bricked up in a castle wall.

title: Stone of Dismissal
notes: In a reversal of the normal tale associated with these stones, if a witness spotted the Devil nearby, she or he had to run around the stone to dismiss Old Nick. The stone was also said to be haunted by a woman in white who had been sacrificed on it.

title: White Woman
location_name: Castle Gresley (Derbyshire) - White Lady Springs, wooded area just outside the village
notes: This small pool of water is haunted by the pale ghost of a woman who is said to have drowned in it many years ago.

title: Glowing Woman
location_name: Chapel St Leonards (Lincolnshire) - Seafront between Trunch Lane and Vickers Point Ingoldmells
notes: A glowing woman dressed in white was observed by four people here. The radiant figure walked towards them before turning towards the sea and vanishing. A local legend says the woman had been spurned by her lover and walked into the sea (which before then was further inland) to drown herself.
address: Trunch Ln, Chapel St Leonards, Skegness PE24, UK

title: White Lady
notes: The white lady who haunted this building was said to have been murdered and disposed of down a well that is now located under the dining room. The lodge is also haunted by a monk in a brown habit, who may be the cause of the disembodied footsteps occasionally heard travelling around the building.
address: 2 The St, Charmouth, Bridport DT6 6PN, UK

title: Drifting Woman
location_name: Charmouth (Dorset) - Road between Charmouth and Bridport
notes: A driver and her passenger both watched a woman in white sweep across the road as they drove towards Bridport. They both reported that the car suddenly became cold at the time of the sighting.

title: Lady of the Lake
location_name: Charnock Richard (Lancashire) - The Dam (lake near Park Hall)
notes: A radiant white female form has been seen a couple of times in the lake within the Hall grounds - it is believed to be a woman who drowned herself after falling in love with a monk. His ghost in turn may haunt the hall.

title: Bess of Hardwick
notes: Wayne Anthony, in Haunted Derbyshire and the Peak District, writes that Bess, who finished constructing her home around 1560, is one of several shades to haunt this house. Mary Queen of Scots is rumoured to walk where she was once held captive, while a grey lady and a white woman haunt other parts of the building. The library is haunted by a transparent figure which drifts from shelf to shelf. The bridge on the grounds is haunted by a screaming woman who was forced to watch her baby drowned after it was thrown into the river by her partner.

title: Silky
notes: A former mistress of a Duke of Argyll, this white woman has yet to leave the bedroom of this house.

title: Madam Pigott
location_name: Chetwynd Aston (Shropshire) - A41, and Chetwynd Hall & surrounding area
notes: Having died in childbirth, Madam Pigott was seen dozens of times carrying her baby and combing its hair. Some also reported the lady also having a cat with her. She was eventually exorcised by twelve parsons, though in 1969 two motorists separately reported a white figure on the road near the church.

title: Edwardian Gentleman
location_name: Chingford (Essex) - Friday Hill House, Simmons Lane
notes: Said to resemble Stan Laurel, this ghost was seen in Friday Hill House by a former occupier. A crying woman in a white veil has also been seen on the site.
address: Friday Hill House, 7 Simmons Ln, London E4 6JH, UK

title: White Lady
notes: Hanged during the Civil War by a traitorous priest, this female member of the Mundesley family still returns home. The beam of wood from which her body dangled is considered lucky if you touch it.
address: Wood Ln, Heskin, Chorley PR7 5PA, UK

title: Cloaked Man
location_name: Chudleigh (Devon) - Bishop Lacy Inn, Fore Street
notes: This figure was seen running up the staircase of the building - when the landlord gave chase, the man vanished into thin air. A white woman has also been seen on site, and at one time a group of bar dwellers watched as a pint of beer was pulled by a phantom hand!
address: Bishop Lacy Inn, 52-53 Fore St., Chudleigh, Newton Abbot TQ13 0HY, UK

title: Lady Banks
location_name: Church Knowle (Dorset) - Stone Bridge along Knowle Hill
notes: Lady Banks held nearby Corfe Castle against the Roundheads during the English Civil War. The defences were only broken after a treacherous Royalist officer opened the gates. Lady Banks has been named as the lady in a white flowing dress who haunts the bridge.

title: White Woman
location_name: Churchill (Oxfordshire) - Crossroads close to Lyneham Longbarrow
notes: A white female phantom is said to walk this area. It is unclear whether the ghost is that of an old woman who once lived in a nearby cave and watched over a hoard of treasure.

title: White Lady
location_name: Cilfynydd (Mid Glamorgan) - Woods in the area
notes: Last seen by a newspaper editor and a friend, this phantom lady darts between the trees on a hillside. Some say she is accompanied by another white figure in a cloak.

title: White Lady
location_name: Clacton-on-Sea (Essex) - Wilson House, Leas Road (no longer standing)
notes: The white lady was said to have been seen by several schoolgirls when this building was a dorm. Sounds of heavy foots could also been heard walking along the corridors late at night.

title: Quake
notes: The rector wrote of a deep rumbling which manifested in the kitchen, like an earthquake, although no furnishings moved. The noise suddenly stopped and was replaced by smashing crockery in the pantry. Together with his servants, the rector approached the pantry, but before reaching the door, it opened and a white woman emerged. Pursued by the rector, the white woman moved upstairs and vanished in front of the rector's son. The woman appeared several more times afterwards, but never so dramatically.

title: White Woman
location_name: Clonmel (County Tipperary) - Unnamed road leading southwards, passing over a bridge
datetime: Late nineteenth or early twentieth century
notes: Walking to a friend's house late at night, this witness spotted a glowing woman in white with long hair cross a bridge ahead of him. The girl vanished without trace, and upon mentioning it when he arrived at his destination, his friend told him that the same figure had appeared at the foot of his bed a few nights previous.

title: Moon or Hitchhiker
notes: Reports of a white phantom in the form of a woman resulted in an approximately sixty car long traffic jam as people tried to spot the spook. Local Councillor Desmond Donnelly said the 'ghost' was more likely to be the moon reflecting in the nearby river.
address: Mullaghmoyle Rd & Brackaville Rd, Coalisland, Dungannon BT71 4HX, UK

title: White Woman
location_name: Cobh (County Cork) - Spike Island - general area
datetime: Unknown (woman and solider), tall figure in May or June 1914
notes: A white female form is reputedly one of the phantoms which haunts the island as is a phantom soldier with two black holes instead of eyes. In 1914, a witness fled from a tall, off-yellow glossy figure that stood looking over the sea towards Cobh.

title: Woman in White
notes: A former member of staff reported seeing a woman in a long white dress in the stockroom, hearing footsteps, and witnessing boxes of shoes jump off shelves. The worker eventually refused to work in the room.

title: Faceless White Lady wearing Perfume
notes: There is little to suggest who this white lady is, but those who have witnessed her standing on the staircase have been in shock for some time afterwards. The accompanying smell of perfume is said to be very strong.
address: East Lodge, 83-84 High St, Colchester CO1 1WR, UK

title: White Woman
location_name: Colne (Lancashire) - Hare and Hounds public house
notes: This pub is rumoured to be the home of a phantom white lady.

title: Simon Cunliffe
datetime: Once a year, middle of winter (reoccurring), sound recorded 26/07/1996
notes: Simon rides the area where he once lived, blowing his horn prior to a tragedy. It is said that he scared his wife to death by riding up the staircase of their home, chasing a fox that had ran inside to escape the hunt (ghost hunters Colin Veacock and Peter Crawley recorded a sound like a riding crop here in 1996).
