Another tale states that the horseman is not Simon but an assassin, killed by a manservant before he could murder the master of the hall. The young daughter of a family living here during the twentieth century told her mother that a 'white woman' would visit her during the night - the sightings stopped when the child changed bedrooms.

title: Bearded Man
notes: A small man sporting a long beard is said to haunt this building, as is a white woman in a flowing gown.
address: High St, Combe Martin, Ilfracombe EX34 0ET, UK

title: Grey Monk
notes: Sightings of this spectral figure date back to the 1770s, when he was first seen drifting among the ruins. Reports also exist of sightings of a white woman, and the sounds of quiet voices and bumping.
address: Castle Hill, Conisbrough, Doncaster DN12 3BU, UK

title: Woman in White
location_name: Coventry (West Midlands) - Private cottage, Wall Hill Road, Allesley
notes: Occupants in this cottage witnessed three ghosts, one a woman in white who resembled a nun, a woman in black, and a man wearing a nightshirt, handing a candle, descending the staircase Doors would open unaided and footsteps crossed the upstairs landing. The family would also hear rattling chains several times a week; when renovation work took place on the roof, a secret room was discovered that chains hanging from a wall.
address: Wall Hill Rd, Allesley, Coventry, UK

title: Lady Mary
location_name: Crieff (Perth and Kinross) - Ferntower House, tower (no longer standing)
notes: Polish troops based at this house claimed the tower was haunted by a white woman. The tower was demolished in 1963.

title: Piper
location_name: Culrain (Highland) - Carbisdale Castle (aka Castle of Spite)
notes: Though only a hundred years old, the castle is said to be haunted by a traditional piper and a lady in white, the latter named as Duchess Blair (or Betty, for short).
address: Carbisdale Castle, Culrain, Ardgay IV24 3DP, UK

title: Monk
notes: A ghostly figure in robes was seen walking towards the gatehouse before vanishing into a wall. It is said the monk sometimes appears on horseback, and the site is also haunted by a white woman.

title: Woman in White
location_name: Darlington (Durham) - Former guest house (no longer operating), Corporation Road
notes: The ghostly woman who haunted this guest house is said to have been killed on the road outside many years earlier. She would be seen standing on top of the staircase by the manager. Poltergeist activity was also reported on the site - items in the kitchen would be moved overnight, lights would turn themselves on, and the manager was 'held' at the top of the staircase by an unknown force.

title: Hunting for the Child
notes: A woman in white is said to haunt the old cemetery, looking for her child. She either died in childbirth or was murdered by men who then stole her baby. A local myth says that by speaking the words 'white lady, white lady, I stole your black baby' she will appear.

title: White Lady
location_name: Delamere Forest (Cheshire) - Deep within the forest, between Frodsham and Delamere Forest rail station
notes: While walking to Frodsham from the Delamere Forest station, two people encountered a white lady. She stared at them for a couple of minutes before floating upwards into the trees.

title: White Lady
notes: Seen on the steps behind the structure, this white lady is known to make brief appearances. A woman in tears has also been seen near the cathedral, carrying a young child. The same location is home to a man wearing old fashioned clothing, not forgetting the former executioner who killed his father and brother who jointly haunts the area.
address: Derby Cathedral, 18-19 Iron Gate, Derby DE1 3GP, UK

title: White Woman
notes: A hanging white woman was said to haunt this piece of woodland, her dress always blowing in the wind to her left.

title: Distressed Woman
notes: This woman, dressed in white, had been observed crying in the hotel. She last appeared at the end of a four poster bed.
address: George Hotel, High St, Dorchester, Wallingford OX10 7HH, UK

title: Screaming White Lady
notes: This sad spirit is said to have been murdered in the 1600's by a servant. The white lady now drifts through the castle corridors, wailing and screaming.
address: Skibo Castle, The Carnegie Club, Clashmore, Dornoch IV25 3RQ, UK

title: Ladies
location_name: Douglas (Isle of Man) - The Nunnery estate
notes: Two women, one in black and the other in white, were said to haunt the land in this area. One version of the story says they are looking for each other to finish a fight they started during life.

title: Old Lady
notes: A cyclist riding along the canal witnessed a glowing elderly woman in white with long hair dancing with a young man. The man wore a seventeenth or eighteenth century military uniform and looked 'normal', unlike the woman who also hummed a tune. The cyclist did not stop.

title: Lipsticked Lady
location_name: Dudley (West Midlands) - Jolly Collier, Holly Hall (former inn)
notes: The Westwood family had lived in the former inn for two years before their ghostly outbreak. A young blonde woman wrapped in white and wearing lipstick was seen in various parts of the house, as was a phantom bald man. Beds were moved, knocking started and footsteps were heard in empty parts of the building. Eventually, as strangely as it had started, the hauntings ceased.

title: Two Horses
notes: As well as the phantom horses, the ruined building is also home to a phantom white lady.
address: Castle Rd, Dufftown, Keith AB55 4GH, UK

title: Lady Waving Handkerchief
notes: Appearing upset, this white woman frantically waves from a high window once every year - it is rumoured she was having an affair with the Cardinal Beaton.
address: 64 Claypotts Rd, Broughty Ferry, Dundee DD5 1LH, UK

title: Lady in Grey
notes: The staircase here is reportedly haunted by a fleeting female in grey, while a beautiful woman in white has been seen in other areas of the building.
address: National Trust - Crook Hall Gardens, Frankland Ln, Sidegate, Durham DH1 5SZ, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: E17 (Greater London) - Council house, Cooper Avenue, Walthamstow
notes: Shortly after moving in, a family demanded to be relocated after a white lady manifested in front of a four year old boy and ordered the child to leave. The family dog refused to enter the boy's bedroom.

title: White Lady
notes: The phantom woman or women who haunt this building are not alone - the sound of a wailing hound has been heard, and disembodied voices emanate from empty rooms.
address: Sutton House, 2 and 4 Homerton High St, London E9 6JQ, UK

title: White Woman with Pointed Head
location_name: East Bridge (Suffolk) - Road towards ruined chapel
notes: Observed by a group of bird watchers, a woman in white was seen drifting down an old trail that leads to the ruins of a chapel. It is unclear if her 'pointed head' was a hat or bonnet.

title: Pale Lady
notes: Locals have reported strange banging sounds coming from the castle, as well as the occasional glimpse of a phantom woman in white.

title: Lady in White
location_name: EC3 (Greater London) - Tower of London, the White Tower
notes: Her identity unknown, this ghostly woman's perfume is said to be overpowering, and has made many people gag around St John's Chapel. One visitor felt tapping on her shoulder, although could not see who could have been responsible. When they mentioned it to a guide, the guide said it could have been 'Daniel', who people have seen wearing a black cloak.

title: White Woman
notes: A white female phantom is said to hover over the graves of the fallen knights on the site. Another tale says a Knight Templar also haunts the church.

title: White Lady
location_name: EC4 (Greater London) - Wardrobe Place, Carter Lane
notes: Appearing soon after the sun goes down, this pale unknown figure drifts around the courtyard.
address: Carter Ln & Wardrobe Pl, London EC4V 5AB, UK

title: White Rider
location_name: Eckington (Derbyshire) - Moss valley, between town and Ridgeway
notes: Two women out walking their dogs watched a white female figure on horseback in the woods. The entity vanished as it rounded a non-existent corner. A local piece of folklore says the area is haunted by a white horse, although traditionally riderless. In 2022, witnesses spotted a hooded grey lady carrying a basket who disappeared into bushes.
address: Gosber Rd, Eckington, Sheffield S21 4BZ, UK

title: Lady Mary Douglas
notes: Lady Mary, manifesting in the form of a white lady, is said to wander along the corridors, looking into the original rooms. Her presence is reported to be calming.

title: White Woman
location_name: Edinburgh (Lothian) - Area where sycamore tree once stood, Dovecote Road, Corstorphine
notes: This white lady stabbed her lover to death by the tree, while fending off his attack. She was executed for the crime, but her ghost holding a sword would return to the tree where the crime was committed. Another version of the story says that she was murdered here.
address: Dovecot Rd, Corstorphine, Edinburgh EH12, UK

title: Woman in White
notes: At least three night porters reported seeing this tall, beautiful woman in white with long black hair move quickly down the corridors. Workers demanded to work in pairs until the ghost had been exorcised.

title: Woman in White
location_name: Edinburgh (Lothian) - Unnamed residence along Blackett Place
notes: This tall phantom female had a preference for the staircase and was blamed for an accident where someone fell (or was pushed?). Another ghost, a male which haunted the dining room, possessed an evil looking face.

title: White Lion
notes: This large albino cat was seen close to the cottages and was reported to resemble a lion. The same area is reportedly haunted by a headless white woman.

title: White Lady
notes: Folklore placed the spirit of a white woman within a house chimney on Oteley Park. Another version of the tale places the woman on the banks of the Mere, shouting at people to stop using her water.

title: Edwardian Lady
notes: This white woman has been seen near the railway, melting away if approached.

title: Doppelganger of Mother
notes: Twice a woman in white has appeared, witnesses mistaking the figure for Mrs Punter (the owner of the house), though she has been in other places at the time.

title: Two Ladies
notes: Two female figures haunt the grounds of this hotel; a lady in grey walks near the staircase where she was killed, and the other (dressed in white) walks the terrace. Two children can be seen and heard crying, standing by the river that runs through the grounds. In March of 2000, a man staying at the hotel reported seeing and feeling a strange concretion of 'baleful' energy in his room, while in a photograph taken in October 2014, an amorphous shape appeared above someone's head. As a side note, the building was used in the original film of 'The Haunting'.

title: White Lady
location_name: Ewenny (South Glamorgan) - Areas known as White Lady's meadow and White Lady's Lane
notes: This white woman was said to point towards Ewenny, where treasure was supposed to be hidden. One legend says that a local man offered to help the ghost. The white woman asked the man to hold her hands, which he did until startled by a dog. Scared by the dog, the man snatched his hands back - the ghost screamed 'I'll be bound for another seven years!' before vanishing.

title: White Lady
notes: This ghost is likely to be responsible for the cold spot located in the moat.

title: White Lady
notes: A family were terrified by poltergeist activity, which included the entity tightening a crucifix necklace around someone's neck. Unusually, a phantom woman in white was also seen on the site by several people, including a neighbour.

title: White Lady
notes: A ghostly white lady haunts this country lane - she is also said to cross a field and vanish into a pond.

title: Knight in Shining Armour
location_name: Forest of Dean (Gloucestershire) - Saint Briavel's castle
notes: Seen in the grounds and along corridors of this former castle (now a hostel) a figure in armour has been known to appear. It is not known if he is responsible for the disembodied footsteps along the walkways. A young child has been heard to cry in the King's Hall (where the bones of a baby were once found walled up), and finally a ghostly woman in white was spotted on the staircase in 1998.

title: White Woman
notes: This phantom woman, dressed in a white dress with pink trim and holding a broom, manifested to a witness and floated through a bedroom. Another witness had a similar encounter in an adjoining room.

title: White Woman
datetime: Prior to the outbreak of the Second World War
notes: Though not seen recently, the stories of the white lady still exist.

title: Construction of Gallows
notes: Not shy of more than a few hauntings, Glamis can claim the following... The sounds of a set of gallows being constructed, Lady Glamis drifting in mist around the clock tower, the unknown ghost in the 'Haunted Room' which caused a previous owner to faint when he went to investigate the mysterious sounds contained within, a white lady that drifts the corridors, 'Jack the Runner' who's long legs carry him down the same corridors, a black page boy that quickly appears and disappears near a sitting room, a pale girl who is dragged away by an invisible force in the courtyard, a giant in armour that stands over and looks down on people in bed, and a re-enacted murder in the parlour.

title: Woman
location_name: Glasfryn, Lleyn Peninsula (Gwynedd) - House, lake and nearby field
notes: One story says that this area is haunted by the ghost of a woman in white who accidently flooded the surrounding lands.

title: Nelly
location_name: Glen of Imaal (County Wicklow) - Coolmoney House
notes: The slim teenager in a white dress which haunts this property on army land had the nickname Nelly. Stories claimed Nelly to be a murdered servant.
address: Glen of Imaal, Gibstown, Co. Wicklow, Ireland

title: Woman in White
location_name: Godley Green (Buckinghamshire) - Road from Gee Cross
notes: Not a 'normal' post-mortem apparition; a woman returning to see her ill brother watched as a ghostly white woman left the building in which her brother lay. She soon discovered her brother had died seconds before her arrival.

title: Funeral Procession
notes: This phantom line of people was reported walking past the building. A white woman, once married to the First Earl of Godolphin, is also said to climb out from a closet and walk along the terrace.
address: Godolphin East, Godolphin Cross, Helston TR13 9RE, UK

title: White Lady
notes: Drifting down the corridors of the upper regions of the building, this figure may be responsible for the poltergeist like actions in one of the bedrooms.

title: White Lady
notes: This pale woman was seen several times in the house.

title: No Locks
location_name: Great Staughton (Cambridgeshire) - Unnamed farm on the moors
notes: Owners of a farmhouse here were said to have once removed all the locks and bars from their doors, as a white lady would open all the doors at night, no matter how securely they were left closed. A workman called in to mend the roof encountered the ghost and refused to finish the job - another builder had to complete the repairs.

title: Dead Woman in Night Gown
location_name: Gresford (Clwyd) - Little Acton Hill (now Bluebell Lane), between Gresford and Wrexham
notes: Two men walking along this dark road at night were scared by an approaching figure dressed in white. As it came ever closer, the men were relieved to recognise it as the wife of one of the men. However, the figure suddenly vanished - the two Welshmen rushed back to the house of the woman, only to find her dead.
address: Blue Bell Ln, Gresford, Wrexham, UK

title: Maid of Rokeby
location_name: Greta Bridge (Durham) - Waters where the two rivers meet
notes: This entity, dressed in white flowing clothes, patrols the banks of the river after dusk. A monk is also reported to do the same, having taken in own life in the river many years previous.
address: Greta Bridge, Barnard Castle DL12 9SE, UK

title: Smashed Furniture
notes: The sounds of furniture being smashed can be heard here, though investigations can never find anything broken. Glimpses of a woman in white have been reported by staff, though she quickly vanishes.
address: Sutton Place, Woking, Guildford GU4 7QW, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: Gunton (Norfolk) - Gunton Park and House (partly destroyed by fire in 1882)
datetime: Prior to death in Suffield family
notes: This ghostly pale woman heard screaming when a death is imminent in the family. She has also been spotted running across the grounds.
address: Gunton Hall, 1 Stable Yard, Hanworth, Norwich NR11 7HJ, UK

title: White Lady Who Slaps
notes: Legend tells of the story of a milkmaid who met a ghost at the castle. The white lady requested that the milkmaid returned the following night, but the girl stayed away. The next day, the white lady hunted down the girl and slapped her for disobedience! More recently, a jogger spotted a woman in a white dress (possibly a wedding gown) standing in the path ahead him. The woman turned and started running just ahead of the jogger. The woman in white suddenly vanished on a bend on the path, and the jogger felt the hair on the back of his neck stand up.

title: White Lady
notes: Seen by local fishermen (even though they cannot remember the exact date they witnessed the phenomenon), the white lady's history is unknown.
address: Hampton Ct Way, Molesey, East Molesey KT8 9AU, UK

title: Nun
location_name: Hanham Abbots (Somerset) - General area, but mainly around the church
notes: The roads of this small village are reportedly haunted by the shade of a nun. A white woman also stalks the area, though this may be the same entity.

title: Lady Jane Stanhope
location_name: Harrington (Northamptonshire) - Tollemache Arms public house, fields behind the building
notes: This white lady, condemned to haunt the area where she murdered her gardener after he accidentally killed a plant, only appears to those who are due to die within a few months.
address: 49 High St, Harrington, Northampton NN6 9NU, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: Harston (Cambridgeshire) - Area between the Queens Head public house and Mill Road
notes: This phantom woman is likely to be the same suicidal figure that haunts elsewhere in the village.
address: Fowlmere Rd, Newton, Cambridge CB22 7PG, UK

title: Suicidal Leap
location_name: Harston (Cambridgeshire) - Road bridge en route to Haslingfield
notes: Here a white lady has been seen throwing herself off the bridge, but like so many other ghost stories, the reason behind it is unclear.

title: White Woman
notes: A farmer reported seeing this pale spectre on several moonlit nights crossing the stable yard - he would always tip his cap to her! The ghost was also observed entering the front door in the hall itself and drifting up the staircase. The rustling of her dress could be heard moving down corridors at night. Another phantom reported her is the ghost of a mason caught playing dice instead of working - he was executed!

title: White Lady
notes: Even though the phantom white lady has not been observed for around one hundred years, she still opens a single door on one night per annum. A 1927 newspaper report claimed the figure to be Charlotte Bronte and that the appearance upset the family's dog, although the vicar denied the story.

title: Dragging
location_name: Hayfield (Derbyshire) - South Head Farm, and surrounding area
notes: A woman was murdered by her lover; her body was dragged down the stairs before being dumped in a pool at Stoney Ford. Many people have reputedly heard the bumps of her body as it descends the staircase. The area around the farm is said to be haunted by a woman in white, most likely to be the murder victim in a state of unrest.

title: Jolly Fat Man
location_name: Hemel Hempstead (Hertfordshire) - Old King's Arms public house
notes: This jovial chap was seen laughing away as he sat on a bed. A white female form has also been reported on site.
address: The Olde Kings Arms, 41 High St, Hemel Hempstead HP1 3AF, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: Hey Houses (Lancashire) - Fancy Lodge (no longer standing)
notes: This small cottage was haunted by a lady in white, who would vanish if anyone came too close.

title: Woman's Singing
location_name: Hickling (Norfolk) - Hickling Broad, south of village
notes: Once a year a gentle female voice can be heard singing. This could be the Woman in White, seen punting from one side for the Broad to another, heading towards a mill. Another ghost is associated with the broad; a drummer is said to be a Napoleonic soldier who drowned while skating across the ice, en route for a secret rendezvous with his girlfriend, can sometimes be heard.

title: White Lady
notes: The area around this farm was said to be haunted by a drifting white woman.

title: Waving White Lady in a Coach
notes: After losing control of the horses, a coach driver and his female passenger died. Now, the phantom vehicle is seen outside the church; the white woman in the carriage waves at witnesses before the coach pulls away and the accident is repeated.

title: White Lady
notes: The gates to this hall are reportedly haunted by a white lady who has unnerved several drivers through the village.
address: Hockwold Hall, Station Rd, Hockwold cum Wilton, Brandon IP26 4HZ, UK

title: Juliet Tewsley, Her Love Unrequited
location_name: Holywell (Cambridgeshire) - Ye Olde Ferryboat Inn, gravestone in bar
notes: Juliet took her own life after being jilted by a lover. The pub was later built over her grave, and she is now seen in the form of a white lady, appearing near the Ferry Boat Inn and walking towards the banks of the river on the date of her death.

title: Rebecca
location_name: Horton (North Yorkshire) - Bracken Hall, village green, and surrounding moor land
notes: Murdered the day before her wedding by her fiancee, Rebecca now appears in her white dress in protest over her rough treatment.

title: Party Girl
notes: This phantom young woman in a white party dress is reported to be barefoot.
address: Drury Ln, Houghton Regis, Dunstable LU5, UK

title: Woman in Nightdress
notes: Several residents and at least one visitor spotted a phantom woman in a white nightdress during their stay in this house. Footsteps were also heard on the site, doors would slam shut, and one person found themselves locked in a room. One of the children injured themselves trying to float down the stairs like their 'friend'.
address: Bigwood Ave, Brighton and Hove, Hove BN3, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: Ipswich (Suffolk) - Gippeswyk Hall (currently occupied by Red Rose Chain)
notes: This ghostly white lady has been seen by several witnesses in the upper part of the hall. One person reported a strange mist emerging from the chimney, though no fires were burning within.

title: Waiting Woman
notes: This railway museum is haunted by a woman in white who is said to wait for a lover who never returns home. This Victorian woman later took her own life by throwing herself under a train.
address: Lavender Line, Isfield Station, Station Rd, Isfield, Uckfield TN22 5XB, UK

title: White Lady
notes: The temperature is said to drop sharply as the phantom white lady materialises. One workman fled the site after encountering her.
address: Red Lion, 24 High St, Kegworth, Derby DE74 2DA, UK

title: White Lady
notes: This phantom white lady, quite possibly a nun, was said to live in harmony with a former owner (a Doctor Bedford). She was seen on the landing and near the staircase. It may be fair to speculate that a white lady that haunts several other local buildings may be the same entity.

title: Walled Up
notes: When they were discovered in each other' arms, the wife of the Hall's owner and her secret lover were forcefully separated - she was locked in her bedroom, and he bricked up behind a wall. They both starved to death. The wife now manifests as the Grey Lady and ascends the staircase, and the lover peers out of a window at passers-by. A white lady also haunts the site, as does a Highlander, and a coachman who walks on the surface of the lake looking for his sunken coach. In 2019, a witness in an empty room watched a child's chair being pulled away from a table and then slide back in, as if someone unseen had sat down.
address: Bradford Rd, Riddlesden, Keighley BD20 5EL, UK

title: White Lady
notes: A female member of the Schivas family was hanged from the bridge after she refused to betray her husband. Her shade was said to haunt the area of her death.

title: White Woman
location_name: Kidderminster (Hereford & Worcester) - Ye Olde Seven Stars, Coventry Street
notes: This ghostly figure was seen standing by the bar, as if waiting to be served. Her presence was also reported at a table.

title: White Lady
notes: A gamekeeper who spotted a woman's face at the window of the empty castle quickly gathered assistance to discover who the intruder was. The men found nothing, and so the white lady was blamed for the sighting. The ghostly lady was also seen by others at the chapel; an old blacksmith tried to speak to her, causing the phantom to disappear.

title: Bloodied Woman
notes: A young lady of the Cromlix family fell in love with Sir Malise Graham (known as the Black Knight of Kilbryde), who returned the feelings by murdering her and hiding the body. The woman's ghost returned, dressed in white and covered in blood, beckoning any witness who spotted her to approach. Most ran away, until one man did as the ghost requested. The woman then led the man to a nearby glen where he discovered a woman's body; the ghost returned no more.

title: White Lady
location_name: Kilcosgriff (County Limerick) - Kilcosgriff Castle (no longer standing)
datetime: Prior to a death in the Langford family
notes: The wife of a former Langford man, this woman now only appears before a death in the family.

title: Lady in White
notes: This female form is said to occasionally appear on a staircase and in the garden. A legend says this ghost (or perhaps others) ensure that no guest will have a restful night within the castle.
address: The Parade, Dukesmeadows, Kilkenny, R95 YRK1, Ireland

title: White Ladies
location_name: Kilmany (Fife) - Area known as Ghouls' Den (aka Gouls Den), near Kilmany Cottage
notes: Two white ladies are said to lurk in the ravine, as are the ghosts of people who found the area so pretty that they were unable to leave.

title: White Woman on Horseback
location_name: Kilpatrick (County Cork) - Road near old quarry
notes: Manifesting when moonlight hit the ground, this white lady is said to have been killed as her horse bolted somewhere north of the village, the panicked creature throwing her into a quarry.

title: White Lady
location_name: Kilspindie (Perth and Kinross) - Lady's Brig, area of land close to Balmyre Farm
notes: The history of this phantom female is unknown, but speculated to have been murdered, or was a murderer, or could have been a nun. Or all three. Or none.

title: Woman in White
location_name: Kineton (Warwickshire) - Area known as Madam's Lane (no longer present?)
notes: A ghostly woman in white would upset anyone using the lane.

title: Small Lady
location_name: King's Lynn (Norfolk) - The Tudor Rose Hotel, 11 Nicholas Street
notes: Shortly after a wedding, the bride was stabbed to death by her new husband in the hotel. Since then, a short woman in a long white dress has been spotted and phantom footsteps heard.
address: Tudor Rose Hotel, St Nicholas St, King's Lynn PE30 1NH, UK

title: White Lady
notes: Her appearance preluded by coughing and shuffling sounds, staff and customers in the building spotted the apparition of an old woman as she passed down the corridors. A later story would emerge that the owner, Jack Bond, created the ghostly tale, asking a friend to wear a white sheet and pass by the building's windows.
address: Station Rd, Kingham, Chipping Norton OX7 6UP, UK

title: White Lady
notes: This woman leapt to her death from the battlements after discovering that her father had mistakenly shot dead her newlywed husband. Since her death, she has been seen walking through locked doors and was blamed for knocking unconscious at least two witnesses. The site was also haunted by a rabbit which attacked guards asleep on duty.
address: OPW, Summercove, Kinsale, Co. Cork, Ireland

title: White Lady
location_name: Kinsale (County Cork) - Road from Fort to Desmond Castle
notes: Julian Hawthorne's minor vampire story Ken's Mystery is said to be based on an encounter with a phantom woman who travelled from the fort to Desmond Castle, where she vanished.
address: Cork St, Town-Plots, Kinsale, Co. Cork, Ireland

title: Pale Woman
location_name: Kirkbean (Dumfries and Galloway) - Road between village and Prestonmill
notes: A woman in white was said to haunt this stretch of road, walking along the treetops and occasionally on the road with (mortal) travellers.

title: Woman in White
location_name: Kirkbean (Dumfries and Galloway) - Road coming in from the north
notes: This phantom woman's past is unknown, although her quiet form was said to startle passersby. One local story says that a local farmer's daughter exploited the story; dressing in a white sheet to meet her lover by the roadside ensured no one came to speak to her.

title: Dunn
location_name: Kirkbean (Dumfries and Galloway) - Three crossroads, near Arbigland
notes: The body of a young stableman by the name of Dunn was found here - the law believed he had committed suicide, but others thought he had been murdered by the brother of a woman he was romantically involved with. Dunn's ghost was said to have returned to the spot, and occasionally the area was also haunted by a white lady.

title: Jenny
notes: When a private house, this building was haunted by a white woman in a beautiful flowing dress. There were also reports of a phantom goose, which would peck at the young girl who lived there.

title: Woman with Dog
notes: A woman dressed in white escorted by a ghostly dog are said to haunt this inn. Other spectres reported here include a headless lady and a man dressed in black.

title: White Lady
notes: This lady's untrusting husband locked her in a room as he went overseas to war - by the time he returned, all that remained was her shade, which now haunts the tower.
address: Golf Club Ltd, Lancaster LA2 0AJ, UK

title: White Woman
notes: Singing has been reported coming from the building when it is empty, and a ghostly woman dressed in white has also been seen. This entity may be the same as the grey lady reportedly seen in the chapel.

title: Woman in White
notes: This female form was briefly seen before vanishing into the corner of a bedroom. At least one guest left suddenly after spending the night in the room.

title: Lady of Lawers
location_name: Lawers (Perth and Kinross) - General area of the ruined village, on banks of Loch Tay
notes: This long deserted village is home to the phantom white lady of Lawers. Workers at a nearby hotel report the feeling of being watched in the area.

title: Woman in White
location_name: Leebotwood (Shropshire) - Unnamed pool in the area
notes: A woman wearing white who took her own life in the reputedly bottomless pool emerges at night and wanders lanes in the area.

title: Abbot
notes: The former Abbot of the abbey walks the area that has been converted into a museum. It is more usual to hear the spirit moving around than see him. Traditionally the entity is said to wear white robes, although the sighting in the 1970s reported the ghost's robes as being brown/red. The abbey grounds are also home to a white female entity named Mary who witnessed her lover committing murder and turned him in.
address: Abbey Rd, Kirkstall, Leeds LS5 3EH, UK

title: May
notes: Several phantoms are reported to haunt this area, including a young woman in white who walks the corridors. She is named as May, who died aged eighteen of tuberculosis. Other phantoms are said to be carriages pulled by black horses which vanish into the trees, a groom who hanged himself in a room above the stables, and a sad looking boy who glazes from an upstairs window.

title: White Woman
notes: This pale woman has been seen moving around the headstones.

title: Disconcerting Monk
notes: There are mixed opinions to whether the library here is haunted by a grey monk or a white lady. A tormented Cavalier has been seen in the Great Hall, a phantom policeman haunts other parts of the building, a ghostly dog has been reported in the courtyard, a black cat in the Great Hall, and at least one witness claims to have been shocked to see a pair of legs manifest from the portrait of Henry Earl of Huntington hanging in the Major's Parlour. Ghost Scene Investigations and Leicester City Museums released video footage in 2006 of what appears to be a ghostly figure crawling across the floor in one room.

title: Slapper
location_name: Leven (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Road near White Cross
notes: It is said the area was once haunted by a headless white woman who would hunt down highwaymen and give them a slap. What became of the entity is not known.

title: Lord Byron
notes: The story goes Lord Byron once met the ghostly black friar that haunted this building for close to 500 years, though contemporary legends state Bryon himself now sits in the corridor, near his own picture. Also reported in the area is a white lady who smells of rose petals, Bryon's pet dog Boatswain, and a woman in black who is considered unlucky.
address: Newstead Abbey, Nottingham Rd, Ravenshead, Nottingham NG15 9HJ, UK

title: White Woman
location_name: Lincoln (Lincolnshire) - Great Hall, near Edward King's House
notes: The woman who haunts this building may be connected to the skull which is bricked up within a wall. The skull once stood in a room, but when it was removed from the building it started to scream. The owners decided it would be for the best to brick it up in a wall to prevent future accidental removal.

title: White Woman
notes: This pale woman is thought to have been a former prisoner who died during her stay. Witnesses have also reported the sounds of doors slamming and keys jangling in the area. One witness heard footsteps and the rustling of a dress - when they looked around, no one could be seen.

title: Mary of Guise
notes: Accompanied by a slight smell of perfume, this phantom lady in a white dress has on occasion been mistaken for a real person. Mary Queen of Scots is also reported to frequent the chapel, and Margaret Tudor haunts Queen Margaret's Bower.

title: White Woman
notes: The abbey was once haunted by a young woman dressed in white. She was seen and heard moving in the upper part of the building, though it is said the haunting stopped once a Major discovered a heart in a metal box in the attic and gave it a proper burial.

title: White Lady
notes: This spook is reportedly a woman whose body was found on the nearby beach. A long dead cat is also reported to play on the beds at night, while a man in 'modern' dress is said to slowly materialise from the feet up.

title: White Lady
location_name: Littlewick Green (Berkshire) - Boundary Elm, Green Lane, Cold Harbour and Knowl Hill
notes: This female apparition closely follows the same route as she calmly drifts through the area.
address: Green Ln, Littlewick Green, Maidenhead SL6, UK

title: Rosina Buckman
location_name: Llanbadrig (Gwynedd) - Area around Wylfa power station
notes: Rosina's ashes were moved when the power station was constructed on the site of her old house, and her pallid wraith now drifts around the vicinity. During the construction of the power station, several workers are said to have quit their jobs after encountering a phantom woman in white.

title: White Lady
location_name: Llangennith (West Glamorgan) - Main road through village
notes: A motorist reported almost running over (or through) a white lady that stepped out on the road in front of him.

title: White Lady
notes: This ghostly figure has appeared several times, manifesting in the middle of the road only to pass through incoming traffic. One male driver stated that the female form looked at him with sad eyes as she passed through the bonnet of his car.

title: White Woman
location_name: Llanvihangel Crucorney (Gwent) - Between Llanvihangel Court and Lady Wood
notes: This pallid figure moves from the hall and disappears into the nearby wood. There have also been sightings of a short man dressed in green.
address: Llanvihangel Court, Llanvihangel Crucorney, Abergavenny NP7 8DH, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: Llanvithyn (aka Llanveithin and Llanmeuthin) (South Glamorgan) - Former monastery (no longer standing)
notes: The monastery was thought haunted by a woman whose husband had sought shelter there - he was finally found, had his leg cut off and was finally bricked up alive within the walls. Centuries after the event, a legless skeleton was discovered in the building, although legend does not state whether this prevented the female phantom from returning.

title: White Lady
location_name: Longforgan (Perth and Kinross) - HMP Castle Huntly, open prison
notes: Her white robes flowing as she walks, the white lady is said to leapt from the battlements to escape punishment after being caught with a manservant. A young boy that also haunts the site is thought to be Richard Paterson who drowned in the Tay in 1939.
address: Castle Huntly, The Dr, Castle Huntly, Longforgan, Dundee DD2 5HL, UK

title: Lady in White
notes: A woman in a flowing white dress is said to haunt the banks of the river close to the village.

title: White Lady
notes: This white lady drowned in the pool, and now dances around the area. One man who saw the entity thought she was real and tried to dance with her, only to find his arms passed straight through her body.

title: Tied Figure
notes: A manor house in Lossiemouth was said to be haunted by a woman draped in white with a heavily scarred throat and her hands tied in chains behind her back. The figure was said to appear every night, and because of the stories, the site remained empty for years before being sold at a highly discounted price.

title: White Lady
location_name: Lower Boscaswell (Cornwall) - Pendeen Vau, aka Pendeen Fogou
notes: This figure in white holds a red rose in her mouth and turns and walks into the fogou once spotted. Some say to see her will bring misfortune, and anyone foolish enough to follow her... well, no one as yet has followed her.

title: Headless Man
location_name: Lulsley (Hereford & Worcester) - Sandy Lane (between Osebury Rock and the river)
notes: One folk story says this road is home to several entities that appear to travellers, including a headless man, a white horse, a black dog, and a sad woman with flowing hair who wears a white robe.

title: Ann
location_name: Luton (Bedfordshire) - Cock and Bull public house
notes: A white figure glimpsed here is thought to be the ghost of Ann, murdered in the pub so long ago that the date has been forgotten. She has been known to talk to visitors who are unaware she is a phantom. The figure may be the same entity which has been seen on the street outside, moving from the pub towards nearby shops.

title: Factory Worker
notes: On several occasions the ghost of a white woman managed to unnerve workers at the plant. She was thought to be the spirit of a former worker who died nine years previous.

title: Woman in Gown
location_name: Lydford Gorge (Devon) - White Lady Falls (aka Whitelady Waterfall)
notes: A phantom white woman in a long white gown is said to haunt the base of the waterfall.

title: White Lady
notes: This pale figure was seen standing over a guest's bed. She has also been observed in the bar.

title: White Lady
notes: One of several ghosts to be found in the hall, this unknown Clifton family member has been seen in various parts of the grounds. One bedroom is said to be particularly haunted, the manifestation complete with clanking chains and stomping feet.
address: Southpark, Lytham, Lytham Saint Annes FY8 4QU, UK

title: White Woman
location_name: Macclesfield (Cheshire) - Kendel Road (area known locally as the Birdy)
notes: This area is reputed to be haunted by a white woman with no feet.

title: Young Girl
notes: Wearing a long white dress, this young girl barely in her teens has been reported in several parts of the village. It is thought she drowned in a flood which swept through the area in the mid-nineteenth century.

title: Hound
location_name: Mainsriddle (Dumfries and Galloway) - Road between village and Prestonmill, area once known as Dairymaid's Hollow
notes: A black dog which was said to haunt this area was once heard invisibly passing by three women, although one of them speculated the sound may have been a peacock. A woman in white was also seen by a doctor using the road.

title: White Lady
notes: This female form causes trouble on the roads as she drifts out in front on oncoming traffic. Some witnesses say the entity is more like a monk than a woman and a cyclist who spotted the figure in the road in 1960 said the phantom figure was twice the height of a normal person.
address: Castle Hill Dr, Manchester M9, UK

title: Woman in White
location_name: Mansfield (Nottinghamshire) - A60 and area around Hall Place (no longer standing)
notes: This phantom woman in a white dress is thought to have once haunted Hall Place before it burnt down - her ghost has now migrated to the nearby road.

title: White Woman
location_name: Mansfield Woodhouse (Nottinghamshire) - The Vale woodland and surrounding area
notes: The woodland is said to be haunted by a white female form. She is thought to be connected to the ruins of a house on the outskirts of the woodland.

title: White Rider
location_name: Marazion (Cornwall) - Area once known as Marazion Green
notes: In the days when horses were the main form of transport, this white woman would leap onto the back of a passing rider and refuse to leave until taken to the Red River, where she would vanish. The last witness, Buck Billy, died in 1840.

title: White Lady
location_name: Market Lavington (Wiltshire) - Old Rectory (no longer standing)
notes: The building was haunted by a white female spectre murdered in the property.

title: White Woman
notes: Before the mansion burnt down, it stood empty for twenty years. The caretaker reported seeing a phantom grey lady within the building, and even though the mansion was boarded up, furnishings and fittings would be found thrown around within. Some believe the grey lady started the fire which finally reduced the mansion to a shell which was demolished.

title: White Lady Riding
notes: A drunk man fell asleep in the barn, waking at midnight to see a woman in white riding around on a buck. She moved so fast that the wind whipped up dust, causing the man to sneeze - the sound causing the lady to vanish.

title: White Woman
notes: Two members of staff, on different occasions, witnessed a woman in a long flowing dress pass through a room in this building. There have also been reports of poltergeist activity, which included a large table being overturned.

title: White Lady
location_name: Milton Abbas (Dorset) - Road through the village
notes: Sightings of this phantom white lady are said not to be uncommon, though any real details are fleeting.
address: Milton Abbas, Blandford Forum DT11, UK

title: Mary Way
location_name: Much Wenlock (Shropshire) - Crossroads two miles southeast of town
notes: This crossroads was said to be haunted by the ghost of Mary Way, murdered on the site. The phantom is reputedly headless and wears a shimmering white dress. A murderer was also said to be buried at the crossroads, although it is not clear whether it was Mary's killer.

title: Monks Cooking
notes: Several shades of religious people have been seen here, including monks, a bishop and a woman in white. The smell of fresh meat being cooked sometimes wafts through the house, even when no one is cooking.

title: Woman with Hollow Eyes
location_name: N16 (Greater London) - 69 Spencer Grove (no longer standing), Stoke Newington
notes: This ghost, dressed in white, was seen emerging from a wardrobe by several members of the family. The family finally left the building after a mysterious fire broke out.

title: White Woman
location_name: N2 (Greater London) - Strawberry Vale, East Finchley
notes: The pallid ghost of a lady was reported here.

title: White Woman
location_name: N3 (Greater London) - Nether Street, West Finchley
notes: One police officer is said to have lost his mind after seeing this phantom woman and became obsessed with reading everything he could about local ghost stories.
address: West Finchley, Nether St, London N3 1NT, UK

title: Man with Hound
location_name: Navan (County Meath) - Skyrne Castle, Hill of Tara
notes: A phantom man, tall and covered by a cloak, once haunted this area, accompanied by his hunting hound. The area is also home to a shadowy nun and a woman in white who screams as she is chased by an unidentified shadow.
address: Skryne Castle, Skreen, Skryne, Co. Meath, Ireland

title: White Lady
notes: Falling in love and becoming pregnant by the lord of the castle, a local girl committed suicide after being shunned by him after their love affair. Though her mother then cursed the evil lord (resulting in his death, and that of the estate's future heir), the young lass still returns to the site.

title: Woman in White
location_name: Nettlestone (Isle of Wight) - Road near the old church
notes: Several motorists are said to have encountered the phantom woman dressed in white that haunts this road, reporting their sightings to the police after driving through her.

title: Bride
location_name: Newton le Willows (Merseyside) - Willow Park (and other meres and woods in the area)
notes: This woman in white is said to be a bride murdered by her husband on their wedding night hundreds of years ago. She may have last been seen by a married couple who spotted a figure with folded arms standing amongst trees. Two years prior to this sighting, two woman passing Castle Hill observed the phantom woman in white.
address: Willow Park, 14 Southworth Rd, Newton-le-Willows WA12 0BG, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: Newton Stewart (Dumfries and Galloway) - Slaughterhouse Woods
notes: The woodland takes its name from a disused slaughterhouse. The white female form seen drifting in the area is thought to be the ghost of a woman involved in a car accident nearby.
address: Woodlands Dr, Newton Stewart DG8, UK

title: White Woman
location_name: North Berwick (Lothian) - Private residence, Glenorchy Road
notes: The staircase of this property is reputedly haunted by a benign white feminine spirit. The house may have been built over the graveyard of a nunnery.
address: Glenorchy Rd, North Berwick EH39, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: North Berwick (Lothian) - The Lodge (aka North Berwick House)
notes: A phantom white lady reputedly walks this area, as does a hooded figure said to be a former pageboy murdered in London.

title: White Lady
notes: Killed by soldiers when she was discovered in a chapel, the shade of this white lady has been reported many times since.

title: Lady in White
notes: Seen wandering around the gate of the church, this woman in white is thought to be looking for an unknown item. The ruined church itself is home to a tall man who has been seen standing at the location the altar once stood.

title: Children
notes: The ghosts of a white woman and of two children have been reported in this wooded area. While the woman in white has been named as Mary Chaworth Musters, the children are thought to have been murdered by their father William Saville, hanged for the crime in 1844. The sound of horses has also been reported.
address: Colwick Woods Ct, Sneinton, Nottingham NG2, UK

title: White Lady
notes: This piece of woodland is reputedly haunted by a white lady, a nun and disembodied screaming.

title: White Lady
notes: Thought to be the same ghost encountered at the nearby church, this white woman is thought by some to be Mary Ann Chaworth-Musters reputedly Lord Byron's first love.
address: Colwick Park, Racecourse Rd, Nottingham NG2 4BH, UK

title: Woman in White
notes: A bus driver passing through the village was left unnerved after watching a woman dressed in white cross the road ahead of him. She disappeared suddenly.

title: White Woman
location_name: Old Catton (Norfolk) - White Woman Lane, and George Hill
notes: Doomed to repeat her actions for reasons unknown, the woman in white has been seen on countless occasions in the area. A lad out on his early morning paper round saw her crossing a field, vanishing without warning.
address: White Woman Ln, Norwich NR6, UK

title: Robert Leigh
notes: One of many ghosts belonging to this building, Leigh was reported to walk around the grounds carrying an unknown person's head under one arm. A woman in white rustling silk glides along the corridors of the building, the sad sounds of a harpsichord can be heard, and on occasion a black dog lurks outside.

title: White Woman
notes: Only appearing in front of children, this pale shade walks the hotel.

title: Faceless Monk
notes: This giant figure is said to have had a skull for a head, a result of black magic rituals carried out in days gone by. He has made at least one witness drop dead of shock (although the death was also said to have been caused by a phantom old lady in black). The grounds are also haunted by a white lady, gliding effortlessly along.
address: Rufford Abbey Country Park, Ollerton NG22 9DF, UK

title: Former Master with Hounds
location_name: Oulton (Norfolk) - High House (now Manor House), Oulton Broad
notes: Even though this place has been exorcised at least twice, the building still appears to possess several ghosts. One former owner has been seen dressed in traditional hunting gear, with his hounds around his feet. A white lady reportedly climbs the staircase located in the older part of the house, while the sounds associated with an old carriage have been heard coming from outside. Finally, poltergeist like activity, such as unusual electrical interference, has been reported.

title: Lady Constance
location_name: Outer London: Bexley (Greater London) - Hall Place
datetime: Constance unknown, Edward in October 1931, poltergeist 1950
notes: One of several ghosts reported here, Lady Constance killed herself after watching her husband die when a stag gored him. Her pale white shade now frequents the grounds. Lady Limerick reported seeing the ghostly Edward, the Black Prince during the First World War, and he popped up again in 1931. The spirit of a servant haunts the loft, while a poltergeist outbreak in 1950 resulted in furniture being tossed around.
address: Hall Place, Bourne Rd, Dartford, bexley DA5 1PQ, UK

title: White Woman
location_name: Outer London: Brentford (Greater London) - Banks of the Grand Union Canal
notes: A torso of a female murder victim was discovered here, with her legs found in a train carriage at Waterloo. Her ghost remained in the area for a while, manifesting as a tall woman wearing a white dress.

title: White Lady
location_name: Panshanger Woods (Hertfordshire) - Area near an abandoned decoy air shelter
notes: This area is rumoured to be haunted by a woman who committed infanticide.

title: White Woman
location_name: Passage West (County Cork) - Road towards Cork, and other roads through village
notes: Not much is known about the phantom woman who was said to haunt the road to Cork. A phantom coach and horses haunted other roads in the area, the noise of its passing causing household furnishings to shake.
address: Pembroke, Passage West, Co. Cork, Ireland

title: White Woman
notes: This pale creature flitters around the building, having died of a broken heart.

title: White Woman
location_name: Penallt (Gwent) - Between the Argoed (private residence) and nearby pond
notes: Dressed all in white, this woman committed suicide in the pond where her ghost now vanishes.

title: Woman in White
notes: A female phantom is said to have left the church via the east gate and walk down the hill. Author Eddie Brazil speculated that the phantom was Ruth Ellis, the last woman to have been executed in England.

title: White Lady
notes: A local legend says that this area is haunted by a white lady and a small child. Minor polt activity is also reported, such as rattling doors, flickering lights and glasses being smashed. The presence is described as playful.
address: Church Rd, Penn, High Wycombe HP10 8NY, UK

title: White Woman
notes: No one really has any idea who this phantom could be, though her appearance is said to warn against approaching bad luck and can bring about improved health and fortune. Some reports say she is green, rather than white.

title: Thomas Hunter
notes: Sergeant Thomas Hunter, with the Anzacs during the First World War, died in the building 31 July 1916 when it was a hospital. Hunter was last seen drifting up the staircase, though he is blamed for moving items around the building at night or placing icy cold hands on people. Other entities said to haunt here include a scruffy man in the cellar, a white woman in the upper part of the building, a young girl in the former operating theatre, and a phantom Roman soldier who stands by a sword that is on display.

title: Pale Lady
notes: Staff and visitors have reported seeing a phantom white lady around this building.
address: The Village, Orton Longueville, Peterborough PE2 7DN, UK

title: Woman in White
location_name: Plymouth (Devon) - Geriatric wards, Mount Gould Hospital
notes: These wards are reputed to be built on the site of a battle and numerous unexplained occurrences are said to have happened. One male staff member reported a white lady who disappeared into a room. On investigation, the room was found empty, but the patient the man was standing beside at the time of the sighting died before morning, even though he was not obviously unwell.
address: 200 Mount Gould Rd, Plymouth PL4 7QD, UK

title: Monk
notes: This park is said to be haunted by a figure wearing a habit near the gates of the lodge, a white lady who drowned in a lake while eloping, and a strange animal with very large eyes.
address: Radford Park, Plymstock, Plymouth PL9 7NY, UK

title: White Lady
notes: Reported to float above the castle ruins, this phantom white lady could be Jane de Afan, the last occupant of the castle.

title: White Lady
notes: This phantom woman appears on or near the castle mound, located close to Saint Mary's churchyard.

title: Lady in Brown
location_name: Portland (Dorset) - Portland Hotel, Portland Square (no longer standing)
notes: Six sailors stayed at this hotel after their ship was unable to leave the docks due to bad weather. After spending time in the bar, the sailors headed to their rooms. Two of the men spotted a woman wearing a long brown Victorian style dress with a white bonnet and shawl on her shoulders standing outside of a room. The figure disappeared before being seen by the other four men.
address: Isle of Portland, Portland DT5, UK

title: Man in Purple Cloak
notes: Named as either Peter Carey (an English invader) or Richard de Burgh (Earl of Antrim), this ethereal figure now walks around the broken towers of the castle. A white lady (possibly Mave Roe) also haunts the place; she died of a broken heart after being denied marrying her true love. Another story tells that, during a fierce storm, the castle's kitchens collapsed into the sea - the screams of the staff can be heard as they forever fall to their doom.

title: Grey Figure
notes: This entity, clad in a grey cloak, is seen entering the churchyard before vanishing. A white lady has also been reported more recently on the site.

title: Woman in Dress
location_name: Radcliffe on Trent (Nottinghamshire) - Grounds of Holme Pierrepont Hall
notes: This lady wearing a white flowing dress has been reported quietly drifting around the grounds.
address: Holme Pierrepont Hall, Holme Pierrepont, Nottingham NG12 2LD, UK

title: The Ayloffs
notes: A few ghosts are reported to haunt this manor. Roman soldiers have been seen walking through walls in the basement, and a lady in white has been seen on the lower half of the stairs. Another lady seen on the stairs is thought to be Martha Ayloff, while Anne Ayloff, wearing a lavender dress, haunts the room adjacent to room 7.
address: Rainham Rd S, Rainham, Hornchurch RM13 7LP, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: Randalstown (County Antrim) - Shane's Castle (aka Shanes Castle)
notes: This pallid female wraith cursed the family with everlasting sorrow and misfortune. She is said to be quite tall and slender, with blue eyes and blonde hair. The figure was last seen in 1966, where the entity was said to be on her knees, crying, bathed in bluish-white light. A banshee has also been heard wailing at the castle; one man who heard the entity as he left the site died before his journey ended.

title: White Lady of Kilcosgriff
notes: The phantom white lady from Kilcosgriff Castle is said to have come to this building when a Major Langford died here. She was seen by a nurse treating the Major.

title: Lady in White
notes: This ghostly woman in white could be heard moving things around the shop and was said to have touched the back of the neck of one witness. It is thought that the woman died in a house fire.

title: Woman in White
location_name: Reagill (Cumbria) - Country lanes in the area
notes: It is not clear if the woman in white, a large black hound and a black hog which once haunted this area were all the same entity. The phantom (or phantoms) would all suddenly grow once seen, before pursuing any witness.

title: Young Boy
notes: The swimming baths are reputedly to be haunted by two phantoms. One is a young boy who diving into the pool while wearing a First World War German helmet his father had brought back from the trenches. The lad landed badly and snapped his neck. The second ghost is that of a woman dressed in white.

title: White Lady
location_name: Rhiwsaeson (South Glamorgan) - Unknown road in the area
notes: A woman in white who haunted the area was said to have tried to locate a newborn baby, the kiss of which would release her from her fate.

title: White Woman
location_name: Rhosili (West Glamorgan) - Paviland Cave, between village and Port Eynon
notes: This cave is haunted by a greedy woman who entered it looking for hidden treasure - a sudden storm blew up, trapping and drowning her.

title: White Woman
location_name: Rhyl (Clwyd) - Start of the Dyserth Road bends
notes: The white figure of a lady was seen standing behind a wall, leaning over the top.

title: White Woman
location_name: Risca (Gwent) - Miner's graves near campsite in nearby forest
notes: This area is home to a phantom woman in white and a strange shadowy figure, speculated by some to be a former miner.

title: White Woman & Coach
notes: It is said that a phantom white woman haunts this area, sometimes seen climbing into a ghostly horse-drawn coach which then drives off.

title: Pale Lady
notes: This phantom woman in white who once frequently walked through walls and closed doors may be connected to the 'Boggart Room', where an infant's coffin was stored to avoid paying Shroud Tax.

title: Arbella Stuart
notes: Some believe the phantom white lady said to haunt the abbey is the ghost of Arbella Stuart, although her connection to the site is a little unclear. A photograph hit the press in 2013 showing a 'white misty figure', although the image is open to interpretation.
address: Rufford Abbey Country Park, Ollerton NG22 9DF, UK

title: Dancing Woman
location_name: Ryde (Isle of Wight) - Royal Victoria Yacht Club
notes: Wearing a white gown, a phantom woman is said to dance alone in the ballroom. Other entities reputed to haunt the side include another woman who is fond of men, a woman in a black dress spotted on the staircase, a couple of children, three drinkers at the bar, and finally the friendly entity known only as Albert.

title: Restless Dead
location_name: Saddell (Argyll and Bute) - Abbey and castle
notes: It is said that a former laird removed the headstones from nearby churchyards to use as construction material for his castle - hence, much of the area is haunted by those disturbed. Particularly noted are the shades of a monk and that of a white lady.

title: White Lady
notes: Some people believe that this phantom figure is Margaret Radclyffe, Queen Elizabeth's favourite Maid of Honour. She died of a broken heart in 1559 shortly after the death of her brother.

title: Phantom Footfalls
location_name: Salisbury (Wiltshire) - Haunch of Venison public house
datetime: Random days between 11.30 & midnight (footfalls), white lady unknown
notes: The footfalls may belong to the owner of a severed hand which was discovered behind a secret panel within the pub. This ghost is also held responsible for moving dirty glasses in the bar. The hand was stolen from the pub in March 2004. Another phantom, that of a white lady, is said to appear at windows.

title: John Wilson
notes: Wilson was murdered just for being in the wrong place at the wrong time, and his ghost still protests. A blonde-haired lady dressed in white also haunts the castle - she was murdered in the late sixteenth century, though her body not discovered for almost three hundred years.

title: Highwayman's Girlfriend
notes: This woman was murdered by a jealous partner after he discovered that she was having an affair with a highwayman; the ghostly lady in white now walks the staircase and the first floor.

title: White Lady
notes: This ghost is believed to be that of a woman who died of head trauma - a skull found in the 1830s near the site possessed a large hole caused by a blunt object. Shooter's Hill also made the news in 1963, as it was combed by police and soldiers looking for a puma-like creature that had been seen by members of the public.

title: Woman in White
location_name: Seaton (Leicestershire) - B672 Road, under the Seaton Viaduct bridge
notes: While travelling home, a driver spotted a woman in white clothing, with a pale face, standing by a gate by one of the bridge arches. Finding it strange for someone to be there, the driver slowed down to take a closer look. The figure was completely motionless. The driver passed her, and looking into the rear view mirror, realised the figure had vanished.

title: White Lady
location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - Beauchief Abbey and Hall
notes: This traditional white figure supposedly haunts the grounds of both the hall and the abbey. The latter location is also the home to a spectral monk.
address: Beauchief Abbey House, Beauchief Abbey Ln, Beauchief, Sheffield S8 7BD, UK

title: White Woman
notes: Wearing a flowing dress, this pale spirit has occasionally been reported.
address: Bowshaw Cl, Batemoor, Sheffield S8, UK

title: White Lady
notes: Little is known about this phantom woman, other than an interesting point to note is that the road was once called Dead Man's Lane.

title: Governess
notes: Now returning to the hall in the form of a white lady, the governess had her throat slit by the hall's Squire after she threatened to tell his wife of their affair. The sounds of their final argument can also be heard drifting around the hall at night. A man staying here also reported a phantom hound which awoke him at night.
address: High St, Mosborough, Sheffield S20 5EA, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: Shepton Mallet (Somerset) - HMP Shepton Mallet (former prison)
datetime: Mid twentieth century (White Lady), Circa 2008 (American Serviceman)
notes: Believed by staff at the prison to be the ghost of a woman executed there during the seventeenth century, this female presence was blamed for every cold spot and freak gust of wind within the building. More recently, staff have reported the ghost of an American serviceman, thought to be related to the period when the site was used by the US military.

title: White Lady
location_name: Shinnel (aka Shinnell) Water (Dumfries and Galloway) - Paul's Pool
notes: A white lady was said to haunt this little waterfall along the river, hovering while bathed in moonlight.

title: White Woman
location_name: Shipton Gorge (Dorset) - Private residence along Dorchester Road
notes: A headless woman in a white gown was said to haunt this location.

title: Nun
notes: This ghostly nun was once watched as she slowly dissolved into nothingness. She has also been heard walking in empty parts of the building, though the footsteps could belong to a white woman (thought to be a murdered maid) who also haunts the castle.
address: Skryne Castle, Skreen, Skryne, Co. Meath, Ireland

title: Slim Woman
notes: This phantom woman in white died of the traditional broken heart after her father drove away her one true love. The entity has been reported exorcised. Another story attached to the site says that the Devil arrived one night to join a game of cards. Old Nick fled after one gambler spotted a cloven foot under the table.
address: Loftus Hall, Loftushall, Fethard, Co. Wexford, Ireland

title: White Lady
notes: After a long period of absence, this phantom woman was spotted by a member of the public in 2006. Staff who work at the building report feeling a strange presence in some of the rooms in this hall.
address: Ayscoughfee Hall Museum and Gardens, Churchgate, Spalding PE11 2RA, UK

title: White Lady
notes: Folklore said a phantom white woman haunted this road.

title: White Woman
notes: This pale, lost shade could belong to one of the mummified bodies that were removed from a nearby tower in the mid-nineteenth century. A monk has been seen on the staircase, offering to help anyone struggling to ascend the tower.

title: Lady de Clare
location_name: St Athan (South Glamorgan) - Area around site of West Orchard castle
notes: Rumoured to have been buried alive by her cruel husband who (incorrectly) believed she was having an affair, this white lady was once said to walk the castle and the surrounding area.

title: White Woman
location_name: St Ouen (Jersey) - Rue a la Pendue
notes: La blianche femme, or white woman, is reputed to haunt this road. She gave chase to a local man in 1900, who had to take shelter in a house close by.
address: Rue a la Pendue, Jersey JE3, Jersey

title: Sarah Harrington
location_name: Staveley (Derbyshire) - The Hagge (aka Hagg) (former mansion, now a farmhouse)
notes: Harrington was a former Maid of Honor to Queen Henrietta and is thought to be the phantom woman in white who haunts this building. A mighty oak which stood in the garden was thought to scream and bled when cut; unfortunately, the tree blew down during a gale in 1883.

title: Lady in White
location_name: Stirling (Stirling) - Rownam Avenue, and area of former manor house
notes: The wife of a former owner of the local manor house, this white woman drifted down the road after dusk for many years after her death. Sometimes the stories colour her green, like so many other ghostly women in this part of the world.

title: Nun
location_name: Stratton St Margaret (Wiltshire) - Church and general area
notes: Dressed in white, the ghostly nun is said to haunt not only the church, but the entire village.
address: Stratton St Margaret, Swindon SN3 4RN, UK

title: Steam Engine
location_name: Stroud (Gloucestershire) - Woodchester Mansion, near the town
notes: A film crew spending a night in the building in 2001 reported loud disembodied banging that climaxed as if the sounds of a stream train charged through the house. The house is also reported to be haunted by a white lady, and the park surrounding the building is home to a Roman soldier, a figure resembling a monk, two American servicemen in Second World War uniforms, a headless horse and a dwarf which somersaults along the driveway. In 2019, a witness watched a horse and carriage driven by a man wearing a tall Victorian hat pass through a locked gate.
address: Trust Woodchester Park, Woodchester Mansion, Nympsfield, Stonehouse GL10 3TS, UK

title: Woman in White Coat
location_name: Studland (Dorset) - Pathway from Studland beach to road near The Banks Arms pub
notes: A woman wearing a white coat and white trousers, with hair down to her shoulders, walked past a group of four friends, telling them, 'I have just been for a swim' before disappearing from view. Four years later, one of the group spoke to a couple who had experienced the exact same encounter in the same area.
address: Manor Rd, Studland, Swanage BH19 3AU, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: Sutton Coldfield (West Midlands) - Westwood Coppice (west side of Sutton Park)
datetime: Reputedly every night (story dates from nineteenth century)
notes: A woman who was betrayed by her lover drowned herself in a nearby pond (that no longer exists). Her ghost, dressed in white, wrings her hands as it drifts through the trees.
address: Westwood Coppice, The Royal Town of Sutton Coldfield, Birmingham, Sutton Coldfield, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: SW14 (Greater London) - Old Ranelagh and Lower Richmond Road
notes: Sobbing for the loss of her lover, this white figure is seen moving across the grounds of Ranelagh and disappearing when she reaches the road.

title: Sarah
location_name: SW4 (Greater London) - Plough public house, Clapham Common
notes: Dressed in white, Sarah has been seen standing in the pub's bedrooms, her raven hair running over her shoulders. The ghost may be linked to the discovery of a secret, bricked up room in 1970.

title: White Lady
notes: The castle grounds are haunted by this crying white lady with a bloody back. Another story has the site haunted by a white horse.
address: Castle Ave, Mumbles, Swansea SA3 4BA, UK

title: Black Lady of Tamworth
notes: Said to be the ghost of St Editha, this phantom nun has a room named after her within the building - she once appeared here and bullied a greedy landlord into returning stolen church lands. Another female, a white lady, also haunts the castle grounds.

title: White Lady
datetime: Late nineteenth or early twentieth century
notes: A man driving a horse and cart through the woods was startled by the appearance of a white lady on the seat next to him - she vanished as he drove past the last tree of the forest. This story could be an early version of the phantom hitchhiker tales.

title: Blue & White Ladies
notes: The blue lady took her own life after watching her lover die during a jousting contest outside the castle - she now patrols the battlements. The white lady is thought to be a nun from a priory which once stood nearby.

title: Patrolling Woman
notes: A white woman has been spotted moving around the battlements at night.
address: Sleaford Rd, Tattershall, Lincoln LN4 4LR, UK

title: Hitchhiker
location_name: Taunton (Somerset) - A38 road near the town
notes: This man dressed in a long grey coat stands by the roadside, and if an oncoming car does not stop to offer him a ride, he throws himself in front of the vehicle. Either way, he vanishes without trace, normally leaving the witness scared and confused. A white female version of the man has also been reported in the same area.

title: White Lady
notes: It is said this woman was once washed up dead on the beach - locals removed her expensive rings and threw the body back into the sea. She is seen standing on the coast, looking into the sea, whenever stormy weather takes a life.

title: Monks
notes: Seen both inside and out, these figures drift forlornly about. Doors also open and close unaided by human hand, while the churchyard is said to be haunted by a screaming white woman.

title: Black Dog
notes: This former pet has been seen on the landing, though it is not known whether the white woman who haunts a bedroom is its mistress.

title: Lady in White
location_name: Thames Ditton (Surrey) - Home of Compassion church
notes: Appearing quite real and substantial, the room that this sad shade haunts remained locked up for many years. She could be one of the nuns who once worked in the building.
address: Home of Compassion, 58 High St, Thames Ditton KT7 0TT, UK

title: Lady in White
location_name: Thringstone (Leicestershire) - A512, near Grace Dieu Priory
notes: The pale spectral form of a woman has been reported on the roadside near the ruins of the priory. She was last seen by a police officer in 1961.

title: Whistling Ghost
notes: One of the spirits here can be heard playing what sounds like a tin whistle, while the other takes the form of a white lady who searches the building for her missing son.

title: White Lady
location_name: Tollesbury (Essex) - Road leading to the drive of Gorwell Hall
notes: Seen walking in this area, the lady is believed to be heading towards Gorwell Hall, the place where she died after having her throat cut.

title: White Lady
datetime: Mostly unknown, but cloaked figure October 1976 and circa March 1995
notes: This pale figure is said to be one of a handful of ghosts reported here over the years, normally seen in the garden. A woman who starved to death in a bedroom and an old nurse also continue to reside here, while a headless horseman has been reported just outside the grounds of the building. In 1995 a member of staff spotted a glowing man wearing a cloak who faded away, remarkably like an event in 1976 where a young man watched a cloaked figure wearing a tricorn hat glide silently across the road outside the hall before passing effortlessly over the hedgerow.
address: Dartington Trust, Dartington Hall, Totnes TQ9 6EL, UK

title: White Lady
notes: A phantom woman dressed in white is reputed to haunt this area, possibly the same entity from the nearby Dartington Hall.

title: White Lady
location_name: Trawsfynydd (Gwynedd) - Farmhouse by edge of lake
notes: The ghostly lady that haunts the village has a connection to an old farmhouse, although the exact details are not known.

title: Young Girl in White Dress
notes: Believed to have been kidnapped, raped, and finally bricked up behind a wall in the 1600's, this white woman is said to haunt the first floor hallway of the club.

title: Mrs Amphlett
notes: In his home along this street, Lord Lyttleton woke and saw a woman in white standing by his bed (named by some as being a Mrs Amphlett) who warned him he would be dead within three days. At 11pm on the third day, he dropped dead into his butler's arms at Pitt Place, in Epsom. The illustration is Lyttleton's encounter from the publication 'The Astrologer of the Nineteenth Century'.

title: Woman in White
location_name: W4 (Greater London) - Private residence, Blenheim Road
notes: A woman in a white nightdress vanished when a child asked 'mummy?'.

title: Panicking Lady
datetime: The hours leading up to midnight
notes: This white woman has been observed darting around, with body language suggesting she is in blind panic.

title: White Woman
notes: An owner of the cottage claimed it was haunted by this lady in white.

title: Murder Victim
notes: His life snuffed out at some point in the 1930s, this shade has remained earthbound since. The hall may also be haunted by a white Lady, thought to be Lady Isabella le Botelor.
address: Bewsey Old Hall, Bewsey Farm Cl, Bewsey, Warrington WA5 9PB, UK

title: White Lady
notes: A phantom white woman is said to occasionally cross the road here.

title: White Lady
datetime: Before a death of the Lord of Hungerford
notes: This phantom white woman would materialise to give warning of encroaching death.

title: White Lady
notes: This pallid female would travel the building, ending her journey on the balcony where she would disappear.

title: George
location_name: Westcliff on Sea (Essex) - Palace Theatre, currently an arts centre
notes: George has been reported sitting next to patrons during performances - they claim to have felt someone take up position next to them, though the entity remains invisible while seated. George is said to be the theatre manager who hanged himself from the fly floor due to financial difficulties just after the building opened in 1912. A white woman has been seen in the building, but it is generally agreed that these are two different forces, rather than the same ghost.

title: Young Woman
notes: The phantom figure of a woman in white was seen by a husband and wife as they drove out of Weston. It is thought that the phantom is a girl who drowned on her wedding day.
address: Lannock Hill, Weston, Hitchin SG4, UK

title: Old Soldiers
notes: The spirits here reportedly include voices that call out to witnesses over the nearby River Carey, and horsemen who gallop silently along. People have also reported pockets of cold air, and watching a white woman glide over the marsh, near where her lover was murdered by Royalists. On the anniversary of the battle, sounds of fighting have been heard.

title: Hooded Woman
location_name: Wimborne St Giles (Dorset) - St Giles Estate
notes: A phantom woman in white, with a hood covering her face, was seen to rush into a hedge. Following this, the sounds of a horse and cart could be heard travelling through woodland, though nothing could be seen.
address: Wimborne St Giles, Wimborne BH21 5NA, UK

title: Tall Man
location_name: Winchester (Hampshire) - Private house along Quarry Road
notes: A tall man wearing a black cloak, a woman dressed in white, and a figure resembling a nun are all said to have haunted this property. Two exorcisms failed to remove the entities.

title: White Lady
location_name: Woburn (Bedfordshire) - The Inn (was the Bedford Arms Inn)
notes: The misty white lady is not alone in her haunting of this building. A ghostly figure is also reported to sit by the fireplace smoking a pipe, a little phantom dog by his side.

title: White Lady Forewarning Death
location_name: Wolterton (Norfolk) - Wolterton Hall, 3 miles northwest from Blickling
notes: Appearing to warn of trouble or a death in the family, the white lady is said to be a member of the Scamler family who once owned the hall.
address: Wolterton Hall, 2 East Lodge, Wolterton, Norwich NR11 7LY, UK

title: White Lady
location_name: Wolverhampton (West Midlands) - Stag's Head public house
notes: Seen walking around the cellar, this phantom also enjoyed turning the beer pump taps off, sometimes up to twenty times a month. The entity disappeared after structural alterations were made to the building.
address: The Old Stags Head, 65 Church Hill, Penn, Wolverhampton WV4 5JB, UK

title: Woman in White
location_name: Wombwell (South Yorkshire) - Low Valley Arms public house
notes: Roger Froggatt, the landlord of this pub, was left terrified after encountering a phantom lady dressed in white in the ladies' toilet after investigating what he believed was a break in during the early hours of the morning. He described the figure as an elderly woman with her cheek and jaw structure missing.
address: Low Valley, Wombwell, Barnsley S73, UK

title: Man in Tricorn Hat
notes: A man wearing a cloak and a tricorn hat is said to drift through this wooded area. A phantom woman in white and the sound of wailing have also been reported.
address: The Pinewoods Woodland Trust, Pine Woods, Woodhall Spa LN10 6QL, UK

title: Young Lady
datetime: Weather Dependent: Stormy weather (de Tracey), early twentieth century (woman)
notes: This woman dressed in white has been observed drifting along the beach, passing effortlessly over hazardous areas of sand. Sir William de Tracey also spends time on the beach, sorting sand as penance for the murder of Thomas Becket. When a storm rolls in and blows his work away, he can be heard screaming.
address: Devon Beach Court, The Esplanade, Woolacombe EX34 7DD, UK

title: Bloodied Hands
notes: This witness was out with a group of friends, messing around in the cemetery. She turned away from the group and spotted a young girl dressed in white, holding out her hands and mouthing words that could not be heard. The witness held out her own hands, and for a fleeting moment perceived that they were covered in blood.

title: White lady
notes: Legend says this lady has scared to death at least one young bell ringer, although she is now said to have a healing effect. Poltergeist activity has also been reported, around the 1950's. A photograph taken in 1975 by Peter Berthelot appears to show a misty white lady sitting behind his wife on a pew.

title: Humming Woman
location_name: Wylfa (Gwynedd) - Area near the power station
notes: This phantom white woman was seen moving slowly along, humming a tune to herself. The ghost would vanish near to the edge of a cliff.

title: Legs
notes: Seen descending a staircase in the building, only the lower part of this male body appears. Two other spirits can be found in the bar area; that of a workman wearing a bowler hat (dressed in Victorian clothing) and a lady in a white dress who watches the fireplace.
